abstract: High-density thin-film neural interfaces face persistent packaging challenges at the electrode–electronics interface, where conventional rigid connectors impose high insertion forces, strict alignment requirements, and limited reusability. We present E-Link (Elastomeric Link), a modular 256-channel mini-pedestal connector designed to facilitate simplified, user-friendly assembly through an insertion-free, alignment-tolerant interface utilizing an elastomeric conductive interposer and a compliant threaded compression mechanism. Finite element analysis and experimental validation confirm uniform contact stress across the 16 × 16 interconnect array. The system achieves stable electrical performance, exhibiting contact impedances of 0.3-0.4 kΩ, an RMS noise floor of 2.68 ± 0.46 μV, and a connection yield exceeding 97% over 100 mating cycles. The detachable architecture reduces chronic head-mounted mass from 6.6 g to 2.8 g while maintaining safe thermal operation (30.5 °C steady-state) under a full 20 kHz sampling bandwidth. Design analysis further indicates scalability toward 1024-channel integration within the same 25-mm-diameter footprint by leveraging fine-pitch elastomeric conductive pillars. This work provides a robust and scalable connection solution for high-density flexible neural interfaces with broad application for bioelectronics.

title: ReCoTR: Reducing Semantic Cognitive Shift via Dual-Consensus Token Compression for Remote Sensing Image-Text Retrieval
authors: Jirui Huang, Yaxiong Chen, Chuang Du, Shengwu Xiong, Xiaoqiang Lu
abstract: With the rapid advancement of vision-language models (VLMs) in general-purpose settings, their application to cross-modal retrieval and semantic understanding of large-scale multimodal remote sensing (RS) data is emerging as a key enabler for urban governance, environmental monitoring, and disaster response. However, the pervasive issue of semantic shift in RS image poses a significant challenge to the transferability of pre-trained VLMs. To address this limitation, we propose ReCoTR, an enhanced CLIP-based cross-modal retrieval framework tailored for remote sensing applications. ReCoTR tackles region-level granularity bias and contextual semantic drift through a Dual Consensus Token Evaluation (DCTE) module, which leverages a mixture-of-experts strategy to fuse inter-modal semantic consensus with intra-modal structural consistency, enabling fine-grained estimation of semantic confidence for visual tokens. Moreover, to mitigate representational contamination caused by background noise, we introduce the Semantic Confidence Token Compression (SCTC) module. This module selectively filters and aggregates tokens with high semantic relevance, thus reducing redundancy and alleviating the noise amplification inherent in CLIP’s average pooling. Experimental results on three benchmark RS cross-modal retrieval datasets demonstrate that ReCoTR consistently outperforms existing methods on bidirectional image-text retrieval tasks, validating its effectiveness and robustness in remote sensing semantic alignment scenarios. Our source codes are available at: https://github.com/Jerry710/ReCoTR.git.

title: PGM: Physics-Informed Generative Meta-model for Few-shot SAR Target Recognition
authors: Ruoxue Li, Shuyuan Yang, Zhixi Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Few-shot target recognition in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery remains challenging due to limited labeled data and complex scattering characteristics. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel Physics-informed Generative Meta-model (PGM) framework that effectively integrates SAR physical principles into a unified self-supervised meta-learning paradigm. Specifically, PGM combines the backscatter relative phase image (BRPI) and the backscatter amplitude image (BAI) to cooperatively learn comprehensive target representations. First, a modality bidirectional guided encoder (MBGE) is introduced to interactively extract complementary features between BRPI and BAI. To effectively leverage unlabeled data, we develop a physics-informed generative pre-training approach utilizing a hierarchical amphi-masking strategy (HAMS). This strategy operates at both the image and feature levels and is optimized via a novel physics-informed triple loss, which incorporates BRPI into the spatial-frequency reconstruction of BAI. Following pre-training, the model undergoes meta-level discrimination adapted for few-shot scenarios. Extensive experiments on the MSTAR-SOC, MSTAR-22, and GF3-Aircraft datasets demonstrate that PGM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both 1-shot and 5-shot settings, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 4.91% on the standard MSTAR-SOC dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/SYLee0212/PGM.

title: An Automated FM Reception and Audio Processing System for Distributed Broadcast Supervision
authors: Kathawut Wattanachukul, Mahasak Ketcham, Pongsarun Boonyopakorn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Reliable reception and monitoring of Frequency Modulation (FM) broadcast signals are essential for regulatory supervision and public communication quality assurance. Conventional monitoring approaches often rely on manual field inspections, which are time-consuming, resource-intensive, and unable to provide continuous coverage. This paper proposes a network-enabled FM reception system designed for automated signal acquisition, processing, and centralized monitoring. The proposed receiver architecture integrates an FM antenna, Software-Defined Radio (SDR) module, embedded processing unit, and network communication interface to support continuous signal reception and remote system management. An automated audio processing framework developed using Windows PowerShell is implemented to handle incoming audio files from distributed reception nodes. The framework performs noise reduction at level 30 and applies high-pass and low-pass filtering to enhance speech clarity in recorded FM audio signals. Experimental evaluation was conducted using ten OGG (Opus) audio files under continuous operation conditions. The results demonstrate stable system performance, with processing times ranging from 3.48 to 4.53 minutes and an average execution time of approximately 4.21 minutes per file. The enhanced audio signals exhibit improved intelligibility, enabling more effective monitoring and verification of broadcast content. The proposed system reduces manual operational effort, improves reception monitoring reliability, and provides a scalable infrastructure for large-area FM broadcast supervision. The findings confirm the feasibility of deploying distributed FM reception nodes with automated processing capability in real-world regulatory environments.

title: Road Lane Detection System Using OpenCV By Enhancing Input Quality
authors: Nikhil Tiwari, Rahul Shivhare, Vishal Kumar, Mukul Kashyap, Mohd. Aahad, Aarti Verma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Find lanes for a vehicle is very safe. Lane detection is a very important part of self-driving cars and driving systems. A car needs to identify the lane corners so it can drive safe, prevent mis happening, and help the driver to see better. There are many computer vision tools available, but OpenCV is widely used because it is easy, efficient, and works in real-time and real world.In this paper, I reviewed existing research on lane detection systems built using OpenCV. The paper covers everything from how images are generated to how the lanes are shown on display. Lane detection faces several challenges in real-world problems - like bad weather, blurry lane markings, and curved roads. I've discussed these issues with solutions.I compared old methods (which uses very old algorithms) with modern methods that mix conventional techniques with machine learning. Finally, I highlighted what OpenCV-based lane detection does good work, where it stuck, and what research is still needed to discover. I've also suggested some ways to improve these systems for good accuracy, reliability and efficiency.

title: Hybrid Big Data-LLM Framework for Intelligent Scientific Literature Mining
authors: Md. Ishtiaque Alam, Ruhul Amin Md Rashed, Partha Chakraborty, Sraboni Clara Mohonta, Hasan Imam, Mohammad Muzahidur Rahman Bhuiyan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rapid increase in scientific publications has posed a serious problem in the effective discovery, analysis and synthesis of pertinent knowledge under various fields of research. The conventional methods of literature mining usually find it difficult to handle large heterogeneous datasets preserving textual background and semantic precision. In order to overcome these shortcomings, this paper will present Hybrid Big Data-Large Language Model (LLM) Framework to Intelligent Scientific Literature Mining that combines scalable distributed data processing with state-of-the-art transformer-based language intelligence. The suggested framework uses big data technologies to ingest, store, and process scholarly repositories in high throughput, and has modules powered by LLMs to understand the semantic information in deep, and to summarize and analyse citations, topic modelling, and research trends. The multi-layer architecture that incorporates data orchestration, neural knowledge representation, and adaptive retrieval mechanisms is presented to improve the accuracy and minimize the information overload. Moreover, the framework supports embedding strategy and reinforcement driven filtering to enhance ranking of relevance and reduce redundancy in the extracted insights. The experimental analysis shows that the hybrid system has a much higher semantic accuracy, retrieval efficiency, scalability and rate of knowledge discovery compared to the traditional literature mining systems. Findings have shown enhanced contextual mapping of research subjects and easy discovery of new scientific pathways. The framework is also useful in facilitating interdisciplinary exploration in the sense that it allows automatic connectivity between knowledge spaces that used to be unconnected. The study will provide an intelligent and scalable scientific discovery paradigm of next-generation scientific discovery systems, which enable accelerated innovation processes and evidence-based research. The model is especially applicable when dealing with data-intensive environments, in which it is important that researchers, institutions and policy-makers quickly gain an understanding of the changing literature.

title: Smart Crop Recommendation System using TabNet and LLM Integration
authors: T. C. Sabareeswari, P. Pattunnarajan, S Vidhyashree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: A crop recommendation system that integrates LLM and uses the TabNet algorithm has been proposed. When applied to large, diverse tabular agricultural datasets, previous models using Random Forest, CatBoost, and XGBoost often suffered from performance and scalability issues. TabNet overcomes these limitations by offering feature selection, high predictive accuracy, and efficient management of raw tabular data. For this study, a dataset comprising essential soil and environmental parameters, including pH, temperature, humidity, rainfall, nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium, was collected. Two subsets of the data are created, one for model training and the other for assessment. The provided dataset was used to train the TabNet model, and metrics were used to assess its performance. With $98.12 \%$ accuracy, 0.9813 precision, 0.9812 recall, and 0.9812 F1 score, the results demonstrated strong predictive performance. The predictions were validated across a variety of crop types using visualisation tools such as ROC curves and confusion matrices. Additionally, the system’s extensive language model enables users to interact with it via queries that support decision-making.

title: Enhancing Product Recommendations with Transformer-Based Sentiment Analysis
authors: Jarin Tasnim Lamia, Bijon Mallik, Ahmed Shafkat, Asir Saadat, Fatema Tasnim Mimia, Md Mahabubur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: In the evolving e-commerce environment, sentimentbased product recommendations have played a vital role in enhancing customer satisfaction. This paper overcomes the limitations of conventional recommendation schemes, which rely heavily on basic measures or one-model solutions, with a tendency to yield lower accuracy and explainability. To demonstrate enhanced performance in e-commerce product review classification, we provide a sentiment analysis hybrid framework that combines traditional machine learning models, a RoBERTa transformer model optimized on gathered dataset, and structured metadata. To guarantee both depth and interpretability of the features, the method uses contextual embeddings for the fusion, VADER and TextBlob sentiment scores, and TF-IDF vectorization. According to experiments, the fine-tuned RoBERTa outperformed CNNLSTM hybrids and traditional models, achieving 97% accuracy and 0.98 F1-score. Additionally, the hybrid method enhanced resilience across several validation folds and encouraged precisionrecall balancing. The results demonstrate the potential of combining structured metadata and transformer models to produce sentiment-based recommendations that are accurate, scalable, and understandable. This work builds a useful foundation for more intelligent, real-time recommendation engines on online stores and advances automated consumer feedback processing.

title: PDXAI: Parkinson’s Disease Prediction by employing Deep learning with Enhanced XAI
authors: Gaganjot Kaur, Debyanshu Tiwari, Niyaz Ahmad Wani, Jatin Bedi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease is a chronic neurological disorder that frequently presents with subtle speech impairments and is often misdiagnosed in its early phases. The process begins when the brain deteriorates the nerve cells that are responsible for dopamine production. This results in the body moving uncoordinatedly. Recently, there is no permanent cure for this disease; it can be treated with physiotherapy or medications; in some cases, surgery is performed, such as deep brain stimulation. To overcome this, we have used a prediction strategy that uses Shapley additive explanations (SHAP) to improve interpretation and a deep learning-based model that uses feedforward neural networks (FNN) for classification with an overall precision of 0.9591±0.0231%. Despite precise forecasting, the explain-ability components identify important clinical characteristics that provide meaningful insights that could aid neurologists in better treatment planning and diagnosis. This research shows how combining explainable AI (SHAP) and neural models can improve clinical trust and technical reliance in the premature identification of Parkinson’s Disease (PD).

title: Edge-Enabled Multi-Source Sensing IoT System with Adaptive Sampling for Ecological Monitoring
authors: Jingxing Liu, Ziqi Wang, Wenxiao Fang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Current ecological monitoring systems are typically confined to single-parameter sensing and suffer from severe power constraints. Traditional fixed-frequency sampling and connection-oriented protocols generate massive redundant data during animal dormancy and deplete energy through repetitive network handshakes. To address these bottlenecks, this paper proposes an intelligent Internet of Things system utilizing a collaborative “Device-Edge-Cloud” architecture for multi-dimensional behavioral monitoring. Diverging from simple hardware integration, miniature multi-sensor terminal nodes employ an edge-assisted, bio-kinematics-driven adaptive sampling strategy alongside the connectionless ESP-NOW protocol. This synergy dynamically scales the sampling frequency from 1 packet per hour to 15 seconds per packet based on real-time motion states, while edge gateways handle heavy communication overheads to the cloud. By bypassing energy-intensive network setups, quantitative theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed architecture achieves an 46.5% reduction in total daily wireless transmissions compared to fixed-frequency baselines. Ultimately, this design effectively propels wildlife ecological monitoring toward a highly integrated, intelligent, and ultra-low-power paradigm.

title: A BERT Model for Chinese Named Entity Recognition in Electronic Information Equipment*
authors: Mengwu Xue, Xiaofang Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In recent years, the deep integration of artificial intelligence and electronic information equipment has created an urgent demand for knowledge extraction from technical documents. Conventional named-entity-recognition (NER) models suffer from performance limitations in this domain because of its high degree of specialization, dense knowledge, and complex entity boundaries. To address these issues, we propose a multi-level NER approach that fuses large language models with deep learning. The architecture consists of three neural tiers: BERT is adopted to generate dynamic contextualized word embeddings; a residual bidirectional LSTM encoder captures contextual semantic dependencies; and a conditional random field (CRF) layer performs globally optimal label decoding. Experiments show that the model attains an F1 of 81.21% on the general-domain SKE benchmark, demonstrating strong generalization, and achieves a breakthrough F1 of 94.49% on the EIED test set—an 8.88-percentage-point improvement over the BERT-BiLSTM-CRF baseline. Ablation studies confirm that the synergy between residual connections and the CRF layer significantly boosts entity-boundary accuracy and effectively mitigates gradient vanishing.

title: Biomedical Relation Extraction via Cluster-Description Co-Evolution Hierarchical RAG
authors: Yufei Shang, Yanrong Guo
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Document-level biomedical relation extraction (Bio-RE) requires reasoning over relational evidence distributed across entire documents, yet commonly relies on highly condensed biomedical texts that lack sufficient contextual information. Although retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) offers a natural way to incorporate external knowledge, existing LLM-based methods typically adopt flat retrieval, which is prone to noisy and topically drifting contexts. To address these issues, we propose CDC Hierarchical RAG, a structured retrieval-augmented framework that organizes external biomedical knowledge into a multilevel hierarchical representation. The proposed framework enables cluster-first retrieval and coarse-to-fine selection, improving retrieval relevance. Moreover, we introduce a Cluster-Description Co-evolution (CDC) mechanism that refines cluster centroids using LLM-generated cluster descriptions as semantic feedback, producing more semantically coherent hierarchical clusters. Experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate strong and competitive performance, particularly for inter-sentence relation extraction.

title: Design of a Gated-VDD Assisted SRAM Cell for Enhanced Standby Power Efficiency
authors: Keith R Fernandes, Ranjitha Shetty, Vinutha D, Sandeep B, Gowtham R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM) remains a vital element in modern VLSI systems because of its high speed, stability, and suitability for low-power applications. The conventional 6-transistor (6T) SRAM cell is widely used in cache and portable electronic devices due to its efficient data storage and fast access capability. In this work, a 6T SRAM cell is designed using 45nm CMOS technology and its read/write behavior, stability, and power consumption are analyzed through simulations in the Cadence Virtuoso environment. In addition to the conventional design, a basic Gated-VDD structure is incorporated, in which a control transistor disconnects the supply during idle periods to reduce leakage current. Functional and power analyses are performed for both configurations to study their active and standby characteristics. The results demonstrate reliable operation of the conventional SRAM cell and highlight the potential of the gated approach for reducing standby leakage in low-power integrated systems.

title: Recognition of Underwater Acoustics Targets via Random Forest with Continuous Spectrum Peak Features
authors: XiaoBin Li, Simao Ye, Xinyu Gu, Chao Ren
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As marine development advances, target recognition has emerged as a critical technical challenge in underwater acoustic signal processing. Traditional physical feature-based methods heavily rely on specialized knowledge and experience, exhibiting poor generalization capabilities in complex real-world marine environments. Existing deep neural networks suffer from low interpretability, minimal manual tuning flexibility, and require significant computational overhead to achieve optimal performance. Therefore, this paper proposes a random forest classification method for acoustic target recognition based on continuous spectrum peak features. By analyzing continuous spectrum characteristics, it extracts multi-band spectral peak feature parameters (including number, location, value, and prominence), statistically analyzes these numerical features, and utilizes the random forest algorithm to achieve target classification without explicit thresholds. Experimental results demonstrate that this method achieves higher average classification accuracy than that of SVM on real ocean datasets. This study provides an effective solution for acoustic target recognition that embodies physical characteristics, is lightweight, and offers high interpretability.

title: Vision-Based Floor Number Recognition and Elevator Interaction System for Indoor Service Robots
authors: Arkom Thaicharoen, Mahasak Ketcham, Pongsarun Boonyopakorn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: The proposed framework consists of a sequential processing pipeline including image acquisition, preprocessing, interface localization, region-of-interest extraction, and semantic recognition. Circular button regions and floor display areas are first localized using a detection mechanism, after which individual regions are cropped and classified to determine their semantic labels, such as floor numbers (e.g., 1, G, LG, LM, B1, B2) and functional controls (e.g., door open, door close, emergency call). The perception outputs are then structured to support higher-level robotic decision-making tasks, including floor selection and elevator status verification. Experimental observations using real elevator panel images demonstrate that the prototype system can achieve moderate detection performance under realistic indoor conditions characterized by reflections, lighting variability, and viewpoint changes. The expected button localization accuracy is approximately 72-82%, while semantic label recognition accuracy ranges from 65-78%, resulting in an overall end-to-end accuracy of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$68-75 \%$</tex>. These results highlight both the feasibility of the proposed approach and the need for further improvements in dataset scale, model training, and reflection-robust perception strategies. The proposed framework provides an initial foundation for developing intelligent robotic perception systems capable of interacting with human-designed infrastructure. Future work will focus on improving recognition accuracy through domain-specific deep learning models, data augmentation, and real-time deployment optimization for service robots operating in smart building environments.

title: Adaptive Real-Time Sign Language Recognition Using Deep Learning and Temporal Modeling
authors: Sugrish V M, Velan R, Lakshminarayanan S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: To fill the existing communication gap between the deaf and hearing communities, it is necessary to go beyond the conventional systems that can recognize isolated and static signs only. In this regard, we have designed a state-of-the-art real-time sign language recognition system that employs a hybrid deep learning model, combining CNNs with temporal modeling to analyze continuous sentences. By using MediaPipe for hand landmark detection and TensorFlow-based spatial feature extraction, our system can turn a conventional webcam into a robust accessibility device.One of the major breakthroughs in our system is its adaptive learning module, which allows the system to adapt to the unique signing patterns of each user. To bridge the gap between the complexity of AI and user trust, we incorporated Grad-CAM visualization in our system. With an accuracy rate of 94.3% at 30 FPS, our modular system is hardware-independent. It provides a scalable and cost-effective solution for multilingual support and text-to-speech functionality, ultimately promoting inclusivity in the healthcare, education, and public service sectors.

title: IRS-Assisted UAV Integrated Sensing and Secure Communication Systems
authors: Shuhan Zhang, Jun Cui, Xueyan Cao, Tao Ren
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems are subject to security vulnerabilities. This paper investigates secure communication enhancement for intelligent reflecting-surface (IRS)-assisted UAV ISAC systems by jointly optimizing UAV active beamforming, IRS phase shifts, and UAV trajectory, subject to sensing-rate constraints. To address this challenging non-convex problem, we propose a proximal policy optimization (PPO)-based deep reinforcement learning framework that enables end-to-end optimization and effectively handles dynamic channel conditions without explicit modeling. The framework incorporates key mechanisms, including dynamic noise decay, advantage normalization, gradient clipping, and a cyclic experience replay buffer, to ensure training stability and efficiency. Simulations demonstrate a 22% improvement in secrecy rate over the deep deterministic policy gradient method, thereby validating the superiority of the enhanced PPO approach.

title: Physical-Constraint-Guided VC-OMP for Ultra-Reliable Short-Packet Communications
authors: Shuyi Zhang, Xiaotong Shi, Ziyun Liu, Jiahao Hou
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Driven by the stringent ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) requirements of the industrial internet of things, this paper investigates compressed sensingbased sparse signal reconstruction for short-packet transmissions. Traditional greedy decoding algorithms suffer from severe noiseinduced error propagation in low signal-to-noise ratio environments. To address this, we propose a prior-aided validity-check orthogonal matching pursuit (VC-OMP) algorithm that leverages the intrinsic structural constraints of industrial data. Specifically, we translate physical state mutual exclusions and communication protocol restrictions into a deterministic invalid index set. By embedding a deterministic masking operator during the atom selection phase, VC-OMP forces invalid subspaces to zero, fundamentally blocking false-alarm paths. Simulation results demonstrate that, while introducing a marginal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}(N)$</tex> lookup overhead, VC-OMP significantly enhances the reconstruction success rate under low signal-to-noise ratio conditions. Furthermore, its decoding latency is substantially lower than that of multi-branch schemes like CoSaMP and MMP, yielding a highly reliable and computationally efficient solution tailored for URLLC scenarios.

title: Faults Detection in Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) Using One Dimensional Convolution Neural Network CNN-1D
authors: Fedi Mokhtar Gran, Khaoula Bouazzi, Wajdi Saadaoui, Abdelhamid Helali
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Nowadays, wireless sensor networks (WSNs) show remarkable technological advances and they are integrated into several critical domains such as healthcare, military, and industrial monitoring applications. Due to environmental changes and unpredictable failures, the quality of measurements can be affected. The study aims to enhance the ability of WSNs to detect and predict faults by leveraging sophisticated machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models, leading to proactive equipment management, reduced downtime, and overall improvements in operational efficiency. The main objective of this study is to explore and analyze various ML and DL approaches for fault detection in data collected from industrial WSNs and to develop our own model. Advanced data preprocessing techniques will be applied to improve the quality of incoming information. A detailed evaluation of the developed model will be conducted to ensure his effectiveness in industrial monitoring applications. A comparison of the developed model with recent works will be undertaken to enhance the value of our work.

title: Frequency Domain Anti-Jamming Decision for Satellite Communication Based on MLSTM-DQN
authors: Peng Wei, Qi Wang, Cheng Li, Xinjin Lu, Shicheng Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the optimal frequency-domain anti-jamming decision-making problem for satellite communication systems with wideband real-time sensing capabilities under complex interference conditions, this paper proposes a Double Deep Q-Learning (DDQN) model based on a Multidimensional Long Short-Term Memory (MLSTM) network, using the transmission success indicator based on an SINR threshold as the reward. Through a preprocessing step applied to the frequency spectrum vector, the effect of an action on the subsequent system state is decoupled. This reformulation simplifies the underlying model to a single-step Markov Decision Process (MDP). Consequently, the MLSTM-DQN model can be equivalently expressed as a single-step maximization problem, which aligns with the Bellman optimality equation for multi-step cumulative discounted rewards. In contrast to existing models such as RCNN and HDRL, the proposed MLSTM-DQN requires only the current time-step state as its input. Its convergence rate and final performance are comparable to those of the best-performing improved RCNN model. Furthermore, our model reduces the number of trainable parameters by approximately 3.5 times and cuts inference computational costs by roughly 40 times. These improvements make the model conducive to rapid training and facilitate its lightweight deployment across various complex jamming scenarios.

title: Critical Factor Influencing AI Adoption in Thai SMEs: Insights from a Delphi Study
authors: Supanut Klinthuesil, Sakchai Tangwannawit
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This study examines the key determinants of artificial intelligence (AI) adoption among Thai small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through a Delphibased expert consensus approach grounded in the Technology-Organization-Environment (TOE) framework. Two iterative Delphi rounds were conducted with a panel of ten experts representing academia, industry, and consultancy. Consensus was assessed using the Content Validity Ratio (CVR), with a minimum acceptable threshold of 0.67. The findings reveal 15 factors considered critical to AI adoption. From a technological perspective, cost and return on investment (ROI), system compatibility, implementation complexity, decision-support capability, and data security emerge as central considerations. Organizationally, emphasis is placed on strategic planning, the ability to recognize business opportunities, and effective resource allocation. In terms of the external environment, competitive pressure, government support, economic uncertainty, and evolving consumer behavior are identified as influential drivers. The results support the applicability of the TOE framework within the context of a developing economy and suggest that AI adoption in Thai SMEs is shaped by a combination of financial considerations, organizational preparedness, and external market forces. This study contributes empirical evidence to the emerging body of AI adoption research and offers practical implications for SME stakeholders seeking to implement AI technologies.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis and Rainfall Forecasting in Cox’s Bazar using Seasonal ARIMA Model
authors: Panna Shil, Md. Istihad Bhuiyan, Md. Eyakub Ali, Md. Shadman Shakib, Badhan Dey, Md. Hasibul Hoque, Fazlul Karim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Assessing climate change, particularly rainfall variability, is a global concern due to its significant fluctuations over time and space. This study develops a Seasonal ARIMA (SARIMA) model to predict monthly rainfall in Cox’s Bazar, Bangladesh, using the latest data from 1981 to 2022 obtained from the Bangladesh Meteorological Department. Among five models tested, the ARIMA $(0,0,1)(1,1,2)_{12}$ model achieved the lowest AIC (3192.96) and BIC (3213.95). Model performance was evaluated using several statistical metrics for both training (RMSE: 5.396, MAE: 3.388, MASE: 0.705, ME: 0.299) and test sets (RMSE: 5.934, MAE: 3.673, MASE: 0.764, ME: 0.389). Stationarity was assessed using the Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test, which returned a p-value of 1.06, indicating nonstationarity in the original data. Residual diagnostics, including the Ljung-Box test ($\mathbf{p}$-value: $\mathbf{0. 4 4 1 8}$), confirmed no significant autocorrelation. The model successfully forecasted average rainfall, such as 30.17 mm for June 2025, with a 95% confidence interval of 18.03 mm to 42.32 mm. This SARIMA model provides a reliable tool for capturing both seasonal and nonseasonal rainfall patterns, aiding in effective water resource management in the region.

title: Interpretable Transformer-Based Model Management for Recommender Systems
authors: N. Hemanth Kumar, Peramalasetty Ramesh, T. Roseline Velankanni, Ramya M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Personalized recommender systems shape content discovery at scale and must balance accuracy, interpretability and operational cost. Interpretable Transformer-Based Model Management for Recommender Systems (I-TransManage) is proposed to address model opacity and lifecycle inefficiencies in modern recommender pipelines. Existing transformer-based recommenders improved sequential modeling but often produced misleading attention and lacked automated lifecycle controls for calibration and cost-aware pruning. I-TransManage is a hybrid architecture that integrates a non-stationary transformer encoder, a graph-aware transformer module and an attention-calibration interpretability layer with a model-management controller that triggers retraining, pruning and explainability extraction. The method combines position-aware time decay, graph fusion and adversarial attention calibration to improve decision fidelity and attribution quality. The method was evaluated on MovieLens Beliefs Dataset 2024 (GroupLens), which contained 1,723,753 historical ratings and 28,457 belief responses from 3,199 users. In experiments I-TransManage increased NDCG@10 by 0.037 (11.9%) versus a transformer production baseline and reduced expected calibration error (ECE) by 0.024 (42.9%) while supporting automated cost-constrained pruning. These results indicate that I-TransManage is suitable for production recommender deployments that require interpretable, calibrated and cost-aware models.

title: DRL-driven Fault-tolerant Routing Strategy in LEO Mega-constellation
authors: Zhiyi Kuang, Hai Yang, Pengge Ma, Jinwang Qian
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Low earth orbit (LEO) mega-constellations are becoming essential infrastructure for global broadband and space-air-ground networking. However, some satellites in constellation may fail inevitably due to the collision of satellite or space-debris or the aging of onboard payloads. Then, the relay capability of the faulty satellite also fails, resulting in interrupted services and deteriorated network performance. To address this challenge, a failure-congestion coupled risk-aware multi-objective joint optimization routing (FCRMJ) scheme is proposed. FCRMJ first formulas the evaluation criteria for satellite failure probability and congestion level in LEO mega-constellation network. Second, it establishes a fault-domain-aware routing framework based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) to optimize the selection of routing transmission path. Then, the paths of diverse services in network will be away from the high failure and congestion probability areas. Simulation shows that FCRMJ scheme could effectively decrease the failure probability of transmission paths in network, and it also improve the whole network congestion and delivery capability with the cost of slightly deteriorated delay performance.

title: Digital Twin Models for Personalized Healthcare Diagnosis, Treatment, and Prognosis
authors: Priti Mishra, Sateesh Kumar Rongali, A Manusha Reddy, Priya Nandihal, Padmavathi R, Kode Jaya Prakash
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper proposes an Adaptive Neuro-Symbolic Twin Framework (ANSTF) that should allow in deciding healthcare fundamentally based on precision by dynamically and individually modeling patient conditions. The proposed system combines the multimodal medical data such as physiological signals, electronic health records, and genomic data with the aid of a hierarchical attention-based data assimilation engine. Symbolic causal reasoning is used to combine deep neural representations to obtain interpretable clinical embeddings that contain not only statistical correlations, but also mechanistic insights. Individualized biophysical simulation of organs dynamics and therapeutic effects then proceeds to permit the virtual experimentations towards the optimization of diagnosis and the treatment of the illness. A reinforcement optimization mechanism is required to criticize the therapeutic strategies in real-time, adjusting them in response to the real-time feedback, and balancing clinical efficacy, safety and patient-specific goals in the ability to ensure adaptive decision-making. Such a hybrid system satisfies the void between the medical explain ability and the data-driven learning as well as providing clinicians with the transparent constantly evolving digital substitutes of prognosis, intervention, and prediction. This type of experimental evaluation on non-homogeneous groups of patients is more accurate, flexible and cognizant compared to existing procedures of profound or guideline-led measures. The ANSTF model takes a fundamental step in the direction of autonomous intelligent health care systems which are ethically consistent and explainable and able to develop with the unique physiologic and clinical course of any specific patient. The proposed ANSTF system achieved an average prediction accuracy of 96.4, which showed its outstanding reliability and accuracy in tailor made healthcare prediction.

title: On Average Symbol Error Rate of Terahertz Communications Using Compound Channel Model
authors: Tingguang Gan, Shuping Dang, Xiangcheng Li, Handing Lu, Zhenrong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the average symbol error rate (SER) performance of terahertz (THz) communication systems modeled through a compound THz channel that jointly captures the effects of multi-path fading, dynamic shadowing, molecular absorption, and propagation loss. Building on a statistical characterization of the end-to-end signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), we derive closed-form expressions for the average SER under various phase-shift keying (PSK) modulation schemes. Additionally, we present asymptotic expressions of the average SER in the high SNR regime to extract key insights into THz system behaviors. Our analysis reveals how key channel parameters, particularly the shadowing and fading characteristics, affect the error performance of THz communication systems. Monte Carlo simulations shown in this paper confirm the accuracy of the derived SER expressions and examine the effects of different compound model parameters on the error performance, offering a comprehensive framework for more complex SER analysis.

title: Optimization Accuracy of DDoS Attacks using Boosting Algorithm in Cloud Computing
authors: Mukul Ahirwal, Puneet Nema, Vivek Richhariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cloud computing (CC) offers scalable and on-demand access to computing resources, but it also introduces critical security vulnerabilities, particularly Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks. These attacks target the availability of cloud services by overwhelming them with malicious traffic from distributed sources. Traditional rule-based detection systems struggle to precisely detect and stop such assaults in real time since cloud systems are dynamic and complicated. This study investigates the application of boosting algorithms—such as AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting Machine (GBM), and XGBoost—to enhance the accuracy of DDoS detection in cloud computing. Boosting algorithms work by combining multiple weak learners into a strong classifier, making them highly effective in capturing subtle patterns in traffic data. This research uses labeled datasets like CICDDoS2019 for training and testing, applying boosting models to optimize detection accuracy, precision, and recall. Results show that boosting algorithms outperform conventional machine learning techniques in identifying attack signatures with reduced false positives. The study concludes that boosting-based models provide a lightweight, accurate, and scalable solution for DDoS detection, contributing significantly to secure and reliable cloud services.

title: Employ Deep Learning for Remote Sensing: Land Use Classification and Change Detection Using Multiband Aerial Photographs
authors: Venkateswaran Petchiappan, Mohammad Kanan, Shiva Shankar Mummidi, G. Sujatha, B. Srilatha, C. Vivek Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: As urbanisation, climate environmental degradation increase, precise and scalable land use monitoring systems are needed. Remote sensing utilising multiband airborne images can be used to study the planet’s surface at large scales. Land-use classification and change detection using human-engineered features and classical machine learning models fail when faced with complex spatial patterns, spectral variability, and large volumes of data. This research uses multiband aerial data to automatically categorise land uses and detect changes, thereby accurately assessing heterogeneous landscapes using deep learning. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained with spectral-spatial data classify places as urban, aquatic, agricultural, or desert. Surface texture and spectral fingerprints can be derived from multiband aerial photos covering visible and near-infrared wavelengths. This improves class separability. A change detection module that leverages deep feature differencing across images from multiple dates can accurately identify land-use changes over time, enabling more precise temporal analysis. The system uses spatial alignment, data augmentation, and radiometric normalisation to improve the model’s generalizability and robustness. We demonstrate that deep learning handles non-linear interactions and spatial dependencies better than standard classifiers.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Deep Learning for Robust Intracranial Haemorrhage Subtype Classification
authors: Bhargava Ram Edla, Dhanalakshmi R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Accurate and reliable intracranial haemorrhage (ICH) subtype classification is critical for timely treatment decisions, yet existing deep learning approaches lack crucial uncertainty quantification for clinical safety. We propose an uncertainty-aware EfficientNetB4 framework that incorporates persistent Monte Carlo dropout to estimate prediction reliability while achieving competitive performance for subtype detection. Our method processes CT scans using radiologist-inspired multi-window fusion and provides explainable Grad-CAM heatmaps. Using the RSNA-ICH dataset to evaluate the system, we observe a mean AUC of 0.919 across six haemorrhage subtypes, while achieving a specificity of 0.93 (avoiding 93% of potential false-positive alerts). Notably, the uncertainty measurement of 0.917 correlates with prediction errors and will enable rapid identification of cases with low prediction accuracy. However, during the calibration evaluation, our current model overestimated the frequency of haemorrhage detection; thus, it is necessary to employ post-hoc calibration methods. This study demonstrates that explicitly quantifying prediction uncertainty alongside diagnostic accuracy yields a clinically viable framework that balances reliable performance with safety-aware confidence estimation, facilitating responsible deployment in clinical settings.

title: Engineering Analysis and System Architecture Innovation of LFMCW Radar via Geometric Modeling and Integrated Sensing-Communication Design
authors: Yansong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive engineering analysis of Linear Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (LFMCW) radar systems, progressing from fundamental time-frequency geometric modeling to advanced waveform design and integrated sensing-communication architecture. By rigorously deriving key performance parameters and evaluating practical trade-offs in multi-target scenarios, the study clarifies misconceptions in conventional radar range equations and proposes a novel receiver architecture based on bandpass undersampling. The integration of communication modulation techniques with radar signal processing is also explored, emphasizing spectral compatibility and hardware efficiency. The findings not only reinforce theoretical foundations but also offer actionable insights for the physical-layer design of next-generation intelligent perception networks. To strengthen practical verification, reproducible simulations are further provided to validate the revised range relations under multi-target scenarios with varying distances and velocities, quantify the proposed bandpass-undersampling receiver performance in terms of sampling efficiency, signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), and multi-target resolution, and characterize the integrated sensing-communication (ISAC) trade-off through spectral utilization metrics and communication bit error rate (BER) analysis. These comprehensive validations address previous limitations in experimental verification and provide concrete evidence of the system's practical feasibility.

title: Energy-Efficient Resource Accounting for IoT-Based AMI Using Federated Reinforcement Learning and TinyML Optimization
authors: Nitin Thapliyal, Anzar Ahmad, Jyoti Kaurav, Nozima Dusmukhammedova, Rakhimjon Rajapboyevich Rakhimov, Karimov Rashid Salayevich
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) and Internet of Things (IoT) deployments require energy-efficient resource accounting to scale in power distribution systems. Existing approaches require centralized data collection, neglect edge compute and quantization costs, and poorly handle temporal nonstationarity. This paper proposes EERAF-Tiny, a Federated Reinforcement Learning (FRL) agent integrated with TinyML optimization for on-device inference and compressed deployment. EERAF-Tiny used Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) in a federated aggregation loop and applied structured pruning, post-training quantization, and compiler-level kernel generation to reduce inference energy and model size. The method learned per-node accounting policies that balanced estimation RMSE, computed energy, communication, and carbon proxies. Evaluation used a public AMI dataset simulated into 1,000 heterogeneous IoT meter nodes under realistic network conditions. Experiments compared EERAF-Tiny to XGBoost and a federated DDQN hybrid baseline. EERAF-Tiny reduced accounting RMSE by 28.3% versus XGBoost and 15.1% versus Fed-DDQN while lowering per-inference energy by 42 mJ, model size by 78 KB, and communication per round by 67 kB. These results indicate that FRL combined with TinyML enables accurate, low-energy resource accounting for IoT-based AMI while preserving data locality and privacy and security.

title: Bayesian Coordination Failure under Belief Asymmetry in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Sanchari Ghosh, Sandeep Mondal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Coordination among autonomous agents is a fundamental challenge in multi-agent systems. It is particularly relevant when agents operate under incomplete information about the preferences or risk choices of others. This paper studies a Bayesian Coordination game in which agents form beliefs about opponent types and choose actions accordingly. It features Bayesian Nash Equilibrium and establishes that the belief asymmetry alone can generate coordination failure, even when coordination is mutually beneficial. To complement the analytical results, a simulation experiment and a real-world-inspired case study are conducted, which together illustrate how variations in agents’ beliefs induce sharp transitions between coordination and miscoordination outcomes. Thus, the study provides a tractable theoretical basis for understanding belief-driven equilibrium selection in multi-agent-based decision systems operating under uncertainty and/or asymmetric information.

title: Optimize Accuracy of Fingerprint Feature Extraction for Biometrics using Convolution Network
authors: Akanksha Mishra, Ritu Malviya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Fingerprint recognition has become one of the most dependable biometric authentication methods because of its unique and consistent characteristics. However, fingerprint-based systems often face challenges such as poor image quality, incomplete impressions, skin flexibility, and attempts at spoofing. To tackle these issues, this study explores the optimization of fingerprint feature extraction using CN architecture. The proposed framework combines image enhancement, minutiae detection, and descriptor learning within a single process. Initially, a CN based U-net architecture is used to improve ridge clarity and estimate the orientation field, enhancing robustness against low-quality inputs. Minutiae points are then detected using a key point-based CN architecture that accurately localizes and determines the orientation of these features. To boost discriminative power, a deep descriptor embedding architecture trained with triplet and contrastive losses is employed, ensuring reliable fingerprint matching even under distortion and partial overlap. Extensive data augmentation strategies, including elastic distortions, noise addition, and rotation, are applied to improve the model's generalization. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets such as SOCOFing show significant improvements in EER and TAR compared to traditional methods. The findings indicate that CN-based feature extraction not only improves accuracy and robustness but also enhances the security and reliability of biometric systems against spoofing and environmental changes.

title: Parallel Block-Based Binary Locally Repairable Codes for Scalable and Efficient Distributed Storage
authors: Ziwen Wei, Yidong Wang, Meijuan Li, Xiaolin Qin, Dan Tang, Junyan Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Locally recoverable codes (LRCs) are widely used in distributed storage systems to reduce repair bandwidth and latency. Traditional LRC constructions derive local and global constraints within a unified algebraic framework, which creates strong parameter interdependence and limits scalability. Meanwhile, many existing schemes also rely on algorithms over high-order finite fields, increasing computational overhead. This paper proposes a class of binary locally repairable codes, named Block-Based Binary Locally Repairable Codes (BBLRCs), that use a parallel block structures with local and global blocks. The global blocks consist of BRS code, and the local blocks are binary matrix representations of quaternary optimal LRCs. Theoretical analysis showed that BBLRCs possess strong erasure-correction capability and support length scalability. Experimental results indicated that BBLRC achieves encoding and global-repair performance comparable to that of BRS codes while significantly outperforming RS and q-ary LRC schemes. Encoding throughput improved by 5-10 times, decoding throughput improved by 7-10 times, and local-repair efficiency was substantially enhanced.

title: Thirsty AI Challenge for a Sustainable Future
authors: Suman Chahar, Urvashi Sugandh, Kuldeep Singh Kaswan, Jagjit Singh Dhatterwal, Anupam Baliyan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The carbon footprint of AI has been increasingly under scrutiny by the public. However, the no less significant water (withdrawal and consumption) footprint of AI has mostly been overlooked. For instance, training the GPT-3 language model in Microsoft’s cutting-edge U.S. data centers amounts to directly evaporating 700,000 liters of clean freshwater, but that information has been hidden from view. More importantly, the AI demand at the world level is predicted to require a withdrawal of 4.2–6.6 bm 3 in 2027, which is higher than the whole annual water withdrawal for four to six Denmark’s or half of the UK. This is alarming given that there is currently a global freshwater crisis. In a response to the world’s water challenges, AI can and also should assume social responsibility and set an example by mitigating its own water footprint. In this paper, we propose a principled approach to measure the water footprint of AI, and we raise the question of spatial-temporal diversities to AI’s runtime water efficiency. Lastly, we emphasize that just like carbon footprint, water footprint must be addressed holistically to realize truly sustainable AI.

title: Enhancing Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis Through Knowledge Graphs and Multiview Representation Learning
authors: Srilakshmi V, Varun Reddy V, Uday Kiran G, B Veerasekhar Reddy, Kanduri Sri Snigdha, D. Cherith Josiah
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The evolution of Fine-Grained Opinion Understanding in User-Generated Content has highlighted the limitations of classical sentiment analysis techniques, which typically operate at the Sentence Level, Document Level, and the like. The contribution of this work includes the development of a Multiview Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA) framework for analyzing sentiment of users towards a specific aspect with respect to syntax, contextualization, semantics, etc. with the goal of identifying (detecting and classifying) Positive vs Negative Sentiment Polarity for a given aspect. To achieve this, a transformer-based (BERT and RoBERTa-like) approach is employed to create contextual representations of user-generated input. Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and Graph Attention Networks (GAT) are used to construct and capture explicit Syntactic Dependencies over Dependency Trees. The proposed Methodology uses GCN-GAT to propagate sentiment information between Aspect Terms and Opinion Expressions and are supported using Lexico-Commonsense Knowledge for Semantic Understanding. In addition, an hierarchical attention-based fusion module is proposed to integrate, within one Unified Feature Space, contextual, syntactic, semantic, and knowledge-aware-based representations for Sentiment Classification tasks. The proposed methodology is evaluated using standard ABSA Benchmark Datasets (including Laptop14, Restaurant14, 15 & 16, etc.) and demonstrates substantial improvements in terms of Accuracy and Macro-Thus-F1 Score metrics over other current State-of-the-Art Approaches.

title: Reliable LLM-Powered Decision Engines for Large-Scale Supply Chain Operations: Architecture, Safety, and Performance Guarantees
authors: Nirmal Kumar Jingar
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Current large-scale supply chains are highly uncertain, dynamic, and disruption prone that are challenging to serve up timely and resilient decisions through traditional rulebased and optimization-only systems. The increasing supply of heterogeneous data sources, such as transactional demand signals and unstructured disruption report, presents a chance of intelligent systems, which could reason, adapt and optimize at the same time. A hybrid architecture that combines large language models (LLMs) with mathematical optimization, probabilistic forecasting, and safety-constrained decision filtering is proposed in this paper as a performance of a Decision Engine, which is called LLM-Powered Decision Engine (LLM-DE). In comparison to purely data-driven or heuristic solutions, LLM-DE integrates semantic reasoning with LLM with a set of performance and safety guarantees that allow safe decision-making in large-scale supply chain processes. The suggested framework enables the end-to-end decision making such as demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and transportation routing and disruption mitigation. The findings affirm that language-based reasoning combined with optimization and formal constraints can be used to come up with not only smarter but also safer and more scalable supply chain decisions. This research provides a new architecture, a complete pipeline of algorithm, and a formulation based on mathematical constructs of the operational decision systems incorporating LLM. The proposed model offers a pragmatic and theoretical basis of the next-generation intelligent supply chain infrastructures that can be implemented to work dependably in the face of uncertainty and massive complexity.

title: Estimating Competing TCP Flows and Bandwidth in Multi-Link Paths: An Indirect Probing Approach
authors: Ihtesham Ibn Malek
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This study addresses the critical challenge of fair bandwidth (BW) allocation in shared-link networks, where dynamic flow competition necessitates accurately estimating the number of active flows. This paper proposes a lightweight algorithm that estimates the number of active flows and the fair BW share using only local TCP throughput, without requiring any explicit signaling. Under a specific simulation setting, the proposed algorithm achieves a flow estimation accuracy of 96.7%, with only 1.46% bandwidth estimation error, and an average convergence time of just 0.67 steps. Across diverse simulation conditions, average flow estimation accuracy remains above 90%, and BW estimation error stays below 5%. Comprehensive parameter sweeps and randomized flow-pattern experiments validate the method’s stability under dynamic network conditions.

title: Adaptive Solar Forecasting: A Data-Driven Exploration of Model Generalizability across diverse US climates
authors: Abishai Tom, Jawahar Sundaram, Nimitha John
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Grid stability depends on accurate solar forecasts, yet current models often fail to generalize across differing geographical locations. The present study aims to address such a limitation by creating one adaptable forecasting model that can be used in a variety of climates. We used a detailed dataset of a combination of PV power output, NSRDB weather data, and system information metadata of three different locations in the US, humid subtropical (Florida), hot desert (Nevada), and high-altitude semi-arid (Colorado). The model proved highly robust by using time series cross-validation along with optimization of an XGBoost architecture with help of Optuna. Having an R-squared of 0.9744 and a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.0404, the research study proves that a combination of modern time-series capabilities and physical nature characteristics of a system makes the solar forecasting robust and universal.

title: Construction of an Intelligent Recommendation System for Personalized Training Plans for Sports Rehabilitation Patients Based on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Yihao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Personalized rehabilitation training is essential for enhancing recovery outcomes in patients with physical impairments, yet current recommendation systems struggle to capture heterogeneous biomedical relationships and adapt to individual feedback. This study proposes an intelligent training recommendation system based on Heterogeneous Graph Attention Networks (HGAT), designed to model interactions among patients, exercises, medical diagnoses, and physiological indicators through a multi-relational graph structure. We construct a heterogeneous graph comprising over 1200 patient records, 180 standardized rehabilitation actions, and 32 ICD-10 diagnosis tags. The proposed HGAT model integrates semantic attention over multiple relation types and optimizes a multi-task loss function accounting for recommendation accuracy, training sequence rationality, and physiological response feedback. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that our model outperforms collaborative filtering, LSTM-based recommenders, and homogeneous GNN baselines, achieving a Precision@10 of 0.692 and significantly enhancing the rehabilitation progress index (RPI) over five weeks. Visualization and ablation studies further confirm the interpretability and robustness of our approach. The system provides a promising step toward adaptive, clinically applicable rehabilitation recommendations.

title: Security–Resource Management in DNA–Optical Cryptosystems for Constrained IoT Devices
authors: Mahima Singh, Vani K A, Vishwanil Suman, Zokir Mamadiyarov, Muzaffar Shojonov, Saidislom Rashidov
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Security–Resource Management in DNA–Optical Cryptosystems for Constrained IoT Devices is presented. A resource aware cryptosystem combined deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA)-based encoding with optical domain encryption to secure constrained Internet of Things (IoT) nodes is proposed. The design co-optimized key representation optical encoding parameters and a lightweight on-node verifier based on relational graph transformer (RelGT) to meet memory latency and energy budgets. Evaluation used two public IoT datasets from 2023 to 2024 to capture realistic telemetry and attack patterns. Experiments compared XGBoost a Transformer plus graph neural network (GNN) hybrid model and RelGT. Metrics included PR AUC, AUROC, ciphertext indistinguishability, key recovery resistance latency, memory FLOPs and energy per inference. Results indicated RelGT improved PR AUC by 10.6% and AUROC by 3.3% versus baselines reduced attacker key recovery success by 77% and cut energy per inference by 65.6% after distillation and quantization. This work specified optical forward modeling DNA token constraints and a verification pipeline optimized for edge deployment. Future work includes hardware testbeds. It also enables practical deployment of high-entropy physical-layer cryptosystems on resource-constrained edge devices and guides design choices for energy-aware cryptographic primitives in IoT.

title: Apply Augmented Reality (AR) to Image Processing: Making Visual Feedback Available in Real-Time for Industrial Quality Control on Production Lines
authors: V. Thanammal Indu, Hayel Khafajeh, Padmesh Tirunelveli Narayanapillai, V. Banu Priya, Balpreet Singh, A. Prakash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Some industries have implemented cutting-edge digital inspection and monitoring systems to meet client demands for flawless, high-quality items. Manual inspection and standalone machine vision solutions are used in traditional quality control systems, but they are inflexible, lack context, and don’t provide operators with feedback. Using augmented reality (AR) and image processing, this research investigates how these two technologies might be combined to provide real-time visual feedback for industrial quality control on manufacturing lines. To improve production efficiency, operator decision-making, and defect detection, the developed system uses computer vision algorithms, real-time photo analysis, and augmented reality visualisation. Through the utilization of processed visual information that is superimposed on real-world components or production settings, operators are able to discover and rectify assembly faults, surface blemishes, dimensional discrepancies, and flaws in real time. AR interfaces, which include smart glasses, tablets, and head-mounted displays, use image processing algorithms to extract significant components from recorded images and display them. These algorithms are used to analyse and display recordings. There is a reduction in inspection time, human error, and disruptions to production when real-time feedback is used. Studying system architecture, data collecting, processing pipelines, and industrial-ready visualization. AR quality control improves training, accuracy, transparency, and productivity. AR/image processing makes quality control smarter, more engaging, and more proactive. Smart manufacturing and Industry 4.0 benefit from real-time, context-aware assembly-line quality assurance.

title: A Low-Cost Wearable Insole System Using FSR for Real-Time Gait Analysis
authors: Pradip Chotara, Yadunath Pathak, Vijay Bhaskar Semwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Quantitative gait analysis is a critical tool in clinical and biomechanical applications, but its widespread adoption is hindered by the high cost and immobility of current systems. This research addresses this gap by presenting the complete design, fabrication, and validation of an affordable, real-time, wearable gait phase recognition system. We developed a custom insole instrumented with Force-Sensing Resistors (FSRs) and a wireless data acquisition unit to capture and transmit plantar pressure data. A machine learning pipeline, utilizing statistical features extracted from 250 ms data windows, was implemented with a Random Forest classifier to perform multi-output classification of four distinct gait phases for both feet. The engineered system demonstrated strong real-time performance and achieved a high classification accuracy, with a macro-averaged F1-score of 95.4% on a test dataset. The primary contribution of this work is a validated blueprint for a low-cost system that offers a viable alternative to expensive commercial solutions, lowering the barrier to quantitative gait analysis for research and clinical practice.

title: Finance-Inspired Throughput & Cost Optimization for Edge-AI Traffic Systems
authors: Ruchira Rawat, Khemraj Sharma, Jyoti Kaurav, Zokir Mamadiyarov, Momogul Ismailova, Dilnoza Butanova
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rapid urbanization in India has exacerbated traffic congestion, causing safety, delay, and environmental problems. This study proposes a finance-inspired framework for throughput and cost optimization in Edge-AI traffic systems. The system integrates distributed IoT sensors with a hybrid Vision Transformer–Temporal Convolutional Network (ViT– TCN) deployed at edge nodes to enable low-latency visual detection and sequential traffic forecasting. Training used the Ranger optimizer (Lookahead + RAdam) to accelerate convergence under streaming conditions. A portfolio-theory inspired objective treated intersection throughput as return and operational cost (deployment + communication + energy) as risk, producing a mean–variance style optimization that balanced throughput gains against cost. Experiments used an anonymized Indian urban dataset collected from multiple metropolitan intersections across Mumbai, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad over 12 months. Results show marked reductions in delay and stop time, improved intersection throughput, and superior throughput-to-cost ratios under constrained budgets and latency requirements. The distributed Edge-AI approach achieved mean decision latency of 68.4 ms, compared with centralized control at 132.1 ms and cloud inference at 215.6 ms. Under realistic budget constraints, an optimal deployment policy raised throughput by 28.1% while reducing deployment cost per intersection by 18.7%.

title: Effectiveness Analysis of Countermeasures Against Active Suppression Jamming for Airborne Satellite Navigation Systems
authors: Xingwei Hou, Lei Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Airborne satellite navigation systems play a critical role in the electronic information systems of military aircraft. Addressing the issue of inaccurate positioning following exposure to suppressive electronic jamming, this study analyzes typical anti-jamming mechanisms across spatial, temporal, frequency and energy domains. It establishes an anti-jamming effectiveness analysis index on the degree of jamming distance reduction and develops its mathematical model. By designing electronic attack-defense scenarios for navigation systems, quantitative analysis results are presented for countermeasures such as main beam formation, narrowband filtering, navigation power enhancement and multi-measure reuse under typical conditions. This provides effective support for the scientific application of anti-jamming measures in airborne satellite navigation systems.

title: Defect Detection in Solar Photovoltaic Panels using Computer Vision
authors: Arva Shah, Devansh Singh, Twasha Vyass, Ankita Gupta, Rahee Walambe, Paresh Nasikkar, Ketan Kotecha, Hussain Shareef
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (SPV) panels are widely being adopted as a primary source of nonconventional energy resources. However, the effectiveness of a solar panel suffers from defects caused due to environmental disturbances or operational short-comings. These defects can be identified using electroluminescence imaging (EL). To deal with the time and cost constraints that affect manual classification of defects in EL images, this work presents an automated framework for the classification of defects in solar cells, using the ‘ELPV-dataset’, a dataset that contains EL images of individual solar cells categorised into Defective, Potentially Defective, and Normal classes. The pipeline of the framework uses image processing techniques and the sliding window algorithm for extracting single solar cell images from the EL images of full solar panels. Following this, classification of the extracted images is done through a pre-trained convolutional neural network (CNN) to quantify the number of cells that lie in each category within the solar panel. Therefore, the CNN classifier gives an accuracy of 80.77%. This method has an accurate Solution as a Quality Detection Strategy in Photovoltaic Manufacturing.

title: Advanced Intelligent frameworks for comprehensive detection and mitigation of zero-day attack vulnerabilities in complex networked environments
authors: Ajay Kumar Punia, Arun Chaudhary, Harish Krishna Reddy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Vulnerabilities for zero-day attacks are security holes in systems or software that the vendor is unaware of and that hackers can take advantage of before a patch or other solution is made available. They are difficult to detect and prevent, leaving systems highly susceptible to attacks and potential data breaches. The work here proposes an advanced machine learning framework for zero-day attack detection and mitigation (ZAV-CRT-GBT-SVM). Initially, the input data is collected from CSE-CIC-IDS2018 Dataset. The input data is pre-processed using the Adaptive Resilience Navigation Filter (ARNF). Then the pre-processed data is fed into Refined Linear Chirplet Transform (RLCT) to extract the relevant features. The extracted features are fed into the Granular Ball Twin Support Vector Machine (GBT-SVM) to detect and classify zero-day attack vulnerabilities in network traffic. Then the performance of the proposed ZAV-CRT-GBT-SVM method is analysed on Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, ROC, Detection Rate (DR) and False Alarm Rate (FAR). Performance of the ZAV-CRT-GBT-SVM method achieves 96.9% Accuracy, 99%Precision, 98.9% Recall and 97% F1 score when analysed through existing techniques such as zero day attack vulnerabilities: mitigation utilizing ML for performance evaluation (ZAV-SVM), a structure for detecting zero-day exploits in network flows (DZE-NF-CNN), zero-day exploits detection and adaptive Wave PCA-Autoencoder adaptive detection of hybrid exploit network (ZED-AWPA-GMCO), respectively.

title: Clock Synchronization Technology for Wireless Rotor Load Measurement of Unmanned Helicopters Based on Ultra-Wideband Communications
authors: Yewen Gu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Address the issue of insufficient clock synchronization precision in unmanned wireless load measurement for helicopter rotors, this paper proposes a wireless clock synchronization method that integrates Ultra-Wideband technology with the IEEE 1588 protocol. By establishing an error model, the study identifies wireless link transmission as the primary source of system delay and offset for the first time. Building on this, a BPSK pulse modulation scheme is designed by leveraging the carrier-free characteristics of UWB. Furthermore, a Kalman filter algorithm is introduced to estimate and correct clock offsets and frequency drifts in real-time. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed scheme effectively suppresses measurement noise and accelerates error convergence. When the synchronization cycle is less than 2 seconds, the timing precision is significantly improved, thereby meeting the engineering requirements for the precise fusion and analysis of rotor load data.

title: Square Labyrinth Shaped Rotationally Symmetric Metamaterial Absorber for Organic Solvent Sensing
authors: Sayed Mahmud, Md. Jahedul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: A square labyrinth shaped rotationally symmetric metamaterial absorber is presented for organic solvent sensing in the K-band. The unit cell measures $2.30 \times 2.30$ mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>| with a $2.00 \times$ 2.00 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>| copper labyrinth resonator printed on an FR-4 substrate of 1.6 mm thickness. A $0.40 \times 0.40 \mathrm{~mm}^{2}$ slot in the ground enhances current confinement and hybrid resonance. The absorber achieves 98.55% absorption at 22.37 GHz with an S11 dip of -18.39 dB. Retrieved parameters show negative permittivity, sub-unity permeability, and near-zero refractive index at resonance. The design exhibits angular stability up to $\theta=80^{\circ}$ and polarization independence. For sensing applications, the resonant frequency shifts to 24.09 GHz (acetone), 24.94 GHz (isopropyl alcohol), and 26.24 GHz (glycerin), corresponding to sensitivities of 0.485,0.783, and $0.637 \mathrm{GHz} / \mathrm{RIU}$. The compact design demonstrates improved sensitivity compared with recent C-band absorbers, making it suitable for chemical detection in the K-band.

title: Joint Cyber-Physical Data-Driven Enhanced Cyber Attack Detection for Power Systems
authors: Luyu Wang, Kaifeng Zhang, Ying Wang, Kun Yuan, Biplab Sikdar
abstract: Rapid, early, and accurate detection of cyber intrusions is increasingly vital for ensuring the security and operational resilience of modern power systems, as cyber-attacks grow in both frequency and sophistication. However, existing approaches primarily focus on either cyber or physical data independently, ignoring critical interactions across these two layers. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a novel Cross-Layer Graph Attention-Transformer Network (CL-GTN), which simultaneously incorporates information from both the cyber and physical layers, and effectively integrates them through a learnable physics-informed point-to-bus projection mechanism. Specifically, the projection matrix constructed from power system sensitivity factors explicitly captures the fine-grained mapping from cyber data points in communication packets to affected physical grid nodes, providing a robust cross-layer fusion basis.
