An ablation study confirms the complementary contributions of spectral band, imaging perspective, and input scale metadata, with spectral band providing the dominant improvement.

title: Real-Time Mine Safety Alert System for Gas Leakage Detection Using Hybrid GWO-PSO Optimized Machine Learning
authors: P Srinivasan, T Keerthi Nethra, K Arulaanandham, M Santhosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Gas leakage is a problematic safety aspect in both residential and industrial settings, such that gas leakage detection must occur with high accuracy in real time. The important aspect of this research is to develop a smart gas leakage detection system by incorporating gas detection with machine learning hybrid with different optimization algorithm. The acquired gas concentration data from sensors are processed through an Arduino microcontroller and analyzed on an optimized decision ML model. The GWO - PSO algorithm with hybrid to random forest and XG Boost dynamically optimizes the thresholds of detection and system parameters, hence improving the accuracy and reducing the false alarm probabilities. Upon detecting abnormal levels of gas concentration, the activated mechanisms include a local alert and sending notifications via wireless communication modules for remote monitoring. Experimental results prove that the response time, accuracy of detection, and reliability of the proposed system are better than conventional approaches that use only fixed thresholds, and therefore, this work suits well for real-time applications intended for safety purposes.

title: Enhancing Information Literacy by Predicting Behaviour Characteristics of Students using DeepNeural Networks
authors: R.M. Mallika, R. Surekha, S. Kiran, R. Hemapriya, R. Sainath Reddy, P. Geetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Information literacy refers to the acquisition of skills and knowledge necessary to identify, evaluate, and use information effectively. It involves the ability to use information to gain knowledge and solve problems. The model evaluates a student’s assessment score in a particular course, analyses the student’s learning behaviour via questionnaires, and employs machine learning methods to predict learning outcomes. Based on these predictions, the student will be directed to an appropriate content mode, such as text, audio, or video, to improve the student’s information literacy. The objective is to improve information literacy, change how it is practised, and enhance educational decision-making. By using Deep Neuro Fuzzy Systems, also known as Fuzzy Deep Neural Networks, the project aims to support the long-term growth of exceptional and innovative talent in the information society.

title: D-RAM: A Dynamic Role-Allocation Mediator for Improving Token Efficiency in Multi-Agent Collaborative Software Development
authors: Zihe Chen, Hao Zhen, Zihao Zhu, Jieyu Jiang, Mingzhe Kong, Xiaoling Gu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As Large Language Models evolve toward Multi-Agent Systems, existing self-optimization mechanisms frequently fall into reasoning deadlocks when handling complex logical tasks with contradictory constraints due to a lack of external intervention, which has become a core bottleneck for the deployment of MAS. To address the critical issue of deadlock susceptibility, this paper proposes D-RAM, a dynamic semantic friction routing and adaptive architecture protocol. We: i) introduce the Semantic Friction Score to monitor agent interaction stagnation in real-time; ii) construct an asynchronous graph topology collaboration framework based on LangGraph; and iii) automatically introduce high-order Mediator nodes to implement non-linear intervention upon detecting reasoning fixation. To verify the architectural effectiveness, we built Conflict-Bench, an adversarial benchmark set containing contradictory constraints and extreme engineering limits, and conducted multi-dimensional comparative experiments. Multi-dimensional experimental results show that the D-RAM architecture achieves a 60.0% success rate on conflict tasks, representing a performance leap of 80% and 20% compared to standalone LLMs and standard multi-agent architectures, respectively. It optimizes computational costs and mitigates reasoning fixation without sacrificing the success rate; furthermore, D-RAM composed of lightweight model clusters demonstrates conflict mediation capabilities surpassing those of large-parameter monolithic models.

title: Automated Disease Classification using ai and image processing: Enhance Models for Accurate Diagnosis
authors: Sengole Merlin, C Ramesh Babu Durai, Enku Yosef Kefyalew, A. Jeyamurugan, K Nandini, N Padmavathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Patient outcomes and healthcare expenditures are both improved by early and precise disease detection. Every day, a plethora of diagnostic images are produced by ever-improving medical imaging technologies, such as X-ray, MRI, CT, ultrasound, and dermoscopy. Particularly in high-volume clinical settings, manual interpretation of medical images is laborious, subjective, and error-prone. This work presents an automated disease classification system that utilises AI and sophisticated image processing to improve the accuracy, reliability, and efficiency of diagnoses. To improve image quality and emphasise clinically significant features, the system employs noise reduction, image normalisation, contrast enhancement, and segmentation. To extract and categorise features, CNNs and hybrid AI architectures are utilised. Reduce training time and maximise generalizability across datasets by leveraging pre-trained models through transfer learning. Additionally, the framework highlights diseased areas, aiding diagnosis and interpretation. Data augmentation and ensemble learning tackle class imbalance and data unpredictability in medical datasets. The system can classify diseases from medical images using multiple criteria. The F1-score, AUC, recall, accuracy, and precision measure the model’s efficacy. Experimental simulations show that, compared with machine learning and manual diagnosis methods, AI-based classification is more accurate and consistent. Automated procedures increase sensitivity and specificity while decreasing diagnostic time.

title: Computational Neural Network Analysis of Waste Pet Fibre Reinforced Rubberised Concrete Beams
authors: R Karthikeyan, M Tholkapiyan, M Sivakumar, K K Gaayathri, K Suguna, s Sudharsanam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: This study focuses on the flexural behaviour of rubberised concrete beams reinforced with waste PET fibre as a means of improving both structural performance and sustainability. Although the incorporation of recycled rubber in concrete contributes to environmental conservation, it results in a reduction in strength. To compensate for this drawback, waste PET bottle fibre was introduced as a micro-reinforcement, and experimental tests were conducted to evaluate the beam’s flexural strength. The experimental tests were conducted on beams under static loading to evaluate the ultimate load and the corresponding deflection. The experimental dataset, along with literature data, was used to develop an artificial neural network model employing the Scaled Conjugate Gradient, Levenberg-Marquardt, and Bayesian Regularisation algorithms with backpropagation. Beam cross-sectional properties, length, compressive strength, yield strength, reinforcement area and modulus of elasticity were treated as input parameters. At the same time, ultimate load and deflection at ultimate load were considered as output data. Model accuracy was assessed using Root mean square error (RMSE), coefficient of determination (R2), and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), and the predictive capability was verified using scatter plots. The results indicate that the Bayesian Regularisation algorithms achieve higher prediction accuracy than the other three algorithms.

title: An Analysis of Insects in Thai Literature
authors: Wanwiwat Ruttanalum
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This study originated from an interest in Patrick Lafcadio Hearn, a literary scholar and writer renowned for introducing Japanese ghost stories and the supernatural to Western readers. One area of Hearn's serious scholarly engagement is the representation of insects in literature, as demonstrated in his influential work Insect Literature, a comparative study of insects in the literary traditions of Greek, English, French, and Japanese. Hearn's work prompted a reconsideration of insects in Thai literature, revealing a significant research gap, as no existing studies have treated insects as a central object of literary analysis. Previous research has generally addressed insects only as part of the natural world, grouping them with other animals rather than examining their distinct symbolic and cultural roles. This research, therefore, aims to investigate the roles and significance of insects in Thai literature through the framework of cultural entomology. As an initial phase, the study focuses on analyzing insect appearances in Thai literary texts. A survey of 420 Thai literary works composed from the Sukhothai period to the reign of King Rama VIII of the Rattanakosin era reveals that 208 texts contain references to insects. These references are classified into eighteen categories. The five most frequently mentioned insect groups are: (1) bees, (2) blood-feeding insects, (3) cicadas, (4) fireflies, and (5) houseflies/fruit flies. Among these groups, bees, cicadas, and houseflies/fruit flies appear prominently in both Thai and foreign literary traditions, whereas blood-feeding insects and fireflies are infrequently discussed in foreign literature but occur frequently in Thai poetry. The presence of insects in Thai literature thus reflects both shared worldviews between Thai poets and those of other cultures, as well as culturally specific perceptions of insects. Notably, bees occupy a particularly significant position: they are referred to by as many as nineteen different terms and are employed by poets not only in literal descriptions but also as metaphors related to sexuality, gender relations, and human sexual behavior.

title: Research on Identity Authentication for Edge Aggregation Control Units Based on Software-Hardware Feature Fusion
authors: Yao Chen, Yiwen Zhu
publish: Proceedings of the 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Digital Media Technology and Social Computing
abstract: None

title: A Multi-Model Computational Framework for Forecasting Pet Industry Markets Using Time-Series and Multivariate Regression
authors: Wuyi Chen
publish: Proceedings of the 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Digital Media Technology and Social Computing
abstract: None

title: Nursing Decision Support System for Lumbar Disc Herniation Integrating Intelligent Algorithms
authors: Dongwei Zhang, Lizhen Wang
publish: Proceedings of the 2nd International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Digital Media Technology and Social Computing
abstract: None

title: Spatial-Spectral Diffusion Model for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Mingkai Liu, Huajun Wang, Shujun Liu, Wenyi Hu
abstract: Medical image semantic segmentation plays a crucial role in assisting diagnosis by accurately extracting key regions within images, thus improving medical efficiency and accuracy. Medical image segmentation technologies have advanced considerably in recent years. However, challenges such as the difficulty of segmenting complex anatomical structures and the presence of ambiguous boundaries still persist. To address these issues, we propose an effective diffusion-based model for medical image segmentation by integrating the powerful global context modeling capabilities of Mamba with the generative strength of diffusion models. Specifically, an encoder module is constructed using Mamba to mitigate detail loss during the sampling process and to establish global contextual relationships, thereby enhancing the model’s feature extraction capacity. In addition, we design a feature optimization module that strengthens inter-channel information interaction and captures multi-scale features for fusion, leading to improved feature representation. We evaluated our model’s performance using three datasets: ISIC, PH2, and MoNuSeg. Experimental results demonstrate that our model exhibits excellent segmentation performance and accuracy. The code for our model is available at https://github.com/lmkxl/EDiffusion.

title: A Virtual Mechanical Interaction Layer Enables Resilient Human-to-Robot Object Handovers
authors: Omar Faris, Sławomir K. Tadeja, Fulvio Forni
abstract: Object handover is a common form of interaction widely used in collaborative tasks. However, achieving it efficiently remains a challenge. We address the problem of ensuring resilient robotic actions that can adapt to complex changes in object pose during human-to-robot object handovers. We propose the use of Virtual Model Control to create an interaction layer that controls the robot and adapts to the dynamic changes in the handover process. Additionally, we propose using augmented reality to facilitate bidirectional communication between humans and robots during handovers. Our controller demonstrated high resilience in experimental tests against various sources of uncertainties with a nearly perfect success rate and an average mean approach time of 4 seconds in one-time rotation or translation tests of four different objects, as well as a success rate of 100% in tests with random object motion and robot starting pose. We also performed a user study with 16 participants to understand human preferences for different robot control profiles and augmented reality visuals in object handovers. Our results showed a general preference for the proposed approach with a 98 success rate and a mean approach time of 4.9 seconds and revealed insights for improving the interaction with the user

title: Unsupervised Light Field Salient Object Detection with Diffusion Models for Enhanced Structural Integrity
authors: Yefan Li, Fuqing Duan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Unsupervised light field salient object detection aims to identify the most visually distinctive regions in a scene based on light field data in an unsupervised way. It eliminates the reliance on expensive pixel-level annotations. Although several unsupervised strategies have been proposed in recent years, ensuring the structural integrity of generated saliency maps remains challenging. To address this issue, we propose an unsupervised framework termed the Structural Integrity Saliency Network, which aims to recover the structural integrity of salient objects from two perspectives: pseudo-label generation and saliency detector design. First, Diffusion Attention-Guided Pseudo-label Generation module leverages the spatial correspondence within self-attention maps of pre-trained diffusion models to mine latent object structures. Simultaneously, it utilizes cross-attention maps as the class-agnostic foreground indicator to suppress background noise, thereby generating structurally complete pseudo-labels. Second, based on these high-quality pseudo-labels, we construct a light field detection network to learn saliency. The network integrates two core components: Focal Prior Localization Enhancement module and Structural Integrity Enhancement loss. The former injects explicit spatial localization cues from the foreground slice of focal stacks into the global context of all-in-focus image, establishing a solid foundation for accurate object localization. The latter leverages the intra-attention similarity of diffusion models to enforce prediction consistency across regions with similar feature responses. By penalizing structural defects, this loss further guarantees the completeness of final predictions. Extensive experiments on four public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our proposed method significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in unsupervised light field salient object detection.

title: Semi-URF: Progressive Uncertainty-Aware Region Filtering and Fusion for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Qingyu Yang, Baiying Lei, Huifang Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Semi-supervised learning for medical image segmentation is often hindered by a conservative approach to unlabeled data, where information-rich yet uncertain regions-such as complex structures and ambiguous boundaries-are typically discarded to ensure pseudo-label quality. This “avoidance” strategy limits the model's ability to learn from the most challenging areas, which are critical for clinical accuracy. To address these limitations, we propose Semi-URF, an Uncertainty-Aware Region Filtering and Fusion framework. Instead of treating uncertainty as an obstacle to be avoided, Semi-URF progressively exploits it as a supervisory signal to enhance learning from unlabeled data. Our approach establishes a synergistic closed loop with three key innovations. First, an Uncertainty Distribution Adaptive Thresholding (UDAT) mechanism adaptively separates reliable from unreliable regions by tracking the model's evolving uncertainty distribution, maximizing the use of informative pixels from unlabeled data. Second, our Bidirectional Uncertainty-Consistent Exchange (BUCE) method facilitates learning from unreliable regions by exchanging patches between labeled and unlabeled data, subject to a semantic consistency constraint. Finally, to enhance the model's foundational capabilities for this task, a Frequency-Enhanced Feature Fusion (FEFF) module uses fast wavelet transforms and cross-attention to sharpen the perception of high-frequency boundary details. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that Semi-URF outperforms state-of-the-art methods, especially in situations where annotated data are severely limited. By transforming uncertainty from a problem into a solution, Semi-URF significantly reduces reliance on costly expert annotations. This approach facilitates the development of more efficient and cost-effective AI models for clinical applications. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/senseiyang/Semi-URF</uri>.

title: Understanding the Impact of Institutional Privacy Violations in Algorithmic Social Media on Information Disclosure and Privacy Fatigue: Insights from the Protection Motivation Theory
authors: Huifang Pan
abstract: Instead of increasing the motivation of some users to protect their privacy, frequent institutional privacy violations have enhanced their privacy fatigue, which may cause them to give up privacy protection and continue to disclose their information even after experiencing the negative consequences of privacy violations. Drawing on protection motivation theory, this study aims to explore how institutional privacy violation experiences of social media users affect privacy fatigue and personal information disclosure behavior through cognitive appraisals. We used structural equation modeling to analyze 526 questionnaires collected from Chinese social media users. The results showed that institutional privacy violations not only have a direct effect on privacy fatigue but also enhance it by increasing privacy concerns, response costs, and decreasing self-efficacy. However, the study did not find a significant link between response efficacy and privacy fatigue, which may be due to the opacity and high degree of individualization of privacy protection measures. In addition, privacy fatigue can increase an individual’s information disclosure, and privacy concerns, self-efficacy, and response costs play an important mediating role between institutional privacy violation experiences and self-disclosure. This study further extends the protection motivation theory by introducing experiential and emotional factors into the protection motivation model. Moreover, these findings add empirical insights into the role of individual experiences and privacy fatigue in privacy decisions, providing platform operators and policymakers with new perspectives on understanding the complexity of privacy behaviors in algorithmic contexts.

title: Command-Filtered Backstepping Control Algorithm for Quadrotor AAVs Based on Extended State Observer
authors: Pham Quoc Khanh, Ha Le Nhu Ngoc Thanh, Truong Nguyen Luan Vu, Vo Lam Chuong
abstract: This paper proposes an extended state observer (ESO)-based command-filtered backstepping control (CFBC) framework for quadrotor autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) subject to external disturbances, parametric uncertainties, and incomplete state measurements. The proposed ESO simultaneously reconstructs unmeasured velocity states and estimates lumped disturbances in real time, thereby eliminating the need for direct velocity sensing while preserving feedback reliability. To address the inherent complexity growth problem of conventional backstepping designs, command filters augmented with compensation dynamics are incorporated, resulting in a reduced-order and computationally efficient control architecture. A unified input-to-state stability (ISS) analysis is rigorously developed for the overall closed-loop system, explicitly characterizing the effects of observer estimation errors, command-filter dynamics, and disturbance approximation inaccuracies. It is proven that the control system are ultimately bounded. Comparative numerical simulations against conventional CFBC and a dynamic sliding mode controller with nonlinear disturbance observer (DSMNDO) demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves superior trajectory tracking performance, smoother control effort, and enhanced robustness under significant disturbances and modeling uncertainties.

title: Neural Spelling: A Spell-Based BCI System for Language Neural Decoding_supp1-3691322.pdf
authors: Xiaowei Jiang
abstract: Objective: Brain–computer interfaces (BCIs) support the study of communication-oriented neural decoding by translating neural activity into text, yet existing non-invasive systems rarely cover the full alphabet in handwriting-based settings. Methods: We propose a novel non-invasive EEG-based BCI framework, Curriculum-based Neural Spelling (CNS), that decodes all 26 English letters by first learning neural patterns associated with handwriting trajectories. A Generative AI (GenAI) module based on large language models (LLMs) is then integrated to transform noisy letter-level neural predictions into sentence-level outputs under explicit neural constraints. Results: The proposed system achieves robust letter-level decoding and improved sentence-level reconstruction under controlled offline evaluation, outperforming conventional EEGNet and hybrid CNN-RNN baselines. GenAI correction further reduces word error rates and enhances decoding fluency. Conclusion: Combining EEG-based neural spelling with generative language modeling supports the study of full-alphabet decoding and improves sentence-level linguistic metrics in a controlled non-invasive EEG setting, but does not by itself establish clinical or real-world usability. Significance: This work demonstrates how integrating GenAI with neural decoding can bridge the gap between noisy signal-level predictions and coherent language-level outputs, establishing a system-level framework for full-alphabet neural spelling and adaptive language-level correction under non-invasive EEG constraints.

title: Diffusion-Based 3D Radar Semantic Perception in Cluttered Agricultural Environments_supp1-3691400.mp4
authors: Jialiang Hou
abstract: Accurate and robust environmental perception is crucial for robot autonomous navigation. While current methods typically adopt optical sensors, such as cameras and LiDARs, as primary sensing modalities, their susceptibility to visual occlusion—such as dirt adhering to the lens or physical blockage of the sensor—often leads to degraded performance or complete system failure. In this paper, we focus on agricultural scenarios where robots are exposed to the risk of onboard sensor contamination. Leveraging radar’s strong penetration capability, we introduce a radar-based 3D environmental perception framework as a viable alternative. It comprises three core modules designed for dense and accurate semantic perception: first, parallel frame accumulation to enhance signal-to-noise ratio of raw radar data; second, a diffusion model-based hierarchical learning framework that first filters radar sidelobe artifacts then generates fine-grained 3D semantic point clouds; and third, a specifically designed sparse 3D network optimized for processing large-scale raw radar data. We conducted extensive benchmark comparisons and experimental evaluations on a self-built dataset collected in real-world agricultural field scenes. Results demonstrate that our method achieves superior structural and semantic prediction performance compared to existing methods, while simultaneously reducing computational and memory costs by 44.3% and 27.5%, respectively. Furthermore, our approach achieves complete reconstruction and accurate classification of thin structures such as poles and wires—which existing methods struggle to perceive—highlighting its potential for dense and accurate 3D radar perception.

title: A Compact Polarization and Pattern Diversity Antenna with Enhanced Azimuthal Gain for Vehicular Communications
authors: Xiaohan Zhai, Jiaxin Kuang, Lei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A compact tri-polarized antenna providing both polarization and pattern diversity is proposed in this article. The antenna integrates a vertically polarized omnidirectional radiator with enhanced azimuthal gain for vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication and a pair of orthogonal dipoles with ±45° polarizations for vehicle-to-base station communication. Unlike conventional omnidirectional antennas that rely on collinear arrays to improve gain, the proposed omnidirectional element employs a cylindrical cavity surrounded by eight parasitic open cavities acting as magnetic dipoles. By carefully optimizing the coupling between the cylindrical cavity and the parasitic elements, a field-interference-based beam-shaping mechanism is realized, resulting in elevation beamwidth compression and azimuthal gain enhancement without increasing antenna height. The proposed omnidirectional radiator achieves an impedance bandwidth of 5.8–6.2 GHz and an azimuth-plane gain enhancement of 2.5 dB. Subsequently, a compact dual-band dual-polarized dipole antenna covering 1.7–1.9 GHz and 3.2–4.1 GHz is integrated atop the omnidirectional radiator to support vehicular LTE and sub-6 GHz 5G-NR communication. The overall antenna size is only 0.37λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> × 0.37λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> × 0.11λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> (λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">L</sub> is the wavelength at the lowest operating frequency), and the port-to-port isolation exceeds 28 dB. Simulated and measured results are in good agreement. The feasibility on metallic platforms is demonstrated, confirming the proposed design a promising solution for compact vehicular communications.

title: Adaptive Gait-Based Control for Assistive Robots Supporting Elderly on Inclined Surfaces
authors: Kishore Vennela, B Balaji, M. C. Chinnaiah, Mudasar Basha, Kalyana C. Veluvolu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive human–robot interaction framework designed to assist elderly individuals in navigating both flat and inclined surfaces using a mobile robotic platform. The proposed method integrates real-time gait estimation and slope detection to dynamically modulate the velocity of the robot, thereby ensuring stability and safety during locomotion. A hybrid sensor architecture, comprising ultrasonic distance sensors and force-sensitive handles, enables accurate estimation of human gait parameters, such as cadence, step length, and center of pressure variations. The inclination angles are computed through geometric analysis of the sensor arrays mounted on the robot, with real-time calibration techniques implemented via FPGA-based hardware acceleration for low-latency response. A torque and velocity control algorithm, informed by biomechanical changes in gait and user-applied interaction forces, adjusts the robot’s behavior to match the user’s walking intent on controlled planar inclines. Experimental validation was conducted with 30 participants on slopes of up to 40◦, demonstrating effective gait synchronization, reduced velocity mismatch, and improved safety through stability-aware motion control, including comparative evaluation against baseline controllers (Section IV-D). The current validation is limited to controlled, rigid ramp surfaces (0◦–40◦). The manuscript scopes claims accordingly and explicitly discusses ultrasonic sensing limitations on soft/irregular surfaces and non-planar terrain.

title: Simplified Methodology to Estimate Minimized Land Impact for Renewable Self-Sufficiency in Small Energy Systems
authors: David Dorta-Hernández, David Cañadillas-Ramallo, Paula Pérez-Romero, José Francisco Gómez, Benjamín González-Díaz, Ricardo Guerrero-Lemus
abstract: This study evaluates how to minimize land use and achieve renewable energy self-sufficiency in small systems, using Fuerteventura, Spain, as a case study. The analysis focuses on the island’s 2030 and 2040 decarbonization targets. The energy mix relies on solar and wind resources, prioritizing self-consumption, with storage using batteries and hydrogen. A conservative approach to full electrification is applied, considering three storage stages: the first 20% of renewable generation operates without significant storage, the next 60% is supported by one day of battery storage, and the final 20% relies on hydrogen storage covering 14 days of consumption. Geographic analysis of existing installations estimates land requirements. Results indicate land demand ranges from 12.3 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in fully photovoltaic scenarios with high self-consumption to 81.7 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> in fully onshore wind scenarios. Findings highlight that, with simple methodologies, small systems can check decarbonization targets, with balanced renewable mixes and optimized storage, minimizing land impact.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning and Multimodal Architectures for Zero-Shot Rare Disaster Recognition
authors: Amrutavarshini Beernalli, Pooja Pattar, Vaishnavi Pagad, Rohan Dixit, Uday Kulkarni, Veena Hunasimarad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Natural disasters have increasingly required rapid and reliable automated recognition because of their far-reaching effect on human life, infrastructure, and environmental safety. With the increasing availability of visual data from satellites, drones, and online platforms, Machine Learning and Deep Learning Methods have played an important role in image based disaster identification. However, conventional supervised models have faced difficulty when rare disaster categories contain limited or no labeled data. Multiple architectures were evaluated in this study for zero shot learning setting and compared Pure CLIP RN50, EfficientNetB0+B1 combined and fine tuned, ResNet50, and a hybrid ResNet50 paired with CLIP RN50 embeddings. The models were trained in four seen disaster classes and evaluated in three unseen disaster types. The ResNet50 + CLIP RN50 hybrid model achieved the highest performance, achieving 98% accuracy on seen classes and 99.92% accuracy on unseen categories, outperforming the other approaches. The findings reflected that multimodal fusion significantly improved zero-shot generalization and contributed to a more reliably recognized disaster of rarity.

title: A Compact Multi-Band Microstrip Antenna with π–H Radiator and Plus-Shaped DGS for mmWave 5G/6G Applications
authors: Kuldip Kumar, Basit Mohi Ud Din Naikoo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This Research paper describes a plus-shaped defected ground structure (DGS) based high-gain triple-band π– H slotted microstrip patch antenna, and is simulated and designed for millimeter-wave (mmWave) applications in 5G and 6G communication. The design idea is motivated by the bandwidth enhancement techniques explained in [1], wherein the optimization of a π-slotted dual-band rectangular microstrip antenna was made with response surface methodology (RSM).Here, another H-slot is incorporated within the π-slot structure to enhance impedance matching further and create another resonance, and a plus-shaped DGS is implemented in the ground plane to increase bandwidth and gain.The antenna is proposed employing Rogers RT6002 substrate having a dielectric constant of 2.94 and thickness of 1.6 mm, simulated in CST Microwave Studio. The designed antenna has triple-band operation at 53 GHz, 59 GHz, and 71 GHz with reflection coefficients of −33 dB, −50 dB, and −37 dB, respectively. The VSWR values are 1.049, 1.0084, and 1.0286, which are good impedance matches in all the bands. The antenna offers gains of 5.45 dBi, 4.13 dBi, and 6.10 dBi and directivities of 6.9 dBi, 6.09 dBi, and 8.02 dBi, respectively. The introduction of the π–H slot combination and plus-shaped DGS greatly increases radiation efficiency and multiband behavior, rendering the antenna a good choice for compact, high-gain 5G and mmWave devices.

title: Joint Temporal-Structural Representation Learning for Distributed Fault Discrimination in Microservice Architectures
authors: Yihan Xue, Yuxiao Wang, Ao Zhu, Xiaoxuan Sun, Chong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Addressing the diverse fault morphologies, complex dependencies, and time-varying operational states in microservice distributed systems, this paper proposes a distributed fault discrimination model based on temporal graph neural networks. This model characterizes the microservice operation process as a dynamic graph sequence evolving, and performs joint representation learning of temporal modeling and structural interactions within a unified framework. First, service-level multisource observation signals are aligned and characterized to construct node feature sequences and their corresponding time-dependent dependencies. Then, a temporal coding module is introduced to extract the dynamic evolution representation of service states, and at each time step, attention-based structured message passing is used to characterize dependency interactions and propagation associations, forming a structure-enhanced temporal node representation. Furthermore, a dual readout mechanism is employed to aggregate the node and temporal dimensions, obtaining a system-level global representation and outputting the fault category distribution. Finally, supervised learning objectives are used to optimize model parameters, enabling the model to learn stable discrimination evidence under complex interactions and multi-source noise conditions. Comparative experimental results show that the proposed method achieves superior performance on multiple evaluation metrics, validating the effectiveness of jointly modeling temporal evolution and dependency structures in improving the distributed fault discrimination capability of microservices.

title: Hybrid Approach for Cloud Intrusion Detection: Feature Selection & Classification
authors: Himanshu Jaiswal, Sreeja Nair, Ritu Ranjani Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cloud computing has grown much in the immediate past, becoming central to every organization; but its distributed design also makes it easy to fall prey to attacks like DoS/DDoS, R2L, U2R, probing, zero-day, and so forth. The detection mechanisms, stage by stage, fail for obsolete threats; hence intrusion detection systems that rely on signatures come short for straight intrusion identification. Being modeled for emerging threats, anomaly-based IDSs face high false alarms. This paper presents a hybrid cloud-native IDS model, which interacts with advanced feature-selection procedures PCA, Information Gain, Chi-square, PSO, RFE alongside machine learning and deep-learning classifiers, supporting SVM, RF, KNN, DT, CNN, LSTM, and CNN-RNN, and hybrid layers and ensembles for maximum balance between accuracy, computation cost, adaptivity, and ML and DL strengths. The Python-implemented IDS, integrated with these features, is experimented upon in cloud settings, aiming at several standard datasets, for example, NSL-KDD, CICIDS2017/2018, UNSW-NB15, Kyoto dataset, and CSE-CIC-IDS2018. Research outcomes ensemble, show that the best performance is offered by the hybrid SVM classifier, yielding 98.24% accuracy along with precision, i.e., 97%, recall, i.e., 95%, and a false alarm rate as low as 0.0156, outdoing the classic ML and DL models. This research underlines that a combination of signature and anomaly detection would greatly benefit in creating a scalable and cost-effective IDS suitable for small and big cloud infrastructures. The final observation of this study is that the proposed hybrid mechanism optimizes the detection reliability while still offering real-time efficiency in the setting of dynamic cloud and IoT environments.

title: Revisiting 1998 Torque Data: a Machine Learning Analysis of Time Series Data for Tapping Experiments
authors: Alaa Al Najjar, Nakul Havaldar, Valentin Plenk, Marco Linß
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of real-time tool condition monitoring in tapping processes using machine learning techniques, with a focus on cross-material generalization and robust fault detection. The study leverages a historical dataset from 1988, comprising 2,195 tapping experiments on two steel alloys- 16 MnCr 5 and 42 CrMo 4 -monitoring torque (Mz) signals to predict binary quality outcomes (good/bad) based on defined quality criteria. To overcome limitations in prior work, the authors introduce a feature extraction method that captures both amplitude and duration characteristics across distinct phases of the torque signal,. The evaluation framework includes increasingly challenging train/test splits: random, run-wise holdout, and cross-material (training on 16 MnCr 5, testing on 42 CrMo 4), enabling assessment of real-world generalizability. Multiple machine learning models are tested using both raw time-series data (after cleaning and normalization) and engineered features. Results show Matthews Correlation Coefficients (MCC) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.40-0.41$</tex> under cross-material testing-indicating moderate but meaningful generalization across materials with different mechanical properties. This performance level suggests that fundamental physical regularities in successful tapping produce consistent torque signatures, enabling transferable detection of anomalies without retraining. Findings support the feasibility of plug-and-play monitoring systems in agile manufacturing environments, where minimal setup and broad applicability are essential.

title: Portfolio Optimization on NIFTY 50 Stocks Using the Golden Jackal Optimizer
authors: Anup, Namita Srivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Portfolio optimization continues to be a challenging problem in computational finance. The presence of boundary constraints and cardinality limits further increases its nonlinearity and non-convexity. Traditional optimization techniques, including quadratic programming, often fail to efficiently handle such NP-hard problems. Consequently, metaheuristic algorithms have gained prominence for their ability to generate near-optimal solutions with acceptable computational cost. This paper applies and compares three advanced metaheuristic algorithms-Golden Jackal Optimization, Rao-1, and Grey Wolf Optimization-for solving the cardinality-constrained mean-variance portfolio optimization problem using real stock data from the NIFTY 50 index. The performance of each algorithm is assessed using key metrics such as portfolio return, portfolio risk, Sharpe ratio, and fitness value. Experimental results show that GJO consistently outperforms Rao-1 and GWO by achieving higher returns, and better alignment with the theoretical efficient frontier. These findings highlight the robustness and practical suitability of GJO for intelligent portfolio construction in the Indian stock market.

title: Analysis of Concentric Circular Antenna Array Based Radar for Conformable Search and Multiobject Hunting
authors: Md. Farhan Shahriar, Md. Selim Hossain, Md. Mominul Hasan Shawon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Hunting multiple objects within the same range, azimuth, and elevation cell is a key challenge in radar technology. This is mainly because objects can overlap in space. Multiple objects are often affected by noise and environmental factors. Traditional radars excel at detecting a single target. The performance of the traditional radars decreases when it comes to identifying and tracking several objects at the same time. This research introduces Concentric Circular Antenna Array (CCAA)-based radar to overcome the challenge. The system utilizes an advanced model that features volume search, cued search, and tracking modes. This proposed system adapts to a complex and challenging environment. Advanced filtering techniques are employed to improve detection and reduce side-lobes. Simulations run in MATLAB demonstrate that the proposed CCAA-based radar can effectively search and track multiple moving targets with an impressive accuracy of about 98.80%. This proposed model gives precise details about their position, range, and velocity. These findings suggest that the CCAA-based radar holds great promise for detecting multi-objects in challenging conditions.

title: A Data-Driven–Expert Hybrid Model for Real-Time Battery State of Health Diagnostics
authors: Ibrahim Zafar, Chodagam Srinivas, Regadi Madhurima, Kenchappa Gari Yogananda, Tanguturi Mohammad Sadiq, Gopireddy Sindhusree
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate estimation of a battery’s State of Health (SOH) is essential for the safety and longevity of energy storage systems used in electric vehicles and renewable energy applications. This paper presents a hybrid intelligent battery management framework that fuses fuzzy logic inference with support vector machine (SVM) regression to improve SOH prediction under diverse operating conditions. Multi-sensor data capacity fade, internal resistance, temperature, vibration, and humidity were synthesized in MATLAB to evaluate realistic degradation behavior over 100 charge-discharge cycles. A Mamdani fuzzy inference system first encodes expert knowledge to generate a preliminary SOH estimate under uncertain or noisy conditions. This estimate is then combined with the original sensor features and processed by an SVM regressor with optimized hyper-parameters, enabling the model to capture complex nonlinear relationships. Comparative evaluations show that the hybrid method reduces estimation bias, achieves lower RMSE, and provides higher R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values than standalone FIS or SVM approaches. The results confirm that integrating expert rules with machine learning improves robustness and interpretability, offering a practical solution for real-time SOH monitoring in advanced battery management systems.

title: Comparing Machine Learning Models for Non-Performing Asset Recovery: A Multi-Paradigm Evaluation in Post-Flood Regional Banking
authors: Gautam Kaistha, Anand Shankar Raja M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The paper compares four alternative Non-Performing Asset (NPA) recovery models using different computational paradigms: Multi-Layer Perceptron, Support Vector Classifier with RBF Kernel, K-Nearest Neighbors and Quadratic Discriminant Analysis. We evaluate their effectiveness with a 36-month portfolio simulation of 10,000 bank customers of the affected regional banking institutions by the 2017 West Bengal floods using a complete dataset. Neural and kernel techniques have outstanding performances with MLP and RBF-SVC reaching final NPA of 2.80-3.05 % and best target achievement within 25-26 months using 12.0 % interest rate policy. This joint approach to essentially different computational methods on the same optimal policies is excellent confirmation of robustness. K-Nearest Neighbors has a paradoxical result: the lowest discriminative score (AUC: 0.645) but the competitive end NPA (2.61%), indicating that it does not have an advantageous global calibration but benefits local learning.

title: Smart Dietary Swaps: Machine Learning for Nutrient-Aware Food Substitution
authors: Mulakala Vrisheeka, Malladi Nanda Gopal, Suresh Anand M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study introduces Smart Dietary Swaps, a machine-learning–driven, nutrient-aware food substitution system that makes recommendations to improve diet while taking into account user health conditions and preferences. Foods were categorized into healthy, moderate, and unhealthy food classes based on nutritional variables including calories, protein, carbohydrate, fat, fiber, sodium, micronutrients, and food type. Two datasets used for training were introduced: nutrient profiles and a large, comprehensive recipe dataset, and four methods were trained, including KNN, RF, a PCA-based MLP, and GNN. Each method was then evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score variables. The GNN outperformed the other methods for both datasets, achieving accuracy scores ranging from 87 to 89% and an F1 score of 95% that remained stable, further suggesting that the GNN generalized well and was balanced in its classification scheme. The PCA-based MLP performed well with accuracy scores of 86-85% and an F1 score of 93%, while KNN and RF achieved moderate results. The system captures user-centric health indicators (i.e., BMI, allergies, age-based nutrient requirements, etc.) and biochemical markers (i.e., iron, zinc, cholesterol, glucose), providing flexibility and personalized food substitution recommendations. In summary, the findings of this study suggest that graph-based learning, particularly the GNN approach, can be used as a reliable and effective basis for developing intelligent, data-driven personalized dietary recommendation systems.

title: Exploring Generative Pretrained Language Models for Opinion Mining in Large-Scale Twitter Social Media Data
authors: Siddhesh Amrale
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Opinion mining has gained a significant role in the last few years for the text data analysis coming from various sources like Twitter, Facebook, Google, and Amazon. It enables companies to enhance their business strategies in a proactive manner. The term "sentiment analysis" refers to the practice of collecting and categorizing customer feedback according to their positive, neutral, or negative feelings using specialized software. This paper article presents an enhanced sentiment analysis framework, which relies on the transformer-based language model to classify and study social media data. The proposed approach delineates the textual context and the semantic relations very clearly, therefore, gives better precision than the traditional machine learning models. The model uses the preprocessing, tokenization, and fine-tuning methods to deal with a variety of linguistic expressions and noisy user-generated text. Experimental findings show that the proposed GPT and ETC model attained 99.23% and 96.10 accuracy, which is much higher than the current models including Naive Bayes (NB), CNN-BiLSTM, SVC (Support Vector Classifier), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Random Forest (RF). The general result is that transformer-based architecture, especially GPT, is capable of reaching out to long-range textual dependencies and emotional context which leads to higher performance in the classification. These results confirm GPT’s robustness, adaptability, and high generalization potential for sentiment analysis across complex and noisy social media data.

title: Design of Wireless Charging Unmanned Vehicle Based on Electromagnetic Compatibility
authors: Haiyan Wu, Liqiang Sheng, Xicheng Zhu, Haoyu Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To enhance the system stability, charging efficiency, and operational safety of wirelessly charged autonomous vehicles in complex electromagnetic environments, this paper conducts a systematic design study addressing electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) issues. By analyzing the coupling mechanism between the high-frequency electromagnetic fields of wireless charging systems and the sensitive electronic equipment in autonomous vehicles, an interference propagation model is established, and a comprehensive EMC design approach is proposed, encompassing interference source suppression, propagation path control, and protection of disturbed devices. The paper elaborates in detail on the electromagnetic optimization of the wireless charging system' s circuit topology, layout and shielding strategies for onboard electronic equipment, vehicle-level grounding and filtering network design, as well as interference mitigation techniques based on software algorithms. Research results demonstrate that by establishing a multi-level EMC protection framework, internal and external electromagnetic interference can be significantly reduced, achieving highly reliable coordination between wireless charging and autonomous driving functions. Real-vehicle test data indicate that the optimized vehicle meets the requirements of international standards such as ISO 11451 and CISPR 25 in terms of both radiated emissions and immunity, with peak radiated emissions reduced by up to 16 dB, the proportion of time exceeding the threshold for radar ranging error decreased by 94.9%, and GNSS positioning loss-of-lock phenomena completely eliminated. Under dynamic charging conditions, the vehicle's path-tracking lateral error is reduced by 65.6%, validating the feasibility and superiority of the proposed solution in improving overall vehicle EMC performancet.

title: Enhancing Battery Efficiency and Driving Safety: Advanced State of Charge Estimation Using the Extended Kalman Filter Algorithm
authors: Ashraf A. Shanaq, Subramaniam Ganesan, Debatosh Debnath, Mohamed A. Zohdy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurately estimating the battery module's state of charge (SOC) is critical in electric vehicle design. The process is essential in providing operators with precise and continuous mileage information. The accuracy of SOC estimation also helps mitigate the risk of overcharging or discharging the battery, ensuring overall driving safety. A fundamental aspect of SOC estimation methodology involves constructing a suitable battery model to achieve heightened response accuracy. This study proposes an electric vehicle SOC model that utilizes the extended Kalman filter algorithm. The research focuses on lithium-ion power batteries and uses real-world data to conduct SOC estimation investigations. The study's findings demonstrate that the proposed model exhibits accuracy when the internal battery temperature aligns with the ambient temperature at 25°C. The model has minimal terminal voltage differences, and the average voltage difference is 9mV. Applying the extended Kalman filter algorithm at room temperature significantly improves the recovery percentage of SOC voltage, surpassing an average of 73%, thereby demonstrating high accuracy. The study employs the extended Kalman algorithm to estimate SOC current waveforms, specifically emphasizing discharge currents of 4A. The simulation results showcase exceptional estimation accuracy and robust applicability in estimating SOC under these conditions. The proposed SOC model utilizing the extended Kalman filter algorithm enhances accuracy and reliability in estimating SOC for lithium-ion power batteries. The results of this study have significant implications for the design and development of electric vehicles, particularly in optimizing battery performance and ensuring driving safety.

title: Integrate Wearable Data into Digital Twins: Using Real-Time Biomedical Feedback for Diabetes Management and Hypoglycemic Event Prediction
authors: Arvind Narsing, Raed Alazaidah, P V Indira, S. Ramkumar, M. Sindhu, Y. Kamala Raju
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: A comprehensive strategy for managing diabetes is necessary to avoid hypoglycemia and its complications; this strategy should incorporate ongoing monitoring, rapid intervention, and personalised treatment programs. New implantable biomedical sensors track vitals, including heart rate, blood sugar, exercise, and sleep. We will be able to use this continuous deluge of data for therapeutic purposes when a significant amount of time has passed. The use of real-time data enables digital twin technology to update a model of a person's physiological state continuously. The authors of this study recommend combining digital twin models with data from wearable devices to better control diabetes and detect hypoglycemia. Users can assess the level of danger by simulating physiological reactions from data acquired by wearable sensors and comparing them to digital counterparts. We investigate the environmental, physiological, and behavioural factors that influence fluctuations in glucose levels using sophisticated data fusion and predictive modelling methods. Hypoglycemia episodes can be better predicted and treated by using the digital twin's physiological mimicry. The primary goals of the framework for long-term diabetes therapy are personalisation, real-time responsiveness, and scalability. The results show that our monitoring method is more effective than conventional methods for both early warning and accurate forecasting. Patients’ quality of life, safety from hypoglycemia, and data-driven diabetes care could all be improved by using digital twins that incorporate wearable data. This study advances smart healthcare systems by integrating responsive digital-twin modelling with continuous biological monitoring.

title: Designing with AI: Exploring Interactive UX Workflows for Adaptive UI Prototyping
authors: Suraj Davariya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence into user experience (UX) design is reshaping how designers prototype and refine digital interfaces. This study introduces Canvil, an AI-powered design assistant embedded within Figma, enabling iterative refinement of language-based UI components. Through a participatory user study with professional designers, it explores how AI-generated content influences UX workflows, comparing structured prompting with freeform interaction models. Findings highlight key challenges in AI-assisted design, including balancing automation with creative control and refining AI-generated suggestions to align with user intent. The results contribute insights into optimizing human-AI collaboration in interactive design environments, paving the way for more adaptive and responsive UX tools.

title: Lightweight Data Sharing Scheme Based on Proxy Re-Encryption in IIoT
authors: Bin Sun, Li Wang, Yujie Fu, Zhen Yang, Fengqun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As cloud computing advances rapidly, Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) devices increasingly delegate substantial amounts of data to the cloud for storage and dissemination. Before uploading data to the cloud, data owners have to encrypt it to guarantee data confidentiality in untrusted cloud environments. Due to its capacity to provide fine-grained data sharing, attribute-based encryption (ABE) is extensively used in IIoT. However, ABE decryption necessitates expensive pairing operations, which puts a significant computational strain on IIoT users with constrained resources. While traditional ABE outsourced decryption approaches can lower decryption computation overhead of the data user, they do not permit updating the access policy, and outsourced decryption results might not be accurate. In this paper, a lightweight data sharing system using proxy re-encryption (PRE) for IIoT is developed. By converting ABE ciphertext into an identity-based encryption ciphertext, flexible data sharing is implemented in addition to lowering the decryption computation cost. The correctness of the re-encrypted ciphertext is confirmed. Additionally, this approach accomplishes effective user revocation. The security of the proposed scheme is formally proven, and the computing efficiency and practicality for implementation in IIoT are confirmed by both theoretical analysis and experimental evaluation.

title: Designing Secure Cryptographic Communication Protocols for Wireless Control of Medical Devices in Smart Healthcare Systems and IoT-Enabled Smart Hospitals
authors: Vandana Roy, Nishi Jain, Disha Maity, Vivek Kushwaha, Swathi Pacha, Suman Punia
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Medical device communication with control servers should be secured, as the failures in the flourishing environment of smart healthcare systems and IOT-enabled hospitals may lead to patient harm and data loss. Conventional cryptography solutions are usually unable to accommodate the various harsh resource and latency requirements of a medical IoT setting. To overcome all these challenges, an innovative solution called Secure Lightweight Cryptographic Protocol for Medical IoT (SLCP-MIoT) is presented. The particular protocol, which is formulated to facilitate the use of wireless communication that is authenticated, confidential and also integrity assured, is targeted to operate within the dynamic and heterogeneous network comprising of wearable and implantable medical devices. The suggested solution introduces some key exchange techniques that are efficient, light-weight encryption, and integrity verification techniques to provide end-to-end security without imposing heavy computational overheads. Its hierarchical design allows real-time user communications, periodic session key handshaking and replay resistance without running afoul of performance. Moreover, the protocol involves secure identity provisioning and mutual authentication process that ensures that devices trust the hospital infrastructure. Adopting a two-pronged approach to security and efficiency, SLCP-MIoT is efficient in solving the problematic healthcare application requirements, when secure and reliable control of healthcare devices is required in real-time. The SLCP-MIoT technique achieved an overall accuracy of 98.7% in safe interaction and authentication of devices in smart medical environments.

title: An Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework for Proactive Cyber Defense in Dynamic Healthcare Information Systems
authors: Tirumala Ashish Kumar Manne, Mahmood Afzal Hussain, Prashant Sumanprasad Bhadoria, Balakrishnan Devaraj, Karthik Bojja, Rishiraj Kohli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Machine Learning and Autonomous Systems (ICMLAS)
abstract: Digitization of healthcare systems, which has become more prominent due to the use of technologies such as cloud computing, the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), and interoperable electronic health records, has put healthcare infrastructures in a greater place at risk of cyber threats. Conventional reactive security systems are not sufficient in the fight against sophisticated, dynamic and zero-day attacks on sensitive patient information and vital clinical services. The paper presents an Adaptive AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework that would be used to proactively counter the effects of cyberattacks in dynamic health care setting. The framework incorporates machine learning and deep learning applications of constant monitoring, behavioral analysis, and predictive detection of threats in heterogeneous healthcare systems. It identifies new and suspicious patterns of attacks by collecting and comparing real-time data on network traffic, system logs, user logs, and external threat information sources. The dynamic aspect of the framework also enables real-time adaptation of the framework, whereby automated detection model and security policy changes are made. Concentrated on scalability, automation, and explainability, it improves the speed of threat detection early, decreases response time, and positively impacts cyber resilience in healthcare systems besides being compliant with healthcare data protection laws.

title: Source Enumeration by Integrating Toeplitz Reconstruction with Elbow-based Method
authors: Xueqin Song, Xiaogang Liu, Bingbing Qi, Desheng Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: An improved Toeplitz-constrained elbow-based source enumeration (EBSE) algorithm is proposed for robust estimation of the number of sources in adverse environments. First, the Toeplitz structure is exploited to modify the covariance of the received data; subsequently, eigenvalue decomposition is performed to extract the eigenvalues, which serve as the input to the ensuing EBSE algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves stable source enumeration under conditions of low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), small snapshots, and coherent sources, significantly enhancing performance at only a modest computational cost.

title: A Secure and Energy-Efficient Cyber-Physical System Framework for Next-Generation Industrial Automation
authors: Divyashree H S, VidyaSagar Adabala, Kasi Viswanathan, Vinay Kulkarni, A Mutharasan, Hazari Naresh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The dynamic increase of on-line physical connections of the next-generation industrial automation demands a safe and energy efficient system to boost the potentialities of real time operation, predictive analytic and high control capabilities. The world that is already industrialized should not only possess the relevant systems which will keep watch over the communications routes, but also possess systems which will be able to optimize the use of the energy besides being dependable alongside the manner in which the networked devices interact with the networks. The model proposes an adjustable structure that can be utilised in achieving confidentiality, integrity, and availability of industrial information using artificial intelligence, blockchain-linked trusts, and spindly packages of cryptography. It will consume less electric power and will show fewer delays at the end in case you collocate energy conscious technologies as well as smart technologies at the border. An emergency countermeasures approach confronts the occurrence of immature threats and intelligent surveillance targets aberration accomplishment. Moreover, the framework concerns the interaction of the heterogeneous hardware with the infrastructures of the clouds computing, and the increase in the outlay at the needs of users on the very large scale of industries. Energy optimization is achieved in regards to dynamic load balancing, and dynamically based choices. The model can help to intimately automate significant infrastructure because security and efficiency form two basic constituents. The plan forms an avenue to the strengthening, intelligent, and nomadic industrial systems according to the objectives of industry 5.0. The high criteria required were successfully met by the proposed system with 96% data integrity of security and 95% policy flexibility, which showed utmost reliability.

title: Remote Sensing Cross Model Text Image Retrieval Based on Attention Correction and Filtering
authors: Chandrakala.R, N. Bharatha Devi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The establishment of a robust semantic relationship between images and their accompanying text queries is crucial for the effective use of remote sensing data. Conventional methods frequently suffer from misalignment, noise and modality gaps, which impair retrieval performance. A cross-modal retrieval framework for distant sensing image–text alignment that is improved with attention correction and filtering algorithms are proposed to overcome these difficulties. In order to reduce noise and maintain image information, the system starts with preprocessing employing an adaptive Wiener filter. For classification, a hybrid LSTM–Vision Transformer (ViT) model is used, producing reliable image embeddings. In parallel, a transformer-based text feature extractor processes text queries or captions to generate high-quality text embeddings. Following that, both modalities are projected into a common embedding space with semantic alignment imposed. Filtering removes superfluous or deceptive information, whereas attention correction highlights essential cross-modal correlations to further improve the embedding quality. Moreover, cross-model retrieval makes precise text-to-image matching possible, facilitating uses like environmental study, disaster relief, and land cover interpretation with the highest accuracy of 94.56%. The proposed approach validation from Python software shows how well filtering and attention-based correction combined to provide dependable and semantically consistent retrieval in challenging remote sensing situations.

title: A Design Thinking Approach to Developing Songtun for Student Assignment Management in Digital Learning Environments
authors: Thanakrit Janchidfah, Thanayos JindaKaew, Bunyalak Ruamsap, Pisit Chansuek
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Task management has become a critical challenge for university students in the digital era, particularly in relation to missed deadlines and delayed assignment submissions, which can negatively impact learning performance and time management skills. Although various digital tools are available to support task organization, many existing solutions are not fully developed through genuinely learner-centered approaches, especially within the context of Thai higher education. This study aims to design and develop a mobile application named “Songtun” to assist students in recording, tracking, and managing academic assignments by adopting the Design Thinking methodology as a human-centered innovation framework. The research involved 125 undergraduate students who participated in a real classroom implementation of the Songtun application. The research instruments included the developed application and a five-point Likert-scale satisfaction questionnaire. The development and evaluation process followed the five stages of Design Thinking: empathizing with users' needs, defining key problems, generating solution ideas, prototyping the application, and testing its usability in authentic learning environments. Participants used the application continuously over a two-week period, after which both quantitative and qualitative data were collected to assess system effectiveness and user experience. The findings indicate that the Songtun application effectively supports students in organizing academic tasks, reducing the likelihood of forgetting assignments, and encouraging more timely submission behaviors. Overall user satisfaction was reported at a high level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(M=4.36, S D=0.52)$</tex>, with usability receiving the highest ratings among evaluated dimensions. These results suggest that integrating Design Thinking principles into the development of educational digital tools can significantly enhance learner experience and promote self-regulated learning practices. The study highlights the potential of the Songtun application as an innovative academic support solution tailored to the needs of students in Thai higher education institutions.

title: Port-Hamiltonian Modeling and IDA-PBC Control of an Extended SEPIC Converter for Bidirectional V2X Applications
authors: Hatem SGHIRI, Marouan MARZOUG, Chaouki Mnasri, Faouzi BACHA
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a rigorous modeling and control strategy based on the port-Hamiltonian (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>) framework applied to a bidirectional DC-DC extended SEPIC converter intended for Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) applications. Unlike commonly used idealized averaged models, the proposed formulation explicitly incorporates dissipative effects arising from parasitic resistances of inductors, equivalent series resistances of capacitors, and switching component losses. The system is described using canonical energy variables, ensuring intrinsic consistency of power exchanges and dissipation. A Lyapunov based stability analysis is carried out, and an Interconnection and Damping Assignment Passivity-Based Control (IDA-PBC) law is developed to guaranty output voltage regulation under bidirectional power flow operation. The desired equilibrium point is analytically computed from the dissipative steady-state equations, avoiding the use of iterative numerical methods. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results confirm the theoretical validity of the proposed approach and demonstrate robust regulation under load variations.

title: Research on Network Anomaly Detection Based on Improved SMOTE and XGBoost: Addressing Class Imbalance and Missing Data
authors: Hanzhou Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of network technologies and the escalating sophistication of cyberattacks, network anomaly detection has become a pivotal cornerstone of modern cybersecurity defense. However, it is plagued by two persistent bottlenecks: class imbalance (where abnormal samples account for only a tiny fraction of datasets, leading traditional models to favor majority classes) and missing data (which erodes feature integrity and distorts detection accuracy). To resolve these issues, this study proposes a hybrid anomaly detection framework integrating Improved SMOTE (ISMOTE), Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE), and XGBoost: MICE preserves feature reliability while handling missing data, ISMOTE mitigates class imbalance without excessive noise, and XGBoost enables efficient, robust detection. Evaluated on CSE-CIC-IDS2018 and UNSWNB15 datasets, the framework boosts abnormal sample detection rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 12 \%$</tex> compared to baseline methods (e.g., Traditional SMOTE+XGBoost) while maintaining high precision, verifying its effectiveness in addressing both core challenges and offering practical value for real-world cybersecurity scenarios.

title: Self-Optimizing Automated Learning Pipeline for Big Data Exploration and Predictive Intelligence Applications
authors: Hrushikesh Panchabudhe, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Rohit Lanjewar, Shekhar Nipane, Aayush Borkar, Tanmay Zalke
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rapid increase in big data, which is characterized by volume, velocity, variety, veracity, and value, has placed a highly urgent need on scalable, secure, and automated machine learning (ML) solutions. The traditional ML pipelines may require a lot of manual tuning, do not operate with efficiency in terms of data management and are scaled in a distributed environment. In this paper, the Self-Optimizing Automated Learning Pipeline towards Big Data Exploration and Predictive Intelligence Applications is introduced, which is an end-to-end framework that comprises secure data ingestion, MinIO-based distributed storage, and Parquet-optimized data handling to improve its functionality and efficiency. To reduce human effort, the proposed system is an automated preprocessing, feature engineering model selection, and containerized deployment system based on Python modules. The interface is configurable, with a number of checkboxes, and is capable of supporting heterogeneous datasets and workflows which can be customized. The pipeline supports various ML models, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Transformer networks, and classic algorithms, such as XGBoost and Random Forest, that allow it to be flexible to wide range of analytical tasks. Experimental results using large-scale datasets reveal significant performance improvements - Transformer model takes 32% of the time and has 28% better storage efficiency as well as up to 94.8% accuracy and the Transformer modeling model is faster than any other predictive model. Besides, user-owned MinIO buckets improve the privacy of data and facilitate distributed processing that is scaled. On the whole, the suggested system provides an efficient, safe, and high-performance system to big data analytics, setting the base to further developments in federated learning and real-time analytics and autonomy in governing the model.

title: Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Fault Detection and Diagnosis in Industrial Systems
authors: Lazhar Khriji, Majdi Mansouri, Khadija Attouri, Abdelmalek Kouadri
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Fault Detection and Diagnosis (FDD) is critical for ensuring safety, reliability, and efficiency in modern industrial systems. Data-driven approaches have gained prominence due to their ability to handle nonlinear processes without relying on exact physical models. However, many machine learning methods lack interpretability, limiting their deployment in safety-critical environments. This paper proposes the use of Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (PolyKAN) for FDD in nonlinear industrial processes. PolyKAN employs polynomial basis functions in a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network framework, enabling transparent and structured nonlinear modeling. The approach is validated on a real cement rotary kiln process under healthy and faulty conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high fault classification accuracy with improved interpretability compared to conventional deep learning models. PolyKAN provides a practical and explainable framework for industrial fault diagnosis.

title: Writing About the History of Technology
authors: David G. Michelson
abstract: As a profession, we share stories and anecdotes concerning the history of technology in order to: 1. Promote awareness of the profession and its accomplishments amongst both policymakers and the public, 2. Promote a sense of identity and feeling of pride within the profession, 3. Share experiences and lessons learned in the past with those in the present, and, 4. Inspire future generations to reach even greater heights.

title: Impact of Deployment Strategies on Wireless Sensor Networks Performance under Security Threats
authors: Aina Mehta, Jasminder Kaur Sandhu, Harshal Patil
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to their decentralized architecture, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are particularly vulnerable to attacks targeting the network layer. This paper investigates the impact of three deployment strategies—Random, Grid, and Hierarchical—on WSN performance under sinkhole attack scenarios. Simulation-based analysis is conducted to evaluate key performance metrics, including E2E, throughput, and packet delivery ratio (PDR). Results indicate that Grid deployment achieves the highest resilience, with a PDR of 98.1%, while Hierarchical deployment offers balanced performance. In contrast, Random deployment experiences severe degradation, with PDR dropping to 26.8% and delay increasing significantly. These findings underscore the critical role of deployment strategy in ensuring network robustness under adversarial conditions. Future work will explore additional network-layer threats such as wormhole and blackhole attacks and focus on prioritizing the integration of intelligent learning models with simulation tools to enable adaptive detection and mitigation of security threats in dynamic WSN environments.

title: Cryptocurrency Price Prediction Using Deep Learning and Ensemble Methods
authors: Harsh Shivhare, Rekha Kaushik, Uday Deshmukh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are characterized by extreme volatility and a decentralized nature, making price predictions very challenging. This work performs a comparative evaluation of a statistical, machine learning, and deep learning methodology for forecasting Bitcoin prices using one year of daily data from March 2024 to February 2025. The models compared are ARIMA, Prophet, XGBoost, LSTM, GRU, Bi-LSTM, and an ensemble LSTM–GRU. Experimental results are presented after comparing machine learning and deep learning models with statistical baselines.
