title: Gliding Control Strategy of Intelligent Measurement and Control Carriers Based on Virtual Target
authors: Yijie Zhang, Junjie Xi, Haipeng Deng, Zhuo Zhou, Jinna Xue, Shaoqi Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The traditional gliding control strategy for gliding intelligent measurement and control carriers has problems with large flight path dispersion and large arrival point angle dispersion. To solve this problem, a mid-course control gliding strategy based on virtual targets is proposed in this paper, and the design method of virtual target gliding control strategy and flight path inclination control loop is introduced in detail. The virtual target is defined as a series of spatial points with position, velocity, and time properties selected along the nominal trajectory, serving as real-time tracking reference points. Through digital simulation comparison, after adopting the virtual target gliding control strategy in the mid-course control phase, flight path dispersion and arrival point angle dispersion are significantly reduced, showing significant advantages over traditional gliding control strategies that adopt a fixed angle-of-attack program or optimal lift-to-drag ratio gliding control.

title: Physics-Informed Diffusion Models for Smooth and Goal-Directed Trajectory Generation
authors: Zhihua Fang, Heng Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The widespread application of location-based services has generated massive amounts of mobility data, highlighting the need for synthetic trajectory generation to address privacy concerns and data scarcity. While recent Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) have shown exceptional capabilities in modeling continuous coordinate trajectories, existing models treat trajectory points simply as abstract numeric values. This ignores the underlying physical properties of human mobility, often resulting in unnatural jitter, sharp implausible turns, and a failure to accurately reach intended destinations. In this paper, we propose KineDiff, a lightweight, physics-informed diffusion framework designed to generate highly realistic, smooth, and goal-directed trajectories. Instead of relying on computationally heavy architectural modifications or external map matching, we introduce a novel composite loss function that explicitly regularizes the diffusion training process. Specifically, we design a Spatially-Adaptive Kinematic Regularization Loss to penalize high-order motion instability (i.e., irregular acceleration) while preserving intricate intersection maneuvers, and an Endpoint Anchor Loss to ensure task-specific destination accuracy. Experiments on real-world urban trajectory datasets demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms traditional generative baselines and standard diffusion models. Comprehensive ablation studies and dual-metric convergence analyses further confirm that KineDiff reduces the trajectory length distribution error by over 90% while maintaining highly realistic spatial density patterns.

title: Dual-Band CSRR-Based Microstrip Antenna Design for Next-Generation 6G Terahertz Networks
authors: Nirzar Barua, Md. Akter Hossain, Md Rakibul Islam Rony, Md Nazmul Islam Sajib, Saikat Barua, Chandan Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This article showcased a compact CSRR-based dualband microstrip patch antenna operating at terahertz (THz) frequencies to support high-speed wireless communication infrastructures. A $25 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ quartz substrate is used to design the antenna to achieve low surface wave loss and mechanical stability. By integrating three complementary split-ring resonators (CSRRs) within the radiating patch, effective miniaturization is achieved without compromising the dual-band characteristics. The structure exhibits resonance at 1.01 THz and 1.31 THz and reflection coefficients of $\mathbf{- 3 6. 2 9} \mathbf{~ d B}$ and $\mathbf{- 3 9. 7 0 ~ d B}$, respectively, by including rectangular and embedded slot features. It demonstrates efficient radiation and stable reflection performance, achieving gains of 6.54 dBi and 7.31 dBi with radiation efficiencies of 75% and 65% at the respective bands. Strong mode excitation of both resonant frequencies and the linear polarization properties are confirmed by the surface current distribution due to the CSRR loading effect. With its compact size, high efficiency, and dualband response, the proposed antenna exhibits strong potential for next-generation THz and 6 G communication systems.

title: AI/ML-Driven vs. Conventional Decoding in VLC: Mitigating Channel Interference and Nonlinear Impairments
authors: M. S. Yuvaraj, Madhamanchi Sreevani, S. Kaliappan, Lakshmaiya N
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Visible Light Communication (VLC) experiences performance loss from LED nonlinearity and ambient/intersystem interference, which undermine conventional decoding methods. The Purpose of this paper systematically compares AI/ML-driven decoders with classical approaches under realistic single- and mixed-impairment conditions. The Objective is to quantify accuracy, robustness, and resource trade-offs across clean, nonlinear, interference, and combined "worst-case" datasets. A controlled dataset generation pipeline (hardware or high-fidelity simulation) injects LED clipping, ambient light noise and multipath; decoders evaluated include thresholding, ZF, MMSE, MAP, ANN, CNN, LSTM and autoencoder end-to-end models. Evaluation employs K-fold cross-validation, ablation sweeps over impairment strengths and sensitivity analyses with BER/SER/MSE and computational-cost metrics. The Key results is the ML decoders substantially reduce BER in high-interference and strong-nonlinearity regimes and demonstrate smoother degradation across mixed impairments, at the cost of higher compute and memory footprints. Findings guide decoder selection for resource-constrained embedded receivers versus high-performance VLC nodes and motivate FPGA/ASIC implementations, generative data augmentation, and adaptive decoding for improved generalization.

title: Real Time Suspicious Activity Detection Using CNN and LSTM
authors: K.Priya, A.Mutharasi, D.Anitha, N. Krishnammal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Public safety monitoring in busy places like transportation centers, markets, and large gatherings is hard because of things like people blocking views, complicated crowd movements, and not enough humans to watch everything. This paper introduces SafeCrowd Vision, a real-time system that uses multiple types of data to find suspicious actions, such as stealing, fighting, and unusual sounds. The system uses CNNs to look at images and LSTMs to understand how things change over time. YOLOv8 is used for fast object detection, and DeepSORT helps track people and objects even when they are hidden. The system checks if people are still with their belongings to spot theft, looking for sudden changes or missing items. At the same time, an audio part listens for strange sounds by looking at features like sound energy and how often the sound crosses zero. Combining video and sound helps the system work better in tough situations like crowded areas or dim lighting. Tests on standard datasets, fake theft situations, and real surveillance videos show that the system is accurate and fast enough for real-time use in smart city systems.

title: An Enhanced Early Alzheimer’s Detection Model Using a Hybrid Decision Tree–SVM Framework
authors: Sumit Kumar, Sonika Thapak, Manmohan Singh, Vijay Dhote, Anubhav Sharma, Shilpa Jain
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: It is necessary to diagnose Alzheimer disease early enough so that it is effectively treated to prevent the progressive development of the condition further. This term paper is a proposal of a more advanced predicting model of early Alzheimer detection based on the adoption of a hybrid system that merges the techniques of Decision Tree (DT) and Support Vector machine (SVM). The Decision Tree algorithm is used to have an efficient feature selection and initial classification whereas the SVM classifier is better able to enhance accuracy and reliability of the final prediction because it is able to work with difficult data patterns. The designed hybrid model will examine data in the field of medicine and cognitions so that the discovery of first signs of the Alzheimer disease can be executed far more accurately. Experimental evidence has shown that the combined DT-SVM method enhances accuracy of classification, minimized prediction error, as well as offered a stronger and more efficient system of diagnostic support than the single machine learning models. The model can help medical practitioners to take accurate and timely decisions in the diagnosis of the Alzheimer at an early stage.

title: A Dual-Phase Optimization Framework for Image Regeneration: PSO-Based Search and GD Refinement
authors: Aarti Verma, Mamta Bansal, Swati Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization-Gradient Descent (PSO-GD) is evaluated for image regeneration, focusing on convergence, quality, and computational efficiency. Experimental results on synthetic and real-world datasets show that PSO-GD outperforms standalone PSO and GD, achieving average improvements of 15.3% in PSNR and 18.7% in SSIM. The hybrid method converges 23.4% faster and maintains a strong balance between exploration and exploitation. Statistical analysis (p ¡ 0.001) sup-ports these findings. These results highlight PSO-GD as a practical approach for image restoration tasks requiring both quality and efficiency.

title: Lightweight Greenhouse Rose Detection Under Cluttered Backgrounds and Illumination Variations via YOLOv13n Enhancements
authors: Haotian Ding, Qian Qiao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Monitoring roses in greenhouses with UAV images is useful for inspection and management, but detection is difficult because of cluttered foliage, occlusions, and changing illumination. In this work, we propose ROSE-YOLO, a lightweight rose detector built on YOLOv13n. To make YOLOv13n work better in greenhouse UAV images, we add three modules: C3HTB for more stable feature extraction under clutter and brightness changes, BLAE for adaptive downsampling that better preserves small-object cues, and CAFM for feature refinement before prediction. Experiments on our HR test set show that ROSE-YOLO achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 3. 9 \%}$</tex> precision, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 4. 5 \%}$</tex> recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 3. 7 \%}$</tex> mAP@ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 0}$</tex>, outperforming the YOLOv13n baseline (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.2 \% \text{mAP} {@} 50$</tex>). ROSE-YOLO also maintains real-time speed (96 FPS), which is close to the baseline (102 FPS). These results indicate that the proposed improvements can enhance detection quality in greenhouse UAV images with only a small speed cost.

title: Construction of A New Power System Line Loss Management And Evaluation System Based On Automation Technology
authors: Haibo Luo, Lingyong Li, Zhenjie Li, Ruqin Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In view of the problems of single dimension (over-reliance on line loss rate), insufficient dynamic prediction ability and low credibility of multi-source data in the current distribution substation line loss management effect evaluation system, this study constructed a new power system line loss management evaluation system based on automation technology, by establishing a multi-dimensional evaluation model covering technical indicators (line loss rate reduction, voltage qualification rate), economic indicators (investment return rate, operation and maintenance cost reduction) and social indicators (user power outage perception, power supply reliability). The LSTM-Attention hybrid network is innovatively used to predict the line loss rate change one year after the management, and the fuzzy comprehensive evaluation method and entropy method are integrated to achieve dynamic optimization of indicator weights. The empirical results of 12 distribution substations in eastern Guangdong show that the system has achieved an accuracy rate of 98.5% in line loss anomaly identification, an average increase of 41.6% in the return on investment of management projects compared with traditional methods, and the average annual power outage time of urban users has been reduced from 12.4 minutes to 6.7 minutes, forming a "prediction-evaluation-feedback" closed-loop mechanism, which provides a quantitative decision-making basis for the improvement of line loss management efficiency and precise resource allocation of power grid enterprises.

title: Efficient Graph Convolutional Networks with Attention Mechanisms for Precise Prediction of Cardiovascular Diseases
authors: Nagarjuna Nallametti, S. Kavitha, Lakshmi H.N
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The novel system is bringing to the table is a way to increase in diagnostic-accuracy for cardiovascular disorders. Whereas, the architecture that relies on Sparse Graph Convolutional Networks (GCNs) is used to model direct dependencies between various nodes, is such that the model provides data flexibility enabling the model to incorporate computational efficiency and scalability. A mechanism to prioritize urgent features and relationships are introduced to the attention model that can reconfigure its focus on the medical indicators and correlations that matter. In attention, the GCN architecture to be used is data that may be of different types like clinical record, image, and molecular biological data which it processes into a comprehensive risk assessment. This system stands out for the following, development of a new graph representation which is sparse to be efficient in terms of computation, the introduction of a novel design of the attention layers focusing on adaptive feature weighting and the proposal of the ability to deal with voluminous datasets with minimal information leakage accordingly. Experiments that were conducted on benchmark cardiovascular datasets reveal.

title: Closed-Loop MUSIC-EKF: Robust Resource Allocation for ISAC Tracking of Maneuvering Targets Under Strong Interference
authors: Meng Li, Zhixiang Deng, Ziyi Wang, Chao Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) systems operating in complex electromagnetic environments face significant challenges in balancing sensing precision and communication throughput. Traditional open-loop resource allocation schemes often struggle with sudden target maneuvers and strong directional interference. To address these issues, this paper proposes a robust, dual-layer closed-loop resource allocation architecture that tightly couples Multiple Signal Classification (MUSIC) spectrum estimation with an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). Specifically, at the signal processing layer (inner loop), the subspace orthogonality of MUSIC is exploited to nullify strong interference, providing the tracker with high-fidelity angular measurements. At the resource management layer (outer loop), a state-driven control law based on Predicted Error Covariance (PEC) is established. This mechanism senses tracking uncertainty: it operates with minimal power during steady-state phases and adaptively mobilizes radar resources during maneuvers to mitigate model mismatch. Comprehensive ablation studies and parameter sensitivity analyses demonstrate that the proposed “computation-for-power” strategy effectively transcends the zero-sum game between sensing and communication. Simulation results show that in environments with an Interference-to-Noise Ratio (INR) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 0 ~ d B}$</tex>, the system maintains sub-meter tracking accuracy (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.2 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>) while reducing redundant radar power consumption. This “on-demand” allocation leads to a 22.4 % gain in energy efficiency and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5. 4 \%} - \text{1 0. 2 \%}$</tex> increase in average communication throughput compared to fixed-power baseline schemes. The real-time feasibility of the architecture is further validated through computational complexity analysis, confirming its suitability for high-dynamic ISAC applications.

title: ResQNow: An Intelligent AI–AR Integrated Emergency Response System with Offline Communication Capability
authors: A. P. Saroon, Medha Bhat, Sufaila T. S., Pranava N. A.
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Delayed emergency response, lack of real-time bystander guidance, and dependence on continuous internet connectivity significantly reduce survival outcomes during medical emergencies. This paper presents ResQNow, an intelligent AI–AR integrated emergency response system designed for reliable operation under both online and offline conditions. The proposed system integrates a Flutter-based mobile application with a FastAPI backend and Firebase services to enable rapid SOS activation, continuous GPS tracking, secure authentication, and responder coordination. Emergency type and severity are identified using a hybrid intelligence framework combining cloud-based conversational AI with an offline rule-based classifier. Augmented reality (AR) guidance provides real-time visual assistance for critical first-aid procedures such as CPR and AED usage. To ensure alert delivery in network-constrained environments, encrypted Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) multi-hop relay communication is employed. Experimental evaluation demonstrates SOS dispatch latency of 2–3 seconds, AR initialization latency below 1.5 seconds, BLE relay reliability above 90%, and emergency classification accuracy of approximately 89%, validating the system’s effectiveness and resilience in real-world emergency scenarios.

title: A Multimodal Approach for Violence Detection in Public Spaces
authors: Rajasekar A, Yugarakshini G S, Shajitha S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Identifying violence in real-world settings remains a challenging task due to the intrinsic complexity of multiple-person interactions, frequent occlusions, and the need for continuous temporal analysis across crowded surveillance scenes. This paper proposes an AI-driven surveillance system that uses a multimodal deep learning approach to detect sustained violent activities by leveraging state-of-the-art object detection, pose estimation, and temporal action recognition techniques. For fast and accurate identification of persons and possible weapons such as knives, bottles, and sticks, it implements a lightweight variant of YOLOv8 and leverages MediaPipe for skeleton-based pose estimation of multiple persons over 33 keypoints per subject. In order to distinguish sustained violent activity from isolated or benign movements, the fused object and pose features go through a two-layer LSTM that models spatiotemporal dependencies over sliding windows of video frames, thus performing effective recognition of evolving episodes of violence rather than an instantaneous event. Temporal reasoning is integrated in this framework with contextual understanding in generating descriptive alerts containing violence probability, timestamp, and camera metadata surfaced to operators on a real-time dashboard and/or SMS-based notifications. Experimental evaluation on Hockey Fight and Crowd Violence benchmark datasets demonstrates that the proposed multimodal model gives higher accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score than BiLSTM-only baselines and shows improved robustness against background motion and crowded scenes. Keeping compactness of the parameter footprint and low latency in design, this allows for the system to conduct real-time deployment on edge devices connected to CCTV networks and further enhances the potential for improving operational effectiveness and proactive intervention in public safety applications.

title: AI-Driven Legal Chatbot: Enhancing Legal Information Accessibility
authors: Onkar Somvanshi, Ankur Jain, Ajay Kumar Phulre, Vipin Jain, Varun Singh, Priscilla Dinkar Moyya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-driven legal chatbot that uses NLP and deep learning to facilitate access to legal information. Precisely, we identify the crucial challenges in the development of legal question-answering systems: the inherent difficulty of legal language and concepts, contextualization, and response generation in real time. Our proposed framework incorporates both macro and micro retrieval approaches that jointly extract the best answers from domain-specific models, such as Legal-BERT and T5, which are fine-tuned over various legal corpora. Given that our chatbot implements contemporary explainable artificial intelligence techniques and simple continuous learning, the information we provide is accurate and compliant with ethical standards. Experimental evaluations conducted on the Hindu Marriage Act dataset demonstrate that our proposed Lexicon-based Hybrid approach achieved an accuracy of 92%, significantly higher than that of purely generative models, thus being able to effectively democratize legal access for professionals and laypersons alike.

title: Obfuscation Resilient Android Malware Detection using GCN for cyber-physical devices
authors: Jagrut Lade, Dev Patel, Shailendra Aote
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Android malware increasingly uses advanced obfuscation to bypass detection, threatening resource-constrained cyber-physical systems. We introduce a lightweight Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) model that achieves obfuscation-resilient malware detection by combining API graph analysis and global app features within a two-layer architecture. An automated obfuscation pipeline systematically trains the model on diverse code transformations. Evaluated on 1,556 Drebin and AndroZoo samples, the approach reaches 87.1% training and 91% external validation accuracy. The compact model is deployable on edge devices without cloud reliance, supporting autonomous protection for industrial, healthcare, and critical infrastructure environments. This research bridges the gap between robust malware detection and practical real-world deployment.

title: Ensemble-Based Detection of Cyberbullying in Bangla-English Mixed Social Media Comments
authors: Muradul Islam, Md. Abdullah Ibne Aziz, Ferdaus Anam Jibon, Marisa Parvez Oishy, Md Shaiful Islam Bapin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Cyberbullying is a major social problem, particularly in multilingual settings such as Bangladesh where the users often combine both Bangla and English in comments posted on social media. The current literature in Bangladesh has mostly concentrated on methods of detecting cyberbullying based on either Bangali or English but not both. This paper fills this gap by suggesting an ensemble-based method to identify cyberbullying in the mixed texts of Bangali and English. Our dataset comprises of publicly available data, of 44,001 annotated Facebook comments, and it has five categories: non-bullying, religious, sexual, threat, and other cyberbullying. We have used a stacked ensemble of Random Forest and LSTM with Multilingual BERT models as the meta-classifier. The ensemble model attains an accuracy of 88.48, which is better than that of individual models and proves to be very resilient in the presence of a class imbalance and linguistic noise. The results of our study point to the usefulness of ensemble learning in multilingual cyberbullying detection and offers a basis to construct scalable and automated code-mixed moderation systems. It is the first structured study to deal with cyberbullying detection in mixed social media texts written in Bangali and English in Bangladesh and the first to provide a methodological innovation, as well as practical implications of online safety.

title: User-Centered Textual Attribute Extraction: attBiLSM-CRF Technique in E-Commerce Platforms
authors: Peng Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the inability of existing methods to capture diverse user interests, we propose attBiLSTM-CRF, a personalized extraction framework. It integrates three key components: (1) a user attention matrix for dynamic preference modeling; (2) a joint prediction function combining this matrix with CRF for optimized inference; and (3) rule-based constraints leveraging a priori knowledge. This approach enables personalized product displays, achieving 92.3% accuracy on the Clothing dataset.

title: DeepHemNet: Automated Acute Leukemia Subtype Classification from Peripheral Blood Smear Images
authors: Dhandapani Samiappan, Jenesh J, Senthilkumar K
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Acute leukemia is a life-threatening hematologic malignancy in which early and precise subtype diagnosis is critical in treating patients. Conventional microscopic interpretation of peripheral blood smear images is cumbersome, subjective and susceptible to inter-observer differences. In order to overcome these drawbacks, we offer DeepHemNet, which is a hybrid deep learning model that incorporates a convolutional neural network (CNNs) and Transformers to classify acute leukemia subtypes automatically. DeepHemNet has CNN backbones (ResNet, DenseNet) to extract strong local spatial representations and uses Transformers to learn long-range morphological correlations between cells. A new ensemble fusion layer is formed on top of these complementary representations, and it is more effective in distinguishing the subtypes that are visually similar. The pipeline also uses stain normalization, color augmentation, and contrast augmentation to guarantee the stain-invariant learning between laboratories and GAN-based minority subtype augmentation to alleviate the imbalance in classes. Besides classification, DeepHemNet also provides the estimation of blast cell ratio by a multi-task extension, which provides clinically relevant quantitative information. Experimental analysis has shown high performance (95% accuracy, macro-F1 of 0.94) and good generalization to external data, indicating that DeepHemNet is a valid, interpretable, and clinically deployable system of diagnostic assistance.

title: Balanced Deep Neural Network (BDNN) for Scalable, Real-Time Phishing URL Detection Using Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Ensembles
authors: Ahsan Ullah, Hm Alvee Hasan, Mohammad Anwar Hossain, Noshin Un Noor, Kazi Hassan Robin, Mst. Nishita Aktar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Phishing remains one of the most pressing security problem with increasingly sophisticated attacks that are difficult to detect by existing systems. In this paper, we propose a new hybrid phishing detection system named as Balanced Deep Neural Network (BDNN) to deal with class imbalance issue and to enhance the detection sensitivity achieved through a mixture of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) models. We propose BDNN that uses weighted loss functions and dynamic sampling to promote detection performance, especially for minority locating phishing examples. Leveraging the merits of ML models for structural URL feature extraction and DL models for learning sequential patterns, our approach could reach high accuracy of 96.5% and recall of 96.6%. The system is deployed as a real-time browser extension and less than 200 ms to classify URLs, guaranteeing users smooth experience. Additionally, it contains an ongoing learning component that is used to counter new phishing strategies. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed BDNN ensemble not only outperforms some state of the art traditional models, but also has a high ease of deployment for real-time applications.

title: The Intelligent Electric Vehicle Using Adaptive Sliding Mode Monitoring for Nonlinear Complex Systems
authors: Lajmi Fatma, Achraf Jabeur Telmoudi, Jaouher Chrouta
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive sliding-mode control (ASMC) framework for nonlinear multivariable systems and applies it to an autonomous electric-vehicle (EV) platform. The approach integrates a high-level interval-valued fuzzy Petri-net (IVFPN) supervisor that optimizes energy consumption and carbon-dioxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{CO}_{2})$</tex> emissions by adaptively managing torque distribution and regenerativebraking behavior. The ASMC guarantees trajectory tracking and robustness against parameter variations, unmodeled dynamics, and external disturbances. Lyapunov-based analysis demonstrates the asymptotic stability of the closed-loop system. MATLAB/Simulink simulations validate the proposed controller under speed-tracking and load-disturbance scenarios. Results show improved convergence, reduced control chattering, and a 22% decrease in estimated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions compared to a conventional sliding-mode controller. The proposed method provides a promising platform for energy-efficient and environmentally sustainable autonomous EVs. Recent advances in autonomous and electric mobility have intensified the need for robust, energy-aware control architectures. Nonlinearities, coupled dynamics, and parameter uncertainties challenge conventional linear control methods. Sliding-mode control (SMC) has been widely recognized for its robustness to modeling errors and external perturbations

title: A Structured Multi-Criteria Decision Making Approach for Lithium Battery Selection in Electric Vehicle Applications
authors: Imen Azzouz, Kais Brik, Faouzi Ben Ammar
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Batteries play a crucial role in determining the performance, safety, and service life of hybrid electric vehicles. Consequently, selecting an appropriate battery technology represents a complex decision-making process that involves the simultaneous consideration of multiple, often conflicting, criteria. These criteria include key performance indicators such as energy capacity, cycle life, specific power, power density, and charging time, as well as economic and operational factors including cost, driving range, accessibility, and the risk of thermal runaway. To address this challenge, this study proposes a structured and systematic evaluation framework based on the PROMETHEE II multi-criteria decision making method. The proposed approach enables a comprehensive comparison of lithium battery technologies by integrating performance, economic, and safety aspects, thereby supporting informed and reliable decision-making for electric vehicle applications.

title: DGE-1DNet: Efficient 1D Channel Estimation for 5G NTN LEO Satellites
authors: Zhimin Huang, Gaosai Liu, Huiling Hou, Guang Liang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication is a cornerstone of 5G Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN). However, the substantial computational overhead of deep learning-based channel estimation poses a significant challenge for energy-constrained satellite payloads. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes DGE-1DNet, a highly lightweight 1D convolutional framework optimized for on-chip deployment. We depart from the high-latency 2D “image-based” paradigm, adopting a symbollevel 1D sequence architecture that eliminates buffering latency. The core of our design is a Ghost-based Inverted Residual structure, which explicitly decouples “intrinsic” spectral features from redundant “ghost” maps using cheap linear operations, drastically reducing parameter count. To compensate for the performance loss inherent in lightweight models under high-mobility LEO channels, a Dynamic Spectrum-Aware Attention mechanism is incorporated to adaptively recalibrate features. Simulation results based on 3GPP TDL-D models show that DGE-1DNet achieves estimation accuracy comparable to heavyweight baselines (e.g., DnCNN) while reducing Floating Point Operations (FLOPs) by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0. 5 \%}$</tex>. This architecture provides an optimal trade-off between computational efficiency and spectral robustness for next-generation satellite terminals.

title: Enhancing Chronic Disease Prediction in IoMT Enabled Healthcare 5.0 Using Deep Learning
authors: Iqra Rizvi, Ashish Tiwari
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rise of healthcare 5.0 which is powered by the Artificial Intelligence and Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), and extensive connectivity brings to light great opportunities for intelligent real time health care solutions. Chronic conditions like diabetes, which require ongoing care and quick intervention to avert serious complications are a focus. This research presents a Deep Learning based approach for early diagnosis of diabetes in an IoMT enabled Health care 5.0 setting. The research uses real time data from medical IoT devices and very advanced neural network structures which in turn do a great job at identifying complex indicators of diabetes risk. Also, the research study using public health diabetes data shows that the developed system does indeed outperform ancient machine learning in terms of accuracy, sensitivity and specificity. The findings emphasize the promise of combining deep learning with IoMT for improving predictive analytics, enabling proactive clinical decision-making, and facilitating next-generation proactive management of chronic ailments.

title: Deep Learning for Retinal Disease Classification: A Comparison of InceptionV3 and MobileNetV2
authors: Samar Ghadhab, Amna Maraoui, Imen Werda
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Early detection of eye diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and cataract is critical to preventing vision loss and ensuring timely treatment. In this study, we propose an automated classification system for retinal diseases using deep learning and transfer learning techniques. Two models based on convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, InceptionV3 and MobileNetV2, pretrained on the ImageNet dataset, are fine-tuned and evaluated on a labeled dataset of retinal fundus images. The images were pre-processed and resized to match the input requirements of each model. Transfer learning allowed leveraging learned features while adapting the networks to the specific task of retinal disease classification. Both models were trained and validated on a balanced dataset containing four classes: normal, diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and cataract. In this study we tested and compared the performance of both CNN models, the obtained results show that InceptionV3 have an accuracy of 0.96 and peaking at 0.994, and MobileNetV2 have a lower accuracy of 0.81 on a publicly accessible retinal ophthalmoscopy image dataset including 4217 images.

title: Advance Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Agricultural Robotics: A Deep Learning (DL) System for Crop Tracking, Yield Prediction, and Weed Identification
authors: C Praveen Kumar, Yousuf Hazaimeh, Aleemullakhan Pathan, M. Srinivasa Rao, Srilakshmi Aluri, B. Sathiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is being used in agriculture due to increasing food demand, difficulties in the labour market, and worries about the environment. Robots equipped with artificial intelligence and deep learning can enhance monitoring, decision-making, and precision farming. Artificial intelligence-enabled agribots can monitor crops, foretell harvests, and detect weeds. Utilising sensors and deep learning, the proposed system examines visual, temporal, and spatial data from agricultural fields. Convolutional neural networks identify crops and weeds simultaneously. Using temporal learning to track crop growth allows for more accurate yield prediction. Field adaptability, resistance to occlusion and light noise, and real-time operation are the fundamental features of the system. By eliminating both human error and chemical pesticides, this technology expedites weed detection and crop monitoring. Evaluation of experimental data enhances weed detection during planting, yield prediction, and crop distinction. Research shows that agricultural robots can use fewer resources while increasing their longevity, efficiency, and production through deep learning. Precision agriculture that is data-driven and scalable is the goal of this initiative.

title: Design and Optimal Sizing of a Containerized Hybrid Microgrid for Remote Military Outposts
authors: Hamadi Bouaicha, Tarek Azzabi, Khalil Ayari
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of providing reliable and sustainable electricity to remote military outposts in Tunisia, which currently rely heavily on diesel generators. The reliance on diesel poses significant operational, logistical, economic, and environmental burdens. To overcome these limitations, this work proposes the design and optimal sizing of a hybrid containerized microgrid, named the “Field Power Container”. The system integrates photovoltaic (PV) panels, a diesel generator, and lithium-ion battery storage within a standard 20-foot shipping container, ensuring transportability and rapid deployment. The sizing methodology combines an empirical sizing based on a detailed load profile and local meteorological data from the Ksar Ghilane site, with a subsequent optimization performed using HOMER Pro software. Simulation results indicate that the optimal configuration, comprising a 15 kW PV array, a 30 kVA diesel generator, and an 80 kWh battery bank, can cover over 93% of the annual energy demand from solar energy. Compared to a diesel-only solution, the hybrid system achieves a drastic reduction in annual fuel consumption (from 29,600 liters to 788 liters), significantly lowers greenhouse gas emissions, and presents a more favorable Net Present Cost (NPC) and Levelized Cost of Energy (COE) over its lifetime. This study demonstrates the technical and economic viability of hybrid containerized microgrids for not just reducing energy costs but also for enhancing energy resilience and sustainability in forward military operations fields.

title: Smart Environmental Monitoring: A Scalable IoT Solution for Air and Water Quality Management
authors: P.Rama, Pandiaraj A, Chandru KS, Shankararaman R
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Abstract- The Smart Environmental Monitoring System for Air and Water Quality Index is a real-time, sensor-driven Internet of Things solution for continuously monitoring air and water quality because of this emerging concern. This system employ several ecological sensors, including the DHT22 temperature and humidity sensor, the MQ135 air pollutant sensor, and the pH and TDS sensors for water quality. The sensors interfaced with Arduino connect to a Flask-based web function that displays the data in real time. This platform enables continuous data intake, storage in CSV format, and apparition of key environmental parameter through dynamic dashboard and charts. It also employs machine learning technique to forecast pH and air quality trends for up to seven days, intelligently categorize conditions, such as "Good," "Moderate," and "Hazardous," and make mock predictions. A gas probability table based on MQ135 sensor readings has also been included, estimating the probability of dangerous gases such as CO, NH₃, and benzene. This addresses some of the SDGs including but not limited to Good Health and Well-Being (SDG 3), Clean Water and cleanliness (SDG 6), and Climate Action (SDG 13). For small-scale communities, academic institutions, and environmental researchers looking for practical insights into prevailing local environmental conditions, this system, which can be replicated affordably, ensures real-time responsiveness. Offering users a clear accepting with which to drive natural safety, public health, and sustainability decision.

title: Adsorptive Removal of Heavy Metals from Wastewater: A Review on ANN based Predictive Models
authors: Hamsa Vardhini M, Bhavana S, Ramya R, K. Balageeta, Nidhi Sharma, Hemavathy R V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Water bodies contaminated by heavy metals such as Pb2+, Cr6+, Zn2+, Ni2+, and Hg2+ pose severe health and environmental risks. Although adsorption is an effective remediation technique, it is complex and non-linear, making predictive modeling difficult. Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) have shown great promise in addressing this challenge, offering high accuracy in modeling heavy metal adsorption. This review systematically analyzes recent literature, emphasizing the correlation between ANN architecture and adsorbent characteristics. A rigorous search was conducted across Scopus, Web of Science, and PubMed, followed by careful screening and critical evaluation to ensure transparency and reliability. Findings reveal that Feedforward ANNs (FF-ANNs) achieve excellent predictive performance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.98) for carbon- and metal- based adsorbents, while Radial Basis Function ANNs (RBF-ANNs) are better suited for bio-based and clay- based materials with localized, non-linear interactions. Hybrid ANN models, which integrate optimization algorithms, consistently deliver the highest accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.99) and are essential for complex systems like nanocomposites. The review highlights the importance of selecting ANN models based on adsorbent type, validating methodological transparency. This integrated framework enables researchers and practitioners to apply predictive modeling efficiently, reliably, and effectively to environmental remediation.

title: Enhancing Cardiovascular Disease Prediction Through Deep Learning: Leveraging Retinal Images with CNN and MobileNet Architecture
authors: J. Sridhar, A. Sree Rishika, M. Teja, J.Maria Arockia Dass, N. Sreya, S. Tharun Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) are a main source of mortality around the world. Early identification and precise analysis of CVDs are pivotal for effective management and sustained outcomes. Retinal imaging has emerged as a safe and practical method for CVD prediction. This study plans to foster a profound learning [1] model utilising convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and MobileNet design to predict CVDs from retinal images. The proposed model leverages CNNs’ ability to extract relevant features from retinal images and MobileNet’s lightweight architecture for efficient organisation. To improve the quality and variety of retinal images for CVD patients, the images are preprocessed by resizing and colour and quality standardisation. It is further used for modelling and assessments. The CNN model engineering is planned to integrate MobileNet as the base architecture, with additional layers for adaptation to the specific CVD prediction task. Through broad preparation and streamlining, the model learns to classify retinal images as either indicative of CVD presence or absence with high precision. Precision and other conventional metrics are used to lead execution evaluations. The growing model offers potential benefits in early detection, risk assessment, and practical discovery, showing encouraging results in predicting CVDs from retinal images. As a potential support system, this model can help medical professionals make informed decisions, which in turn can facilitate easier mediations and preventive medical procedures. Once clinical ethics and settings have been approved, the clinical utility and effect can be assessed.

title: Cross-Architecture Evaluation of Hybrid and Ensemble Models for Wind Power Forecasting: Performance and Robustness Analysis
authors: Harshavardhan Kangala Dayanand, Samriddhi Agarwal, Santanu Kumar Dash, Apirach Rattanaudompisut, Pratap Sekhar Puhan, Soumya Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate wind power forecasting remains challenging due to its highly dynamic and non-linear nature. This study presents a cross-architectural analysis of four hybrid and ensemble models: BiLSTM-GRU, BiLSTM-GRU+RF+LR stacked hybrid, RF-XGB-LR stacked ensemble, and a K-Fold Voting ensemble to assess their robustness and predictive accuracy. The models were trained on a structured wind power dataset from Texas and evaluated using standard regression metrics such as RMSE, MAE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> Scores. Results indicate that the stacked hybrid model: BiLSTM-GRU+RF+LR, outperforms standard ensemble-only methods and achieves the best overall performance. The findings demonstrate that combining sequential deep learning models with robust ensemble regressors yields improved accuracy and reliability in short-term wind power forecasting.

title: Performance Comparison and Simulation Analysis of Key Technologies in MIMO Communication Systems Under Rayleigh Fading Channels
authors: Yihao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a performance comparison and analysis of key technologies in Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) communication systems under Rayleigh flat-fading channels. The research focuses on Alamouti space-time coding, which exploits diversity gain, and the VBLAST architecture, which targets spatial multiplexing. For the VBLAST, this paper investigates and simulates Zero-Forcing (ZF) and Minimum Mean Square Error (MMSE) linear detection algorithms, as well as non-linear detection algorithms incorporating Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC). Through Monte Carlo simulations under Rayleigh fading channels with different antenna configurations and various modulation schemes (4-QAM, 16-QAM, 64-QAM), this paper evaluates the performance of each scheme using Bit Error Rate (BER) as the core metric. Simulation results demonstrate that Alamouti coding provides optimal reliability at low signal-to-noise ratios due to its superior diversity gain, while VBLAST combined with MMSE-SIC algorithms can effectively enhance the capacity of multiplexing systems at medium-to-high signal-to-noise ratios, although its performance is limited by error propagation. The research findings clarify the performance trade-offs among different technologies, providing guidance for technology selection and receiver design in MIMO systems under various scenarios.

title: Time-Series Forecasting for Forex: A Comparative Study on GBP/USD, AUD/USD, and AUD/NZD
authors: Ratul Barua, Md Zahidul Kabir Khan, Mohammad Imtiaj Hossen, Meharaj Hasnain, Farzana Rashid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The FOREX market is a critical component of global finance, yet forecasting currency trends remains a complex challenge due to high volatility and long-range temporal dependencies. Despite the widespread usage of models such as LSTM and GRU in time series forecasting, most existing studies are constrained to limited model architectures or single time frames. This study presents a comprehensive evaluation of five models of learning like LSTM, GRU, CNN-BiLSTM, Autoformer, and Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) on three major currency pairs: GBP/USD, AUD/USD, and AUD/NZD, across three different time frames: 4 hour, 1 day, and 1 week intervals, using historical closing price data. Model performance is assessed using standard regression metrics: RMSE, MAE, and $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$. Results indicate that GRU consistently performs well across all time frames, particularly on smaller datasets. However, for datasets with higher temporal resolution and larger instance counts, such as the $\mathbf{4}$ hour interval, the Autoformer model notably exhibits exceptional performance, attaining an $\mathbf{R}^{2}$ score of 0.99 on GBP/USD data, 0.98, and 0.99 on AUD/USD and AUD/NZD data, respectively. Comparative analysis further reveals that, among the remaining models, TFT outperforms LSTM and CNN-BiLSTM in most settings. These findings highlight the importance of model selection based on dataset granularity and underscore the prospect of transformer-based architectures in high-frequency financial forecasting.

title: Privacy-Preserving and Explainable Hybrid Learning Framework for Career Guidance Using Psychometric Indicators: A Synthetic-Data-Driven Study
authors: Sameer Nanivadekar, Sivaneasan Bala Krishnan, Dilip Kumar Jang Bahadur Saini, Prasun Chakrabartiline
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Data-driven modeling has been a key factor in developing career guidance systems by matching students' attributes with changing occupational categories. However, centralized machine learning approaches have raised concerns about privacy of the user's data, fairness of the results and interpretability of the models. In this study, a hybrid framework for privacy-preserving and explainable learning is presented. The proposed Privacy-Preserving and Explainable Hybrid Learning Framework (PX-CGF) integrates both psychometric indicators and skill-based profiles as well as interpretable reasoning techniques through federated learning. To evaluate this new framework, a synthetic dataset was created with the psychometric traits, academic indicators and occupational fit categories. Evaluation experiments showed that PX-CGF resulted in greater accuracy compared to baseline models, as well as increased fairness and interpretability. Several tables were created to illustrate the dataset used, comparisons of the models evaluated, and the metrics used to measure these evaluations, which demonstrate the professional structure of the proposed framework.

title: Design of Linear APSK Constellation Shaping for LDPC Coded Physical-Layer Network Coding
authors: Xiang Zou, Fengmao Lin, Changwei Shi, Zhaopeng Xie, Xiangming Cai, Pingping Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In this paper, we propose a physical-layer shaped network coding (PLSNC) scheme that integrates physical-layer network coding (PLNC) with constellation shaping for amplitude phase-shift keying (APSK) systems. Two source nodes employ low-density parity-check (LDPC) and linear shaping codes to prevent ambiguous detection. By biasing a subset of the LDPC output toward zeros, lower-energy symbols are transmitted more frequently. This characteristic enables the relay to directly recover the shaped network codeword without decoding individual nodes' shaping bits. Furthermore, we investigate the design criteria for linear shaping codes and introduce an iterative decoding architecture comprising the APSK demodulator, shaping decoder, and LDPC decoder. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly improves both error performance and shaping gain compared to conventional unshaped systems.

title: Edge-GIS: Precision Monitoring of Soil Erosion and Moisture Using Georeferenced Smart Cameras
authors: Naji Guedri, Ali Nsaibi, Rached Gharbi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Critical irrigation water losses and decreased productivity result from agricultural soil erosion, which is made worse by climate variations such as heavy rainfall, earthquakes, and snowfall. This article introduces Edge-GIS, an edge computing system that uses a network of georeferenced Hikvision smart cameras placed at different locations throughout a farmland plot to precisely monitor soil erosion and moisture. Before every irrigation, photos are taken remotely using PoE injectors and locally processed using OpenCV and Python (in VSCode) to identify erosion contours using pixel and geometric shape analysis. An interactive map (geojson.io, QGIS Cloud, or Leaflet) is displayed in real time using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4 \mathrm{G} / \text{LoRa}$</tex> after detected anomalies are automatically injected into a Geographic Information System (GIS) in GeoJSON format with accurate GPS coordinates. Our field experiments in Tunisia show accurate detection after climate events, improving irrigation choices. For precision agriculture, Edge-GIS offers an affordable, scalable, and non-invasive solution.

title: Spark-Based Optimized IPO Recommendation System Using LLM-Driven Data Extraction and Parallel Scoring
authors: Amara Pranav, Joshika Somisetty, Koduri Lakshmi Vinugna, Manju Venugopalan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The decision of investing in the Initial Public Offering’s involves real time analysis of heterogeneous financial information of several sources. This paper introduces a full-pipe intelligent system that incorporates LLM driven web data mining, Apache Spark-based processing in parallel and optimizing portfolios of the retail investor. The system automatically retrieves the IPO information on large financial portals, normalizes it with the help of the Perplexity API, enriches the records with the help of distributed Spark processing, and works with multi-factor scoring algorithms. A Web-based interface is built on Streamlit that gives explainable investor recommendations, maximizing user-specified budgets and time horizons, based on Mixed Integer Linear Programming, or on smart greedy fallback policies. According to experimental results, a high accuracy of field extraction, 4 times faster with parallel processing, 96.7% efficiency of the budget used to explain investments, and explain investments decisions using factors, are obtained. The framework is concerned with key issues in the heterogeneity of data, the scalability of processing and explainability of investment decisions.

title: Personalized Nutritional Guidance for Optimized Health
authors: A. Bhagyalakshmi, S.Kusama Lahari, V.Shanmukha Dhanush Raju
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Growing numbers of people are eating an increasing amount of processed food, which is concerning because it’s likely that they’re ingesting harmful substances and preservatives without comprehending the potential effects on their health. Convenience foods have become increasingly popular, and this trend is growing both offline and online. However, these foods can pose long-term health risks. Our aim is to create an innovative software tool called Processed Food Detector This new method aims to assist users in selecting better food choices by recommending food products in real time based upon various factors such as age, health problems, and eating habits. The application will assist users to make choices and decrease the unintended eating of unhealthy and unwanted preservatives and additives by evaluating the above factors for identification of food products that align with the user’s health objectives. The application will increase healthy eating, provide pertinent information to users, and improve individual and population health, while reducing health risks associated with food. The goal of this project is to develop the intelligent Processed Food Detection System to allow users to make better food choices. It uses the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js) to handle data quickly and an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) algorithm to analyze it in real time. And use Natural Language Processing to process and identify nutrients and also harmful keywords. At last, we use the decision tree classifier algorithm to decide whether it is healthy or not and show it in an interactive way. A small user study (N = 30) showed a 90 percent recommendation acceptance rate, supporting the system’s practical utility. For safety classification, our hybrid model attains an F1-score of 0.84 and ROC-AUC of 0.91, indicating robust detection of risky food items.

title: A Novel Design for Private Information Retrieval with Logarithmic Client Overhead
authors: Wenshuo Guo, Li Li, Xuelei Li, Luying Li, Fei Gao, Ruyang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Private information retrieval enables a client to retrieve records from a database without revealing the query intent. This paper presents a novel design for the conventional PIR model without replication that employs the technique of block-wise computation with recursive reduction. In contrast to the Kushilevitz-Ostrovsky protocol, which reshapes the database into a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex>-dimensional matrix and incurs a client overhead of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}(d \sqrt[d]{\eta})$</tex>, our approach achieves a lower overhead of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{O}\left(l \log _{l}(\eta)\right)$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> representing the block size and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta$</tex> the database size.

title: Explainable AI and Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Loan Default Prediction in Cooperative Banking Networks
authors: Shilpa Shivshankar Mathpati, Abhijit Chirputkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Co-operative banking networks in emerging economies, like India, have to perform the increasingly arduous task of manage loan defaults with ever increasing needs for data Privacy and demands for transparency in decision making. Centralized machine learning models have been widely used for The task of loan default prediction typically require data centralization that exposes sensitive customer information to data breaches, among other similar incidents, thus breaking laws. such as the DPDP Act 2023 of India. Further, complex models, are usually such that is based on deep neural networks. Not transparent, therefore able to undermine regulatory compliance. and may further erode stakeholder trust. This work proposes a robust framework that synergistically integrates Federated Few-shot learning with explainable AI to enable privacy preserving by: and interpretable loan default predictions within cooperative banking networks. In this work, a synthetically generated dataset of 30,000 instances is used, similar to the UCI Credit Card Default dataset, in emulating a federated setup across three cooperative banks with non IID data distributions. Here, the core model is a Random Forest classifier trained via FedAvg aggregation, augmented with SHAP for both global and local explanations. Extensive experiments show that the federated model achieves an accuracy of 81.98%, precision of 64.87%, recall of 44.89%, and F1-score of 52.84%, close to the centralized baseline of 82.34% accuracy, with no data sharing. The feature attribution analysis underlines repayment status as the most important predictor, namely PAY 0, which contributes as much as 25% to the default risk scores. Ablation studies confirm the efficacy of XAI in explaining model decisions, with SHAP values permitting counterfactual explanations at the level of individual predictions. Further assessments of adding differential privacy noise provide additional protection against inference attacks. The contribution of the proposed architecture will be to towards a decrease in NPA, improved regulatory compliance and ethics in AI deployments. Our approach links the strong privacy guarantees of FL with the transparency afforded by XAI and provides a scalable paradigm for cooperative banks; It would probably help lower the default rates by 5–10% with proactive interventions. Extensions of this work may be done Using Blockchain for Secure Aggregations and Temporal Dynamics in credit histories.

title: Land Use and Land Cover Classification Using Reflectance-Based Remote Sensing Techniques
authors: Apeksha P. Sonare, Rohini S. Ochawar, Komal Chambhare, Huda T. Fatema, Shreya Verma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: For sustainable environmental management, accurate Land Use and Land Cover (LULC) analysis is essential. By examining reflectance values across several spectral bands, this study employs high-resolution multispectral pictures from the Maharashtra Remote Sensing Application Centre (MRSAC) to differentiate between vegetated (trees) and non-vegetated (land) locations. The Sentinel Application Platform (SNAP) was used to calculate reflectance, acquire datasets, and perform preprocessing (normalization, radiometric calibration, and atmospheric correction). The findings demonstrate clear spectral patterns: land has lower reflectance in the same bands as trees, which have higher reflectance from Bands 1 to 4, with the highest reflectance in the near-infrared (NIR) band (RT = 0.59718). Land, on the other hand, aids in separation by displaying higher reflectance in the shortwave infrared (SWIR) bands (particularly RL = 0.37787 in Band 6). These results demonstrate how well a combined NIR and SWIR-based strategy works for improved LULC categorization. The suggested system offers a scalable basis for environmental monitoring and greatly increases mapping accuracy in challenging terrains. In order to enhance classification robustness and facilitate real-time land management applications, future research will concentrate on integrating machine learning approaches.

title: Advancing Mental and Medical Healthcare through AI: Conversation Analysis and Clinical Text Understanding
authors: Mohammed Adam Ansari, Atharva Vasant Angre, Vineet Shinde, Nikhita Mangaonkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly advancing within healthcare, offering data-driven, effective, and personalized solutions. This review provides an overview of recent developments in AI-based psychiatric conversation analytics, AI-based clinical text summarization, and AI-based information extraction. It demonstrates their potential to transform mental health care delivery, enhance the resource efficiency of clinicians, and support evidence-based discharge planning. Emerging trends, challenges, and opportunities in the real-life implementation of AI-driven therapeutic and diagnostic platforms are explored. Furthermore, this paper presents a methodology comparing different AI models for clinical conversation summarization, highlighting the superior performance of transformer-based architectures like BART over baseline models, particularly when fine-tuned on relevant dialogue data. Our results, based on evaluations using ROUGE and BERTScore, underscore the importance of domain adaptation for clinical accuracy. The goal is to identify a framework for utilizing intelligent systems to improve patient outcomes and increase clinical efficiency in mental health care.

title: Automated Wildlife Species Classification Using Deep convolutional Neural Network(CNN) : Enhancing Biodiversity Monitoring through convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Shashank Somashekhar Madabal, Swaroop, Kavyanjali, Priyadarshini M
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Wildlife species identification is essential for biodiversity monitoring and conservation, yet manual approaches are often slow, labor-intensive, and error-prone. This project introduces an automated recognition system based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with ResNet-101 as the backbone for robust and efficient feature extraction. The dataset of wildlife images was preprocessed using resizing, normalization, and augmentation techniques, while oversampling was employed to address class imbalance and one-hot encoding for multi-class classification. The pretrained ResNet-101 model was fine-tuned to retain generic feature layers and adapt higher layers for species-specific learning. Bounding box localization was integrated to focus on animal regions, reducing background noise and improving detection accuracy. The model was trained using cross-entropy loss and the Adam optimizer, with dropout and batch normalization applied to minimize overfitting. The system achieved an accuracy of 69.32%, precision of 79.61%, recall of 69.38%, and an F1-score of 70.73%, demonstrating its robustness and reliability in identifying even visually similar species. The combined use of oversampling, fine-tuning, and bounding boxes significantly enhanced classification performance, making the framework scalable and adaptable for real-world conservation applications. Furthermore, this system supports automated biodiversity surveys, reduces manual effort in wildlife monitoring, and assists ecologists in rapid decision-making. Overall, the proposed model bridges the gap between artificial intelligence and ecology by providing a reliable, scalable, and sustainable solution for global biodiversity protection.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Based Fake News Detection Methods in Social Media: A Comprehensive Survey and Future Directions
authors: Anjali Bhadre, Rushikesh Hippargekar, Omkar Pokale, Sachin Choure, Harshvardhan Ghongade
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rampant dissemination of misinformation on social media threatens informational quality and public discourse. In this paper we survey the recent state of AI-based detection of fake news, covering 120 papers from 2022-2025, from traditional machine learning to deep learning methods, with hybrid and ensemble approaches reporting accuracies over 95%. Our analysis finds three trends for methods: the fusion of different modalities, including textual and visual information as well as graph-based factors related to the network itself; transformer architectures to capture more context; and explainability of the detection models. We note persistent challenges, such as adversarial attacks, and domain transferability, as well as deepfake media detection. Finally, we highlight five open challenges in the detection of fake news, including poor multilingual approaches, not detecting news in real time, poor benchmark standards, a neglect for adapting to evolving fake news, and little study to the ethics of automated content moderation. This survey will provide a blueprint of current techniques for researchers and illuminate paths towards more robust, scalable and responsible detection technologies.

title: An Experimental Study of Retrieval Order in Text-Based and Multimodal Figure Retrieval
authors: Tanuja D Ratan, Gouri N Banapurmath, Swajeet Chikhali, Parth Hasolkar, Meena S M, Sanjana Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Scientific papers interleave narrative text with figures, tables, and diagrams, which makes information retrieval substantially more challenging than standard text-only search. This paper studies how contemporary retrieval systems operate on scientific figures and their captions, with a particular focus on retrieval order effects and their connection to position bias. The study builds on prior work in dense retrieval, multimodal representation learning, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and evaluates these ideas on a curated subset of 2,000 samples from the SciMMIR dataset. Two retrieval pipelines are considered. The first is a text-only RAG system based on Dense Passage Retrieval (DPR) and FLAN-T5, which relies solely on caption text. The second pipeline uses multimodal models that jointly encode images and captions, including CLIP, SigLIP, and OpenCLIP. Experimental results show that the text-only pipeline is highly sensitive to retrieval order, yielding mean Exact Match (EM) and F1 scores of 0.0 and exhibiting a large variation of ΔEM = 0.33 across different retrieval permutations.In contrast, the multimodal models demonstrate markedly more stable behavior. Among them, OpenCLIP achieves the strongest zero-shot performance, with Recall@1 of 0.2285, Recall@5 of 0.4015, and a Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) of 0.3144, outperforming both CLIP and SigLIP. These results indicate that caption text alone does not provide sufficient grounding for visually rich scientific material. They also show that retrieval order can significantly affect the behaviour of text-only systems. Taken together, the findings underline the importance of incorporating visual information and of treating ranking effects explicitly when designing future retrieval-augmented systems.
