The results demonstrate the importance of robust feature representation and lightweight neural architectures for reliable SER, and we provide reproducible protocols to facilitate future research.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework Combining Swin Transformer and Mask R-CNN for Dismantled Weapon Part Detection
authors: P Shanthi, B Yamini, A Vishnupriya, S Hariprabha, K Vidhya, R Panneer Selvi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The growing prevalence of dangerous weapons being misused in public spaces presents a major danger to the public. As a result, society needs intelligent surveillance systems to enable early identification of threats. The challenge with identifying dismantled weapon parts is based on their small size, ability to be hidden, and resemblance to other non-threatening objects. The current methods of detecting weapons primarily rely on identifying assembled weapons and are limited by conventional CNN-based architectures. These limitations of current solutions result in a lack of contextual understanding of the surrounding environment and limited effectiveness in identifying threats that are occluded or surrounded by clutter. This paper proposes a framework for identifying dismantled weapon parts using a Mask R-CNN architecture with a Swin Transformer as its backbone. The Swin Transformer enables the extraction of hierarchical, multi-scale features using shifted-window self-attention. With the Mask R-CNN architecture, instance-level classification, bounding-box regression, and accurate segmentation of each weapon component are possible. The results of the experiments demonstrate that this system performs exceptionally well: it has demonstrated a 96.8% accuracy, 95.9% precision, and 96.3% recall. Moreover, this system supports proactive threat identification, enabling police to take more effective steps to keep the public safe.

title: TRIPLE-NET Fusion for Deepfake Detection
authors: R S Ramya, Akshaya, B H Priyanka, Chandana L D, Charan B
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The generation of deepfakes has progressed at a very high rate, thus making it possible to develop extremely realistic manipulated videos that pose a threat to the digital security of media content. The single-stream detection systems in use today that are based on traditional methods, which use purely spatial features, have not been able to withstand modern deepfakes. To address these shortcomings, TNFDD(Triple-Net Fusion for Deepfake Detection) is introduced. It is a deepfake detection model, which is a combination of CNN, GNN, and RNN architectures, which will be implemented as a single multi-stream architecture. The CNN module computes fine-grained spatial textures and pixel-level artifacts, whereas the GNN module conditions relational inconsistencies across patches of the face by modeling depictions of each frame as a structured graph. Simultaneously, the RNN (LSTM) module also responds to time anomalies like unnatural motion patterns and inconsistent dynamics of the face across sequential frames. Further, a fusion layer is used to merge features of all three streams to form a high-discriminative representation. The proposed Triple-Net system is shown to be much more generally and stability-wise robust with Celeb-DF dataset with an overall accuracy of 99.8%, a high level of precision, recall and F1 Score as well as adds to the credibility of AI and safe online media.This work presents a scalable and reliable approach for real-world deepfake forensics, contributing toward trustworthy AI and secure digital media environments.

title: General Network Big Data Community Detection and Correlation Structure Based on Graph Neural Network (GNN)
authors: Yihui Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With the continuous growth and complexity of network scale, traditional community detection methods face problems such as insufficient accuracy, poor scalability, and difficulty in capturing high-order structural information when dealing with large-scale heterogeneous networks. To this end, this paper proposes a general community detection and correlation structure analysis model based on Graph Neural Network (GNN). The specific methods include: (1) joint representation learning of network node attributes and structural features through graph convolutional layers; (2) using attention mechanism to improve the modeling ability of important structural neighbors; (3) introducing modularity optimization and clustering methods in the embedding space to achieve high-quality community division; (4) combining with correlation structure analysis module to model the interaction patterns and evolution laws between multiple communities to reveal the potential dynamic connections of complex networks. Experiments on real social networks and academic collaboration networks show that this method improves the modularity index by about 13.3% compared with traditional spectral clustering and improves the NMI (Normalized Mutual Information) by 16.5% compared with DeepWalk, while maintaining linear scalability on million-level node datasets. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed method for community detection and correlation analysis in complex networks.

title: Research on Cross-Selling Precision Marketing Strategy Based On Xgboost Model and SHAP Explanatory Framework
authors: Ke Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cross-Selling is an important strategy for enterprises to increase customer lifetime value (CLV) and market share. Traditional cross-selling methods rely on rules of thumb or recommendation systems based on static rules, which lack precision and dynamic optimization capabilities. This paper proposes a data-driven precision marketing approach that combines XGBoost (eXtreme Gradient Boosting) and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) explanatory frameworks to optimize cross-selling strategies.Based on the real business data of a large retail enterprise, this study constructs an XGBoost classification model to predict the probability of customers accepting cross-sell recommendations. Through feature engineering and hyperparameter optimization, we improve the prediction performance of the model, and use SHAP method to analyze the contribution of each feature to the prediction results to ensure the interpretability of the model. Experimental results show that the proposed method is superior to traditional methods in key indicators such as accuracy, recall rate and AUC (Area Under Curve), and can provide highly transparent personalized recommendation strategy. This study also discusses the application prospect of this method in finance, insurance, telecommunications and other industries, and shows the feasibility of its cross-industry promotion. The research results not only have guiding significance for enterprises' marketing decision-making, but also provide technical support for government agencies in the formulation of policies such as precision marketing and consumer protection. This study shows that data-driven cross-selling optimization strategies can increase marketing ROI (Return on Investment) and have a profound impact at the industry and country level.

title: Instant Fraud Detection Gateway for Secure Digital Transactions
authors: Januvarthini S, Ilamathi S, Poonkuzhali Ramadoss
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rapid expansion of digital payments in India, led by the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), has significantly enhanced financial accessibility and transaction efficiency, processing over 12 billion transactions per month in 2024. However, this growth has been accompanied by a sharp rise in fraud, with reported losses exceeding INR 1,400 crore in 2023–24, primarily due to phishing, identity spoofing, money mule networks, and exploitation of dormant or fraudulent accounts. This paper presents an Instant Fraud Detection Gateway, a real-time pre-transaction security layer that integrates seamlessly with existing payment infrastructures. The system performs recipient validation against authoritative databases—including GSTIN, PAN, NPCI UPI registries, and bank KYC records—to identify inactive, compromised, or high-risk profiles prior to transaction execution. A hybrid risk scoring model, combining rule-based logic, Isolation Forest-based anomaly detection, and signals from a privacy-preserving shared fraud intelligence ledger, generates a composite risk score to drive adaptive decision-making: low-risk transactions proceed uninterrupted, medium-risk cases trigger user alerts with trust indicators, and high-risk transfers are automatically blocked. Evaluated on a synthetic dataset of 100,000 transactions constructed using statistical distributions from NPCI and RBI reports, the proposed framework achieves 95% fraud detection accuracy, a 2.6% false positive rate, and a 92% reduction in simulated financial loss, while maintaining end-to-end latency below 200 ms and supporting scalability to 15,000 transactions per second. The gateway ensures full compliance with RBI and MeitY regulations and strengthens ecosystem-wide resilience. By enabling proactive fraud prevention at the point of transaction initiation, this work establishes a robust foundation for secure, trustworthy, and sustainable growth of India’s digital payment ecosystem.

title: Research on LiDAR and GNSS Coordinate System Calibration for AGV Based on SVD in Hybrid Environments
authors: Qian Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In indoor-outdoor hybrid scenarios, Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) usually fuse LiDAR and GNSS for seamless localization. However, inconsistent coordinate frames between the two sensors impede direct data fusion. This paper presents a systematic calibration method for unifying LiDAR and GNSS coordinates in indoor-outdoor hybrid environments. By leveraging the classic Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) algorithm for point set registration, the proposed approach addresses the practical challenges of spatial-temporal asynchrony and abrupt observation changes in transition zones. Without extra calibration targets, the proposed method is highly automatic and effectively improves localization consistency in transition zones. It offers a reliable solution for multi-sensor fusion localization in mixed indoor-outdoor environments.

title: Explainable AI for Financial Risk Forecasting using DNN and LSTM with SHAP and LIME
authors: Amirtha Lakshmi S, AnuRadha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Financial risk forecasting plays crucial role for supporting investors, financial institutions and stakeholders in decision-making. Many traditional deep learning and machine learning models often behave as black-boxes and leading to misleading performance evaluations because of highly imbalanced datasets. The current research proposes an explainable financial risk forecasting approach that combines class imbalance handling methodologies with Deep Neural Networks(DNN) and Long Short-Term Memory(LSTM) models. To improve the identification of high-risk instances, cost-sensitive learning and Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique(SMOTE) are adopted. By decreasing noise and complexity in the data an autoencoder improves feature representations. Explainable AI methods like SHAP and LIME have been implemented to provide both global and local interpretability of forecasts for models in order to improve trust and transparency. The models that are trained using SMOTE perform significantly on unbalanced datasets, based on experimental results. In particular, the DNN model performs well in balanced metrics that includes TPR, TNR, and MCC, while the LSTM model achieves approximately 96.8% accuracy. The effectiveness of the proposed explainable deep learning architecture for accurate and transparent financial risk forecasting has been verified by these results.

title: Projection of epigenetic markers of Type 2 Diabetes Reveal Pancreatic Cancer Prognostic Tumour States
authors: Subhradyuti Basu, Bibekanand Mallick, Amrita Singh, Khalid Baig Mirza
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Type 2 diabetes (T2D) and Pancreatic Duct Carcinoma (PDAC), are two diseases with a chronic metabolic disorder related to a deregulated cellular energy metabolism, insulin resistance and an increased inflammatory reaction within the organism. T2D Literature suggests that the metabolic dysregulations related to T2D are known to be implicated in the progression of PDAC, yet it is unclear whether the epigenetic alterations associated with T2D are similarly altered in pancreatic tumours. In this study, we introduce a novel cross-disease epigenomic framework to quantify T2D-associated DNA methylation patterns within PDAC. Using this epigenomic framework, we found that there was considerable inter-tumoural heterogeneity of epigenetics relating to T2D. PDAC tumours that had high T2D methylation scores exhibited consistent methylation patterns, suggesting a communal T2D "signature", and they also demonstrated significantly less survival than their counterparts. In Cox proportional hazards models of the entire sample, there was a significant increase in mortality associated with increasing T2D methylation score. The findings support that T2D-associated epigenetic dysregulation has potential clinical relevance to pancreatic tumours. The study provides an interpretable and scalable method for cross-disease epigenomic projection, which provides new insight into the relationship between metabolic and epigenetic alteration in PDAC.

title: Enhancing Zero Trust Network Security with AI-Powered Analytics and Blockchain Verification
authors: Venkateswaran Petchiappan, Vinitha Vadivel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Despite protections, complex assaults threaten corporate networks, data, and infrastructure. The article employs ZTA for business network security. Using the “never trust, always verify” principle, Zero Trust secures all network traffic. AI and blockchain enable dynamic, tamper-proof Zero Trust. AI-based real-time threat, anomaly, and adaptive authentication enable networks to respond more quickly and effectively. Identity management, audit trails, and network transparency are secure thanks to blockchain’s decentralisation and immutability. This white paper analyses how AI and blockchain enhance Zero Trust’s microsegmentation, identity verification, and least-privilege access. Traditional security designs and Zero Trust are compared to prevent data breaches, insider attacks, and APTs. Case studies on adopting AI and blockchain with Zero Trust illustrate how organisations across sectors employ them, highlighting their benefits and drawbacks. AI-based threat detection and blockchain technology strengthen company networks and reduce unlawful access. The report finishes with business Zero Trust Architecture best practices and AI and blockchain cybersecurity predictions.

title: A Unified Neural Ablation and Robustness Analysis Framework for Small-Sample Heterogeneous Tabular Prediction: A Physical Fitness Assessment Case
authors: Xuanxuan Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Physical fitness assessment data constitute a typical small-sample heterogeneous tabular prediction problem, where limited observations, mixed variable types, and nonlinear interfeature associations make reliable modeling and interpretation difficult. To address this issue, this study develops a unified neural analysis framework for small-sample structured data based on a multilayer perceptron under a controlled experimental protocol. The framework integrates group-wise input ablation, cross-task prediction, robustness evaluation under partial feature loss, and permutation-based contribution analysis, enabling systematic examination of feature-group utility, model stability, and indicator-level importance within a single computational pipeline. Using physical fitness assessment as a case study, five crossindicator prediction tasks are constructed by treating each performance variable as the target in turn. Different input group combinations are evaluated under identical training settings, and the best-performing configurations are further compared with conventional regression baselines. Results show that auxiliary performance indicators provide stronger predictive value than environmental variables in most tasks, while the proposed framework maintains competitive predictive accuracy and stable explanatory capability across multiple targets. More importantly, the study demonstrates that the proposed pipeline offers a reusable neural modeling and analysis strategy for small-sample heterogeneous tabular data beyond the specific application scenario.

title: Evaluating Autonomous Computational Agents for Complex Logic and Security-Oriented Task Environments
authors: Ashutosh Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study presents an analytical evaluation of autonomous computational agents designed to solve complex, logic-intensive challenges in dynamic digital environments. The framework explores how reasoning-based algorithms, adaptive learning mechanisms, and multi-step decision strategies can be employed to achieve goal-oriented automation without domain-specific pretraining. Through systematic experimentation, the research examines agentic behavior under variable task conditions, emphasizing adaptability, robustness, and problem decomposition efficiency. Empirical analysis demonstrates that structured reasoning and iterative self-correction can enhance performance consistency across diverse computational tasks. The findings contribute to advancing intelligent agent architectures in computer science, offering insights into scalable, general-purpose systems for autonomous decision-making and digital security problem solving.

title: TrustX: An Explainable and Cryptographically Verifiable Deep Learning Framework for Multimodal Manipulation Detection
authors: B. Vijay, J Chandra, N Nagendra, R.S. Shanmugasundaram, K. Saranya, K. Ananthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In this study, a sophisticated model that combines deep learning, cryptographic verification, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) is presented to address multimodal manipulation risks in digital media. The proposed system uses a Hierarchical Multimodal Transformer (HMT) to model hierarchical relationships among facial movement, audio tone, and textual semantics. The Contrastive Cross-Modality Alignment (CCMA) mechanism improves the ability to distinguish authentic from doctored material by leveraging cross-modal contrastive learning. An XAI Forensic Analyser provides interpretability by using Grad-CAM++, temporal attention mapping, and saliency sequence visualisation to trace a transparent decision. Moreover, the Zero-Knowledge Cryptographic Verifier (ZKCV) is used to validate the model’s outputs with tamper-proof libsnark cryptographic hashing. The hybrid system takes multimodal CNN, WaveNet and BERT encoders’ embeddings and attains a detection accuracy of about 90 per cent and 92 per cent on benchmark data. This architecture provides a sustainable, explainable, and verifiable basis for multimedia authenticity, enabling a consistent, reliable multimodal forensic detection system.

title: Cricket Bat Ball Segmentation Using Mask Regional Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Dhinakaran Krishnan, J.Jai Jaganath Babu, P. Vanitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The recognition of Cricket Bat and Ball by using image processing techniques is a hot research topic nowadays. The detection of the Cricket Bat and Ball in the match will solve various problems of umpires towards decision-making. In image-based intelligent identification of Cricket Bat Ball, segmentation is a key step. The goal of the segmentation network is to predict Bat and Ball during the match for various purposes such as Ball tracker, Bat and Ball edge detection, and so on. For this purpose, we have proposed Mask Regional convolutional neural networks (MRCNN) to perform automatic Bat and Ball segmentation during the match. Though Mask R-CNN is a commonly used algorithm among several segmented methods, it can make a manual influence on the quality of image segmentation. Whole experiment images were acquired from the Bat Ball Dataset.

title: The Role of Blockchain Technology in Ensuring Security, Transparency, and Trust in Healthcare Data Sharing
authors: Joshitha Sai Reddy Madduri, Sowmini Bandaru, Sangeetha G, Kandeepan R, Priya Nandihal, V Manoj
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The skyrocketing nature of healthcare information and the need to smooth the flow of data between and among stakeholders of healthcare information example hospitals, laboratories, insurance firms and research institutions have given rise to issues of privacy, transparency and trust. The conventional healthcare data management systems have been found to be affected by a centralized control problem, breach of data, non-traceability, and limited interoperability. The system presented by Blockchain technology is decentralized, unalterable and untouchable that have a positive effect on data integrity, give an open chain of access and offer adequate safeguard of information delivery without any third party involvement. The paper presents a blockchain-based model of health care data exchange that uses smart contracts and cryptography solutions to secure the patient record and, on the one hand, guarantee compliance with privacy rules. The proposed system will enable a fine-grained access control interacting well with as many platforms as possible and resulting in the trust to the parties involved by keeping audit records. Our system is more efficient than the existing centralized systems and more reliable. Experimental analysis shows the maximum benchmark of the proposed system that is Data Specialty with 98 score and Trust Score with 97 score. This viability is achieved properly and safely in data sharing of healthcare information.

title: A Multi-Modal Architecture for Document Forgery Detection
authors: Amrit Koul, Henny Prajapati, Heer Shah, Harshita Swali, Sudhir Dehkane
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Document fraud has evolved into a technically sophisticated threat, involving high-resolution print–scan recapture, selective region manipulation, and adversarial rewriting. This work presents a multimodal deep architecture for robust forgery detection that integrates visual texture cues, structural edge patterns, and textual semantics. The system employs an MobileNet-based visual backbone augmented with CBAM and a lightweight ASPP module to capture fine-grained manipulation artifacts. Parallel pathways model structural inconsistencies via edge analysis and linguistic deviations through OCR embeddings, targeting splicing and font substitution attacks respectively. The three modalities are fused through a normalized integration block and classified using an MLP head. Experiments conducted on a 10k-image IDNet-Estonia-derived corpus demonstrate an accuracy of 97.8 and an AUC of 0.9992 across seven fraud categories. Qualitative visualizations further confirm the model’s ability to localize manipulation artifacts, significantly outperforming unimodal baselines in complex attack scenarios.

title: AI-Driven Urban Waste Reporting and Tracking Platform Using Deep Learning
authors: S. Fizur Rahman, R. Ajith, P. Dineshkumar, S. Pavithra, Kandasamy Sellamuthu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The problems that exist in urban waste management are constant, such as delayed collection, overflowing bins, ineffective allocation of resources, and poor participation of the citizens. The current paper describes an Urban Waste Reporting and Tracking Platform, an AI-based software-centric initiative that reduces the efficiency, transparency, and responsiveness of urban waste management. The system allows the citizens to inform about overflowing bins via text, voice, or visuals and detects waste with the use of the YOLOv8 deep learning model, which recognizes waste and classifies it. Having a geolocation-based routing mechanism, each and every validated report is automatically hitched with the closest collection unit to guarantee the timely and efficient collection of wastes. The centralized web-based dashboard gives municipal authorities real-time visualization of the report status, collection progress and operational performance measures. Furthermore, the reward system encourages citizens to take an active part and this will bring about responsibility and communities. The accuracy of waste classification, the quick allocation of tasks, and the accuracy of the monitoring are proven to be high in the experiment, which talks in favor of the applicability of the system on a grand scale in the urban environment. The proposed platform is much more efficient by utilizing artificial intelligence, data analytics, and citizen engagement and can enhance operational efficiency, urban cleanliness, and sustainable waste management activities.

title:
Comparative Performance Analysis of Organic and Inorganic ETLs in CsSnBr
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    -Based Perovskite Solar Cells
authors: Md. Abul Bashar, Md Shahidul Islam, Sk Minhajul Abedin, Md Jannatul Naime, Atowar Rahman, Arifuzzaman Rajib
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are the most promising candidate for the so-called third-generation photovoltaics owing to their tunable bandgap, high absorption coefficient, and low-cost fabrication process. In this paper, a comparative simulation has been conducted on CsSnBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>-based PSC with two organic ($\mathbf{C}_{60}$, PEIE) and two inorganic (IGZO, CdS) Electron Transport Layers (ETLs) to investigate the potential of a lead-free PSC based on CsSnBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> absorber under SCAPS-1D simulation software. The structure of the devices was $\mathbf{N}: \mathbf{C u O}_{\mathbf{2}} / \mathbf{C s S n B r}_{\mathbf{3}} / \mathbf{E T L s}_{\mathbf{2}} / \mathbf{F T O}$, where ETL varied in four types: IGZO, CdS, C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">60</inf>, and PEIE. The influence of the main parameters, thickness, doping concentration, and defect density, was studied for the absorber layer and ELTs. The N: CuO2 HTL was examined with different thicknesses and doping levels, demonstrating its excellent stability up to over $10^{18} \mathrm{~cm}^{-3}$. The optimum efficiency of the back metal contact work function was found at 5.35 eV, identical to Ni as well. The performance was found to be lower at higher temperatures $(278-338 \mathrm{~K})$ with a decrease in PCE and Voc. We have conducted initial Quantum Efficiency (QE) measurements that demonstrate a near broadband response from $300-900 \mathrm{~nm}$ and greater than 88% QE in the visible. Among all ETLs, IGZO showed the highest overall device performance with an Open Circuit Voltage (Voc) of 1.33 V, Short Circuit Current ($\mathrm{J}_{\mathrm{SC}}$) of $19.99 \mathrm{~mA} / \mathrm{cm}^{2}$, Fill Factor (FF) of 81.19%, and Power Conversion Efficiency (PCE) output of 21.53%. These results indicate that IGZO is a promising ETL material for highly efficient, stable, and ecofriendly CsSnBr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> PSCs.

title: Full Stack AI System For Detecting and Monitoring Illegal Landfills In Villages Using Satellite Imagery
authors: Shaik Afzal S, Vishagan D, Kalingarani G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Optimal detection of illegal landfills in rural villages remains challenging due to limited ground inspection capacity and spectral confusion with agricultural land. This paper presents a hybrid AI framework combining Sentinel-2/Landsat satellite imagery with U-Net and DeepLabV3+ semantic segmentation, NDVI vegetation filtering, cloud removal, GIS heatmaps, and time-series landfill growth tracking. The proposed system generates landfill probability masks with IoU of 0.86 and F1-score of 0.90, tracks expansion patterns across multiple years, and provides a mobile dashboard for community validation. Experimental results demonstrate 15–20% improvement in detection accuracy over standalone models and effective identification of high-risk dumping corridors near roads and water bodies. The framework enables scalable, low-cost monitoring for policy interventions in underserved rural regions.

title: Robust Beamforming Optimization and Transmission Performance Analysis for Dynamic Vehicle Meeting Scenarios
authors: Yanjie Pu, Wei Wang, Deliang Dong, Zhaobing Su, Wanlong Qi
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a robust sensing-assisted beamforming approach based on second-order cone programming (SOCP) for reliable wireless transmission in dynamic vehicle meeting scenarios, particularly under rainy conditions and positioning uncertainties. By shaping the antenna pattern with constraints on side lobe leakage and interference suppression, the proposed method ensures stable communication links. A comprehensive channel model is developed that accounts for both distance-dependent path loss and rain-induced fading. Using this framework, we evaluate the system performance in terms of achievable data rate, focusing on four critical factors: the number of antenna elements, communication distance, positioning accuracy, and main lobe width. The results demonstrate the robustness of the proposed design under challenging conditions and provide practical insights for system deployment.

title: Efficient Matrix Multiplication through Algorithmic Optimization and Cross-Platform Implementation
authors: Bhargava Rathod
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive study on optimizing matrix multiplication algorithms to enhance computational efficiency on both x86 and ARM platforms. The research explores several approaches, including loop reordering, matrix blocking, Strassen’s algorithm, and the application of parallelization techniques like OpenMP, as well as instruction set optimizations using SIMD/NEON. The findings demonstrate that while Strassen’s algorithm offers theoretical improvements, it underperforms with smaller matrices due to overhead. However, by integrating loop optimizations and leveraging memory continuity, significant performance gains are achieved, particularly for large matrices. The final optimization, which combines matrix blocking with instruction set optimization and parallelization, yields an over 2000-fold increase in efficiency compared to the naive brute-force algorithm. The study also highlights the creation of a cross-platform matrix multiplication library, capable of executing efficiently on both x86 and ARM architectures. This work not only demonstrates the practicality of advanced algorithmic techniques but also provides a robust solution for large-scale matrix computations, with potential applications in various computational fields.

title: A 1GS/S 10Bit Time-Interleaved SAR ADC
authors: Yuanchuang Liu, Hongyu Zhao, Peng Wei, Hao Ruan, Wei Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a high-speed analog-to-digital converter (ADC) based on a Time-Interleaved Successive Approximation Register (TI-SAR) architecture. The ADC employs 5-channel time-interleaving technique to achieve a sampling rate of 1 GS/s with 10-bit resolution. The chip is implemented in 65nm CMOS process. Operating under a 1.2V supply voltage, the ADC consumes 28.4 mW of power, achieving an effective number of bits (ENOB) of 9.76 bits, a signal-to-noise-and-distortion ratio (SNDR) of 60.56 dB, a spurious-free dynamic range (SFDR) of 67.41 dB, and a figure of merit (FOM) of 32.7 fJ/conv.-step. This design is suitable for applications such as 5G communications, radar communications, and high-performance digital oscilloscopes.

title: Aggregate Cyber Loss Modeling with Tvarconstrained Premium Optimization: A Monte Carlo Framework with Spliced Severity and Systemic Dependence
authors: Aomduan Jeamaon, Tanagrit Chansaeng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Cyber insurance pricing is challenged by three fundamental characteristics of cyber risk: heavy-tailed loss severity, dependence-driven portfolio aggregation, and dynamically evolving threat environments. This paper proposes a Monte Carlo–based financial modeling framework for optimizing cyber insurance premiums under tail-risk constraints. The framework integrates four key components: (i) a portfolio-level collective risk model incorporating explicit policy terms, (ii) a spliced severity distribution combining a Lognormal body with a Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail to capture extreme cyber losses, (iii) a two-layer dependence structure that models portfolio-wide accumulation through a mean-preserving lognormal common shock and Poisson-driven systemic cyber events, and (iv) a Sample-Average Approximation (SAA)–based premium optimization procedure under Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Tail Value-at-Risk (TVaR) constraints. Simulation experiments on a synthetic portfolio of 1,000 insured entities with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> Monte Carlo paths (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\alpha=99 \%$</tex>) demonstrate that incorporating systemic cyber events substantially amplifies tail risk: TVaR<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">99</inf> increases by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 6 \%}$</tex> while the expected loss rises by only 5.9%. The resulting optimal loading multiplier produces a premium level approximately 150% above expected loss while maintaining the underwritingloss probability below a 5% risk tolerance threshold. Sensitivity analysis further reveals that portfolio tail risk grows nonlinearly with the systemic-event arrival rate, highlighting the critical role of systemic cyber risk in insurance capital adequacy and premium determination. These findings underscore the importance of tail-aware pricing frameworks for managing accumulation risk in cyber insurance portfolios.

title: Spectral-Temporal Graph Transformers: Leveraging Spectral Gated Attention for Noise-Resilient Traffic Forecasting
authors: Sherly A, Mary Subaja Christo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Accurate traffic flow forecasting is critical for intelligent transportation systems (ITS) yet remains challenging due to the non-stationary and highly noisy nature of loop detector data. Traditional deep learning approaches, such as LSTMs and standard Transformers, operate in the time domain, where they often overfit to high-frequency sensor noise and fail to capture global cyclic patterns. In this paper, we propose a novel Spectral-Temporal Graph Transformer (STGT) with Spectral Gated Self-Attention (SGSA) algorithm that mitigates these limitations by performing attention mechanisms within the frequency domain. Unlike canonical architectures, our model utilizes a Frequency-Gated Attention Mechanism that selectively amplifies dominant harmonic components while suppressing high-frequency jitter associated with sensor errors. The empirical data reveals that SGA effectively discriminates between structural temporal patterns and stochastic interference, achieving a notable SNR improvement of +1.96 dB and a Correlation of 0.8653. Furthermore, the model's ability to maintain a stable loss floor (~0.0100) across varying dataset scales proves its robustness and its capacity for strong generalization in real-world, noisy environments.

title: Multiclass Classification of Alzheimer's Disease Stages from MRI Using a CNN
authors: Nour El Houda Boulkrinat, Samir Saada, Hamza Taourirt
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The detection of Alzheimer's disease is attracting increasing interest. It profoundly affects the central nervous system, resulting in progressive, irreversible neurodegeneration. Earlier detection can enable more effective management, help attenuate disease progression, and enhance patient well-being. Nevertheless, diagnosis remains challenging and is still largely based on clinical assessment, complemented by medical imaging, particularly MRI. In this paper, we propose a multiclass classification approach based on a CNN model trained on the ADNI dataset. T1-weighted MRI images are preprocessed and classified into three classes: Alzheimer's disease (AD), mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and cognitively normal (CN) subjects. The proposed model is compared with several pretrained CNN models (MobileNet, Inception, VGG16, ResNet50, EfficientNet, and ConvNeXtTiny) and with previous studies. Experimental results show that our approach achieves 98.78 % accuracy, outperforming the compared methods.

title: Early Detection of Data Leakage in Multi-Tenant Clouds Using Machine Learning
authors: Monica Charate, Ankit Temurnikar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rapid adoption of multi-tenant cloud environments has intensified concerns regarding data confidentiality and unauthorized access. In such settings, multiple tenants share computational and storage resources, increasing the risk of inadvertent or malicious data leakage across tenants. Traditional rule-based and signature-driven mechanisms often fail to provide timely and scalable protection, especially against zero-day and insider threats. This paper proposes a machine learning-based framework for the early detection of data leakage in multitenant clouds. The approach leverages tenant activity logs, access patterns, and resource usage behaviors to extract discriminative features that enable proactive identification of anomalous activities indicative of leakage attempts. A comparative study of supervised learning models demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves improved accuracy, reduced false positives, and faster detection compared to baseline methods. Experimental results highlight the feasibility of integrating machine learning into cloud security monitoring pipelines, providing cloud service providers with an adaptive and effective solution for safeguarding sensitive tenant data.

title: Synergistic Control of Boost Converter Using Linear Quadratic Regulators with Integral Action
authors: Padmashree K S, Ciji Pearl Kurian, Bindu S, Suprabha Padiyar U
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This study presents a control strategy for a DC–DC boost converter that uses a linear quadratic regulator (LQR) combined with an integral action to achieve precise and robust voltage regulation. The goal is to achieve zero steady-state error while improving dynamic performance in the presence of disturbances and parameter variations. To accomplish this, the converter is represented in an augmented state-space form that incorporates the integral of the output error, enabling the controller to enforce accurate tracking while preserving the optimal features of the LQR design. The effectiveness of the proposed controller is examined through simulations and compared with four commonly used approaches: proportional– integral–derivative (PID) control, state-feedback control with integral action, standard LQR, and LQR with integral action. These comparisons are carried out under varying input conditions, rapid load changes, and model uncertainties. Results show that the proposed synergistic controller achieves faster settling, smaller overshoot, and stronger disturbance rejection than the benchmark methods, while maintaining stable operation and efficient control effort. The findings demonstrate that integrating optimal control with an error-integrating mechanism yields a practical and high-performance solution for regulating the boost converter. This approach enhances accuracy, robustness, and dynamic response without introducing significant design complexity, making it a strong candidate for power electronic systems requiring consistent and reliable voltage control.

title: AI-Powered Smart Cloud Kitchen with Personalized Recipe Recommendation System
authors: Rimjhim Srivastava, Raktima Gupta, Ashok Kumar Tripathi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cloud kitchens are growing in popularity, transforming the food industry by prioritizing automation and efficiency. This paper proposes an AI-powered smart cloud kitchen system that offers personalized recipe recommendations based on the ingredients users have on hand and their food preferences. A hybrid recommendation model that combines content-based filtering and collaborative filtering generates relevant suggestions while addressing the cold-start problem. A lightweight NLP model, all-MiniLM-L6-v2, is used for efficient recipe embedding and semantic matching. The backend is developed using Spring Boot with MongoDB integration, while the frontend is built using React for an interactive interface. The proposed system demonstrates efficiency and real-time adaptability, making it suitable for scalable deployment in commercial kitchen environments.

title: Cardiovascular Disease Classification Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Weicheng Liu, Rapeeporn Chamchong, Pornntiwa Pawara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains a major global health burden, motivating the development of accurate and scalable risk screening methods. This study presents a comparative evaluation of supervised machine learning models for CVD prediction using routinely collected clinical and lifestyle data. A unified machine learning pipeline was constructed, incorporating data preprocessing, feature transformation, and systematic model tuning. Multiple classifiers were evaluated under identical experimental settings, including logistic regression, support vector machine, multilayer perceptron, and ensemblebased tree models. Experimental results on a large public dataset show that ensemble methods consistently outperform classical classifiers. In particular, XGBoost achieves the best overall performance, with an accuracy of 87.02%, an AUC of 94.83%, and an F1-score of 8 5. 1 0%. These results highlight the potential of gradient-boosting-based machine learning models to support accurate and scalable cardiovascular disease risk screening using routinely collected health data.

title: Contrastive Representation Learning for Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia: A SimCLR and EfficientNet-B0 Approach
authors: Mohammad Mehedi Hasan Munna, Nakib Aman, Saiful Bari, Md Shabbir Mia, Acramul Haque Kabir, Reefka Fabliha Tulona, Iftekhar Iqbal Enan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a SimCLR-EfficientNet-B0 framework for self-supervised representation learning in the classification of Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (ALL). This approach is able to handle challenges in limited labeled medical data and high intra-class morphological variability by leveraging the contrastive learning of unlabeled blood smear images to extract discriminative and invariant visual features. The pretext SimCLR pretraining reached stable convergence, with clearly separable feature clusters as shown by t-SNE and PCA projections. In the downstream classification task, the proposed framework achieved an overall accuracy of 98.96% and a macro F1-score of 0.9888, thus outperforming conventional supervised CNN models. These results show that self-supervised contrastive pretraining significantly enhances feature representation and generalization, offering great potential for this framework in clinical decision support systems toward automated, large-scale leukemia diagnosis.

title: Electro-Thermal Behavior of AlGaN/GaN HEMTs on SiC, Si, Sapphire and Diamond Substrates
authors: Swapnil Gurung, Santashraya Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This investigation presents a comparative electro–thermal and electrical analysis of AlGaN/GaN High Electron Mobility Transistors (HEMTs) fabricated on silicon carbide (SiC), silicon (Si), sapphire, and diamond substrates using three–dimensional TCAD simulations. The results show that substrate thermal conductivity and thermo–mechanical compatibility strongly influence saturation current, channel temperature, and thermal reliability. The analysis reveals that GaN–on–diamond achieves the lowest thermal resistance (3-5 K/(W · mm)) and the highest saturation current (1.4–1.6 A/mm), representing the thermal performance limit. However, the investigation demonstrates that GaN–on–SiC provides the optimal overall balance, exhibiting low specific on–resistance (4–6 mΩ · cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), a maximum channel temperature below 140 <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>C, and superior thermal–cycling suitability due to its low coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch. These characteristics make GaN–on–SiC well suited for space–qualified power switching transistors operating in the 600–1200 V range, X–band (8–12 GHz) RF power amplifiers, and high–frequency DC–DC converters used in satellite power systems and electric–vehicle charging applications.

title: Fake Review Detection in Fashion E-commerce Using Machine Learning
authors: Laxmi Bankadamani, Vaishnavi R Honnagudi, Bhoomika Gudi, Shashank Bhagoji, Channabasappa Muttal, Guruprasad Konnurmath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The widespread growth of fashion E-commerce in the last several years has made online reviews one of the most influential factors for the consumer’s decision, brand credibility, and platform trust. The issue with the rising number of deceptive or fake reviews is that they confuse users regarding the products and reduce their trust in the platform. Most existing approaches which only consider text features or use traditional machine learning models are less effective in detecting sophisticated opinion spams. Our study presents a hybrid fake review detection system that integrates deep semantic understanding with behav-ioral and metadata features. DistilBERT, a fine-tuned model, obtains the most relevant and contextual review representations while LightGBM models reviewer behavior and activity patterns. Employing a weighted ensemble strategy allows the system to be more robust and accurate in classification. The endeavor with 100,000 Amazon fashion reviews produces impressive outcomes and is attainable for the real world.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Clinical Risk Prediction
authors: Atul Kumar, Harsh Sharma, Vivek Kumar, Mahendra Kumar, Ashish Baiswar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are revolutionizing healthcare analytics by enabling data-driven decision-making for diagnosis, treatment, and patient management. Using a dataset of 53,462 anonymized patient records (MIMIC-III and institutional EHRs) with 56 features, this study evaluates four ML algorithms like Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. XGBoost demonstrated the highest performance (90.3% accuracy, 0.937 AUC), supported by SHAP-based interpretability identifying key predictors such as blood glucose, systolic blood pressure, BMI, and age. The study underscores the potential of transparent ML models in clinical decision support, emphasizing fairness-aware learning and privacy preservation. Future work extends toward federated learning and real-time multimodal data integration.

title: Plant Disease Classification Using EfficientNetB3 for Tomato Leaf
authors: Akshaya Reddy Nalla, D. Khalandar Basha, Arapelly Archana, K Srinidh Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate detection of plant diseases is important for maintaining the health of crops and increasing agricultural productivity. In this research, an automated system for classifying tomato leaf diseases based on deep learning methods is developed. The process begins with pre-processing steps which include image resizing, normalization, and augmentation for standard improvement of the model’s generalization as well as for the imbedded image Recognition System supplied on the leaves of tomatoes. The EfficientNetB3 architecture captures feature extraction at classification level due to its scalability of network depth, width, and resolution to achieve the set Computation Time Efficiency Metrics. At classification level, the model classifies all the diseased leaves into Bacterial Spot, Early Blight, Late Blight, Septoria Leaf Spot, Yellow Leaf Curl Virus, and also classifies some other leaves as healthy. The results obtained from the method has demonstrated optimized performance and the model is responding well in regards to its intended use in precision agriculture and intelligent crop monitoring system. Through EfficientNetB3, the proposed model an accuracy of 93% is achieved which is comparatively higher than the existing CNN with LVQ Algorithm model with an accuracy of 86% and additionally a new class Early Blight which is not present in the existing is added to the proposed model.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Combined with Lyapunov for Dynamic Resource Scheduling in Maritime Unmanned Swarms
authors: Zizhong Peng, Mengya Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Reliable transmission services are crucial for maritime unmanned swarms (MUS) to perform maritime search and rescue missions. However, MUS's bandwidth is limited, making buffer queue congestion and head-of-line blocking prone to occur when transmitting large amounts of data and high-priority control data. To address these issues, it is essential to implement a dynamic resource allocation mechanism, and this has commonly been achieved by combining Software-Defined Networks (SDN) and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). However, frequent jumps in DRL output actions can easily lead to queue instability and a significant increase in overhead for the SDN control plane. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a resource scheduling algorithm (L-DDPG) combining Lyapunov optimization and the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) to balance queue stability and control overhead. First, a multi-source service priority mapping and a queue model are established. Based on this, the Lyapunov drift-plus-penalty theory is introduced. This approach transforms the long-term queue stability constraint into a deterministic single-slot optimization problem. Experimental results show that under sudden heavy traffic congestion, L-DDPG effectively alleviates head-of-line blocking and guarantees low end-to-end latency for high-priority critical services. In addition, it maintains a low instantaneous control plane overhead, which effectively suppresses control plane signaling storms.

title: A Logical Evaluation of Healthcare Logistics Management and Supply Chain Performance through ICT-assisted Learning Approach.
authors: S. Surendharan, M Krithika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: An effective healthcare logistics and supply chain organisation is essential in terms of fair service delivery, particularly in the case of an emergency such as a pandemic. This paper presents a new ICT-based hybrid architecture that combines logical modelling, semantic reasoning, cognitive digital twins, and federated learning to maximise logistics performance within distributed healthcare networks. The system was used to predict cold-chain failures with $\mathbf{9 6. 0 \%}$ accuracy via a cognitive digital twin simulation. Reinforcement learning based on logic improved real-time inventory reallocation, achieving an average score of 0.90, and a neurosymbolic vendor evaluation provided clear scoring, with the best vendor accuracy at $92.3 \%$. Flow validation based on an ontology minimised workflow inconsistency by $87 \%$, and federated metatransfer learning enhanced model generalizability across hospital nodes, achieving a global prediction accuracy of 91.0%. The framework is used to implement proactive disruption prediction, safe cross-institutional coordination, and explainable decision-making in procurement, routing, and coldchain logistics. The work describes significant shortcomings of conventional siloed logistics and provides a logically grounded, scalable, and ethically appropriate solution for next-generation healthcare infrastructure.

title: EVM Testing Method for Programmable RF Transceivers
authors: Shuheng Lin, Xuewu Li, Cenyue Zhang, Fan Zhang, Lang Zeng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Error Vector Magnitude (EVM) is a pivotal metric for evaluating the modulation quality and comprehensive performance of digital receivers. As programmable Radio Frequency (RF) transceivers evolve toward System-on-Chip (SoC) architectures-where RF analog front-ends and digital basebands are deeply integrated-traditional testing methods relying on external Vector Signal Analyzers (VSA) face significant challenges, such as inaccessible digital interfaces and fragmented testing links. This paper proposes an FPGA-based on-chip EVM selftest method. By constructing a virtual VSA and Vector Signal Generator (VSG) within the system, a complete workflow for QAM modulation/demodulation and EVM calculation is established. Through the optimization of shaping filter parameters and improvements in timing and carrier synchronization algorithms, the proposed method achieves high-precision measurement while maintaining hardware resource efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that this method offers excellent stability and engineering feasibility, providing a low-cost, deployable solution for the performance verification of highly integrated RFSoCs.

title: IMAC-Net for Interpretable Multi-Label ECG Classification via Multi-Scale Attention and Label Correlation
authors: Feiyan Zhou, Xiaozhe Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) classification is essential for arrhythmia screening, yet existing methods often struggle to capture complex temporal patterns, exploit label correlations in multilabel settings, and provide clinically meaningful explanations. This paper proposes IMAC-Net, an interpretable multilabel ECG classification framework that integrates an efficient multi-scale attention (EMA) module and a correlation network (CorNet) into a ResNet backbone to enhance feature representation and label-dependency modeling. To mitigate class imbalance, a weighted label-smoothing binary cross-entropy loss is introduced. For interpretability, we develop an enhanced SHAP-based explanation strategy with dynamic thresholding and weighted aggregation, complemented by frequency-domain analysis. Experiments on the CPSC2018 dataset using 10-fold cross-validation achieve an average F1-score of 0.868, outperforming representative baselines. The proposed approach improves both diagnostic accuracy and transparency, supporting reliable and interpretable ECG decision-making.

title: WasteBelt: A CNN-Based Smart Waste Sorting System with Conveyor Automation and ESP32-CAM Integration
authors: Hanif Mia, Ali Hasan Pappu, Md Yusuf Hossain, Mohamamd Sakib
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Contemporary cities are expanding ecosystems that encounter new difficulties as a result of increased population needs. Waste management is one of their many current issues, and it has grown to be a critical challenge in need of innovative solutions. This project presents a prototype waste management system named Wastebelt, which utilizes a conveyor belt and a camera module to automatically classify waste using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The system is built with an Arduino Uno, an ESP32-CAM for capturing images, an IR sensor for detecting objects, a servo motor for sorting, and an LCD display for real-time updates. Although real-time image transmission to a cloud server was not implemented, the ESP32CAM successfully captured images of waste locally. For model training and evaluation, we used a Kaggle waste dataset consisting of three types of bottle images: white, brown, and green. When an object is detected on the conveyor belt, the IR sensor triggers the ESP32-CAM to capture an image, which is then processed by the CNN to classify the waste type. The LCD displays the classification result and the system’s operational status, while the servo motor sorts the waste into designated bins accordingly. The CNN model achieved an average accuracy of 95.6% and an F1-score of 96.0%, demonstrating effective performance in waste classification. This automated system enhances the efficiency of waste sorting, reduces manual effort, and supports sustainable recycling practices in urban environments.

title: BART-SCE: A Dialogue Summarization Model Enhanced by Sentence Centrality
authors: TiCheng Li, Yuchen Song, Meiling Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Dialogue summarization suffers from heterogeneous role perspectives, dynamic topic migration, scattered key information, and excessive GPU memory consumption, while existing methods fail to balance summary quality and computational efficiency. To this end, we propose BART-SCE, a lightweight BART-based dialogue summarization model enhanced by a Sentence Centrality Enhancement (SCE) module. The SCE module models sentence importance from two complementary dimensions of topic salience and sentence relevance via K-Means topic clustering and hidden-state reweighting. Meanwhile, our model integrates three dedicated memory optimization strategies and a role prompt mechanism, enabling multi-role summary generation with a single model while reducing training overhead. Experiments on the CSDS Chinese customer service dialogue dataset show that BART-SCE achieves state-of-the-art overall performance, with the ROUGE-1 score of user summaries reaching 59.36. Compared with vanilla BART, BART-SCE converges 300 steps earlier and reduces GPU memory consumption by 32% for 512-token inputs. Ablation studies verify the independent effectiveness of each core component of our model.

title: Aware of Latency Moving to Edge–Cloud Computing Collaborative Mobile Computing: Real-Time Decision-Making Algorithms
authors: Muruganantham Angamuthu, Yousuf Hazaimeh, Chandra Doddapalli Ramachandra Reddy, R. Ramesh, G. Ramkumar, G Karuna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Problems with computing latency, energy economy, and service quality have arisen as a result of the explosion of data-intensive apps and mobile devices. Due to network congestion and high communication latency, traditional mobile cloud computing architectures cannot meet real-time demands. Edge computing might cause a major shakeup by bringing data storage and processing closer to the people who use it. It is not advisable to rely solely on edge resources to support complex and large-scale applications due to their inherent constraints. Presenting real-time decision-making approaches and investigating collaborative, latency-aware mobile computing, this work aims to maximise job execution across distributed computing types. Intelligent compute-offloading algorithms that can determine in real time whether mobile tasks are better handled in the cloud, on the edge, or locally are the primary focus of this research. Application delay limits, device energy levels, workload considerations, and network conditions are all evaluated in real time to help us make these determinations. Latency-aware decision algorithms optimise the distribution of computational load across diverse resources and reduce end-to-end response time. The proposed system uses adaptive scheduling, resource allocation, and real-time feedback to adapt to changing mobile environments. A multi-dimensional latency model that accounts for migration overhead, processing time, transmission delay, and queuing delay is this study’s key contribution. Real-time monitoring and predictive analytics allow decision-making algorithms to anticipate network changes and alter offloading strategies.

title: LangChain Orchestrated Ecosystem for Token-Aware LLM Routing, Hybrid Semantic Retrieval and Explainable Conversational Automation with Dynamic Data Handling
authors: Hanumanth Sastry Sistla, Bahubali Shiragapur, Vinay R, Keshav Sairam, Akshath Vijay Kumar, Karan A
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of institutional workflows and information management, there is a growing need for intelligent platforms that can adapt to dynamic data requirements and ensure secure, scalable interactions. In our work, we have used FastAPI for high-performance backend services, replacing Flask to accommodate asynchronous operations and robust API handling. FastAPI leverages LangChain for orchestration of advanced chains and agent-based workflows, enabling Robotic Process Automation (RPA) in response generation using prioritized AI-Based hybrid semantic data retrieval System. A conditional logic pipeline has been implemented wherein the token size of retrieved context determines the processing route: if the token count is below a defined threshold, lightweight LLMs such as Llama 3 accessed via externally hosted APIs through a unified LLM wrapper are used for inference. For larger token contexts, data is first distilled through older models like GPT-2 or BART before being passed to newer LLMs, thus improving both efficiency and answer quality. Additionally, tools such as LangSmith have been integrated to streamline debugging, evaluation, and prompt optimization. Furthermore, the memory module within LangChain is leveraged to enable reinforcement learning-based adaptation and iterative performance improvements.Evaluations show the hybrid LLM pipeline achieved a BLEU score of 91.2, ROUGE score of 88.4, and precision of 90.1, with user upvote rates reaching 96% and fallback rates as low as 2%. The adoption of FastAPI contributed to significantly reduced response times under concurrent user loads. The LangSmith-integrated feedback loop accelerated prompt refinement and error reduction across sessions. This work contributes to the field by demonstrating a generalized framework for intelligent academic automation, integrating conditional LLM routing, explainable evaluation, and modular RPA pipelines for institutional applications.

title: Design and Realization of BMC Trusted Startup Based on a Dual-Core System
authors: Yubing Lin, Yong Wang, Zhuochen Ping, Weifeng Gong, Wei Gong, Yan Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The server has become the central part of the information society, which means that the safe startup of the server is essential for people. Hence, a novel architecture of the baseboard management controller (BMC) trusted startup is introduced based on the intelligent platform management interface (IPMI). The novel architecture is a dual-core system according to the multi-core chip. Firstly, the design of the dual-core system is discussed. The cores are divided into the functional core and the trusted core. The cores are independent of each other and communicate with each other by the inter-processor interrupt (IPI) and the shared memory, which indicates a strong isolation characteristic. The independent hardware resources mean high safety for the trusted core and the system startup. Then, the trusted core and Trusted Platform Module (TPM) are used as the trusted root of the server. Based on this, the dual operation mechanism is described. Besides, the realization of the BMC trusted system is also introduced by dividing it into the hardware design and the firmware design. Finally, the safe boot process is shown based on the foundations of the theory, hardware, and firmware. The Real-Time Operating System (RTOS) running on the trusted core starts earlier than the Linux running on the functional core to perform trusted computing after the verified U-boot starts. Therefore, all imported codes and data will be verified during the startup, which transfers the chain of trust and ensures that all codes and data are credible. In a word, the dual-core system ensures that the trusted computing operates in a hardware-isolated environment and will not be tampered with the risk factors by dividing into a functional core and a trusted core, which improves greatly the safety of the BMC startup.

title: A Unified Generative AI System for Synthetic Image Creation and Detection
authors: Savita Mane, Sunita Barve, Amol Popalghat, Vaibhav Jadhav, Ayush Rewatkar, Mayur Kapgate
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The fast development of generative artificial intelligence has enabled highly realistic synthetic facial imaging, raising significant concerns about identity fraud, deception, and digital security. Traditional approaches don’t provide a complete understanding of the interconnected evolution of deepfakes; instead, they concentrate on either deepfake creation or detection independently. This paper suggests a single generative AI system that addresses both the creation and detection of deepfake images by carefully integrating four distinct deep learning architectures. The system uses diffusion models and generative adversarial networks to create high-fidelity synthetic facial images and variational autoencoders and vision transformers to recognize and classify deepfakes with reliability. The framework performs exceptionally well in terms of both generation quality and detection accuracy metrics after being trained and assessed on the Kaggle Deepfake and Real Dataset, which consists of real and artificially created facial images. Grad-CAM visualization and attention mapping are two explainable AI approaches that the system uses to improve the interpretability and transparency of the model. Real-time image production and verification capabilities are made possible by an intuitive Streamlit interface, which supports both forensic applications and scientific advancement. In digital media forensics, this integrated approach shows that unified multi-model systems can successfully close the gap between deepfake development and detection while preserving transparency, dependability, and usefulness.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Vertical-Axis Turbine for Renewable Electricity Generation in Water Distribution Systems
authors: Abdul Ahad Mahmud Rafi, Md. Anawar Ibrahim, Istiaq Ahmad, Tasfia Tasnim Hrishita, Debashis Roy, Abu Saleh Mohammad Nasrullah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This article presents the design, simulation, and experimental validation of a compact vertical-axis water turbine for in-pipe hydrokinetic power harvesting. The turbine is specially designed to be inserted into standard 200 mm PVC water pipes without requiring pipe modification. A four-bladed design was optimized for performance with minimal pressure loss. The turbine was tested at two water flow speeds $(6.67 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $8.9 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$) with direct mechanical coupling to a 3.5 kVA generator for measurement of power output. Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) was used to model the flow of water and examine the generation of torque. At the same time, Finite Element Analysis (FEA) assessed mechanical strength, equivalent strain, distribution of stress, and factor of safety. Experimental results confirmed a maximum flow velocity power output of approximately 2.42 kW, demonstrating the viability of the system. The design incorporates an affordable, plug-andplay clean energy generation alternative for high-rise building pipe systems and municipal water supply systems, especially well-suited for urban development in developing countries like Bangladesh.

title: Toward Intelligent Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing: A Review of Google’s Willow Processor and AI Integration
authors: Tejaswini Ghosh, Shankaramma, R Charis Susanna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: With quantum computing’s potential to solve classically insurmountable problems, quantum computation has emerged as a transformative paradigm across various fields such as cryptography, materials science, and artificial intelligence. The pursuit of scalable and fault-tolerant quantum computation is at the heart of this evolution. The examination examines the evolution of quantum computing architectures, such as superconducting qubits and quantum error correction (QEC) codes until the recent advancement in Google’s Willow processor. This review highlights this progression. We examine the development of Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) devices and the first instances of below-threshold QEC, where an increase in qubit count leads to exponentially lower error rates. By using Willow’s 105-qubit architecture, it is possible to perform real-time error correction on a 77 logical qubit grid and complete random circuit sampling in under five minutes, which would otherwise be an insurmountable task. We examine the way Willow’s method is compared to other quantum platforms and discuss its potential impact on early fault-tolerant quantum computing (EFTQC), scalability, and the future of intelligent quantum systems. In conclusion, the paper highlights some of the open issues and opportunities in quantum hardware, artificial intelligence (AI), and fault-tolerant computation.

title: The Efficiency Comparison of Stroke Disease Prediction Models Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Nipaporn Chanamarn, Aumarin Wichaiphin, Mettai Phomhom, Tanapon Wandee, Rattanaporn Chaitamat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This study aims to compare the performance of machine learning models for stroke disease prediction by using five algorithms, including Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Random Forest, and Artificial Neural Network. The experiments were implemented using the Python programming language in conjunction with the Scikit-learn library. A dataset from the Kaggle database consisting of 5,110 records and 11 features was partitioned into training and testing sets using five different ratios: 50:50, 60:40, 70:30, 80:20, and 90:10. The study found that the Random Forest model was the best at predicting stroke disease. It had the highest accuracy (95.30%), precision (96.00 %), recall (95.00 %), f1-score (93.00 %), and MSE (0.0470). These findings indicate that this result can facilitate the development of an initial system for screening stroke risk or for formulating strategies to prevent stroke disease.

title: A Machine Learning Approach For Securing OTT Platforms: Threat Analysis and Detection
authors: Ritvik Kamble, Shikha Agrawal, Piyush Kumar Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: OTT services has changed the pattern of seeing the digital content and also affect the balance of media consumption. In this review article, we discussed the security problems of OTT platforms briefly and tells that, why it is necessary of security of users data, in increasing digital dangers. This study proposes a multi level security structure, which helps in securing the models of OTT platforms & architecture. In this paper, we present a brief study of security threats in OTT platforms like content piracy, unauthorized access, and leakage of users Pll.. Thatswhy this study try to make a flawless security framework using encryption, access management and authentication against cyber-attacks. Anticipated outputs: This project will help in developing the detailed security policies, best practices and a model security framework whish can be integrated with existing OTT platforms. These will helps in robustness of digital media ecosystem and also helps in various kinds of security protocols related to OTT platforms.

title: Multi-Objective Scheduling for Large Language Model Inference with Prompt-Level Cost Prediction and SLO Awareness
authors: Jiajing Liao, Feng Chang, Yihan Xue, Tianjian Xia, Zeyu Huang, Yuxiao Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference in multitenant clouds is becoming an increasingly important contributor to data-center carbon emissions, yet existing carbon-aware scheduling techniques target long-running training jobs and are illsuited for the short, bursty, SLO-sensitive nature of online serving. We propose CAPS (Carbon-Aware Prompt Scheduling), an online bi-objective scheduler that jointly optimizes goodput and perrequest carbon cost for multi-tenant LLM inference. CAPS first employs a lightweight prompt complexity predictor to estimate token generation cost and latency risk for each incoming request. It then combines real-time grid carbon intensity, GPU energy profiles, and per-tenant SLO tiers to route each request to one of three execution pools: a low-latency pool, a low-carbon pool, or a delay-tolerant batch pool. A composite reward function balances goodput, carbon emissions, and SLO violation rate. In trace-driven simulations using public conversation traces and regional carbon intensity data, CAPS reduces average carbon emissions per <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 K}$</tex> generated tokens by 26.8 % compared to round-robin scheduling while achieving an SLO attainment rate that matches or exceeds a dedicated SLO-aware baseline.

title: Smart Retrieval of Industrial Standards with LLM and Elastic search
authors: Poornima Jayaraman, Pachaivannan Partheeban, Kothalam Krishnan Nagarajan, N Koushik, G Nikil Manikandan, J.Jerauld Alwin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Manufacturing, energy, and construction industries significantly use the services of ASME, API, and NFPA standards. But the retrieval of precise information from these large code libraries can be a slow process. For these reasons, a new AI-based hybrid document retrieval system is discussed in this paper. The new system will not only use keyword searches using Elasticsearch with traditional AI methods, but it will also use transformer-based large language models (LLMs) for providing precise information related to industrial standards. The system will use tokenaware preprocessing techniques, fall-back approaches for large queries, or a summary to ensure clarity. In contrast to conventional Retrieval-Augmentation-Generation (RAG) approaches, it will divide the two processes for faster execution. The system will demonstrate sub-two-second latency measurements with precise alignment of source documents.

title: The Dual-core Simple-12: Design and Development of the World’s Smallest Multi-core Processor
authors: Mohammad Abdul Qayum, Rasin Mohammed Ihtemam, Riasat Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: In the era of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Artificial Intelligence (AI), parallel computing has become essential for efficiently processing large data sets. Today, this parallelism is not only confined to clusters of computers in data centers but also extends to multi-core processors within a single chip in personal computers and smartphones. As a result, the study of multi-core processors has become a part of introductory computer architecture courses. However, the complexity of the technology poses challenges for students to fully understand the concepts. In this paper, we present a simple dual-core processor, Simple-12, which is currently the smallest multi-core processor, designed using Verilog HDL. The two cores are connected via shared memory, and the Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) consists of only 17 instructions. A max-finder program is executed with both symmetric and asymmetric distributions of initial data. The Cycles Per Instruction (CPI) for symmetric operations is 0.74 for 20 elements and 1.46 for 40 elements.
