For asymmetric operations with 30 elements, the CPI is also calculated to be 1.46, similar to 40 elements symmetrical load.

title: IoT-Enabled Water Quality Monitoring with Auto-Scalable Cloud DevOps Pipelines
authors: Tharun. B, Rishi Ganth. K.A, Rohini G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: IoT-Enabled Water Quality Monitoring with Auto-Scalable Cloud DevOps Pipelines introduces AquaGuardian, a distributed Internet of Things (IoT) and cloud-integrated platform designed for ongoing assessment of important water quality parameters, including pH, temperature, turbidity, and Total Dissolved Solids (TDS). The system uses ESP32 microcontrollers connected to environmental sensors to capture real-time measurements and send them securely to AWS IoT Core with the MQTT protocol. The incoming data is stored in cloud services like Amazon DynamoDB and Amazon S3, where it is further examined to spot anomalies in water quality. When the system detects abnormal readings, it automatically sends user notifications through AWS Simple Notification Service (SNS) via SMS or email. To support scalable, reliable, and automated deployment, AquaGuardian incorporates a CAI/CD DevOps pipeline using GitHub, AWS CodePipeline, and AWS Lambda. This setup allows for continuous updates and operational efficiency. The solution offers real-time insights, high reliability, and less need for human intervention, making it ideal for large-scale smart water monitoring applications.

title: Detection of Corn Plant Diseases Using Advanced Deep Learning Architectures
authors: Ahmed Wakil Muttaki, Mohammed Sowket Ali, S.M. Khaladuzzaman, Tahmina Sultana, Md. Shafiul Alam Chowdhury, Md Zahim Hassan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Corn is one of the most widely grown crops around the world, and it is very easy to get affected by different kinds of diseases like fungal, bacterial, and viral infections, which can lower the amount of harvest. Using traditional ways to identify these diseases takes a lot of time, needs special knowledge, and is often not practical for checking large areas, which shows the need for faster and easier solutions. This study looks at using automatic, efficient, and scalable methods to detect leaf diseases in maize (corn) using deep learning models. Three advanced CNN models-InceptionV3, ResNet101V2, and MobileNetV2-are used to classify diseases from a set of 4,507 leaf images. These images are divided into four groups: Gray Leaf Spot, Common Rust, Blight, and Healthy. The images are processed using different color space transformations, and the models are trained with transfer learning and data augmentation to improve their performance and avoid overfitting. Among the three models, MobileNetV2 performed best when the images were processed in the HSV (Hue, Saturation, and Value) color space, achieving an accuracy of 94.23%. This shows that it can effectively detect even small signs of disease. Which can help improve farming in a precise way and support food security globally.

title: Joint Beamforming and Semantic Compression Algorithm for Integrated Sensing and Communication Networks with Transformer-Based Semantic Encoding
authors: Jianxu Wei
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper studies a semantic-aware integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) network that jointly optimizes semantic compression, transmit beamforming, and sensing receive filtering. A dual-driven Transformer semantic encoder is introduced, where self-attention extracts communication semantics and cross-attention injects echo-side sensing cues for token pruning and bit allocation. An end-to-end multi-objective problem minimizes semantic distortion under achievable-rate, transmit-power, and Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) constraints and is solved by a low-complexity alternating algorithm that updates compression ratios, beamformers, and receive filters. In a multi-user MISO ISAC setting, the proposed method reduces semantic distortion by more than 30% at 0 dB relative to separated and conventional baselines, while maintaining competitive throughput and better sensing accuracy. The results indicate that semantic-aware resource coordination is effective for future 6 G ISAC systems.

title: Zero-Copy Proxy Tunneling via Linux Splice for Cloud Infrastructure
authors: Yihong Jin, Ze Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Cloud-hosted proxy servers are critical components of modern infrastructure, forwarding encrypted traffic between clients and backend services across use cases ranging from content delivery and microservice meshes to API gateways. The traditional proxy data path copies each byte twice through user space, consuming CPU cycles that scale linearly with throughput and impose hard limits on serving capacity. This paper integrates the Linux splice system call into Apache Traffic Server (ATS) blind tunnel mode, enabling direct socket-to-socket data transfer through kernel pipes without any user-space copying. We introduce PipeIOBuffer, a polymorphic extension of ATS's buffer hierarchy that requires changes to only four components while preserving the existing event-driven I/O architecture. Experiments on AWS C6in instances demonstrate that our implementation doubles maximum proxy throughput (from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 275$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 550 \text{MB} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>), reduces proxy-induced latency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40-55 {\%}$</tex> at moderate load, and cuts CPU utilization by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20-30 {\%}$</tex>. These gains are applicable to any opaque-forwarding proxy deployment, including emerging workloads such as AI inference API gateways.

title: On What GPT-Style Initialization Contributes to Sensor-Only Traffic Forecasting a Parameter-Matched Case Study on Two PEMS Benchmarks
authors: Hongbo Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Recent traffic forecasters initialized from GPT-style checkpoints report gains in low-label settings, but the source of those gains remains unclear. In the sensor-only setup, text is absent at inference time; a text-pretrained Transformer is used primarily as initialization for a numerical sequence model. We revisit this setting under a parameter-matched protocol where backbone, tokenizer, prediction head, optimizer, and compute budget are fixed, and only initialization varies: GPT-2, random Xavier, or self-supervised pretraining on unlabeled traffic windows. Across PEMS04 and PEMS08, covering full-shot forecasting, few-shot learning, cross-dataset adaptation, horizon-wise error analysis, and missing-observation stress tests, GPT-2 initialization consistently improves over random initialization. At the same time, traffic-domain pretraining is stronger in most settings and deteriorates more gradually under corruption. The results suggest that, within the sensor-only setting studied here, a substantial part of the gain associated with GPT-style initialization may come from pretraining itself-especially when the pretraining task is aligned with spatio-temporal structure-rather than from language-specific knowledge.

title: Dynamic Big Data Summarisation Using Transformer-Based Generative Models
authors: Evha Rozario, Md Nazmussakib, Gazi Touhidul Alam, Tushar Roy, Muhammad Adnan, Md Imtiaz Faruk
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The sheer proliferation of non-homogeneous big data that are produced as a result of cloud solutions, social media, IoT systems, and enterprise applications has necessitated the need to summarise data efficiently and adaptively. The process of extractive and rule-based summarisation approaches fails to provide the contextual relevance, semantic coherence, and temporal dynamics in large scale and ever-changing datasets. To overcome these shortcomings, this paper suggests a dynamic big data summarisation model based on transformer-based generative models that can be used to generate summaries that are context-sensitive, scalable, and real-time. The approach proposed takes advantage of self-attention structures and already trained generative transformers to capture long-range dependencies among distributed data streams enabling the system to produce abstractive summaries that maintain semantic fidelity and greatly reduce redundant information. In order to manage data velocity and concept drift a dynamic adaptation module is added that allows incremental summarisation without having to retrain the entire model. The framework is planned to work effectively in the distributed big data environments through incorporation of parallel processing and memory-efficient inference plans. Large-scale textual datasets that have been experimentally evaluated show that the proposed method performs better than state-of-the-art summarisation methods in ROUGE scores, contextual consistency, and latency. The findings demonstrate the usefulness of transformer-based generative models to generate short, coherent and timely summaries that can be applied in real-world big data analytics. The paper offers a scaling basis of intelligent knowledge condensation in advanced data-driven systems of the future.

title: Constitutional Compass: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Indian Legal and Constitutional Analysis
authors: Dileep Reddy Bolla, Aniketh Joshi, Nandeesh HM, Pranav S Devang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The Constitutional Compass project presents a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework designed to enhance access to the Constitution of India and related legal information. The system integrates semantic embeddings generated through In Legal BERT with lexical retrieval using BM25, combining both through Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and refining the results via a Cross Encoder reranker. The top-ranked constitutional articles are then summarized using a domain-adapted Large Language Model (LLM) implemented through Ollama. This hybrid retrieval architecture ensures improved accuracy, contextual understanding, and interpretability compared to traditional search-based systems. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an F1-score of 89.4 Percentage, confirming its effectiveness in generating concise, legally sound summaries for complex constitutional queries. The framework contributes to AI-driven legal assistance and serves as a foundation for future intelligent governance applications

title: Communication-Computation-Efficient Federated Learning with Sparse Aggregation and Shared Anchor Data
authors: Jiayang Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training without sharing raw data, but its practical deployment on resource-constrained edge devices is severely limited by high communication and computation overhead, as well as performance degradation under non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data distributions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight and communication-computation-efficient federated learning framework, termed Federated Sparse Aggregation (FedSA). Instead of dynamically adjusting pruning structures, FedSA employs a fixed sparse aggregation mechanism to significantly reduce bidirectional communication and client-side computation while maintaining stable training. In addition, a small Shared Anchor Dataset (SAD) is introduced to participate in local training, effectively mitigating representation drift caused by severe non-IID data. Experimental results show that sharing only 5 % of public data can improve model accuracy by up to 20.73 %. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10 and SVHN under various non-IID settings demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive accuracy with up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 4. 4 \%}$</tex> uplink communication reduction and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 0. 5 \%}$</tex> computation savings, indicating strong practical potential for real-world federated systems.

title: Human and Machine: Reimagining Creativity through Artificial Intelligence in Visual Arts Practices
authors: Bhardwaj Sarita, Jaiswal Ajay Prakash, Gule Ganesh, Das Suprabha, Yadav Charu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Art has always represented human cultural, emotional, and spiritual evolution. With the rapid growth of artificial intelligence, a new era of artistic creation has emerged, raising questions about creativity, authorship, and the meaning of beauty. This paper examines the convergence of AI and fine art, focusing on how machine learning, neural networks, and generative adversarial networks (GANs) are reshaping artistic practices. Using secondary data such as scholarly articles, exhibition reviews, market reports, and global case studies, the study highlights how AI tools like DALL•E, StyleGAN, and Deep Dream help artists create complex visuals, experiment with hybrid styles, and make art production more accessible and decentralized. However, this technological shift brings major ethical concerns, including algorithmic bias, data privacy issues, and unclear copyright ownership, challenging existing legal and philosophical norms. In India, a culturally rich nation with centuries-old artistic traditions, AI-generated art creates both excitement and hesitation. Some artists use AI to reinterpret classical themes, while others fear losing cultural authenticity and emotional depth. The paper also emphasizes how AI is transforming the role of artists into co-creators or curators. Overall, strong ethical guidelines are necessary to ensure responsible, transparent, and culturally sensitive AI-driven art.

title: Effective Intrusion Detection in Industrial IoT Networks Using a Hybrid GAN-CNN Framework
authors: Amal Bouazzi, Mariem Benslama, Abdelhamid Helali, Wajdi Saadaoui
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has created new cybersecurity challenges, including protecting industrial control systems from advanced attacks. Conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS) frequently fail to detect uncommon or unknown attacks due to data imbalance and limited generalization capability. This paper presents IDS-GAN-CNN, a hybrid model integrating generative adversarial networks (GANs) and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to optimize intrusion detection performance in IIoT contexts. The GAN generates credible synthetic attack samples, thereby balancing the dataset and strengthening the representation. The CNN is then trained on the enhanced dataset to differentiate normal traffic from malicious traffic. This combination greatly enhances detection performance over existing approaches in terms of classificationonly models and rebalancing techniques, while considering IIoT constraints such as latency and resource consumption. Experimental evaluation on the X-IIoTID dataset shows improved performance, achieving an accuracy of 98.95%.

title: Predictive Machine Learning Model for Accurate and Scalable Credit Risk Assessment in Digital Lending Platforms
authors: Sai Vamsi Chennupati, Sasikanth Yangalasetty, Somnath Banerjee
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: It has severed the rapid development of online financial sectors hence exacerbating the necessity to guarantee intelligent, scalable and open systems that could characterize loyalty of borrowers within information-based lending environments. The conventional techniques that rely on narrow credit histories are typically unable to represent the intricacy of the heterogeneous borrower behavior particularly in the underserved markets. In order to overcome these weaknesses, in this paper, a novel framework referred to as Heterogeneous Adaptive Risk Intelligence Network (HARINet) is proposed that incorporates multi-view representation learning, temporal-behavioral modeling, hybrid predictive mechanisms, and federated optimization. HARINet is a system that builds a complete profile of borrowers with a combination of various streams of data, such as formal financial data, digital footprints of transactions, and others, such as comporting metrics, through graph-based and variational encoders. A transformer-based adaptive gating temporal learner is used to observe the sequential borrowing activities to discern important patterns of repayments. Bayesian uncertainty quantification as a hybrid neural-boosting core improves the reliability of predictions and close-to-optimal reinforcement-based calibration would be used, so that the dynamic readjustment of the risk thresholds can be done dynamically at the portfolio level. Privacy federated training is a secure method to enable various financial establishments to jointly train models without disclosing sensitive information. Also, since it offers transparency and mitigation of bias, there are integrated interpretability and fairness modules, which aids ethical and regulatory compliance. The suggested framework has a potential of transforming the concept of assessing borrowers through consolidating predictive accuracy, scalability, credibility, and inclusivity of digital lending. The HARINet system had a total accuracy of 94.6% in the risk of credit evaluation.

title: An Explainable Federated EfficientNet–Transformer Model for Lung Cancer Classification from Histopathological CT Images
authors: Sinthiya Tabashoum, Aysha Anis Roza, Md. Abdul Masud, Arjon Golder, Rakibul Hasan Sezan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Lung cancer, a leading cause of cancer-related death, necessitates early and accurate classification. Due to regulatory constraints imposed by HIPAA and GDPR, access to medical data is highly restricted, necessitating the adoption of federated learning approaches to advance sensitive and critical tasks, such as liver cancer classification. However, there remains a significant gap in research concerning the interpretability and explainability of federated medical AI classification models. To address this, we propose an explainable federated deep learning framework that incorporates a hybrid, customized deep neural architecture alongside an advanced aggregation strategy, FedProx. This framework emphasizes model transparency while maintaining data privacy. In This approach the global model achieved almost 98.0% held-out test accuracy over 8 federated rounds, outperforming existing deep learning and federated learning models in both efficiency and accuracy. Furthermore, interpretability analysis using Grad-CAM confirmed that the model reliably attended to neoplastic regions. These results underscore that the combination of federated learning, multi-head attention, and explainable AI provides a strong foundation for advancing digital pathology and establishes a new benchmark by delivering a rare balance of diagnostic accuracy, statistical robustness, and clinical relevance.

title: Kalman_MASAC: An Enhanced Reinforcement Learning Algorithm Optimised for Cloud-Edge-Device
authors: Yusen Nie, Xitai Na, Huan Liu, Yuheng Ding
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The scaling of drone-assisted cloud-edge-terminal collaborative systems often necessitates multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) coordinating with cloud infrastructure to serve numerous user terminals, placing stringent demands on task offloading decisions and resource allocation capabilities. This paper investigates multi-UAV-assisted cloud-edge-terminal collaborative task offloading and resource allocation under user energy constraints. An optimisation model is designed to minimise the weighted sum of task processing delay and energy consumption. An interactive framework combining Kalman filtering with reinforcement learning is proposed for solution. During interactions between the reinforcement learning agent and the environment, the agent observes states that have undergone filtering and noise addition. Experiments conducted across multiple configurations of environmental parameters demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms baseline algorithms in multi-UAV cluster scenarios. It exhibits superior performance in terms of task timeout rates, user energy consumption, and training convergence, thereby validating its effectiveness and generalisability in complex environments and collaborative task scenarios.

title: Exploring Heavy Metal Contamination of Plastic Polymers in EV Battery Packs
authors: Krish Bafna, Shweta Loonkar, Karishma Desai
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to the accelerated adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), end-of-life research on battery materials has increased focusing on improved sustainability. While cathodes, anodes, and electrolytes have been extensively studied and intricate recycling techniques have been developed for them, investigation into recycling techniques for polymer-based separators and casings in EV battery packs remain very limited. This leads to incorrect sorting and improper recycling of the polymers. The aim of this research is to undertake an investigation to identify and analyse plastic polymer samples from EV battery packs for heavy metal contamination. Fourier-Transform Infrared Spectroscopy (FT-IR) was used for polymer identification, followed by Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Energy-Dispersive X-Ray Spectroscopy (EDS) to evaluate surface structure and measure the quantity of heavy metal impurities. Findings revealed the presence of surface-adsorbed contaminants including manganese, nickel, and cobalt in varying concentrations, with microporous topologies acting as metallic traps. The presence of contaminants highlights the concern of both environmental and material risks if unprocessed polymers end up in landfills. This research shows that accurate polymer identification followed by contamination detection and measurement directly improves recycling efficiency, reduces EV battery manufacturing costs, and minimizes environmental risks. This study is innovative in creating a link between microstructural analysis and heavy metal contamination evaluation. In doing so it increases policy discussion on circular economy models for EV battery waste management.

title: DelowlightSplat: Feed-Forward Gaussian Splatting for Lowlight 3D Scene Reconstruction
authors: Fuzhen Jiang, Zengtian Xie, Zhuoran Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Novel-view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from sparse posed images are central to robotics and AR/VR. Yet, feed-forward 3D Gaussian reconstruction fails under lowlight due to noise, color shifts, and unreliable correspondence. We propose DelowlightSplat, a lowlight-aware feed-forward Gaussian splatting framework for clean novel-view rendering. We build a controllable multi-view lowlight benchmark by degrading only context views while keeping target views clean. We introduce a lightweight Lowlight Adapter for residual enhancement to improve matchability, and couple it with cost-volume-based multi-view inference to directly predict clean 3D Gaussians. Experiments show that DelowlightSplat significantly outperforms previous feed-forward method and two-stage pipeline under lowlight conditions.

title: Automatic Road Extraction and Alert Generation for New Roads from Satellite Imagery
authors: M.C. Babu, K Sriram, B Suganidhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: This paper design a deep learning algorithm named StripUNet-MCSA for new road detection and recognition. The StripUNet-MCSA algorithm comprises an encoder-decoder struc- ture where the encoder part is constructed with ResNet50 and the decoder is designed with two branches where one branch involves the use of the MCSA block. It is pertinent to mention here that none of the existing techniques uses any kind of algorithm for new road identification; however, the present research work will make use of new road recognition based on the comparison between the predicted map and the GIS road map. According to the experimental outcomes on the DeepGlobe data set [1], the StripUNet-MCSA algorithm performs better than other existing algorithms including U-Net, DeepLabV3+, DLinkNet, and UBR- Net in terms of F1-Score and IoU scores with values of 81.20% and 68.35%, respectively.

title: PI-SGF: Physics-Informed Swin-GeoFormer for Unsupervised Pore Aspect Ratio Prediction in Reservoir Characterization
authors: Wenbiao Yang, Wenli Shang, Lianhai Wang, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pore aspect ratio (α) is a key microstructural parameter that controls elastic wave propagation in reservoir rocks, yet it cannot be directly measured from well logs, and existing estimation methods rely on computationally expensive iterative inversion or pseudo-labels. This paper presents PI-SGF (Physics-Informed Swin-GeoFormer), a physics-informed framework that couples log-sequence learning with the Berryman–Gassmann rock-physics model as a differentiable constraint, enabling fully unsupervised learning of α alongside supervised estimation of porosity (ϕ), clay content (Vclay), and water saturation (WS). The framework further incorporates geology-sensitive sequence modeling and progressive constraint strengthening to preserve formation-boundary information and stabilize the coupled supervised-unsupervised optimization. On strict cross-basin blind tests spanning three geologically distinct basins on two continents (Junggar, Sichuan, and North Sea), PI-SGF achieves R2 ≥ 0.90 for α against independent inversion-derived references and velocity reconstruction R2 > 0.95. These results indicate that α can be estimated from conventional logs in a physically consistent manner under the evaluated cross-basin setting.

title: ComSoc Training
authors: N/A

title: A Hybrid Modulation Strategy for Enhancing the Reactive Power Output Capability of Three-Phase Isolated DC-AC Matrix Converter
authors: Chaofan Wei, Jiatao Yang, Rui Li
abstract: The three-phase isolated DC–AC matrix converter is a promising isolated converter topology featuring high efficiency and high power density. However, existing studies mainly focus on unity power factor operation, while the reactive power capability of this converter has not been systematically investigated. This paper first analyzes the reactive power limitation of the converter from the perspective of current synthesis. The maximum achievable power factor angle under different DC voltages is determined through numerical analysis. Based on this observation, analytical expressions for the DC voltage and the maximum AC-side power factor angle under conventional two-line voltage modulation are derived. To overcome this reactive power limitation, a hybrid modulation strategy is proposed. By introducing an additional line-to-line voltage within the switching period, the proposed strategy enables the generation of the required reactive current when the conventional two-line voltage modulation reaches its limit. The converter automatically transitions between the two-line and three-line voltage modulation modes according to predefined conditions. Experimental results at multiple DC voltages verify the effectiveness of the proposed strategy and demonstrate that a power factor of 0.8 can be maintained over a wide DC voltage range with current THD below 3%.

title: Reddit as a Semantic Sensor: Forecasting Forex Movements via Emotionally Weighted Signals in Computational Social Systems
authors: Lersak Phothong, Anupong Sukprasert, Prapassorn Wareesri, Nantana Ngamtampong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: This study presents an empirical framework for forecasting directional movements of the EUR/USD foreign exchange market using sentiment signals extracted from Reddit. Sentiment is classified with FinBERT (a financial-domain adaptation of Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers, BERT) and transformed through an engagement-weighted formulation to capture social amplification effects, with a two-day behavioral lag applied to align sentiment with market responses. Feature engineering incorporates linguistic attributes, interaction terms, and rolling sentiment averages. Four machine learning models—Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost (XGB), and Neural Networks—are evaluated using walk-forward time-aware cross-validation. Results indicate that XGB achieves the highest average predictive performance, with accuracy around 0.78 and consistently high AUC under walk-forward evaluation, relative to standard price-based and naive baseline models. Model interpretability is examined using SHapley Additive Explanations, while a posthoc semantic analysis employing BERTopic, WordClouds, and uniform manifold approximation and projection (UMAP) shows that correct predictions are associated with coherent and recurring discourse patterns related to market conditions and sentiment expression. Overall, the findings demonstrate that engagement-weighted Reddit sentiment provides a robust and interpretable signal for short-horizon Forex forecasting.

title: Verification and Synthesis of Compatible Control Lyapunov and Multiple High-Order Control Barrier Functions
authors: Chuanrui Jiang, Hongkai Dai, Andrew Clark
abstract: Stability and safety are important properties of control systems. Control Lyapunov Functions (CLF) and Control Barrier Functions (CBF) have been proposed to enforce stability and safety properties respectively. However CLFs and CBFs could fail to guarantee joint safety and stability when there does not exist a common control action that simultaneously satisfies both the CBF and CLF constraints. In this paper, for control-affine dynamical systems both with and without input limits, we propose the necessary and sufficient condition for compatible CLFs and multiple High-Order Control Barrier Functions (HOCBF). We derive the compatibility condition using Farkas' Lemma and Positivstellensatz, and formulate an SOS program to numerically verify the compatibility condition. Based on the proposed verification framework, we propose a bilinear-alternating method to synthesize compatible CLFs and CBFs with a large safe region and region-of-attraction. Moreover, we derive the necessary and sufficient condition for the continuity of the CLF-CBF-QP controller, which we can verify through solving an SOS program. We showcase our approach in several numerical examples, including adaptive cruise control (ACC), an inverted pendulum, and a 13-state quadrotor. The code is open-sourced at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/hongkai-dai/compatible_clf_cbf</uri>.

title: HALO: Language-Conditioned Overhead Monocular Aerial Exploration and Navigation
authors: Yuezhan Tao, Dexter Ong, Fernando Cladera, Jason Hughes, Camillo J. Taylor, Pratik Chaudhari, Vijay Kumar
abstract: We demonstrate real-time overhead aerial metric-semantic mapping and exploration using a monocular camera paired with a global positioning system (GPS). Our system, named HALO, addresses two key challenges: (i) real-time dense 3D reconstruction using vision at large distances, and (ii) mapping and exploration of large-scale outdoor environments with accurate scene geometry and semantics. We demonstrate that HALO can plan informative paths that exploit this information to complete missions with multiple tasks specified in natural language. In simulation-based evaluation across large-scale environments of size up to 78,000 sq. m., HALO consistently completes tasks with less exploration time and achieves up to 68% higher competitive ratio in terms of the distance traveled compared to the state-of-the-art semantic exploration baseline. We use real-world experiments on a custom quadrotor platform to demonstrate that (i) all modules can run onboard the robot, and that (ii) in diverse environments HALO can support effective autonomous execution of missions covering up to 24,600 sq. m. area at an altitude of 40 m.

title: Novel Synthesis Design for Absorptive Frequency-Selective Reflector
authors: Yun-Jie Zhu, Wanping Zhang, Bo Li, Lei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication presents a novel synthesis design method for absorptive frequency-selective reflectors (AFSRs) with arbitrarily specified reflection bandwidths. The proposed method integrates an ultrawideband impedance matching network with a bandstop filtering network to achieve flexible control over absorption and reflection characteristics. Detailed circuit models for both networks are proposed and cascaded to construct AFSRs with either wide or narrow reflection bands. A customized synthesis procedure maps desired real-rational functions to the transfer functions of the respective circuit models, enabling precise determination of circuit parameters based on given design specifications. To validate the theoretical predictions, the single- and dual-polarized AFSR prototypes with the wide and narrow reflection bands are designed, fabricated, and measured. The wide band prototype operates from 1.38 to 8.68 GHz (FBW = 145.1%) with a reflective 3dB bandwidth of 3.31–6.70 GHz (FBW = 67.7%) for TE-polarized incident wave. The narrow band prototype operates from 1.26 to 8.29 GHz (FBW = 147.2%) with reflective 3dB bandwidths of 4.90 to 5.19 GHz (FBW = 6.1%) for both TE- and TM-polarized ones. The simulated and experimental results consistently confirm the effectiveness of the proposed synthesis design method for designing AFSRs with arbitrarily specified reflection bandwidths.

title: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems

title: Modular High-Efficiency Bidirectional Isolated DC/DC Converter with Synchronous Control for Energy Storage System
authors: Yu-En Wu, Po-Chun Liu
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This study proposes a modular, high-efficiency bidirectional isolated DC/DC converter with synchronous control. The converter adopts a modular design, where the power of a single module is 500 W. Paralleling three modules with synchronous control allows the total power rating of the converter to reach 1.5 kW. The modular architecture allows flexibility to adjust according to the application requirements, thereby reducing the development cost. Both step-up and -down operations only require a single pair of complementary signals, which significantly reduces the control complexity. Furthermore, the design provides electrical isolation, enhancing the operational safety, while some switches achieve zero voltage switching (ZVS), thereby minimizing the switching losses. The feasibility and the correctness of the proposed converter are verified through theoretical analysis, software simulation, and hardware implementation. The experimental results show that the maximum efficiency of a single module reaches 97.12% and 97.11% in the step-up and -down modes, respectively. For the three modules operating in parallel, the maximum efficiency reaches 96.1% and 96% in the step-up and -down modes, respectively.

title: Robust Distributed Optimization for Multi-Agent Systems with General Linear Models
authors: Tong Liu, Michelangelo Bin, Ivano Notarnicola, Thomas Parisini, Zhong-Ping Jiang
abstract: We propose a general approach to the problem of distributed feedback optimization for both continuous- and discrete-time, linear uncertain multi-agent systems. When the agent's dynamics are subject to disturbances and parametric uncertainties, and the gradients of the local objective functions can only be measured through each agent's output, the problem falls outside the scope of traditional distributed optimization algorithms. This paper combines an internal-model-based controller with a distributed optimization algorithm to provide a solution that guarantees robust asymptotic stability of the optimal steady state. The analysis is based on Lyapunov theory, on the time-scale separation properties of the considered optimization algorithm, and on the robustness of internal-model-based control.

title: Enhancing Mouth Stick Usability for People with Tetraplegia: A Quantitative Evaluation of UI Target Size and Placement
authors: Izumi Mizoguchi, Hiroyuki Kajimoto
abstract: For individuals with tetraplegia due to spinal cord injury, a mouth stick is a low-cost and practical assistive technology for operating smartphones. However, established UI design guidelines that consider its unique operational characteristics are lacking, and existing guidelines (e.g., a minimum target size of 44 iOS pt) can significantly impair their usability. This study aims to quantitatively determine the optimal target size and a recommended placement area that minimizes interaction errors in smartphone UIs, thereby improving accessibility for mouth stick users. We created an experimental environment where 10 able-bodied participants experienced simulated tetraplegia by physically restricting their trunk and neck movements. Participants performed a tapping task that combined five target sizes (44 iOS pt to 132 iOS pt) and 25 screen locations under two levels of simulated impairment. Task completion time and the number of mis-taps were recorded and analyzed as performance metrics. Notably, the minimum sizes recommended by existing guidelines (e.g., 44 iOS pt or 48 iOS pt) were found to be impractical, leading to frequent mis-taps. To significantly reduce interaction errors and time, a target size of 88 iOS pt or larger is strongly recommended. Furthermore, the optimal placement area for buttons was identified as the lower-central region of the screen, while the screen edges, especially the top, should be avoided. This study is the first to provide specific UI design guidelines based on quantitative data specialized for mouth stick interaction. The target size and layout recommendations proposed herein are an indispensable contribution for UI designers aiming to develop truly accessible applications for users with tetraplegia.

title: Meta relation-aware Metric Learning Framework for Few-Shot Uncertain Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Xinyan Wang, Kuo Yang, Jian Liu, Xianan Li, Rui Hua, Kuan Xu, Zixin Shu, Xuezhong Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Uncertainty is a fundamental aspect of human knowledge, widely present in various domains such as medicine, biology, and finance. Uncertain Knowledge Graph Completion (UKGC) tasks encompass both link prediction and confidence prediction. However, the inaccessibility of high-quality knowledge results in sparse knowledge graphs, where a large number of entities exhibit relatively sparse neighborhoods. This sparsity severely hinders the performance of few-shot UKGC models, which generally rely on neighborhood aggregation to enhance entity representations. In this study, we propose a Meta relation-aware Metric learning framework designed for few-shot UKGC, named MMUC. To mitigate neighbor sparsity, MMUC introduces a relation-centric paradigm that decouples representation enhancement from background graphs, integrating an uncertainty-driven similarity measure through a learnable confidence learner to achieve robust, non-linear fusion for joint link and confidence prediction. Extensive evaluations across multiple benchmarks demonstrate MMUC's superior performance through rigorous link and confidence prediction, ablation studies, and sparsity analysis, highlighting its particular resilience to neighbor sparsity in few-shot uncertain KGs. The source code can be accessed in <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/sienna-wxy/MMUC</uri>.

title: Development of the Fully Automatic Robotic Storyteller – Comparing Manual, Semi-Automatic, and LLM-Based Emotional Story Annotation for Multimodal Robotic Storytelling
authors: Sophia C. Steinhaeusser, Albin Zehe, Sophia Maier, Andreas Hotho, Birgit Lugrin
abstract: Storytelling has accompanied mankind since its beginnings and receiving stories is still a popular leisure activity. Today, new storytelling media are emerging. For example, social robots can act as storytellers, using different modalities such as facial expressions or body postures. With the goal of automating robotic storytelling, we annotated stories with emotion labels which a robot can use to automatically adapt its behavior to. Doing so, three different approaches and a baseline are compared in two studies in this paper: 1) manual labels by human annotators (MA), 2) software-based word-sensitive annotation using the Linguistic Inquiry and Word Count program (LIWC), 3) a LLM approach using ChatGPT (GPT), and 4) random annotations as baseline (RBL). In an online study showing videos of a storytelling robot, the annotations were validated using a backwards annotation task, with GPT closely followed by MA achieving the best, and the RBL the worst results. In a subsequent lab user study, all versions of the story were compared regarding storytelling experience, emotions, and robot perception, revealing no significant differences for any of these variables based on the annotation approach, but a significant decrease of negative affect and fatigue over the storytelling in all conditions. Taken together, the results indicate suitability of both the manual and GPT approaches. On this empirical basis, the Automatic Robotic Storyteller was implemented using GPT-based annotations and bodily expressions. The reduced programming effort of our system makes robotic storytelling accessible to a broader audience, strengthening its deployment in the field of affective computing.

title: Probabilistic Collision Risk Estimation through Gauss-Legendre Cubature and Non-Homogeneous Poisson Processes
authors: Trent Weiss, Madhur Behl
abstract: Overtaking in high-speed autonomous racing demands precise, real-time estimation of collision risk; particularly in wheel-to-wheel scenarios where safety margins are minimal. Existing methods for collision risk estimation either rely on simplified geometric approximations, like bounding circles, or perform Monte Carlo sampling which leads to overly conservative motion planning behavior at racing speeds. We introduce the Gauss–Legendre Rectangle (GLR) algorithm, a principled two-stage integration method that estimates collision risk by combining Gauss–Legendre with a non-homogeneous Poisson process over time. GLR produces accurate risk estimates that account for vehicle geometry and trajectory uncertainty. In experiments across 446 overtaking scenarios in a high-fidelity Formula One racing simulation, GLR outperforms five state-of-the-art baselines achieving an average error reduction of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$77\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> and surpassing the next-best method by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$52\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, all while running at 1000 Hz. The framework is general and applicable to broader motion planning contexts beyond autonomous racing.

title: Robust Complete Synchronization of Coupled Boolean Networks with Stuck-at Fault
authors: Xiaofan Ma, Xiaowei Jiang, Ming Chi, Zhi-Wei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper investigates the robust complete synchronization of coupled Boolean networks (BNs) subject to stuck-at fault. When stuck-at fault occurs, certain nodes become permanently fixed and the original synchronization conditions may no longer be applicable. To address this issue, we propose a new concept of fault-preserving subset to characterize the admissible invariant state evolution induced by stuck-at fault. Based on this concept, necessary and sufficient conditions are derived to determine whether complete synchronization remains valid without reconstructing the faulty network model. Furthermore, for coupled Boolean control networks (BCNs), a robust feedback complete synchronization scheme is developed by designing state feedback controllers based on maximal control fault-preserving subset. Finally, several numerical examples are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed results.
