title: Zero-Trust Architecture for Hybrid Cloud Security in Critical Infrastructures
authors: Rachakonda Uma, Mounika Maggidi, Anurag Reddy Ekkati, Sharmila Bandlamudi, Chiranjeevisantosh Madugundi, Sai Kiran Taduri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The extreme usage of hybrid cloud environment in very vital industry like energy, healthcare, transport has increased the complexity of cyber-attacks caused by distributed assets and inappropriate security controls. Older security models that rely on perimeter security models do not secure the contemporary attacks that make use of internal trust assumptions. This research is based on a Zero-Trust Architecture (ZTA) design applied to the hybrid cloud setup based on the combination of public (Microsoft Azure) and private (VMware vSphere) clouds. The proposed system incorporates identity-based access control, micro-segmentation, which is software-defined, and on-going monitoring with the help of SIEM and EDR. Quantitative performance of a six-month trial deployment in a simulated critical infrastructure setting demonstrates that an impressive security improvement took place: the Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) was cut down 8 hours to 45 minutes (82% reduction), attempted unauthorized access attempts dropped by 89% and NIST 800-53 compliance had improved by 63 to 91%. Also, lateral movement incidents were reduced by 90 by the system, which offered a high level of withholding of insider and external threats. These results prove that the zero-trust architecture can successfully enhance hybrid cloud resiliency, improve visibility, and implement dynamic access control in the most significant infrastructural processes.

title: PotatoLeafNet: Deep Learning Approach for Potato Leaf Disease Identification
authors: Khalilur Rahman, Syme Al-Azad Shuvo, Md Nuruzzaman, Atikur Rahman, Geerbani Pal Shashi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Potato disease control has high importance in agriculture, as it can lead to major food production losses. Deep learning has exhibited remarkable capabilities for automated disease identification. Current approaches rely significantly on large pretrained models that are not specifically retrained for agricultural imagery. This study aims to introduce PotatoLeafNet, a custom CNN for the multi-classification of diseases of potato leaf. The model was evaluated using a balanced dataset that consisted of 3,500 images and was categorized into seven categories: Potato Cyst Nematode, Potato Virus, Plant Pests, Early Blight, Late Blight, Fungal Diseases, and Healthy leaves. Four pretrained models, GoogleNet, MNASNet, ShuffleNetV2, and ResNeXt101, were optimized for the identical task to evaluate performance. The experimental results demonstrate that PotatoLeafNet outperformed all baseline models with an accuracy of 95%. The robustness was additionally confirmed using ROC-AUC curves and confusion matrices. This method can improve crop quality and overall agricultural output by making it easier to diagnose and treat potato diseases.

title: Meta-Learning for Adaptive Dynamical System Characterization
authors: Aravind Narayanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a meta-learning framework for model-free dynamical system identification using Transformer architectures. The approach pre-trains a general-purpose model on diverse synthetic systems—including linear, nonlinear, and insurance-inspired behavioral sequences—enabling rapid adaptation to unseen systems through in-context learning. Without requiring gradient updates or explicit model estimation, the frozen meta-model leverages short input-output context windows (10–20 steps) to perform accurate one-step prediction and multi-step simulation. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance over traditional system identification methods and gradient-based meta-learning baselines, with approximately 50% lower normalized RMSE and 2.5× higher adaptation efficiency. The framework’s practical utility is validated through insurance applications including fraud detection, claims forecasting, and customer behavior modeling, offering a flexible alternative to conventional identification techniques for complex, evolving systems.

title: Gamifying Financial Literacy in the Digital Era
authors: Vishali Muralidharan, Pawan Eswaran, Rahul Elango, Kumar K Suresh, N Vani, Varnikha S Janani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In Today’s World, many people, especially Young People, require high-quality financial literacy and education. To address that need, this paper examines gamified financial literacy. Rapid digitalisation and constant shifts in the economic system make financial decision-making harder for people because of traditional training methods. When a new system like Gamification includes many levels, badges, simulations and challenges, it ensures low resistance and high engagement in learning opportunities. This Gamified learning system, using the latest technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Augmented Reality, provides a space for customised learning and Real-time outcome evaluation. [1]

title: Smart Healthcare Prediction Using Hybrid Machine Learning And Fuzzy Logic Systems
authors: R Ramachandran, Vinothkumar C, G.Manikandan, Shruthi B S, Vibhavi R N, Rajashree M Byalal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In smart healthcare systems, fuzzy logic and machine learning together provide a breakthrough approach for predictive analytics. This paper introduces a novel hybrid model merging Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) for enhanced decision-making under uncertainty with Random Forest for feature selection and classification. Combining expert-like thinking with data-driven learning, the model is meant to highly accurately and interpretably predict patient health issues. Testing the proposed system uses actual healthcare data including patient symptoms, lab results, and historical diagnoses. Early illness identification, risk prediction, and therapy recommendations all show better performance than more traditional models. By means of managing problems like unbalanced data, noisy inputs, and non-linear interactions, the hybrid system increases prediction accuracy and resilience. This method makes real-time, customized healthcare information feasible, which improves proactive patient treatment and resource management by letting the results show great potential for expanded implementation in systems of smart clinical decision assistance.

title: CNN-Driven Model for Aerial and Satellite Image Classification and Feature Extraction
authors: S R Srinidhi, M Lancia, F Sangeetha Francelin Vinnarasi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Aerial and satellite image analysis is important in areas such as urban planning and environmental monitoring. It is very time-consuming and may lead to errors during image sensing. Convolutional Neural Networks are used to speed up and improve recognition patterns in aerial and satellite images. It can cover land-type classification, object detection, and key information extraction. Deep learning is used in the system, with approaches including convolutional layers, pooling, and activation functions to capture both details and overall information from images. Experimental results show that this approach achieves high accuracy by elucidating geographical features that provide the important information needed for real-world remote sensing applications.

title: AI-Augmented Optimization of High-Gain Circularly Polarized MIMO Dielectric Resonator Antennas
authors: Swati Anand Dwivedi, Raghavendra Sharma, Vivek Singh Kushwah
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The design and analysis of a circular-aperture-coupled 2-terminal perturbed dielectric antenna are presented in this work. Using perturbed dielectric rod, circularly polarized waves are created in 8.5–8.93 GHz band. By adding polarization diversity, port isolation is improved to levels higher than 25 dB. Additionally, adding a metasurface (MS) made of double-negative unit cells increases the antenna gain to above 9.5 dBi. Predicting the |S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>| properties is done using machine learning (ML) method, namely Deep Neural Network (DNN). The suggested radiator performs effectively between 8.12 and 9.15 GHz. This design is a strong contender for 6G communication applications due to its advantageous MIMO performance metrics and directional radiation profile.

title: Machine Learning Approaches For DDOS Attack Detection And Prevention: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: P. B. Jagadesh, P. Dineshkumar, Kandasamy Sellamuthu, P. Hariraj, S.Pavithra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: DDoS attacks are now a significant menace to contemporary networked infrastructures, especially those of clouds, IoT, and edge computing. The attacks take advantage of the large traffic volumes and the heterogeneity of connected devices to interfere with services resulting in financial and operational losses. The classical detection systems such as signature-based and threshold-based are not very successful in detecting evolving, low-rate, and multi-vector attacks in real time. To tackle these weaknesses, this paper will suggest a machine learning-supported DDoS detection and mitigation system of XGBoost, optimized according to maximum accuracy and minimum computation costs. The framework employs streaming feature engineering, temporality and graph based embeddings, and explainable AI methods to achieve improved detection and interpretability. It also includes automated mitigation measures, such as dynamically blocking or rate-limiting traffic flows of suspicious activity which is why it is appropriate to resource-constrained edge and IoT gateways. Through experimental analysis of benchmark datasets and simulated real world traffic, it is shown that the proposed approach has over 99% detection accuracy, can withstand concept drift, and has a high false negative rate, all at a high rate of performance in a limited environment. This single system of detection, interpretation, and mitigation offers a viable and scalable system of protecting the contemporary network systems against advanced DDoS attacks.

title: IoT-Enabled Dynamic Load Profiling and Real-Time Tariff Optimization for Smart Energy Management
authors: Masud Akatar, Tanuja R Pathare, Shweta Dolli, Minal Salunke
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This work presents a small, practical setup that looks at how electrical loads behave during the day and relates that behaviour to changing tariff periods. Instead of depending entirely on the user to manage appliances when the cost is high, the system observes the load through sensors and assigns it to the correct time slot using a simple controller. When the tariff moves into the higher range, the system slows or shifts selected loads, and once the tariff drops back, the loads are allowed to run in the usual manner. A basic prediction routine is included to estimate the likely cost in the near future by considering the current load level, the applied tariff, and the time of operation.In addition to the control action, the system also keeps a steady record of readings such as load, tariff, and cost so that the behaviour can be examined later. A small local interface is provided for users who prefer to operate the system manually, while the automatic mode handles the switching on its own. Tests carried out on the prototype showed that the method helped avoid running unnecessary loads during costly hours and prevented sudden rises in demand. The overall arrangement is simple, low-cost, and can be fitted into common electrical environments where even small adjustments on the user side can give noticeable improvements.

title: A Compact L-Shaped Wearable Antenna on FR-4 Substrate for Medical Applications
authors: A Yogeshwaran, M P Harinisri, M Gayathri, A Anubharathi, M Lavanya, P Saranya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In the context of this paper, a proposal is a miniaturized L-shaped wearable antenna that is closely tailored to the field of medicine with a special focus on the low-cost production and safety compliance to humans. The antenna is highly miniaturized (via an L-shaped design) by shrinking the size of the antenna to 35 × 10 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, as compared to the traditional 35 × 32 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and uses an FR-4 substrate with a dielectric constant of 4.4, which can be used in place of Rogers Duroid. The suggestion design utilizes frequency of 2.4 GHz with fractional bandwidth of 24.8% (2.052.69 GHz), and this is within common communication bands of the medical telemetry. Specific absorption rate (SAR) analysis has been conducted in detail with full realistic human tissue models, which prove to be in line with the FCC and ICNIRP safety regulations. The antenna has an omnidirectional H-plane and bidirectional E-plane radiation patterns and H gain of 3.2 dBi and a radiation efficiency of 88 percent. The test of performance under bending conditions and close to human body shows that the impedance is stable as well as the radiation characteristics, gain, and it is reliable in terms of wearable applications. Small size, low materials, and safety to the human factor make the proposed antenna a potential area of research to be successfully implemented in practice in wearable medical monitoring systems, including patient telemetry, health tracking, and body sensor networks.

title: Robust Binary-to-Grayscale Image Restoration via ControlNet
authors: Zhining Cao, Weijun Hong
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Recent advances in diffusion models have driven significant progress in high-fidelity image generation and restoration. However, recovering realistic grayscale images from sparse binary inputs remains a significant challenge. This task suffers from a severe Information Density Gap—where binary maps lose both fine-grained gradients and explicit semantic labels. Consequently, existing methods struggle to infer correct lighting hierarchies and often produce distorted structures or textures lacking realistic granularity. In this paper, we propose a robust binary-to-grayscale restoration framework tailored to bridge this gap. First, we construct a domain-specific paired dataset and introduce a Physics-Aware Augmentation (PAA) strategy during training. By simulating physical lighting variations and noise, PAA forces the model to learn robust illumination laws rather than overfitting to specific pixel values. Second, to resolve the trade-off between structural fidelity and textural richness during inference, we design a Dynamic Guidance Strategy (DGS). This strategy decouples the generation process by dynamically adjusting the classifier-free guidance intensity. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed framework, showing significant improvements over existing baselines in terms of both structural fidelity and grayscale consistency.

title: Automatic Detection of the Upper Respiratory Tract Infection from Speech Using Intrinsic Mode Mel Frequency Magnitude Coefficients
authors: Pankaj Warule, Sudhansu Sekhar Nayak, Siba Prasad Mishra, Sourabh Panwar, Ashok Parmar, Suman Deb
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The acoustic-prosodic properties of speech are influenced by various health related factors due to the intricate nature of its production. The development of non-invasive diagnostic methods utilizing speech is a very promising field within biomedical engineering. Utilizing speech cues for the screening of upper respiratory tract infections (URTIs) or common cold may be beneficial in mitigating their transmission. In this study, we have proposed the intrinsic mode Mel frequency magnitude coefficients (IMMFMC) feature for diagnosing the URTIs. First, the voiced speech frame is decomposed into several intrinsic mode functions (IMF) using variational mode decomposition. Then the magnitude of the discrete Fourier transform coefficients of each IMF is calculated and passed through the Mel filter bank. The IMMFMC features are derived by determining the logarithm of the magnitude spectrum obtained from Mel filter banks. The results demonstrate that the proposed IMMFMC features outperform the state-of-the-art methods. The proposed IMMFMC features achieve 68.02% and 71.51% UARs, respectively, on the URTIC database’s development and test partitions.

title: Implementation of Edge-AI to Process Photographs on Mobile Devices: Compact Neural Network Models for Tracking Wildlife in Distant Forest Areas
authors: K Ravishankar, K. Maniraj, Sivakumar Kaliyappan, G Pradeep, V Samuthirapandi, P Vijaya Vardan Reddy S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In light of the increasing expansion of biodiversity monitoring activities, we want real-time wildlife tracking devices that are robust, low-power, and deployable in forested areas located in remote areas. Cloud-based image processing pipelines are not ideal for remote wildlife surveillance due to unpredictable connectivity, high inference latency, and high energy consumption. The purpose of this study is to investigate the use of compact neural network models for processing images of forest wildlife using EdgeAI on mobile devices. Tiny-ViT, ShuffleNet, and MobileNet are examples of transformer-based and lightweight convolutional architectures used by the proposed technique. These architectures are then improved through pruning, quantisation, and knowledge distillation to achieve exact ondevice inference with minimal CPU overhead. The study evaluates different model compression approaches to assess their accuracy, latency, memory footprint, and energy efficiency in environments representative of the real world. We utilised a one-of-a-kind dataset of wildlife photographs featuring a variety of lighting conditions, occlusions, and species to benchmark. Tuned models have the potential to reduce storage size by 48 per cent, enhance inference performance by 37 per cent, and cut energy consumption by 30 per cent without compromising detection accuracy, according to experimental findings. To ensure data persistence and the adaptability of model updates, hybrid edge-cloud synchronisation enables opportunistic data upload whenever cloud connectivity is available. According to the findings of this study, compressed neural networks have the potential to enable mobile devices to distinguish species and follow their movements in a nearly real-time manner, even in situations when resources are limited. For conservation teams, ecologists, and forest management authorities, the results offer a sustainable, scalable, and costeffective alternative to power-hungry, dedicated edge hardware. The findings establish a framework for smart, mobile-based animal-tracking systems for conservation and environmental monitoring.

title: Deep Learning-Based Multi-Class Classification of Plant Leaf Diseases with CNN and VGG16
authors: Aadesh Shalikram Borey, Shirish Kulkarni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Plant leaf disease detection is a critical application in precision agriculture aimed at improving crop health and yield. It involves identifying infections in plant leaves and classifying them as healthy or diseased based on image analysis. Traditional methods are often time-consuming, manual due to interpretation of human and lack of early detection capabilities. This research addresses these limitations by employing DL models for automated disease detection. A labeled dataset from the Kaggle repository, containing 38 classes of plant leaves, was used and split into training, testing, and validation sets in a 60:20:20 ratio. Among the models, EfficientNet-B2 achieved the highest accuracy of 94%, outperforming others in terms of consistency and precision. The model is deployed through a user-friendly GUI for real-time prediction. This study demonstrates a scalable approach and opens future scope for enhancements using GANs and data augmentation.

title: Energy Efficient PV System Monitoring Using IoT and Hybrid World Cup Optimisation ANN Model
authors: P Srinivasan, T A Sivakumar, B Anushraj, Sunil S. Harakannanavar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) systems play an important part in sustainable energy generation. Still, they are very sensitive in detecting faults commonly caused by defective materials or inadequate protective mechanisms. Traditional fault detection techniques lack the responsiveness and adaptability needed for real-time fault mitigation. This work integrates IoT and machine learning to enable intelligent monitoring, fault detection, and optimisation of the PV system. Specifically, a comparison is made between Feed-Forward Neural Networks (FFNN) and World Cup Optimisation-based Neural Networks (WCO-NN) in terms of fault-detection efficiency, recovery time, implementation complexity, and real-time applicability. Simulation results indicate that WCO-NN outperforms FFNN, offering superior accuracy and faster response in identifying and recovering from PV system faults. The findings show how merging advanced hybrid technologies can improve the efficiency and reliability of modern photovoltaic plants.

title: EV Battery Health Prediction and Personalized Recommendations using LightGBM and SHAP
authors: Aditya K, Arya Bhat, Samarth G Ghodke, Satish Chikkamath, Kaushik M B, Suneeta V Budihal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are the best application of Renewable energy. Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are the heart of EVs. The ability to predict battery health has great importance in the field of battery technology and is a challenging task. By taking EV battery parameters as input, we can calculate the battery health via Machine Learning (ML) techniques, and to take this a step further, we take inputs from EV users and give them personalized recommendations based on their driving style and the climate of the area where they reside, for reliable and proper battery management. The dataset was created synthetically, keeping the public dataset as a base for the ML model to function in the worst-case scenario in real-life situations. The performance of our model is meticulously tested, and we got an accuracy that is accurate enough for practical use cases. We also tested the model on a hardware device for its robustness in real life. Index Terms—EV battery, Lithium-ion battery, Machine Learning, SoH, LightGBM

title: Research on an Improved YOLO Network Architecture for Small Object Detection
authors: Kun Fang, Gaori Yang, Qi Zhang, Lin Tan
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Small object detection remains difficult because targets occupy only a few pixels, contain sparse discriminative cues, and are easily submerged by background clutter. Conventional YOLO detectors still suffer from missed detections and unstable localization in dense small-object scenes. This paper presents an improved YOLO architecture for small object detection by jointly redesigning the backbone, feature aggregation neck, detection head, and training strategy. In the backbone, a spatial-depth conversion module and residual attention blocks are introduced to preserve fine-grained information while strengthening local saliency. In the neck, an adaptive feature aggregation (AFA) module replaces the conventional PAN pathway, combining channel reweighting with interpolation-free upsampling to enhance shallow features and retain high-frequency details. In the head, a high-resolution P2 detection branch and a dynamic taskaligned assignment mechanism are used to improve scale-aware label matching. During training and inference, slicing-aided hyper inference and a focalized IoU regression term further improve robustness in crowded scenes. Experiments designed around the COCO small-object subset and the VisDrone benchmark show gains of 4.2%–6.7% in mAP@0.5 and 8.9%-11.3% in small-object recall over the YOLOv8 baseline while maintaining real-time speed above 30 FPS. The proposed design offers an effective solution for UAV imagery, remote sensing analysis, and intelligent surveillance.

title: Multi-Class Anemia Classification from CBC Profiles Using a Feature-Refined and SHAP-Augmented Machine Learning Pipeline
authors: Shafwat Mahiya, Tahmina Hasan, Rezowan Sifat, Samiha Muntaha Mahin, Sumaya Rahman, Sara Karim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Anemia is a significant global health concern; therefore, it is necessary to have the quickest and most accurate tests. In the present work, a powerful machine learning model is trained and tested to recognize anemia and the informed subtype based on the Complete Blood Count (CBC) data is trained and tested. We conduct a complete comparative analysis of the different machine learning models, including classical models (XGBoost, LightGBM, AdaBoost, Gradient Boost, CatBoost), modern ensemble learning models (SVM, KNN, Naive Bayes, LR) and neural networks models (SLP, MLP, DNN). We apply a systematic feature engineering process to increase the efficiency of the models and focus on the most informative clinical markers, which eliminates an initial pool of 24 predictors to the 12 most informative features using a combination of the Chi-square test and Mutual Information. Under Interquartile Range (IQR), outlier elimination, Yeo-Johnson power adjustment and Min-max normalization all characteristics are pretreated. The performance of the models is strictly measured by accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and AUC. To address the black-box problem of clinical AI, we apply SHapley Additive explanations (SHAP) to both provide a global and local interpretability of model predictions of the LightGBM. SHAP analysis confirms the clinical relevance of characteristics such as hemoglobin, MCH, and HCT, enhancing the transparency and reliability of the model as a potential clinical tool as a multiclass diagnostic tool.

title: FinChronos: An Ensemble Model Framework for Stock Market Forecasting
authors: Snehal S. Bandgar, Tejaswini P. Pagare, Chinmaya B. Kolhe, Roshani Raut
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate short-term stock market forecasting remains challenging due to nonlinear price behaviour, volatility, and rapidly changing market conditions. Traditional analytical approaches struggle to capture these patterns, creating a need for robust data-driven models. This study presents FinChronos, an ensemble-based stock forecasting framework that integrates machine learning (Random Forest, SVR) and deep learning (LSTM) with more than ten technical indicators including RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, and EMA. Historical OHLCV data from Yahoo Finance covering multiple stocks and a 10-year duration were used to ensure reproducibility and model stability. The framework includes detailed preprocessing, feature extraction, hyperparameter tuning, and a weighted ensemble optimized using grid-search to minimize validation MAE. The model predicts stock closing prices 7 days ahead and is benchmarked against classical baselines such as ARIMA and Prophet. Experimental findings show that the ensemble model achieves the best overall performance (RMSE = 8.92, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.1787), outperforming individual models and statistical baselines. The results demonstrate that combining domain-specific indicators with ensemble learning significantly strengthens short-term predictive reliability. Practical implications for traders and investors are discussed, and future enhancements involving sentiment-aware transformers and reinforcement-learning-based trading systems are proposed.

title: Secure Systems for the Air Force with 5G Communication: Improving Network Resilience and Real-Time Data Exchange for High-Intensity Operations
authors: Chandra Doddapalli Ramachandra Reddy, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, C. Senthilkumar, T A Sundaravadivel, Sami Anand, Bharathi Panduri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Air Force operations in heavily contested locations use network-centric combat, upgraded sensor technology, and real-time data interchange. Latency, bandwidth, scalability, and endurance concerns plague traditional military communication systems in fast-paced conflict. This project improves network resilience and real-time data transfer for air force safety using 5 G communication technologies. Due to its high data throughput, low latency, mass device connectivity, and network slicing, fifth-generation wireless technology is suited for mission-critical security applications. 5G’s adaptive networking, intelligent traffic management, better encryption, and secure infrastructures may help the Air Force communicate in perilous situations. This study examines how 5G networks affect aviation’s human and unmanned systems, situational awareness, intelligence sharing, command and control, and real-time cooperation. This study examines the operational benefits, resilience, security, and system architecture of the Air Force 5 G secure communication framework. Tough missions require data security, quick decision-making, and efficient operations. Studies show that secure 5G-enabled technologies boost mission effectiveness and operational supremacy by improving communications reliability, responsiveness, and agility. This study covers the 5G’s effects on air force safety and robustness.

title: Generative AI for Synthetic Cyber Threat Simulation and Defense Training
authors: Bhavinkumar Jayswal, Naveen Doraiswami
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The growing complexity, magnitude, and occurrence of cyberattacks have revealed critical shortcomings in the conventional approaches to cybersecurity training which are typically based on fixed datasets and pre-determined attacks. Such strategies are not sufficient to equip security systems and practitioners with zero-day, adaptive, and emerging threats. To overcome this difficulty, this paper suggests an artificial intelligence (GenAI)-based model of synthetic cyber threat simulation and defence training, which would allow forming realistic and diverse, as well as dynamically changing environments of attacks without exposing sensitive organizational data. The proposed framework uses the developed large language models based on transformers, generative adversarial networks, and diffusion models to generate high-fidelity threat artifacts, including phishing schemes, patterns of malware behaviour, network intrusion evidence, and social engineering tactics. The system can optimise attack simulation through the integration of the reinforcement learning-inspired adversarial agents to simulate behaviour of the real-world threat actors in order to enhance the resilience and versatility of defensive mechanisms. Moreover, the architecture is designed with privacy-protective features like federated learning and differential privacy to enable sharing of threat intelligence in collaboration, without having to disclose the data. We perform automated feedback on defence performance on the basis of major cybersecurity indicators, such as detection rates, response time, resilience, and false-positive rates, in addition to adaptive training of security analysts and AI-based defence systems. Through experimentation, it is established that the suggested GenAI-based simulation environment will significantly increase the preparedness to threats, decrease training expenses, and better detectives than traditional rule-based cyber range models. The framework is scalable and can be used to deploy across cloud-edge environments, thus it can be applied to enterprise security operations centres, critical infrastructure protection, and autonomous cyber protection ecosystems. This publication underscores the radical nature of generative AI to redefine cybersecurity as a reactive protection to proactive, intelligence-driven resilience in cybersecurity, thus providing the basis of the next-generation cyber defence training platforms.

title: Optimized ECG Based Detection of Heart Abnormalities Using Federated Machine Learning
authors: Vedant Kesharia, Atharva Rajmane, Pranay Khandagle, Vedant Samant, Prachi Satam, Vinaya Sawant
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The condition of hypertensive heart disease is a significant issue health-wise and needs a quick and efficient diagnosis. Usually, conventional ECG reading is tedious and requires experienced experts. Although machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) have enhanced the classification of ECG, much of these methods require structured digital data and non-consideration of the practical issues of paper based ECG record, which complicates the expansion in practice. This study constructs an entire platform, which transforms printed ECG images into the digital format and classifies them automatically. The pipeline does grayscale conversion of data, removes all grids, extracts signals using contours and finally separates them into 12 ECG leads and converts them to one dimensional waveforms. Features are reduced and optimized, using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and t-SNE Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and t-SNE. A variety of machine learning models (SVM, KNN, Logistic Regression and XGBoost) and deep learning models (MLP and LSTM) are considered. Optuna is used to hyperparameter engineer performance. XGBoost has the most accurate result of 95.57%, compared to the individual models. In order to address the privacy issues and the deployment problem, this system employs a federated learning approach where it could train models in many sites and retains sensitive patient information secure and local. This research fills the gaps between traditional ECG graphs and the latest AI technology, as it is a valid and privacy aware technique of diagnosing hypertensive heart disease.

title: A Strategic Framework for Sustainable Technological Innovation in Volatile Global Environment
authors: Kritsana Lakkhongkha, Theerawut Tantiathimongkhon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: In an era of intensifying geopolitical fragmentation and rapid digital shifts, managing technological innovation requires a transition from traditional market-driven tactics to a more resilient, leadership-led strategic framework. This study aims to analyze the impact of Geopolitical Dynamics (GD) on the direction of Sustainable Technological Innovation (STI), investigate the pivotal role of Strategic Digital Leadership (SDL) in fostering Innovation Ecosystem Collaboration (IEC), and empirically validate the structural relationships between these macro-environmental factors and organizational responses. Utilizing Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) to test six hypothesized paths, the research demonstrates that while Geopolitical Dynamics pose significant challenges, they act as a primary catalyst <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\beta=0.64)$</tex> forcing firms to enhance technological resilience. The findings reveal that Strategic Digital Leadership is the strongest internal driver, significantly influencing ecosystem collaboration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.79$</tex>) and directly steering sustainable outcomes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta=0.38$</tex>). The structural model achieves an acceptable fit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CMIN} / \text{DF}=3.072, \text{GFI}=.815, \text{RMSEA}=.100$</tex>), confirming that long-term sustainability in technology management is achieved through the synergy of digital leadership, collaborative networks, and strategic agility. This framework provides a statistically validated roadmap for technology managers to navigate global volatility and maintain a competitive edge.

title: An Optimized SVM Ensemble Framework with Deep Feature Extraction for Sugarcane Crop Disease Identification
authors: Vishal Vijay Mahale, Omkar Pattnaik
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Sugarcane is one of the world’s most important cash crops, yet its productivity is threatened by multiple leaf diseases, resulting in significant yield losses. This paper presents an advanced framework for disease detection by combining deep feature extraction with a stacked ensemble of Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers. Features were extracted from leaf images using pre-trained VGG16 and ResNet50 models to ensure comprehensive representation of disease characteristics. Individually optimized SVM kernels (linear, RBF, polynomial, and sigmoid) were employed as base classifiers, and their outputs were integrated using XGBoost as a meta-learner.Experimental evaluation demonstrated that while individual kernels achieved high accuracy, with the linear kernel reaching 97.32% and the RBF kernel 97.15%, the stacked ensemble significantly outperformed them with a final accuracy of 98.45%. A comparative study with existing CNN and hybrid CNN-SVM approaches, which reported accuracies between 91% and 96.74%, confirmed the superiority of the proposed framework. These findings establish a robust and scalable solution for automated sugarcane disease detection, offering potential application in precision agriculture and real-time decision support systems.

title: Deep Generative Adversarial Networks for Automating the Coloring of Images
authors: Patiyuth Pramkeaw, Tawan Tapianthong, Setthawut Kitdanarakorn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Adding color to black and white pictures by hand is hard and takes a lot of time. This paper shows how deep learning techniques, such as the generative adversarial network (GAN) in the Pix2Pix architecture, can be used to create a system that automatically changes black and white pictures into color. This system learns how grayscale images (the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L$</tex> channel in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L A B$</tex> color space) and color information (the ab channels in the LAB color space) are connected by using the COCO Sample dataset. The generator model creates color information by using a U-Net structure with a ResNet18 backbone. The Discriminator model, which is based on PatchGAN, figures out how real the color images are. Experimental results show that the model can make color images that look real and believable to some degree. The discovery makes it possible to use this technology in new ways to fix old photos and do other graphic tasks. But there are still some problems with color accuracy for some types of objects and the amount of computing power needed for training. These problems show where future work should go.

title: Narrative Driven Layered Image Generation For Character and Scene Composition
authors: Srihari Krishna, Srilakshmana Amaresh, Shashank Udyavar, Shreyas Karthik, V.R Badri Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Recent advances in text-to-image generation have enabled highly accurate generation of images from text descriptions. However, most existing approaches involve holistic generation, wherein the entire image is generated at once. This offers limited control with reusability of individual image components. This paper introduces a framework for narrative-driven layered image generation. It takes short stories as input and converts them into visual representations by generating background and foreground layers separately, then combining them to form a coherent image. The proposed system uses a modular pipeline to extract character and scene descriptions from unstructured text, turning them into their corresponding visual representations, and integrating them into a unified scene. To improve realism, the ground regions are identified, and the foreground is appropriately scaled down and placed correctly. Experimental results show that this approach improves narrative consistency across multiple backgrounds and also improves reusability in contrast to conventional image generation models. The proposed framework demonstrates the effectiveness of multimodal pipelines for applications such as screenplay visualization, digital storytelling, and visual content generation.

title: SWOA: A Hybrid Sailfish–Whale Optimization Algorithm for Multi-UAV Path Planning in Complex Rescue Scenarios
authors: Xiaokun Wang, Chuanzhi Zang, Yetian Tian, Xiaoming Han, Xin Wang, Zhijia Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Multi-UAV cooperative path planning in complex rescue environments poses a critical yet challenging problem; existing methods exhibit three key limitations:(1) conventional metaheuristic algorithms suffer from premature convergence when navigating high-dimensional, obstacle-dense airspaces, (2) single-strategy optimizers fail to balance global exploration with local exploitation across varying terrain complexities, and (3) current multi-UAV formulations treat individual trajectories independently, neglecting joint makespan optimization and inter-vehicle coordination. To overcome these deficiencies, we propose SWOA—a Hybrid Sailfish-Whale Optimization Algorithm embedded within a unified multi-UAV planning framework. Specifically, SWOA introduces (1) a phase-adaptive switching mechanism that transitions from SFO-driven cooperative exploration to WOA-based spiral exploitation at the midpoint of evolution, (2) a greedy host-feeding acceptance criterion that enforces monotonic fitness improvement at each iteration, and (3) a joint trajectory encoding strategy that encodes all UAV paths into a single <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M D$</tex>-dimensional vector, enabling simultaneous task allocation and trajectory optimization. Extensive experiments on eight standard benchmark functions and three-dimensional rescue path planning scenarios demonstrate that SWOA consistently outperforms WOA, GWO, and PSO in convergence speed, solution quality, and obstacle avoidance capability.

title: An Improved Coati Optimization Algorithm via Cubic Chaotic Initialization and Cauchy Mutation
authors: Zhikai Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The original Coati Optimization Algorithm (COA) may suffer from uneven initial population distribution, weak global coverage at the early search stage, and a tendency to stagnate in local optima during late-stage exploitation when solving continuous optimization problems. To address these limitations, this study develops an enhanced COA by integrating Cubic chaotic initialization with a Cauchy mutation mechanism. First, the conventional random initialization strategy is replaced by the Cubic chaotic map so that the initial population becomes more evenly dispersed and more traversable across the search space. Second, after the exploitation phase, a Cauchy mutation operator with a heavy-tailed perturbation is introduced to improve the algorithm's ability to escape local basins of attraction. Without altering the original two-stage search structure of COA, the proposed method strengthens global exploration and local exploitation in a coordinated manner. The effectiveness of the improved method is examined on several representative benchmark functions and compared with the basic COA under identical experimental settings. The results show consistently better mean and standard-deviation values, together with competitive best values, indicating that the combination of Cubic chaotic initialization and Cauchy mutation can effectively improve the optimization performance of COA.

title: Kubeflow for Healthcare: Zero-Touch Model Retraining in HIPAA-Compliant Clouds
authors: Shalmali Joshi, Ramkumar Raju Djeabalaradjou, Rama Krishna Kumar Lingamgunta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Healthcare AI systems learn from previous data sets and models. This leads to a slow but continuous change in past data. These changes affect the accuracy and reliability of the model with time, which results in needing a retrain often. They require a lot of resource, even in HIPAA settings, and manually retraining them is lengthy and inconsistent. An architecture for a fully automated zero-touch model retraining framework using cloud-based HIPAA, Kubeflow and Kubernetes-native MLOps components is proposed in this paper. We propose an all-in-house solution that includes safe FHIR/DICOM uptake, statistical drift examinations, auto hyper parameter optimisation, robust governance check-ups on ML model performance and GitOps based deployment. Testing produced excellent AUROC improvement (up to 9.4%) on CT/MRI/CXR/EHR/large scale datasets and end to end training time 7x faster than before without loss of performance under varying patient loads. The system works accurately and reliably; that is, it has a 98.4% compliance success rate. The findings support that the proposed framework makes a clinically applicable solution for the continuous-learning AI systems in a safe and scalable manner.

title: An Integrated AI-Driven Optimization Framework for Energy-Aware, Low-Latency, and Reliable IoT Communication in Smart Healthcare Systems
authors: Karthickkameshwar S, Karan A, Dhandapani Samiappan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The attention to the IoT-based smart healthcare has raised the demand urgently to find ultra-low-energy communication protocols enabling continuous monitoring of patients and increasing their devices battery life. Conventional IoT protocols usually find it quite difficult to maintain energy efficiency, reliability and low latency at the same time. This paper suggests an AI-assisted optimization scheme of low-energy IoT protocol and combines six new algorithms: Adaptive Sleep Scheduling (ASS), Predictive Data Aggregation (PDA), Energy-Aware Routing Matrix (EARM), Contextual Priority Encoding (CPE), Reinforced Transmission Control (RTC) and Dynamic Load Balancing Scheduler (DLBS). The machine learning models dynamically tune the protocol parameters of the sensor data, the activity patterns of the patient, and the network conditions. The outcome of the simulations indicates that there are substantial energy savings and packet loss as well as enhancement of the latency performance, as opposed to the traditional methods. The suggested framework can offer scalable and smart solution of the next-generation smart healthcare networks, which can successfully support the long existence of devices, credible data transfer, and responsiveness tied to patients.

title: Maternal Health Risk Assessment with Interpretable Machine Learning: Evidence from Bangladesh
authors: Ahsanur Parul, Sajid Ahmed, Md Miskat Hossain, Karib Shams, Mohammad Rifat Ahmmad Rashid, Raihan Ul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Maternal health remains a critical public health concern, particularly in regions with limited healthcare resources. This study adopts a data-driven approach to predict maternal risk levels during pregnancy. Using real-world clinical data from a general hospital in Kurigram, Bangladesh-including age, blood pressure, blood sugar, body mass index (BMI), mental health indicators, and medical history-we developed predictive models to classify pregnancies as binary classification (high risk or low risk). Eight machine learning algorithms were trained and evaluated: Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Neural Network, XGBoost, Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Gaussian Naive Bayes, and Gradient Descent. Preprocessing included label encoding, min-max scaling, and feature selection through chi-square tests and ANOVA. To enhance interpretability, SHAP and LIME were applied to identify feature contributions. Among all models, Random Forest achieved the highest performance with an accuracy of 97%. Results highlight BMI, blood sugar, and preexisting diabetes as key predictors. The findings demonstrate how combining statistical analysis, machine learning, and explainable AI can support healthcare professionals in identifying high-risk pregnancies early and making evidence-based decisions for safer maternal care.

title: Advancements in AI-Based MPPT Algorithms for Optimizing Solar Power Systems in Electric Vehicle Charging Stations
authors: Ambrish Pati Tripathi, Abhimanyu Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rapid expansion in the infrastructure for electric vehicle (EV) charging together with the increase in integration with renewable energy sources is magnifying the requirements for highly efficient photovoltaic (PV) systems. For ensuring an optimal power point of the PV array under changing environmental conditions, an efficient tracking technique called Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) is required. This literature review offers a detailed exploration of the breadth of both conventional methods and advanced AI-assisted methods for MPPT, along with their evolution, capabilities, and limitations. Conventional methods, for example, P&O and INC, are widely used due to their simplicity but cannot track well under partially shaded situations amid fast solar irradiance fluctuations and nonlinear PV characteristics. AI-based techniques like artificial neural networks, deep learning architectures, and nature-inspired optimization algorithms bring an appreciable improvement in terms of tracking accuracy, adaptability, and avoided oscillations. These improvements will go a long way in improving the energy captured in the next generation of solar-powered EV charging systems and in making microgrids smarter. The paper has aimed to provide a compiled account of new advancements to realize the difference in performance trends and identify the significant research gaps lying in complex computations, real-time implementations, and scalability. From the present status of the field of research, this consolidated review provides a basis for furthering the development of more intelligent, hybrid, and highly efficient MPPT solutions for the growing number of modern PV applications.

title: IoT Based Smart Safety Mask for Real-Time Chemical Monitoring in Industrial Environments
authors: Dhana Sekar V, Dhayananth B, Kavin Kumar S, Hariharan R
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In industrial workplaces exposure to hazardous chemicals is a major concern, posing risks to the health and safety of the workers. The traditional personal protective equipment (PPE) has limited protection for the workers and lacks real-time monitoring of air quality and emergency alerts. This paper introduces an IoT-enabled smart safety mask system designed to continuously monitor airborne toxins and environmental conditions for workers in industry. The developed system uses an ESP32 microcontroller for processing and the sensing unit is integrated with chemical and environmental sensors including MQ-135 and MQ-2 modules for detecting gases like ammonia, volatile organic compounds, carbon monoxide and methane, a PM2.5 sensor for monitoring particulate matter in the air and a BME680 sensor for temperature and relative humidity measurement. To alert the workers in emergencies, a buzzer and a vibration actuator are incorporated with the hardware. In addition to local alerts, data is continuously logged to a cloud platform for real-time remote monitoring and further detailed analysis. The proposed system offers a scalable and cost-effective way to enhance the industrial safety of workers.

title: A Cloud-Edge Collaborative IoT Architecture for Intelligent Indoor Air Purification: System Design and Development
authors: Shutchapol Chopvitayakun, Mahasak Ketcham, Jedbodin Phutklam, Suthinee Wareeyan, Sonsophin Phonsanga, Piyanan Chueakutkhamin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Air pollution, particularly fine particulate matter (PM2.5), remains a major global health risk associated with respiratory and cardiovascular diseases. Although commercial air purifiers are widely used in indoor environments, most systems operate based solely on local sensing or manual user control without integrating real-time outdoor air quality intelligence. Recent advances in Internet of Things (IoT) architectures and lightweight messaging protocols enable adaptive environmental control systems capable of cloud–edge collaboration This study proposes the design and implementation of an intelligent air purification system integrating MQTT-based communication, n8n workflow automation, and an ESP32 edge controller. The system retrieves real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) data via external APIs, applies automated decision logic, and transmits control commands through an MQTT broker to an embedded purification unit consisting of a HEPA filter, negative ion generator, PM2.5 sensor, and ozone monitoring module. An ozone safety override mechanism ensures compliance with recommended exposure limits Operational and environmental data are logged for performance analysis. By combining cloud automation, publish–subscribe messaging, and edge-level safety control, the proposed architecture contributes a scalable and cost-effective framework for adaptive indoor air quality management.

title: Intelligent Task Scheduling for Humanoids with Novel Heuristic Prediction Control
authors: Subhradip Mukherjee, Lavanya K, Viji K, M Thilagaraj, Ashish Ramesh, Natansh N
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Humanoid robots are increasingly required in environments where human-like interaction, multitasking ability, and reliable automation are essential. However, the most of current humanoid systems are task specific. They do not have multipurpose perception and actuation skills, in real-world applications. It is essential to have a humanoid robot that can do a range of tasks, from basic object manipulation to guest help, while maintaining a high level of physical accuracy and responsiveness. This work presents the development of a multifunctional humanoid robot that integrates facial recognition, speech recognition, text-to-speech responses, and servo motor–driven gestural communication. The robot is further enhanced with the capability to move physically and shift objects, making it more versatile than conventional humanoids. Performance optimization is achieved through an advanced control strategy that significantly improves transient response characteristics. The hardware architecture consists of a Raspberry Pi 4 as the primary computational unit, paired with an Arduino Mega that that integrated with 12 servo motors. This hardware design allows distributed processing, ensuring low-latency gesture control. The integration of machine vision technique enables reliable facial recognition and speech processing. Experimental results demonstrates that the proposed humanoid robot achieves a 0.4-second reduction in settling time and over 12% improvement in overshoot control compared to existing approaches.

title: CB-DSIF: A Dual-Stream Interactive Fusion Model for Network Intrusion Detection Based on 1DCNN and BiGRU
authors: Tinghui Huang, Yu Wang, Yuming Qin
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Existing network intrusion detection methods often struggle to achieve further breakthroughs in detection accuracy due to incomplete feature extraction and inadequate fusion mechanisms. To address these issues, based on a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1DCNN) and a bidirectional gated recurrent unit (BiGRU), this paper proposes a novel dual-stream interactive fusion model (CB-DSIF). First, the model constructs a parallel feature extraction module, utilizing the 1DCNN to capture local features and the BiGRU to extract global features from network traffic. Second, to overcome the representation gap caused by conventional static feature concatenation, a dual-stream interactive fusion (DSIF) module is designed. This module maps heterogeneous features into a unified dimensional space and employs a dynamic gating mechanism based on a multi-layer perceptron (MLP) to adaptively evaluate and assign fusion weights. Finally, a 1 × 1 convolution is utilized to reshape the fused features, coupled with a global residual connection to preserve original low-level information. Extensive binary classification experiments on the CICIDS2017 and CSE-CICIDS2018 datasets demonstrate that CB-DSIF achieves exceptional detection performance, attaining high accuracies of 99.91% and 99.00%, respectively, explicitly outperforming representative baseline models.

title: Real-Time Face Recognition System for Age and Gender Prediction: A Robust CNN-Ensemble Approach with IMDB-WIKI and UTKFace Datasets
authors: Subrata Kumer Paul, Mahi Akter, Md. Parves Islam, Kaushik Ahamed, Rakhi Rani Paul, Dewan Nafiul Islam Noor, Md. Ekramul Hamid, A. F. M. Mirza Rashidul Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a cutting-edge real-time face recognition system that nails age and gender prediction by fusing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with ensemble learning techniques like Random Forest and Gradient Boosting. Featuring a Multi-Task Cascaded Convolutional Network (MTCNN) for precise face detection and a CNN with three Conv2D layers (64, 128, and 256 filters), MaxPooling, dropout (0.5), and dense layers. It delivers an impressive 97.8% gender accuracy and a 2.9 -year Mean Absolute Error (MAE) for age, trained on the extensive IMDB-WIKI and UTKFace datasets. With a swift 25 ms inference time at 30 FPS, it adeptly handles real-world challenges such as tricky lighting, varied poses, and diverse demographics, showcasing robust performance across complex scenarios. The system’s architecture, optimized for efficiency, leverages a $224 \times 224 \times 3$ input layer, ensuring compatibility with highresolution video feeds, while ensemble methods enhance prediction reliability by mitigating overfitting. Its practical deployment is validated through rigorous testing, making it a standout performer in the field. Applications shine in retail for tailored ads, security for access control, healthcare for patient insights, and smart cities for crowd analysis, promising a smarter and more connected future.

title: Zero-Shot Traffic Classification for Unseen Applications Using Convolutional Neural Network and Word2Vec
authors: Prerna Awati, Aishwarya A, Nishchita N, Sonu Vaishnavi, Uday Kulkarni, Veena Hunasimarad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Encrypted and Virtual Private Network (VPN)-based traffic increasingly undermines the effectiveness of traditional supervised classification models, especially when new or previously unseen applications emerge . This work presents a zero-shot traffic classification framework that integrates symbolic feature quantization, Word2Vec-based semantic embeddings, a lightweight ResNet-1D encoder, and Prototypical Networks for similarity-driven prediction . Flow-level attributes from the ISCX Scenario-B dataset are transformed into symbolic tokens, embedded using Word2Vec, and projected into a discriminative latent space through a ResNet-1D encoder. Classification is performed by computing Euclidean distances between query embeddings and class prototypes, enabling the model to recognize unseen traffic types without retraining.Experiments on 10,782 samples achieve an overall accuracy of 65.83%, demonstrating strong generalization to unseen applications. The results highlight the promise of zero-shot learning as a scalable and effective approach for encrypted traffic analysis in modern network environments .

title: Optimizing Phase-Scheduling with Throughput Trade-offs in AQFP Digital Circuits
authors: Robert S. Aviles, Peter A. Beerel
abstract: Adiabatic Quantum-Flux-Parametron (AQFP) logic is a promising emerging superconducting technology for ultralow power digital circuits, offering orders of magnitude lower power consumption than CMOS. However, AQFP scalability is challenged by excessive buffer overhead due to path balancing technology constraints. Addressing this, recent AQFP works have proposed design solutions to reduce path balancing overhead using phase-skipping and phase-alignment. Phase-skipping is a circuit-level technique that allows data transfer between AQFP gates clocked with non-consecutive clock phases. In contrast, phase-alignment is an architectural approach involving repeating input patterns to allow data transfer between AQFP gates across multiples of full clock cycles. While both techniques individually mitigate the area overhead of path-balancing, they have not yet been jointly explored. In this work, we present the first clock phase scheduling algorithm that combines phase-skipping and phase-alignment. We first present a minimum area method that on average, achieves a 25% area reduction compared to phase-skipping alone and a 11% reduction compared to phase alignment. We then extend the method to enforce a target throughput, enabling efficient area-performance trade-offs. With our throughput constrained optimization, we achieve on average 6.8% area savings with a 2.62x increased throughput compared to the state-of-the-art phase-aligned method.

title: Hybrid VAE-PSO Strategy for Rapid and Reliable Inverse Design of Square-Loop FSS
authors: Chenyin Yu, Kexin Yang, Xibao Wu, Yiqun Wang, Qili Chen, Wenbai Chen, Nan Ma
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a hybrid deep learning framework for the efficient forward prediction and inverse design of square-loop frequency selective surfaces (FSS) to enhance patch antenna performance. Traditional design methodologies typically rely on exhaustive full-wave simulations and manual optimization, which are computationally expensive and time-consuming. To address these challenges, a forward prediction model integrating Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks is developed to accurately map geometric parameters to transmission characteristics. A hybrid inverse design strategy is introduced by synergizing a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO). In this framework, the VAE generates physically viable initial candidates from target spectra, while PSO performs localized refinement to ensure high-precision matching. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed forward model achieves exceptional fidelity in resolving resonant behaviors. For inverse design, the VAE-PSO strategy attains a Mean Square Error (MSE) of 0.023, representing a 42.5% improvement in design accuracy and a 65.5% increase in convergence efficiency compared to PSO. The framework is validated through a microstrip antenna application, where the designed FSS significantly improves the gain in the 4.2–6.2 GHz range while maintaining the operating bandwidth. This work provides a robust and automated tool for the rapid synthesis of complex electromagnetic structures.

title: All-fiber-based interplay of bunched, noise-like and dissipative solitons in nearly zero-dispersion EDFL
authors: Chun-Yen Lin, Maolin Dai, Chih-Hsien Cheng, Yu-Chieh Chi, Kouichi Akahane, Atsushi Matsumoto, Sze. Y. Set, Shinji Yamashita, Gong-Ru Lin
abstract: To meet the demand for the generation and control of pulsed photon waveforms for future secure and quantum applications, the manipulated interplay of passive mode-locking mechanisms between random soliton bunching and noise-like pulsation in a self-initiated erbium-doped fiber laser (EDFL) configured at zero-dispersion regime is demonstrated herein. By managing intracavity dispersion using different-length segments of positive-dispersion-compensated fibers (DCFs), the EDFL cavity can be operated in a negative dispersion regime, producing a conventional soliton with a nearly transform-limited pulse width of 405 fs and a spectral bandwidth of 6.7 nm. By lengthening the DCF from 29.2 m to 55.4 m for approaching the nearly and exactly zero-dispersion condition, the mode-locking mechanism can be switched among conventional soliton, bunched soliton, dissipative soliton, stretched and noise-like pulsation states through intracavity polarization state adjustment, as originated from the intracavity birefringence induced by a twisted single-mode fiber. With inserting 29.2-m-long DCF, the original chirped soliton pulsewidth is shortened from 220 fs to 109 fs accompanied with its linewidth broadening from 12.3 nm to 25.1 nm under a time-bandwidth product (TBP) of 0.32. Lengthening the DCF to 55.4 nm obtains the dissipative soliton, while detuning the birefringence within 4° of polarization orientation enables the switching between the 75-fs stretched pulsation and 150-fs noise-like pulsation with strong and weak chirp.

title: Design of Passive Optical Network Based O-RAN X-haul: A Systematic Approach
authors: Atri Mukhopadhyay, Dinesh Korukonda, Goutam Das
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The development of high data rate communication technologies has resulted in cell densification, which in turn has led to the development of centralized radio access networks (C-RANs) followed by open radio access networks (O-RANs). The O-RAN segregates the base station into three logical entities; the central unit (CU), the distributed unit (DU) and the radio unit (RU). The CU, DU and RU require low latency, low jitter and high data rate connections for seamless operation, which is known as X-haul. A passive optical network (PON) is a potential solution for X-haul design. However, conventional PON uplink protocols are not inherently suitable for X-haul requirements. The packetization procedure of PON introduces jitter to the X-haul bit stream. Further, the delay requirements of the X-haul limit the number of sources that can be connected to the X-haul. Advanced features like coordinated multipoint requires synchronization among the different X-haul bit streams as well. Therefore, in this paper, we develop an optimal uplink system that allows PON to be used as an X-haul connection technology. The proposal maximizes the throughput of the PON while conforming to the delay and synchronization requirements. Moreover, the proposal nullifies the jitter introduced by the PON scheduler. We have performed extensive simulations for verifying our results.

title: Event-Based Secure Control for Cyber-Physical Power Systems Under Stochastic DoS Attacks
authors: Rongni Yang, Ruifeng Zhang, Yanzheng Zhu, Yi Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: In this paper, a new dynamic event-triggered secure control problem for the cyber-physical power systems (CPPSs) under stochastic denial-of-service (DoS) attacks is studied. Particularly, the considered DoS attacks are characterized by the stochastic property with periodic occurrences, such that a class of discrete-time cyclic dwell-time Markov systems with both stable and unstable subsystems is presented to model the concerned CPPSs under these DoS attacks. Meanwhile, a novel dual-mode dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) that works in both non-attack and attack scenarios is introduced to save limited network resources. Next, the stochastic stability and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> performance analysis are carried out for the augmented systems by establishing a cyclic piecewise Markov Lyapunov function (CPMLF) with greater design freedom, and the explicit controller is designed to ensure the security of the considered CPPSs. Finally, the applications of 1,3-area CPPSs under stochastic DoS attacks with periodic occurrences are illustrated to verify the effectiveness and advantage of the proposed control method.

title: Stability-Aware Communication via Weighted Potential Games for Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction
authors: Bin Wang, Wei Liu, Wei She, Fengxiao Tang, Nei Kato
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Multi-agent trajectory prediction relies heavily on information exchange, where incomplete state sharing, limited interaction frequency, and communication uncertainty readily induce collaborative instability. Current approaches primarily rely on heuristic interaction rules, failing to explicitly quantify the trade-off between communication overhead and prediction gains. This implicit assumption often leads to suboptimal information exchange and lacks evolutionary stability for communication strategies in dynamic traffic environments. Specifically, we propose the Stability-aware Communication via Weighted Potential Games (SC-WPG) framework. First, we leverage the continuous-time dynamics of Liquid Neural Networks (LNNs) to design a dynamic phase-coupling mechanism that aligns the temporal evolution of agent states, alleviating strategy asynchrony. Second, we establish a liquid state-sharing pool to exchange compact latent representations encoded from local observations, while promoting efficient implicit state fusion through spatio-temporal attention mechanisms. Finally, communication scheduling is formulated as a weighted potential game to explicitly quantify the trade-off between gains and costs. A stability-aware weight adaptation mechanism based on latent-state variation is introduced to regularize communication behavior under dynamic constraints. Experimental results on the NGSIM and HighD datasets show that SC-WPG achieves the lowest average root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.89 and 0.55, respectively. These results correspond to improvements of 10.1% and 6.8% over the strongest competing baselines under the same communication-constrained setting.

title: IEBench: Benchmarking Unlearning Identity-Event Entangled Concepts for MLLM-Based Systems in Agentic Edge Networks
authors: Zhaopan Xu, Pengfei Zhou, Weidong Tang, Jiaxin Ai, Wangbo Zhao, Kai Wang, Wenqi Shao, Xiaojiang Peng, Sicheng Zhao, Hongxun Yao, Kaipeng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in multimodal applications. Their reliance on large-scale, internet-sourced data raises significant concerns regarding security and privacy. These concerns are especially critical when MLLM-based systems are deployed in agentic edge networks, where limited computational resources make retraining impractical for addressing privacy and security issues. Machine Unlearning (MU) has emerged as a critical technique for dealing with these issues. It enables the selective removal of targeted information from pre-trained models without costly retraining. However, the evaluation of MU for MLLMs in agentic edge networks remains inadequate. Existing benchmarks focus narrowly on entities and overlook unlearning of broader visual concepts and their semantic couplings. To bridge this gap, we introduce IEBench. This benchmark enables thorough assessment of MU for MLLM-based systems in agentic edge networks. IEBench features a fictitious dataset of identity entities and corresponding event scenes. It supports the evaluation of unlearning across these distinct yet entangled concepts. We use IEBench to evaluate eight MU methods and reveal their individual strengths and weaknesses. Our findings show that unlearning one concept can unintentionally degrade performance on related concepts within the same image. We term this challenge cross concept interference. Furthermore, we demonstrate the difficulty of unlearning identity and event concepts simultaneously. We also explore potential methods to mitigate these conflicting objectives.

title: Robust Distributed UAV Location and Beamforming Optimization via Multi-Agent RL and Convex Design in Multi-UAV Networks
authors: Zain Ali, Saud Althunibat, Rula Ammuri, Mazen Omar Hasna, Khalid Qaraqe
abstract: This paper addresses the joint problem of distributed UAV placement and multi-antenna beamforming for downlink Rate-Splitting Multiple Access (RSMA) in multi-UAV networks with uncertain user locations. We propose a practical two-stage hybrid solution that (i) learns robust, decentralized UAV placements via a QMIX-based multi-agent deep Q-learning framework that encodes the environment as fixed-resolution multi-channel spatial maps and employs CoordConv to preserve absolute spatial information, and (ii) optimizes per-UAV multi-antenna precoding via a tractable semidefinite-programming (SDP) relaxation augmented with successive convex approximation and difference-of-convex programming to handle nonconvex log-terms and rank-1 constraints. The learning stage provides centralized training with fully decentralized execution and yields a single trained agent that generalizes across varying user counts and cluster configurations without retraining. The convex precoding stage enforces user rate, power and RSMA SIC constraints and is solved efficiently using an interior-point SDP solver. Extensive simulations (varying user counts, UAV counts, grid resolutions and cluster patterns) demonstrate fast convergence of the proposed QMIX placement (convergence in  4×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> training rounds in our setup), substantial sum rate gains over two competitive benchmarks, and favorable scalability and computational profiles.

title: Two-Dimensional Constant Envelope Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing
authors: Hao Chen, Yue Xiao, Lilin Dan, Xiaobei Liu, Erry Gunawan, Yong Liang Guan
abstract: Constant envelope orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (CE-OFDM) has attracted growing interest as a competitive low-power waveform for future wireless systems, since it maintains a zero peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) through phase modulation. However, conventional CE-OFDM relies on an independent, one-dimensional (1D) block structure, which inherently precludes the exploitation of temporal correlation, thereby compromising detection robustness. Against this backdrop, we propose a novel two-dimensional (2D) CE-OFDM framework that orchestrates joint data mapping across the time–frequency domain through a 2D Hermitian-symmetric constraint. Building upon this architecture, we conceive a low-complexity 2D phase receiver designed to harness the enhanced time–frequency diversity. Simulation results substantiate that the proposed 2D CE-OFDM scheme achieves superior bit error rate (BER) performance compared to its 1D counterpart over both additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) and frequency-selective fading channels, while imposing only comparable computational complexity.
