Tests were run on a locally set up Kubernetes environment, where pod crashes were manually triggered multiple times.

title: Precision Optimization of Personnel Localization Based on a Grey Prediction Model in Complex Underground Scenarios
authors: Lijun Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In order to solve the issue of invalid zero-value location data in underground coal mine UWB personnel positioning systems under long-range coverage scenarios, which is caused by complex environmental interference and communication link interruptions. The paper proposes a positioning accuracy optimization method based on a grey prediction model. This method employs a two-stage logic of pre-filtering of data validity and grey model prediction. First, invalid data is filtered out, and then the grey model' s predicted values are used to compensate for missing data that is abnormal. Experiments show that this method effectively handles invalid zero-value data. Compared to the Kalman filtering and gray prediction models without data filtering, this method can control the error of invalid data within 1 meter, significantly improving the stability and accuracy of the positioning system and providing technical support for personnel safety management in underground coal mines.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning Defense Against Jamming in Intelligent Wireless Edge Systems
authors: Poojitha D, Syed Abrar C, Ishvarya G, U Kumaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Wireless edge networks, crucial for real-time intelligent device communication, are highly susceptible to sophisticated jamming attacks that severely reduce reliability; traditional, rule-based defenses fail against dynamic jammers. To provide an intelligent solution, we propose a novel reinforcement learning-based adaptive defense framework utilizing the Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) algorithm, enhanced by curriculum-guided training, which models the defense as a continuous decision-making process to dynamically optimize power control and channel selection based on observed interference and signal strength. Experimental results in a simulated IEEE 802.11 environment confirm the framework’s superior performance, achieving a 250% improvement in SNR and a 70% reduction in packet loss (more than doubling throughput) compared to baseline methods, demonstrating robust resilience against both random and reactive jammers and confirming its effectiveness as a scalable foundation for autonomous anti-jamming protection

title: Comprehensive Approach to Cloud Security Using Trusted Mobile Cloud and Computing Technologies
authors: Vishal Mehra, M. Prashanthi, Amit Sahu, M Ramesh Naik, Seema Raj, Anup Singh Negi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The limitations of cell phones can be addressed by mobile cloud computing (MCC), but it does not provide the requisite degree of security and processing performance assurances. In this part, we offer a hybrid approach to trustworthy MCC by integrating security precautions with efficient probabilities monogrammed computing. The proposed approach enhances computing performance with a better keyword searching technique and strengthens MCC security with authorization and authentication. An innovative stochastic monogrammed computing approach is developed for achieving efficient and accurate retrieval of information by utilizing indexed synonyms searching using weighted terms and rankings for keywords. Using a password-based authorised exchange of keys mechanism using Chaotic Maps additionally offers safe authentication and access control. Relevant multi-keyword-based and fuzzy keyword-based search methods were found to be inferior to the proposed Efficient Computation along with Authentication and Authorization in Mobile Cloud Computing (ECAAMC) model in terms of time to computation, efficiency, and performance. The results show that ECAAMC provides a safer computing environment, which enhances data security, boosts search performance, and decreases destructive behaviour. This hybrid approach provides a solid framework for safeguarding and improving MCC, making it a viable choice for scenarios involving mobility and limited resources. Outperforming FKWBS and MKWBS, the suggested ECAAMC method achieves the best accuracy at 93.2%.

title: An Optimized Framework for E-catalog Categorization in Large-Scale Heterogeneous Datasets
authors: Meenal N. Pande, Sanjay E. Yedey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In the realm of digital commerce, product classification stands as a pivotal determinant of organizational success, particularly given the escalating volume of data, imperative for enhanced customer experience, and optimized operational efficiencies. Persistent challenges to efficient classification stem from the inherent complexity of product-associated data and the dynamic nature of market environments. This research provides a comparative analysis of these classificatory impediments, detailing methodologies and technologies employed to address issues such as data inconsistencies, high-dimensional data, and dynamic attribute variability. Furthermore, the study investigates the impact of data integration and real-time processing on product classification outcomes, quantifying the correlative strength between these factors and classification efficacy. The proposed approach leverages Python software, integrating K-Means clustering to enhance classification by grouping products based on salient features like price and ratings. A Hybrid Feedforward-Recurrent Network (HFRN) model is introduced, demonstrating superior accuracy and reliability in categorizing products (e.g., 'Premium Electronics,' 'Trendy Fashion Items') through feature extraction and sequence modeling applied to pre-clustered data. This hybrid methodology achieves a 99% accuracy rate, outperforming conventional models such as Fully Connected Neural Networks, Support Vector Machines, and Logistic Regression by 3.39%.

title: Online Learning-Based Beamforming for Time-Varying Near-Field XL-MIMO Channels
authors: Jinsong Ruan, Hongxia Miao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Deep learning beamforming for near-field XLMIMO is typically trained offline and degrades under time-varying channels. We propose a supervised online adaptation framework with an experience replay buffer to update the beamforming network stably without exploration overhead. Simulations show consistent gains over a fixed offline model under abrupt channel distribution changes.

title: Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Control of an ARVIN Bidirectional DC–DC Converter for V2G/G2V Applications
authors: MARZOUG Marouan, GHAFFARI Fakhreddine, JOUILI Mabrouk, BACHA Faouzi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The widespread deployment of electric vehicles (EVs) and the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources are driving the development of advanced power electronic interfaces capable of bidirectional energy exchange. Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) technologies allow EV batteries to operate as distributed energy storage units, contributing to grid stability, peak shaving, and ancillary services. In such applications, bidirectional DC–DC converters must provide high voltage conversion ratios, fast transient response, and reliable DC-bus voltage regulation under highly variable operating conditions. This paper proposes a Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Control (STA) strategy for DC-bus voltage regulation of an ARVIN bidirectional DC–DC converter. The proposed approach combines an analytical feed forward duty-cycle computed from the steady-state voltage conversion ratios with a STA-based feedback term, resulting in a hybrid feed forward-feedback control structure. The performance of the proposed controller is evaluated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations under step changes in power demand and bidirectional operating-mode transitions. The obtained results demonstrate fast transient response, reduced voltage ripple, and strong robustness against disturbances, confirming the suitability of the proposed control strategy for V2G/G2V applications.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Business-Driven Variational Autoencoder Models for Stable Customer Segmentation in E-Commerce
authors: Rakshita Janadri, Srushti Savadi, Devika Desai, Vinay Talekar, Guruprasad S Konnurmath, Channabasappa Muttal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: E-commerce platforms generate huge streams of behavioral and transactional data. Therefore, sharp customer segmentation becomes of critical importance in personalizing and guiding marketing efforts. However, conventional methods of clustering are likely to fail when capturing nonlinear behavioral patterns and provide unstable fragile segments. In this regard, the present work contrasts three VAE-based architectures for unsupervised customer segmentation: lean, optimized baseline VAE; business-oriented, enriched with domain-specific features VAE; and a more advanced VAE that combines feature prioritization with stability-aware clustering. All these models will be assessed within one unique experimental framework regarding silhouette score, Davies–Bouldin index, Calinski–Harabasz score, and Adjusted Rand Index. The results point out that enhanced VAE gives the most coherent and repeatable clusters, outperforming baseline models in terms of separation quality and stability. That is, fusing business-driven features with deep representation learning secures actionable and more reliable customer segments for real-world e-commerce personalization.

title: Miniaturized Multi-Band THz Microstrip Patch Antenna for Next-Generation 6G Mobile Networks and Spectroscopic Applications
authors: Mohammed Rakibul Hoque, Nirzar Barua, Mohammad Abid, Most. Tajrima Akter Oishee, Chandan Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This paper presents a miniaturized, highperformance microstrip patch antenna designed for narrowband applications in the terahertz (THz) frequency range. The proposed rectangular patch is fabricated on a $4.6 \mu \mathrm{~m}$ quartz substrate with a thin copper layer, achieving a compact footprint of $97.27 \mu \mathrm{~m} \times 76.07 \mu \mathrm{~m}$. The antenna resonates at 1 THz, $1.5 \mathrm{THz}, 1.89 \mathrm{THz}$, and 2.13 THz, with return loss values of $-29.4 \mathrm{~dB},-38.4 \mathrm{~dB},-31.2 \mathrm{~dB}$, and -26.4 dB, demonstrating excellent impedance matching and efficient radiation. Optimized over the $\mathbf{1 - 2. 4} \mathbf{~ T H z}$ spectrum, it enables high-resolution spectroscopy and chemical sensing for precise identification of gases, explosives, and pharmaceuticals. In imaging applications, it supports non-destructive and through-barrier inspection, benefiting security screening, semiconductor diagnostics, and packaging analysis. Moreover, its performance in the $\mathbf{1 - 3 ~ T H z}$ range allows ultra-fast, short-range wireless communications with terabit-per-second data rates, making it suitable for future 6 G and beyond networks. The combination of compact size, multi-resonant operation, and high efficiency positions this antenna as a versatile solution for sensing, imaging, and next-generation THz wireless applications.

title: Blockchain-Based Cybersecurity Solutions for Decentralized Network Management
authors: Bharathi Ravishankar, S. Sivagami, M. Vijaya Maheswari, V Sujatha, Pappala Balaakrishna, Dinesh Babu M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cybersecurity solutions that are based on blockchain are developing into a revolutionary method of an increase in safeguarding of a decentralized network management. Conventional centralised security systems are not always suitable in distributed infrastructures because of the single point of failure, data manipulation, lack of transparency and much more. Due to its irreversible records, consensus systems, and distributed confirmation, cryptocurrency will provide a higher degree of credibility and resilience against an ever-evolving array of online dangers. The use of smart agreements for remote management and artificially intelligent identification of anomalies strengthen privacy by making auditing data patterns and storage immutable. Additionally, cryptography software provides security for personal information in dissimilar settings, and decentralised management of identities provide identification that is favourable to confidentiality. It becomes even more impervious to distributed denial of service (DDoS) and info quality intrusions when cryptocurrency is coupled with advanced protection technologies, which removes the possibility of insider’s assaults. Moreover, bitcoin's democratic, accessible, and verified protections might enable distributed platforms to safely self-regulate, fostering responsibility and trustworthiness in vital sectors like medical care, insurance, and logistics systems. This combination is a major step towards building contemporary safety systems that can withstand and react to new threats. Under normal circumstances, the suggested system had a detection accuracy of 93% and under stress, it had an audit trust score of 90%.

title: Improved Estimation of Wind Turbine Bearing Lifespan via Advanced Machine Learning Models
authors: Ameni JELLALI, Hamida MAATALLAH, Kaïs OUNI, Lilia EL AMRAOUI
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Accurately predicting the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of wind turbine bearings is essential for enhancing system reliability and minimizing maintenance costs in renewable energy infrastructures. Building on our earlier work where Linear Regression unexpectedly yielded highly precise RUL estimations, this study explores whether more advanced machine learning models can deliver equal or superior performance. We conduct an extensive comparative analysis involving both traditional and cutting-edge methods, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Leveraging features such as temperature, viscosity related damage, and dynamic load, the models are assessed using RMSE and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> metrics. The results reveal that ensemble learning techniques substantially outperform linear models, achieving near-perfect predictive accuracy. These findings underscore the effectiveness and scalability of modern machine learning approaches for predictive maintenance in wind turbine applications. keywords: Remaining Useful Life, Wind Turbine, Machine Learning, Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Predictive Maintenance.

title: Joint-Level Intent Recognition of Upper Limb based on XGBoost and Viterbi Decoding
authors: Qi Zhang, Yong Tao, Zikang Xiao, Xingsong Wang
abstract: sEMG-based upper-limb motion intent recognition is challenging due to the high flexibility of upper-limb joints. Existing methods that classify overall actions often struggle to generalize to unseen movements, lack joint-level interpretability, and suffer from prediction jitter in real-time frame-level outputs.To address these issues, this study proposes an upper-limb intent recognition framework based on XGBoost and Viterbi decoding. Complex movements are decomposed into two functional sub-models for the shoulder and elbow joints. An automatic activation detection method based on time–frequency energy changes is introduced, and Viterbi decoding is applied to improve temporal continuity.Experimental results show that the proposed framework can effectively recognize unseen complex movements, achieving an average accuracy of 94% in typical daily activities. Compared with raw frame-level predictions, Viterbi-based temporal optimization significantly reduces label switching frequency and improves prediction stability.

title: Trust and Interaction-Driven Resilient Distributed Secure Learning Algorithm and Its Application
authors: Fuxi Niu, Xiaohong Nian, Te Ma, Shiling Li, Yong Chen, Maolong Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of distributed non-convex secure learning for multi-agent systems in non-secure and non-trusted environments, where only local datasets are available. To enhance the system's security and privacy during information exchange, we propose a trust and interaction-driven robust adaptive learning algorithm. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed algorithm effectively protects agent privacy while mitigating the impact of various types and arbitrary numbers of non-cooperative agents, without incurring additional communication or computational overhead. Finally, experiments on the image recognition task validate the algorithm's performance, showing that it outperforms existing methods in terms of security and privacy.

title: Real-Time Continuous Object Segmentation Using High Temporal Resolution Event Cameras via Random-Coupled Empowering Neural Network Optimized by Chef Leader Algorithm
authors: C Selvan, Soumyalatha Naveen
abstract: The concept of event-based cameras has emerged as possibly helpful solution to the problem of high-temporal and low-latency capturing dynamic scenes and is therefore extremely applicable to the continuous object segmentation problem. Scarcity and asynchronous nature of event information, though, pose severe issues in feature description and precision of delineation. The given work proposes a systematic deep learning model on continuous object segmentation in event-based vision that is composed of the preprocessing, feature extraction, attention modeling, and optimization strategies. The framework uses EfficientNet-B3 to achieve the spatial features extraction, the attention mechanism to boost the temporal representation, the Random Coupled Empowering Neural Network (RCENN), to boost the interaction of features, and Chef Leader Optimization (CLO) to carry out the stable and effective optimization of the parameters. The proposed model is evaluated on EOS event-based data with regards to classification and segmentation. The experimental results indicate that the framework has excellent segmentation performance with the Intersection over Union (IoU) of 0.82, Dice coefficient of 0.86 and mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) of 0.81 and the classification performance is excellent. Ablation experiments and comparison are also effective in demonstrating the contribution of each aspect towards the enhancement of performance. Although the results are promising, the performance relies on the properties of datasets and experimental conditions and is yet to be demonstrated on more diverse real-life situations. Generally, the framework presented is a scalable and efficient solution to the event-based segmentation of dynamic environments.

title: Reactive Motion Generation via Phase-varying Neural Potential Functions
authors: Ahmet Tekden, Dimitrios Kanoulas, Aude Billard, Yasemin Bekiroglu
abstract: Dynamical systems (DS) methods for Learning-from-Demonstration (LfD) provide stable, continuous policies from few demonstrations. First-order dynamical systems (DS) are effective for many point-to-point and periodic tasks, as long as a unique velocity is defined for each state. For tasks with intersections (e.g., drawing an “8”), extensions such as second-order dynamics or phase variables are often used. However, by incorporating velocity, second-order models become sensitive to disturbances near intersections, as velocity is used to disambiguate motion direction. Moreover, this disambiguation may fail when nearly identical position–velocity pairs correspond to different onward motions. In contrast, phase-based methods rely on open-loop time or phase variables, which limit their ability to recover after perturbations. We introduce Phase-varying Neural Potential Functions (PNPF), an LfD framework that conditions a potential function on a phase variable which is estimated directly from state progression, rather than on open-loop temporal inputs. This phase variable allows the system to handle state revisits, while the learned potential function generates local vector fields for reactive and stable control. PNPF generalizes effectively across point-to-point, periodic, and full 6D motion tasks, outperforms existing baselines on trajectories with intersections, and demonstrates robust performance in real-time robotic manipulation under external disturbances.

title: A Three-Stage Vision-Based Transformer for Channel Gain Map Construction
authors: Tongyi Wei, Kun Tang, Hao Wu, Wenjie Feng, Wenquan Che, Quan Xue
abstract: The channel knowledge map (CKM) is regarded as a digital twin of the wireless channel and provides location-specific channel information for environment-aware wireless communications. The channel gain map (CGM) is a typical form of CKM that stores spatially indexed channel gains over a target area, enabling fast link scheduling, coverage planning, and beam selection. Existing CGM construction methods mainly rely on propagation modeling or data-driven spatial completion, which can be computationally expensive or may inadequately capture site-specific propagation characteristics, thereby limiting prediction accuracy. To address this issue, we propose a three-stage Transformer-based CGM construction framework for predicting channel gains at arbitrary locations. In the first stage, the city map is discretized into grids, and the channel gain values within each grid are normalized to facilitate continuous spatial prediction. In the second stage, a Transformer-based coarse CGM prediction method is proposed to extract environmental features from the city map and generate a coarse CGM. In the third stage, a lightweight refinement network is designed, which leverages a small number of sparse measurements to refine the coarse predictions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches across all performance metrics. In particular, compared with the existing optimal scheme, the proposed method achieves 32.81% and 20.63% reductions in normalized mean square error (NMSE) and root mean square error (RMSE), respectively, while improving the structural similarity index (SSIM) and peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) by 2.08% and 3.71%, respectively.

title: Meta-CT: A Meta-Learning-Based Spectrum Sensing Scheme Using CNN-Transformer for Low-Altitude Intelligent Networks
authors: Mingyue Si, Wensheng Zhang, Bin Qi, Xiaotian Zhou, Jian Sun, Haixia Zhang
abstract: In low-altitude intelligent networks (LAINs), spectrum sensing is crucial to ensure efficient spectrum utilization especially for distributed unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) users. However, the dynamic and complex electromagnetic environments in LAINs pose new challenges to spectrum sensing. To address specific challenges such as sparse data, low signal-to noise ratio (SNR), and dynamic spectrum conditions in LAINs, this paper proposes a novel spectrum sensing scheme, which integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Transformers in a meta-learning framework, namely Meta-CT scheme. The proposed Meta-CT scheme adopts a meta-learning architecture, in which CNNs and Transformers are jointly integrated as the base learner. By combining convolutional operations and self attention mechanisms, the weak features of low-SNR signals can be extracted. Furthermore, the proposed scheme can achieve spectrum sensing with sparse data by leveraging the inherent few-shot learning ability of meta-learning. The Meta-CT scheme can also optimize the initialization of model parameters, enabling rapid adaptation to new dynamic spectrum environments. Simulation results indicate that the proposed Meta-CT scheme significantly outperforms traditional spectrum sensing schemes, achieving superior accuracy and better robustness in sparse data and low-SNR scenarios. The proposed scheme provides a novel and efficient spectrum sensing paradigm for dynamic and heterogeneous electromagnetic environments in LAINs.

title: Stacking Deep Neural Networks to Detect Multiple Types of Cardiac Arrhythmias
authors: Din Sadia, KangYoon Lee, Sghaier Guizani, Ateeq Ur Rehman, Habib Hamam
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Heart arrhythmias are associated with serious cardiovascular diseases and can result in fatal outcomes if not diagnosed early. Electrocardiograms (ECG) are generally used to diagnose heart arrhythmias. Prior studies employ deep learning architectures including Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Transformer based methods to diagnose arrhythmias from ECG signals, achieving promising results. However, most of the deep learning models when used individually exhibit inherent limitations. CNNs can learn spatial features effectively but is limited in learning temporal characteristics. LSTMs capture temporal characteristics, but struggle with capturing long-range dependencies. Transformer models are effective at modeling long-range dependencies but they are often prone to overfitting. To address the limitations of individual models while leveraging their feature learning capabilities, this work proposes a two-level stacked ensemble framework for ECG arrhythmia classification. At the first level, deep learning models including a Deep Neural Network (DNN), CNN, LSTM, and Transformer models are trained as base learners while at the second level, the predictions generated by the base learners are combined and passed to the Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) which acts as the meta-learner. MLP learns how to weigh the predictions of the first level models to generate improved final predictions. The combination of these models in a stacked ensemble framework helps exploit the strengths of all models, enabling fusion of spatial, temporal, and long-range dependency features from the ECG signal. The proposed framework is evaluated on the MIT-BIH and INCART arrhythmias databases to classify the input ECG signals into five arrhythmia categories. Comparison against the base models and prior approaches demonstrates that the proposed stacked ensemble model outperforms the base models and state-of-the-art (SOTA) techniques, achieving an F-score of 99.79% on MIT-BIH and 99.62% on INCART dataset.

title: Semantics-guided Reconstruction of Weak-Manhattan Indoor as-built BIMs from 3D Point Clouds
authors: Xuzhe Wang, Juntao Yang, Zhizhong Kang, Yutao Zhang, Jianghong Zhao, Wen Xiao, Xue Zhang, Qingtian Zeng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The reconstruction of indoor as-built BIMs from 3D point clouds has garnered considerable interest due to its significant potential in applications. However, the challenges posed by severely occluded and cluttered environments, along with the complexity of indoor building structures, complicate the automatic reconstruction task. To address these issues, we propose an effective pipeline for reconstructing weak-Manhattan indoor as-built BIMs directly from raw 3D point clouds. Based on fine-grained semantics derived from our previous studies, we first develop a semantic-guided room segmentation method without the down-sample operations for fixed-size input, thus preventing the loss of room boundary details, especially for large-scale indoor scenes. Following this, we present a novel room-wise vectorization method that does not rely on strict Manhattan assumptions. This process allows us to achieve regular room borders while preserving their original shapes as much as possible. Finally, the principal building structures and in-wall elements are converted into a popup 3D model, allowing for the subsequent assembly of comprehensive BIMs. To validate the reliability and robustness, our approach is conducted on the available S3DIS and Matterport3D datasets from both qualitative and quantitative perspectives. Results from our approach achieves higher performance than contemporary state-of-the-art methods, retaining finer boundary details and achieving decimeter-level modeling accuracy. Our code is publicly released at https://github.com/wangxuzhecode/FloorSG.

title: Aging Behaviors of Ester-Insulated Oil–Paper Composite Systems under Combined Electrical and Thermal Stresses – Part II: Aging Characteristics and Microscopic Mechanisms of Oil-Impregnated Paper
authors: Jiaming Yan, Guangliang Liu, Wenyu Ye, Yixin He, Tianxiang Ding, Xinhan Qiao, Jianwen Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: To investigate the aging characteristics and differences of insulating paper in various ester-based insulating oils, this study conducted accelerated electro-thermal aging tests on soybean-based natural ester and palm fatty acid ester (PFAE) insulating oil-paper composite insulation systems. Molecular dynamics simulations were employed to reveal the underlying mechanisms of the aging characteristics of the insulating paper. The results showed that during the early stages of aging, cellulose with a higher degree of polymerization formed more hydrogen bonds with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, leading to stronger binding energies, which accelerated the degradation of cellulose. After aging, the number of hydrogen bonds and binding energies between cellulose and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O/H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> decreased, slowing the aging rate. As a result, the aging process of the insulating paper exhibited a characteristic pattern of rapid aging initially, followed by a slower rate. Crystalline cellulose, which is less prone to forming hydrogen bonds with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, is less susceptible to hydrolytic aging, leading to a limit on the degree of polymerization at the end of the aging process. Compared to soybean-based natural ester, PFAE insulating oil showed stronger net interaction energies with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, which reduced the number of hydrogen bonds between cellulose and H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O/H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>O<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup>, thus delaying the hydrolysis of cellulose. Therefore, the aging degree of insulating paper in PFAE insulating oil is lower, and the final residual degree of polymerization in the insulating paper of PFAE insulating oil is approximately 140 higher than that in soybean-based natural ester. This paper provides theoretical insights into the aging characteristics of insulating paper in ester-based insulating oils under electro-thermal combined effects from both experimental and molecular scales.

title: Secure Fuzzy Tracking Control of Multiagent Systems: A Dynamic Sampled-Data-Based Event-Triggered Perspective
authors: Chenxi Song, Yuan Li, Yin Sheng, Zhigang Zeng
abstract: This paper develops a novel dynamic sampled-data-based event-triggered (DSET) secure fuzzy tracking control scheme for a category of unknown nonlinear heterogeneous multiagent systems (MASs). Asynchronous DSET mechanisms are developed, in which the triggering thresholds evolve dynamically with the system states. The proposed mechanisms only require discrete state sampling and triggering evaluation, thereby avoiding continuous monitoring and reducing triggering frequency. Moreover, the triggering interval is shown to admit a positive lower bound that is not solely determined by the sampling period. Under denial-of-service (DoS) attacks, distributed DSET observers are constructed to estimate the leader's system matrices and states, where only a subset of followers requires access to the leader information. A detection scheme is further designed to identify the termination of DoS attacks. Furthermore, a fuzzy fault-tolerant control scheme is proposed based solely on output measurements. The convergence of observations and tracking errors is rigorously established. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach.

title: Hybrid GRU-Based Pointer-Generator Model for Legal Clause-Level Summarization
authors: Anagha Hegde, Chinmayi P Hosur, Nishita P Jahagirdar, Tanushree Manjunath, Sumaiya Pathan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Legal contracts and statutory provisions use complicated and lengthy languages, which causes manual reviews to take a long time, and becomes difficult and there is possibility of making mistakes. In order to automate this process, a very powerful and fluent AI-powered legal summarisation that is factually accurate, able to cope with specific legal language and free of redundancy is proposed in this paper. The Legal AI system built on a hybrid Sequence-to-Sequence framework that utilizes the Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs), an attention mechanism, and the Pointer-Generator Network (PGN) for clause-level abstractive summarization. The Pointer-Generator Network (PGN) grants the model the ability to switch between the two extremes of legal generation, i.e., creating new abstractions or directly copying the most important legal terms, whereas the Coverage Mechanism and specific repetition control methods work together to eliminate redundant outputs and to improve the contextual accuracy. The application of Enhanced Beam Search decoding guarantees that summaries produced are coherent and complete. The results of the experiments based on the metrics ROUGE-1 (0.5040), ROUGE-2 (0.2851),ROUGE-L ( 0.3630), BLEU score (0.1756), and METEOR score (0.3274), show that the proposed NLP system creates brief summaries that are both legally faithful and accurate. The new system not only beats the baseline models, but also surpasses them significantly. This research presents a trustworthy and legally correlated method that can be used for understanding legal documents, thus making it possible to perform contract analysis quicker and more transparently.

title: Enhancement of Smart Antenna Systems Driven by Artificial Intelligence for Optimal Performance in Upcoming 5G and 6G Networks in Real Time
authors: S.B. Prakalya, A Thenmozhi, B Yamini, D Shobana, Nithya Dorairajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: For networks to keep up with wireless communication technologies, particularly when 5 G networks upgrade to 6 G, network performance needs to be significantly enhanced. Improved data transfer rates, dependability, ultralow latency, and network capacity are all benefits of this. By controlling radiation patterns and directing signal transmission, smart antenna systems help achieve these aims. AI can greatly enhance its responsiveness to dynamic conditions by enabling real-time adjustments. To optimise 5 G and 6 G smart antenna systems in real time, this study employs AI-driven algorithms. AI, particularly ML and DL, can alter smart antennas. As the number of users, their movement, interference, and channel conditions change, smart antennas adapt accordingly. Through real-time beamforming, resource allocation, and signal optimisation, AI enhances the signal quality and energy efficiency of antenna systems. Intelligent power control, AI-based beamforming optimisation, and interference management are the topics of this research. Smart antennas are made more effective and long-lasting by artificial intelligence systems that use reinforcement learning to adjust their parameters based on environmental feedback. Spectrum utilisation, device mobility, connectivity for busy users, and heterogeneous network performance are all enhanced by AIpowered smart antenna systems. 5G and 6G networks require smart antennas improved with artificial intelligence, according to this study. These antennas enhance dependability, throughput, latency, and energy expenditure. Improve the dependability of wireless communication infrastructure with smart antenna systems that use AI to control complex network parameters in real time.

title: A Hybrid Complex-Valued CNN-Conformer Architecture for Specific Emitter Identification
authors: Yanghao Wang, Yi Wei, Yuan Zeng, Yuan Qiu, Zhou Ye, Zhen Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Specific emitter identification (SEI) distinguishes wireless devices from their radio-frequency fingerprints. Existing complex-valued convolutional networks preserve I/Q coupling effectively, but their receptive fields remain mainly local. This paper introduces a two-layer Conformer encoder into a nine-layer complex-valued CNN backbone and studies the resulting cascade on a 90 -class R F emitter dataset [6]. Across 48 ablation runs covering 16 configurations and three random seeds, the no-skip reference configuration improves closed-set accuracy from 90.13% for a pure CNN with six pooling layers to 95.88%. Relative to full nine-layer pooling, selective pooling adds 4.24 percentage points. We treat auxiliary heads and metric-learning supervision as supporting training modules rather than the core contribution and further evaluate the same model family on 10 -way, 20 -way, and 30 -way few-shot tasks with 1,5, and 10 shots. The reference configuration reaches a global few-shot accuracy of 80.40% ± 0.56% after equally averaging the nine task settings, and the aggregated silhouette coefficient decreases from 0.2254 to 0.1680 and further to 0.1229 as task difficulty increases. The results show that adding a Conformer back end to a complex-valued CNN consistently strengthens long-range sequence modeling for SEI, while the auxiliary and metric-learning components provide secondary training support.

title: Enhancing Deepfake Detection with an Ensemble of Specialized Neural Networks
authors: B. Rajesh, R. Tamilkodi, K.Suryakala, Parasa Lakshmi Sruthi, Bandaru Anusha, K M D S Pradeep
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rise of deepfake technology poses a serious threat to information integrity, personal security, and public trust. Current detection methods often depend on one analytical model, which can be bypassed by more advanced generation techniques. This paper presents a strong, multi-modal ensemble system for detecting deepfake videos. Our proposed system combines three different, cutting-edge neural network models: Face X-ray, which identifies boundary artifacts; LipForensics, which looks for inconsistencies in lip movements; and a top-performing EfficientNet model from the Deepfake Detection Challenge (DFDC), which offers powerful general classification. By combining the predictive scores from these various models, the system achieves better and more consistent detection. The whole analysis process is handled through an easy-to-use web interface, making advanced deepfake detection accessible to those without expertise. We propose a model which works using ensemble approach with different detection methods, improving the ability to identify deepfake video content compared to single model system.

title: An Encrypted Deduplication Cloud Storage System with Efficient Data Access by Using the Pipelined Mechanism
authors: Chengjian Liu, Dong Liu, Tao Jiang, Lin Lin, Yihong Li, Lixin Liang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Data security, storage efficiency and efficient data access are critical for cloud storage. Encrypted deduplication is widely used to implement client-side encryption and cloud-side deduplication, thus ensuring both data security and storage efficiency. However, the required security operations in encrypted deduplication lead to significant performance overheads, which degrade the latency and throughput of data access. To solve this problem, we propose an encrypted deduplication cloud storage system with efficient data access and study its data access optimization. The system adopts a pipelined mechanism to overlap key operations for optimizing read and write processes, with full consideration of data security, storage efficiency and data access performance. We also explore the optimal data size for the pipelined mechanism to achieve better performance. Evaluation results show that configuring an appropriate data size is essential for the pipelined mechanism to realize superior data access performance in the system.

title: Energy Management and Techno-Economic Optimization of a Hybrid PV/Wind/Diesel/Battery Microgrid Using Metaheuristic Algorithms
authors: Abidi Lobna, Saadaoui Wajdi, Bouazzi Khaoula, Helali Abdelhamid
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Hybrid PV/wind microgrids with battery storage and diesel backup are increasingly deployed in isolated sites and weak grids, yet renewable intermittency and storage constraints complicate their techno-economic design. This paper presents a simulation-based sizing framework for a PV/wind/diesel/battery microgrid operated under a renewable-priority, rule-based energy management strategy over a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}-\mathbf{h}$</tex> horizon with state-ofcharge and converter constraints. Three continuous sizing variables (PV scaling, wind scaling, and battery capacity) are optimized using Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Differential Evolution (DE) under identical bounds and evaluation conditions. To improve robustness while preserving computational tractability, the objective is evaluated over two representative days (high-load and high-wind scenarios) extracted from annual data. The normalized objective combines average diesel-energy consumption and indicative capital expenditure (CAPEX) values. Over 20 independent runs per solver, all algorithms converge to near-identical techno-economic solutions, achieving substantial diesel-energy reduction relative to a neutral reference configuration. PSO demonstrates the highest robustness and lowest dispersion across runs, whereas DE achieves the lowest runtime. The results highlight the importance of multi-run statistical assessment and solver-agnostic workload metrics when benchmarking metaheuristic approaches for microgrid sizing.

title: Time–Frequency EEG Feature Extraction for Hand Gesture Recognition Using CNN-BiLSTM Architecture: A Temporal Dynamics Analysis
authors: Sanskruti V Bedasur, Rajashri Khanai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper outlines a way to analyze EEG signals to decode intentions for hand movement direction. It employs a deep learning model that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks (CNN-BiLSTM). We collected EEG signals using a 14-channel Emotiv EPOC+ headset at 128 Hz and processed them in the 0-30 Hz range. Using arithmetic and weighted averages, we retrieved time-frequency information from the typical EEG bands (Delta, Theta, Alpha, and Beta). This resulted in a compact 14×4×2=112-dimensional feature representation for each window. We assessed the performance of Support Vector Machines SVM), Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), EEGNet, and the proposed CNN-BiLSTM. Additionally, we tested a hybrid approach that combines a Random Forest (RF) trained on a subset of 36 features with a CNN-BiLSTM. Reducing the overfitting that emerged with all 112 features was the aim of this approach. Using data from four individuals and focusing on three classes - Right, Left, and Rest - the proposed model showed improved test accuracy with significant improvements over traditional methods (McNemar, p < 0.05) [10]. The consistent gains were confirmed by cross-validation with early halting (Friedman, p < 0.05; Nemenyi post-hoc). These findings indicate that applying CNN-BiLSTM to model spectral-temporal dynamics greatly enhances EEG-based directional movement decoding. The RF-36 hybrid also boosts reliability in situations with low confidence.

title: Towards 6G: A Compact High-Efficiency Triple-Band Microstrip Antenna for mmWave Applications
authors: Nirzar Barua, Saikat Barua, Md Nazmul Islam Sajib, Md Rakibul Islam Rony, Md. Akter Hossain, Chandan Saha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The rapid advancement of fifth-generation (5 G) wireless communication necessitates antenna designs that support high data rates, low latency, and improved spectral efficiency. In this study, a compact wideband single-patch microstrip antenna with overall dimensions of $12 \times 6 \mathrm{~mm}^{2}$ is proposed for $\mathbf{5 G}$ and millimeter-wave applications, operating at three resonant frequencies of 27.59,32.02, and 39.78 GHz. The antenna is implemented on a Rogers RT5880 lossy substrate $\left(\varepsilon_{r}=2.2, \tan \delta=\right.$ 0.0009) with a thickness of $\mathbf{1.6 ~ m m}$ and copper metallization. A symmetric slot-based patch geometry is employed to improve impedance matching and extend bandwidth. Electromagnetic simulations in CST Microwave Studio confirm strong resonances with return loss values of $-34.8,-30.3$, and -46.6 dB, along with bandwidths of 800,400, and 700 MHz. The antenna also exhibits excellent VSWR ($\approx 1$), high efficiency (up to 90%), and stable radiation performance across all operating bands. The results demonstrate that the proposed antenna provides a compact, costeffective, and reliable solution for next-generation wireless devices requiring efficient millimeter-wave communication and sensing capabilities.

title: A Personalized Course Recommendation System Using Semantic Similarity for Bridging the Skills Gap
authors: Pattarapol Tongyodkaew, Surasit Uypatchawong, Kritanat Chungnoy, Pokpong Songmuang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: In the present day, the demand for professionals with specialized skills is increasing. However, identifying the gap between knowledge gained from university education and the skills required by the job market remains a challenge. This paper presents a personalized course recommendation system that leverages semantic similarity between job descriptions and course descriptions to suggest the most relevant coursework for learners. After filtering out courses a user has already completed, the system ingests web-scraped job postings from JobsDB and official university course catalogs, then transforms all text into vector representations using four embedding techniques, TFIDF, Doc2Vec, and Sentence-BERT. Cosine similarity between a target job profile and each candidate course generates a ranked list of recommendations. The system's performance is quantitatively assessed through Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG) ranking based on relevance judgments from domainspecific undergraduate students, enabling direct comparison of each embedding strategy. By combining multi-modal data sources with NLP models and rigorous NDCG evaluation, this framework offers a promising solution for closing the gap between learners' academic backgrounds and real-world job demands.

title: Transformer-based Deep Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection in Smart Grids
authors: Gaurav Vinchurkar, Umesh Kumar Lilhore, Vimal Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: As smart grids grow in complexity and interconnectedness, they become susceptible to more cyber threats that can impact grid operations and have great financial implications. Traditional IDS systems have a hard time detecting new-generation cyber-attacks, especially the ones that are sophisticated or novel. We present a new Transformer-based deep learning model for real-time smart grid intrusion detection in this paper. Our model uses the Transformer for anomaly detection and an Autoencoder for unsupervised learning to identify deviations from regular grid performance. The performance of the model is tested on the CIC IDS Dataset, and it is shown that our approach leads to much better detection precision as compared to existing state-of-the-art methods. In particular, the proposed model provides 95% detection accuracy, improving 20% over traditional signature-based IDS, with a false positive rate improvement of 15%. Moreover, the system can detect both short-lived intrusions (over 10s of seconds) like burst power spikes and long-term attacks (e.g., data exfiltration with a much slower creep), rendering it practically flexible for dynamic and sophisticated cyber hazards. The solution is scalable, can work as part of smart grid infrastructure, and presents a resource-efficient, real-time, comprehensive response against cyberattacks.

title: Clinical Decision Support System for Multi-Class Cancer Classification Using Machine Learning
authors: Harshini Anagha, Rajesh Chelluboina, Jaya Lakshmi G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Early and correct diagnosis of cancer is essential in enhancing the survival of patients and effective treatment planning. The paper presents a machine learning-based Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) for classifying cancer into multiple classes, utilising somatic mutation data available in the Genomic Data Commons (GDC). The dataset, which consisted of about 70,000 patient samples across various cancer types, was thoroughly preprocessed, including data cleaning, normalisation, feature selection, and class imbalance regulation using the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). Three supervised learning algorithms were implemented and assessed using performance measures such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC), and ROC-AUC. The experimental findings show that the Random Forest model was more effective than other models, achieving 99.12% accuracy a consistently high performance across all evaluation measures. The optimised model was then incorporated into a CDSS with a Streamlit-based user interface and implemented using Docker to ensure scalability and real-time use in clinical settings. This system shows how genomic data, when coupled with machine learning methods, can be used to provide accurate, efficient, and scalable cancer classification to enable better clinical decision-making and enhance precision oncology.

title: A Robust Dual-Stream Microseismic Phase Picking Model on Ocean Bottom Seismometers
authors: Dinghan Lu, Yongming Huang, Yejun Sun, Hengzhi Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Accurate seismic phase picking on Ocean Bottom Seismometer data is challenged by extremely low signal-tonoise ratios. We propose CWT-EQT, an ocean microseismic phase picking model based on continuous wavelet transform, with three key innovations. First, it employs a four-channel input strategy utilizing hydrophone data to compensate for noisy horizontal components. Second, it integrates a Continuous Wavelet Transform branch to effectively separate seismic signals from ocean noise via multi-scale focusing. Third, an adaptive dynamic feature fusion mechanism is designed to automatically weigh time- and frequency-domain features. Experimental results on the OBS dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, reducing P - and S-wave Mean Absolute Errors to 0.16s and 0.29s, respectively, with an outlier fraction of 0.03. CWT-EQT exhibits superior robustness and precision compared to PickBlue.

title: Code Sphere Ledger: A Blockchain-Based Freelancing Ecosystem Leveraging Smart Contracts and NFTs for Trust and Transparency
authors: Mukul Garg, Mayank Jain, Kiran Narang, Avantika Sharma, Kartikey Mishra, Laxman Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In this paper, a blockchain-based system is introduced to facilitate the creation and sustenance of trust among employers and freelancers in the freelancing environment. The system is intended to solve core problems like the absence of transparency, delayed or unjust payments, and fraudulent behavior that are common in conventional freelancing platforms. Our CodeSphere Ledger project is a real-world application of this idea — a Decentralized Freelancing System that exists on the Solana blockchain. By taking advantage of blockchain’s immutability, smart contracts, and decentralized infrastructure, CodeSphere Ledger provides secure transactions, verifiable work history, and open interaction between all stakeholders. The system enforces contracts automatically with smart contracts, guaranteeing payment release only after employers and freelancers fulfil their respective terms. It also uses cryptographic verification to secure the integrity of data and secure against tampering, building an accurate audit trail for each project. The system also adds a decentralized reputation system that enables users to establish credibility from their on-chain activity instead of centralized comments. By these developments, CodeSphere Ledger fosters responsibility, equity, and efficiency clearing the road to a more trustworthy and decentralized future of digital freelancing.

title: A Comparative Study of Statistical and Recurrent Neural Network Models for Next-Word Prediction in Marathi
authors: Adnan Khan, Ankush D. Sawarkar, Atul R. Halmare, Rajesh Mapari, Atul Thakare, Pravin Sonarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper addresses the important challenge of creating foundational Natural Language Processing (NLP) tools for low-resource languages, with a particular focus on Marathi. Although Marathi is the third most spoken language in India, it lacks the large-scale datasets and robust benchmarks available for high-resource languages. To help address this gap, this study provides a comprehensive comparative benchmark of models for next-word prediction. We expand upon previous work by using an augmented dataset, combining the L3Cube-Pune corpus with Marathi Wikipedia text, and by leveraging pretrained FastText subword embeddings. We implement, train, and evaluate a suite of models: a classical statistical 3-gram model, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, and a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) network. Our results show that the GRU model achieves the highest predictive accuracy at 26.95ing the LSTM. More significantly, the GRU (Perplexity 232.37) demonstrates substantially lower uncertainty compared to the LSTM (Perplexity 388.99). We also establish a strong statistical baseline with the 3-gram model, which achieves a Perplexity of 11.066 on this task. This study provides a new, stronger set of performance baselines for Marathi language modeling and demonstrates the clear benefit of combining pretrained subword embeddings with modern recurrent architectures.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Detection using EfficientNet Models
authors: Srikanth Cherukuvada, Dipanwita Debnath, Sachin Mahesh Nalluri, Jethin Pokala, Hansika Sudula
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the most prevalent microvascular complications of diabetes and a major global cause of avoidable blindness. Effective clinical care and vision preservation depend on the early recognition and precise severity classification of DR. However, traditional screening techniques depend on skilled ophthalmologists manually analyzing retinal fundus images, which is laborious, subjective, and challenging to scale for large patient populations. An automated Deep Learning-based framework for multi-class severity classification and diabetic retinopathy identification is proposed in this study. To leverage complementary feature representations and improve classification resilience, the proposed system uses an ensemble of EfficientNet-B2 and EfficientNet-B3 models. To solve the class imbalance and enhance generalization, a large-scale retinal fundus image collection was created by merging the EyePACS, APTOS, and Messidor datasets with significant data augmentation. To successfully adapt the pretrained models to DR-specific features, a two-stage fine-tuning technique and standardized image preprocessing were used. Five DR severity levels were used by the ensemble framework to categorize images: No DR, Mild, Moderate, Severe, and Proliferative DR. According to the experimental results, the ensemble technique performed better than the individual models in terms of accuracy and stability, suggesting that it is appropriate for diabetic retinopathy screening applications in the real world.

title: PEIN: Perceiver-Based External Information-Enhanced Network for Fake News Detection
authors: Jialin Chen, Zhijing Yang, Jinghui Qin
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The rapid development of social media has intensified the spread of multimodal fake news, severely affecting social order, making efficient detection methods a research priority. Existing models have three limitations: insufficient cross-modal semantic capture, lack of effective external information fusion mechanisms, and no adaptive weight allocation capability. To address these issues, we propose a Perceiver-based External Information-Enhanced Network (PEIN) for multimodal fake news detection. Taking the Perceiver architecture as the core, the model introduces fixed-dimensional latent features as the hub for crossmodal information interaction, relying on its global attention advantage to effectively break the modal dimension barrier and realize Perceiver-based Cross-modal Semantic Interaction. Meanwhile, external knowledge is integrated to enhance semantic effects, and an Adaptive Fusion mechanism is designed to dynamically evaluate the contribution of each modality and feature, accurately allocate attention weights, suppress redundant interference and focus on core information. In the experimental phase, the model performance is verified on the Twitter and Weibo datasets respectively. The results show that PEIN outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) multimodal fake news detection models.

title: Acad Sync - Advancing AI-Enabled Personalized Education
authors: G Senthil Kumar, N Kirubakaran, Balaraman Sundarambal, A Afnan Sharmeen, K Sivasakthi, V Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: This research assessed the AI-enhanced personalized education platform that facilitates enhanced performance, engagement among learners and completion of a course because of adaptive content delivery. Experimental outcomes revealed that the proposed system had an overall effect of boosting the average test marks of the experimental group (58.2-73.4) as compared to the control group (58.2-64.6) by an average of 73.4. Also, the levels of engagement achieved in the traditional LMS (135 minutes every week) and in the AI-based system (208 minutes every week), as well as the overall result (course completion), improved significantly (68% and 91%, respectively). Statistical result showed that these increases were substantial and $\mathbf{p}{\lt}0.001$ and a high effect size (Cohen’s $\mathbf{d}=1.42$). The suggested adaptive model based on the Decision Tree was able to predict the performance of learners with 84% accuracy and change the content difficulty according to these results. These results show that AI-based personalization can perform better than the traditional learning platform, proving to be useful in one-on-one pacing, timely feedback, and better learning directions. Nevertheless, the paper also sheds light on the ongoing issues of equity in access, privacy, transparency, and explainability, stating that designing something ethical and implementing it in a human-centered manner is necessary. Altogether, the findings can justify the puzzle of AI-enriched personalized education as the means to make inclusive, resilient, and lifelong learning environments.

title: VaultX: A Decentralized and Secure Framework for Verifiable Document Management
authors: Shubhangi Neware, Pranay Mantri, Atharva Rajurkar, Om Deshmukh, Gaurav Mantri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The growing shift toward digital records has made it harder for individuals and organizations to keep documents safe, unchanged, and accessible over long periods [1]. Many existing systems rely on central servers, which leaves room for tampering, accidental loss, or unauthorized access [2]. To address these challenges, we introduce VaultX, a decentralized framework designed to store documents securely while making it easy to verify their authenticity whenever needed. In VaultX, cryptographic proofs are recorded on a blockchain so that users—or even third parties—can confirm the state of a document without seeing its contents [3], [4]. The files themselves are encrypted on the user’s device and stored using Lighthouse, a Filecoin-based layer built on top of IPFS that ensures long-term availability [5], [6]. The system also incorporates OCR and metadata extraction to help users search through their stored documents without exposing sensitive information [7]. Smart contracts automate the publishing and retrieval of verification records, removing the need for a central authority [8]. Thanks to its modular design, VaultX can support a range of real-world uses, from validating academic certificates to preserving legal or business documents [9], [10]. This paper explains how the system is structured, how we built it, and what we observed during testing, showing that VaultX offers a practical and privacy-conscious alternative to traditional storage solutions.

title: TRIDENT: An AI-Powered Observability Framework for Distributed Microservices
authors: Suchitra Dutta, Saikrishna Koorapati
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Observability in microservices remains difficult due to heterogeneous telemetry (logs, metrics, traces), evolving topologies, and limited high-quality labels. We present TRIDENT, an AI-powered observability framework that (i) fuses multi-modal telemetry into joint representations, (ii) ranks root causes using graph-attentive reasoning on the service dependency graph, (iii) evaluates canary rollouts with risk-aware gates in CI/CD, and (iv) monitors drift for governance. On production-like financial workloads, TRIDENT reduced mean time to recovery (MTTR) by 25, decreased monthly SLO violations by 18, cut false positives by 30, and improved p95 latency. We provide a detailed system design, algorithms, theoretical considerations, and extensive evaluation including ablations, robustness to load, sensitivity analysis, and an operator study.
