title: Research on Wearable Biomedical Sensor Data Anomaly Detection Model and Algorithm Based on Neural Network
authors: Yidong Yang, Yuhua Wei, Guanhua Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: With the rapid development of wearable biomedical sensor technology, its application in health monitoring, disease early warning and other fields is becoming more and more extensive. It can collect human physiological data in real time and provide important basis for medical decision-making. However, due to sensor failure, environmental interference and the complexity of human physiological state, the collected data often contains abnormal values, which not only affects the accuracy of the data but also may lead to wrong medical diagnosis. Therefore, this paper designs a complete data preprocessing process, including sliding window segmentation, noise processing, missing value filling and standardization; builds a neural network architecture based on the encoder-decoder structure, introduces Bi-LSTM and multi-head self-attention mechanism. Then this paper trains the experiment in a specific hardware and software environment. The results show that the model can process data effectively. Each signal in the MIT-BIH benchmark test presents corresponding physiological characteristics. The wearable device has the best performance in processing PPG signals (F1-score reaches 95.3%, etc.), and there is room for improvement in EDA and Temp signal processing performance, which verifies the reliability and generalization ability of the model.

title: Elevation-Adaptive Weighted Joint Estimation for Positioning and Synchronization in Single-Satellite LEO IoT Networks
authors: Jiawen Qin, Guangxia Li, Jian Cheng
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the challenges of simultaneous positioning and high-precision time-frequency synchronization for lowEarth orbit (LEO) satellite IoT terminals under single-satellite visibility conditions, this paper presents an engineering-efficient elevation-adaptive weighting strategy for the joint estimation of position and synchronization based on pseudo-range and Doppler measurements. By constructing a joint estimation model for terminal position, clock offset, and clock drift, and leveraging the characteristic that observation errors increase significantly at low elevation angles, an adaptive weighting strategy dependent on elevation angle is designed to effectively suppress the impact of low-quality observations. Simulation results demonstrate that compared with the traditional equal-weight least squares method, the proposed algorithm achieves approximately 40 % improvement in positioning accuracy, 30 % in time synchronization accuracy, and 39 % in frequency synchronization accuracy. The algorithm provides a practical software-defined solution with low computational overhead, eliminating the need for hardware modifications and making it highly viable for power-sensitive IoT terminals in emerging LEO constellations.

title: Optimize Diagnosis and Secure Medical Records: Apply Federated Learning Frameworks by Integrating Blockchain with AI-Driven Healthcare
authors: P. Thirusenthil Kumaran, Safeyah Tawil, V. Samuthira Pandi, T A Sundaravadivel, Vikas Verma, Bh. Prashanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Artificial intelligence can improve patient care, treatment planning, and diagnosis due to the exponential growth of medical data from healthcare digitisation. Data centralisation undermines privacy, security, regulatory compliance, and company credibility. Federated learning and blockchain technology improve diagnosis accuracy and data security in the new healthcare system. Using federated learning, geographically dispersed healthcare institutions can train AI models in compliance with regulations without disclosing any patient data. The distributed, immutable, and transparent ledger that is a part of blockchain technology improves the security of data transfer, model modifications, and access management. This unified system guarantees data integrity, transparency, and trust while facilitating AI-driven healthcare decision-making. System architecture, federated learning, healthcare AI model improvement, and blockchain security are the main areas of this research. Regarding cybersecurity, interoperability, data silos, and confidence in centralised systems, the suggested method addresses them. When healthcare companies work together securely, it improves research, patient outcomes, and diagnostic accuracy. Blockchain technology and federated learning have the potential to revolutionise healthcare systems, according to the research. Intelligence gathering, patient care, and healthcare security would all be enhanced by this.

title: Reconfigurable 2-D-Array EM-Resonator for Crack Orientation and Profile Monitoring
authors: Jun Zhang, Yuelin Ou, Zehao Huang, Bei Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) enable vibration, temperature, and pressure monitoring. However, existing sensor nodes suffer from high power consumption, limited coverage, and high cost, which restrict their adoption in structural health monitoring (SHM). A crack sensor based on a reconfigurable resonant-ring array is proposed in this paper to solve the problems of missed detection of cracks with different orientations in a single resonator and the small coverage area. Taking the resonant frequency shift as a feature, the sensor regulates the excitation sequence of the resonant-ring array through RF switches to realize crack detection and shape reconstruction. The measured results show that the detection sensitivity is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27.5 \text{MHz} / \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> for crack depth and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$76 \text{MHz} / \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> for width. The crack path is obtained by sequentially scanning each resonant-ring and converting its frequency shift to crack area. The proposed sensor can offer a lowcost and distributed solution for metal-component health monitoring and early warning.

title: BF-YOLO: YOLOV11 with BiFPN for Efficient Multi-Scale Object Detection
authors: Yanqing Yan, Zenghui Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Object detection in optical remote sensing images is challenging due to extreme scale variations, dense small targets, and complex backgrounds. To address these issues, this paper proposes BF-YOLO, an improved detection model based on YOLOv11s. Specifically, the traditional CIoU loss is replaced with Wise-IoU (WIoU v3) to better handle low-quality samples without adding computational overhead. Additionally, the default PAN neck is replaced with a Bi-directional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN) to enhance multi-scale feature fusion. Evaluated on the DIOR dataset, BF-YOLO achieves a mAP50 of 0.837 and a mAP50-95 of 0.661. This represents a +1.4% improvement in mAP50-95 over the YOLOv11s baseline. Notably, despite the structural enhancements, BF-YOLO remains lightweight with only 1 0. 1 3 M parameters, proving more efficient and accurate than the YOLOv8s baseline (11.1M parameters).

title: Bond Graph Linear Functional Observer for Sensor Fault Diagnosis
authors: Rim Attafi, Sami Souissi, Habib Dallagi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: When a system is unstable in open loop, it is possible to stabilize it by applying on the control a state feedback. This type of command is therefore called a state feedback control. In order to set up this command, it is preferable to use observers of linear functions of states, to estimate only the variable of the command. By applying a graphical tool named Bond Graph (BG), the functional observer is designed and it applied on the diagnose area. This paper addressed to the graphical functional observer design by applying the BG tool for sensor fault diagnosis purpose.

title: Phoneme-Viseme Mapping Challenges: Marathi Visual Lip-sync
authors: Monica Sagar Dhorte, Ramchandra P Bhavsar, Bhausaheb V Pawar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Human speaking is a multi-modal process .It mainly involves phonemes, sounds and also visemes. The Marathi language's phoneme-to-viseme mapping in visual lip-sync is explored in this study work. The practice of synchronizing lip movements without the use of sound is known as visual lip-sync. The study also focuses the several published studies on lip-sync in various Marathi language, arranged chronologically, covering the stages from the basic concepts , methodology, lip extraction process with various tools or models to phoneme-to-viseme mapping. Our research demonstrates the numerous difficulties of visual lip-sync in Marathi Language across a range of phonemes. Just the first three digits of the Marathi language successfully undergone from lip-sync procedure till now. At an especially basic level, we also concentrated on various Viseme groupings with visual appearance for each group in the Marathi language. It makes it rather clear that future academics working with the Marathi language have a variety of options in this area.

title: Compact Slotted Hexagonal MIMO Antenna with Enhanced Isolation for Advanced 5G Sub-6 GHz Applications
authors: Naresh Kumar K, Niranjan R, Mohan C
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This work presents a compact two-port multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) antenna specifically developed for sub-6 GHz fifth-generation (5G) wireless communication systems. The proposed antenna operates at 6 GHz and employs two identical hexagonal radiating patches loaded with H-shaped slots to improve impedance matching and broaden the operational bandwidth. The antenna is fabricated on an FR4 dielectric substrate with a relative permittivity of 4.4 and a thickness of 1.6 mm, occupying an overall size of 80 × 35 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. A metallic decoupling strip is positioned between the radiators to effectively suppress mutual coupling, achieving isolation better than –28.6 dB. Simulation results demonstrate an impedance bandwidth of 550 MHz (5.7–6.25 GHz), peak gain of 4.6 dBi, and radiation efficiency exceeding 86%. The MIMO performance metrics show an envelope correlation coefficient below 0.005, diversity gain close to 10 dB, and group delay within 0.38 ns. Owing to its compact dimensions, strong isolation, and stable radiation performance, the proposed antenna is well-suited for modern 5G handheld devices, IoT terminals, and portable wireless systems.

title: Intelligent Identification and Localization Method for Cable Fault Types Based on Deep Residual Network and Reflection Method
authors: Jian Huo, Shizhe Xia, Shuai Tao, Weihan Liu, Zhe Ren
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Cable faults pose significant risks to power system reliability and safety. This paper proposes an intelligent method for cable fault type identification and precise localization by integrating deep residual networks (ResNet-50) with time-domain reflectometry (TDR) reflection signals. The approach processes raw or preprocessed reflection waveforms through a 50-layer ResNet architecture to extract hierarchical features, enabling simultaneous multi-class fault classification (normal, short-circuit, open-circuit, low-resistance ground, high-resistance ground) and regression-based distance estimation. Experimental results on a dataset of 5,000 simulated TDR signals (with SNR 20–40 dB) and supplementary real measurements demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 96.5% fault type identification accuracy and average localization error below 1 meter across fault distances up to 1000 m, substantially outperforming conventional TDR, SVM, standard CNN, and LSTM baselines. The method exhibits strong robustness to noise and offers a promising solution for automated, intelligent cable fault diagnosis in power distribution networks.

title: AI-Driven Compliance and Audit Framework for Manufacturing Infrastructure in Automotive Connected Services and Financial Ecosystems
authors: Subramanian Sendil Kumar, Anil Lokesh Gadi, Goutham Kumar Sheelam, Dwaraka Nath Kummari, Hara Krishna Reddy Koppolu, Avinash Pamisetty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The accelerated way of digitization in manufacturing and financial sectors has led to greater complexity of data, compliance burden, and risk of cyber attacks. Current compliance monitoring solutions are not scalable, cannot be easily extended in real time and do not preserve privacy resulting in costly audit of processes and delayed detection of anomalies. This paper proposes AI-Driven Compliance and Audit Framework for Interconnected Manufacturing Infrastructures, Automotive Connected Services and Financial Ecosystems. The system also embeds Federated Learning combined with MLOps orchestration, validating distributed model training, always-on monitoring and automatic compliance checks while preserving privacy for the data. Making use of Smart Manufacturing IoT-Cloud Monitoring and Internet of Vehicles, the system eagerly scrutinizes operational, transactional, and sensor data to detect irregularities or regulate compliance. This paper present experimental evaluation with superior performance achieving 98.6% accuracy, 97.8 % F1-score, 0.985 AUC-ROC, and a recall of 96.9%, surpassing the state-of-the-art accepted centralized AI models. The study validates an AI-derived compliance tool for privacy protection—scalable, explainable and that preserves such privacy by forming consistent decision and reinforcing real-time governance over various digital environments.

title: Lightweight High-Throughput Screening of Flavor Formulations Based on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Xing Li, Yi Liu, Yikun Chen, Sheng Chen, Likun Miao, Fuyou Qi, Zhiming Ma, Zhao Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the high cost of sensory evaluation and the prolonged trial-and-error cycle in flavor and fragrance development, we propose a lightweight framework for flavor prediction and formulation prescreening based on graph neural networks (GNN). Specifically, a GNN is employed to extract 196-dimensional latent representations from the Principal Odor Map (POM) of individual molecules. For multicomponent mixtures, instead of modeling computationally expensive full-component interactions, the framework introduces an innovative maximum a posteriori (MAP)-based dimensionality reduction strategy. By automatically excluding solvents and base materials with no sensory contribution, the component with the highest physical proportion is identified as the core molecule to represent the overall formulation. On this basis, a generalized linear model (GLM) is further applied to enable rapid multilabel prediction of base flavor notes. Experimental results on a real-world industrial flavor dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high reliability and accuracy.

title: An Optimised U-Net with Quantum Particle Swarm Optimisation for Multimodal MRI Brain Tumour Segmentation
authors: S. Anusuya, Sengole Merlin, S. Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Automatic medical image segmentation helps in clinical diagnosis, treatment planning and disease prediction and monitoring. MRI brain tumour segmentation accurately identifies lesions or tumours in the image and assists physicians in distinguishing healthy tissue and its nature. This research work is a study of various deep learning models to segment brain tumours in multimodal MRI sequences, T1, T1Gd, T2, and FLAIR of BraTS 2021. The models include a basic U-Net and a U-Net optimised with Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO). U-net optimised with Quantum Particle Swarm Optimisation (QPSO) is trained and evaluated on the BraTS 2021 dataset. Results are analysed with metrics such as Accuracy, Precision, Sensitivity, Specificity, Test Loss, Mean Intersection over Union (mIoU), Dice Coefficient (DC), and class-wise Dice scores of necrotic, oedema, and enhancing tumour. The results show that the U-Net optimised with PSO and QPSO is more powerful than the standard U-Net. U-Net_PSO has produced results with $99.78\%$ of accuracy, while U-Net_QPSO shows better performance regionwise, with a Mean IoU of 0.9971 and a Dice Coefficient of 0.9985. These results prove that U-Net optimised with PSO and QPSO contributes to effective feature representation and robust segmentation, especially in cases of dense tumour heterogeneity. U-Net_QPSO is effective for analysing scalable brain MRI features and helps achieve Sustainable Development Goal 3 related to Good Health and Well-Being.

title: Enhancing Data Privacy in Edge Computing Through Hybrid Machine Learning and Blockchain Technologies
authors: M.Anitha, K.Sivaraman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The fast proliferation of edge computing has come up with serious issues of data privacy and trust within the distributed networks. The paper introduces a new hybrid system combining machine learning (ML) and blockchain platforms to provide an improved level of data privacy in edge environments. The presented approach is a hybrid of federated learning and blockchain-based secure consensus, which will allow training the models decentrally without exposing sensitive information. An encryption layer that preserves privacy guarantees the safety of the data transfer between edge nodes, whereas smart contracts handle access control and authentication independently. The hybrid infrastructure uses AI to identify anomalies and use the mitigation of threats based on their adaptability, and blockchain with a ready-to-trace immutable ledger generates transparent data. Through experimentations, it is shown that the proposed framework outperforms conventional edge privacy schemes on privacy protection, latency, and data integrity. The model obtained ~98% data privacy protection. The study adds to the coherent model that provides the connection between security, scalability and efficiency in the privacy-sensitive edge-working applications like IoT, medical, and smart cities.

title: Physical Layer Identity Authentication in Optical Networks Based on Active Feature Embedding and Deep Learning
authors: Chiboyang Zhao, Zimo Guo, Guanjun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: We propose a physical layer identity authentication scheme based on active feature embedding and FIR filters. By designing a customized fingerprint filter and combining it with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to complete identity recognition and authentication, the simulation achieves a maximum authentication accuracy of 99.3 %. The scheme maintains excellent robustness under different Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) scenarios, balancing the effectiveness of authentication and network compatibility.

title: Visible and Low-Resolution Infrared Images Fusion Detection Under Low-Visibility Conditions
authors: Yuyang Wu, Tiezhu Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Single-modality object detection often suffers from significant accuracy degradation under low visibility, while multi-modality fusion using high-resolution infrared cameras is expensive. To address these issues, we construct a pedestrian and vehicle dataset comprising paired visible and low-resolution infrared images under various low-visibility scenarios. Utilizing this dataset, we propose an efficient dual-stream detection network named Filtering Difference Fusion Network (FDF-Net). FDF-Net integrates a Median Filtering Module to suppress noise and artifacts caused by infrared image upsampling. A Difference Enhancement Module is designed to facilitate cross-modal feature interaction and enhancement at multiple scales. Finally, an Adaptive Fusion Module utilizes spatial and channel attention mechanisms to dynamically weight and fuse the multimodal features. Experimental results demonstrate that FDF-Net achieves an mAP50 of 84.8% and an mAP50:95 of 55.3%, outperforming the baseline by 4.4% and 3.2%, respectively. The proposed method significantly surpasses mainstream single-modality and fusion algorithms, providing a robust and cost-effective solution for visible and infrared image fusion detection.

title: X-Ray Intelligence: Instant Hand Bone Fracture Detection & Localisation Using Yolov8 Deep Learning Model
authors: K. Kavitha, N Rajkumar, P. Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Fracture diagnosis is difficult due to the complex structure of the hand. A radiologist needs to examine numerous X-ray images to arrive at a correct diagnosis. This process can cause treatment delays due to its length and the high likelihood of human mistakes. We hope that our deep learning-based automated fracture localisation and identification system will facilitate easier diagnoses and faster treatment times for patients. Medical picture classification and feature extraction are very effective with convolutional neural networks (CNNs), especially ResNet18. Our system can detect fractures using ResNet18 and localise them with YOLOv8. First, images from public medical libraries such as MURA, NIH, and Kaggle are acquired and calibrated to a 150x150 pixels. These are further fine-tuned to achieve a $99 \%$ localisation rate before being compared with state-of-the-art approaches. There are two groups into which they fall: those with fractures and those without.

title: When Fixed Thresholds Fail: A Negative Result Study of Reconstruction-Based GNN Anomaly Detection on SWaT
authors: Hachol Dabthong, Mahasak Ketcham, Pongsarun Boonyopakorn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with reconstruction-based anomaly scoring have demonstrated strong results on Industrial Control System (ICS) benchmarks. However, in this study, we report a negative result. A fully implemented TCN-GAT reconstruction autoencoder (PGIL-v2), trained on the SWaT dataset, achieves only F1 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.281$</tex> under fixed-percentile threshold calibration, significantly lower than the reported state-of-the-art <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1=0.886$</tex>. Through detailed analysis, we observe a substantial distribution shift between validation and test anomaly score distributions, with an estimated scale difference of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$53 \times$</tex>. While Precision remains high at 0.962, indicating that anomalies are correctly identified when thresholds are exceeded, recall is severely degraded due to threshold misalignment. As a result, only 6 of 18 attack segments are detected. These findings suggest that the performance drop is mainly caused by calibration failure under distribution shift. We further analyze segment-level behavior and discuss practical implications for improving threshold robustness in ICS anomaly detection.

title: Augmented Reality Assisted Drone Navigation with AI-Based Obstacle Detection for Enhanced Human Drone Interaction
authors: Tanatat Hansakwong, Mahasak Ketcham, Davids Makararpong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This paper presents an augmented reality (AR)-assisted drone flight control framework designed to enhance navigation efficiency, situational awareness, and operational safety in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) applications. The proposed system integrates onboard sensing modules, artificial intelligence-based environmental perception, real-time path planning, and AR-based visualization to support intuitive human-drone interaction. Sensor data collected from the UAV platform are processed using computer vision algorithms for obstacle detection and spatial estimation, while the navigation module computes a safe and optimal flight trajectory. The resulting guidance information is overlaid onto the operator's visual interface through an AR visualization engine, enabling contextual navigation assistance during flight operations. To evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed framework, comparative experiments were conducted under multiple operational scenarios using three navigation configurations: a conventional ground control interface, an AR-based visualization interface, and the proposed AR+AI framework. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reduces mission completion time, decreases collision events, increases minimum obstacle distance, and lowers operator workload compared with baseline approaches. Although the integration of AR and AI introduces additional computational latency, the system maintains real-time responsiveness within acceptable operational limits. The findings indicate that combining augmented reality guidance with intelligent environmental perception can significantly improve the safety and usability of UAV navigation systems in complex environments.

title: AngerNet: HyperFused Multi-Stream CNN–Transformer Architecture for Real-Time Anger Detection via Facial Muscle Tension Mapping
authors: A.Deepa, S.Ponmaniraj
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Anger recognition Automated recognition of anger is important in behavioral analysis and human computer interaction as well as clinical uses. The current methods usually depend on immobile images or mono modalities and this restricts their capacity to record fine, high intensity muscle activities. In this paper, it presents a HyperFused Multi-Stream CNN Transformer-based anger-detection framework named AngerNet, that uses facial muscle-tension mapping in real-time to detect anger. The suggested model involves the combination of RGB pictures, optical flow, and surface electromyography (sEMG) signals, as a result of which it is possible to extract the spatial, temporal, and physiological features in a comprehensive way. An angular displacement and skin strain measurement of key muscles corrugator supercilii, levator labii and masseter are quantified using a novel Muscle Tension Encoding Network (MTEN) resulting in increased interpretability and accuracy. CNN layers are used to extract local features and Transformer layers are used to extract cross-muscle correlations and all the modalities are fused into a single representation by multi-stream fusion. The heatmaps based on attention show predominant parts of the muscles during the onset of anger, which is explainable. Experiments on MMI Facial Expression Database and SAMM Long Videos Dataset indicate that AngerNet has a accuracy of more than 97 percent and can maintain a real time performance of (25-30 FPS). The framework demonstrates a considerable breakthrough in the ongoing affective monitoring system, which is highly accurate, interpretable, and efficient in clinical, behavioral, and interacting applications.

title: Reduced THD Seven Level Switched Capacitor inverter with Self Capacitor Balancing Capability
authors: Md Mehedi Hasan, Md Ahsanul Alam, Md Mominur Rahman Sheikh, Meherun Nesa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Increasing adoption of low-voltage based renewable energy system demands of inverter with better power quality and low harmonics distortion. This paper proposes a novel low frequency switching technique based optimized firing angle to reduce the Total Harmonics Distortion (THD) of a 7 level Switched Capacitor Multilevel Inverter to $\mathbf{1 1. 5 3 \%}$. This topology used 7 switches, $\mathbf{3}$ diodes, $\mathbf{2}$ capacitors and single battery power source. Capacitor voltages are systematically added with the dc voltage source to generate higher voltage levels. Charging and discharging of these capacitors are automatically balanced throughout cycles by employing appropriate switching states without requiring additional sensors or control hardware. PSO technique is applied to achieve the optimum switching angle for reduce THD and enhanced voltage quality. The THD of the voltage generated at the load terminal is evaluated through MATLAB/SIMULINK platform using resistive load. The superiority of the proposed topology is assessed through extensive comparative analysis with the similar one reported in the past.

title: Blockchain-Enabled IoT Biometric Pad for Secure Digital Rental Agreements with eStamp Integration
authors: M. Lakshmanan, Gaurav Londhe, Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, Sriramkumar R, Mude Nagarjuna Naik, Neeraj Jain, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The requirement to have a secure, trustful, and augmented system for the performance and confirmation of rental agreements is a consequence of the increased digitalization of legal documents. Even among the different measures through which agreements are executed, the very traditional means of paper-based systems and those that are semi-digital continue to be vulnerable to forgery, handling delays, and the absence of any clear documentation of the process. The work in this paper is about a process of agreement execution that is legally compliant and tamper-resistant and that is done through the use of a Blockchain-Enabled IoT Biometric Pad. One more important element involved in these is the eSign and eStamp services based on Aadhaar, which are included in this system. It is seen as a well working digital cyber-physical setup that combines biometric identity verification, cryptographic hashing, and ledgering on blockchain all in one to provide contracts with the characteristics of being authentic and immutable and without the possibility of repudiation. The software in this hardware–software co-design co-design runs on an embedded microcontroller, a biometric sensor, and a secure API interface that facilitates real-time blockchain synchronization. The results showed that the system has reduced the signing latency by 28%, it scales up the blockchain verification process by 38%, and there is a 34% power consumption decrease over the usage of eSign platforms that are already existing. The proposed framework delivers a ready to use, easy to grow, legal, and trustworthy connection between a person's biometric identity and the decentralized blockchain so that safe, soft, and easily audited e-transactions are enabled.

title: Phishing Urls Detection Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Preksha Patel, Raunak Raj, Pooja Sapra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Phishing attacks represent a major cybersecurity risk because they use false websites to acquire important user information, such as passwords and financial credentials. Conventional detection methods, including blacklist-based strategies, exhibit slow response times and low effectiveness against newly emerging phishing websites. This study introduces a detection framework that integrates machine learning (ML) methodologies to detect malicious URLs via extensive feature analysis. The proposed methodology examines various URL attributes, such as structural patterns, length metrics, and embedded keywords, employing a range of algorithms that include decision trees (DT), support vector machines (SVM), random forest (RF), XGBoost, and logistic regression (LoG). The proposed model has been trained and tested using a dataset of 5000 URL samples and shown exceptional performance. Results from experiments indicate that the integrated ML methodology much surpasses conventional detection techniques while preserving computing efficiency appropriate for practical implementation. This research provides a strong and scalable approach for proactive cybersecurity defense against advanced phishing threats.

title: A Deep Learning Based Intrusion Detection Framework for Smart Grids using CGAN, CNN, and SVM
authors: Mariem Ben Slama, Amal Bouazzi, Saadaoui Wajdi, Helali Abdelhamid
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Smart Grids (SG) are critical infrastructures that integrate advanced communication technologies such as 4 G, 5G, 6G, and the Internet of Things (IoT) to improve energy management and system efficiency. However, this high level of connectivity increases the exposure of Smart Grids to cyber threats. Common attacks include denial-of-service (DoS/DDoS) attacks and malware, which can seriously compromise network reliability and security. To address these challenges, efficient intrusion detection systems (IDS) are required. In this work, we propose an IDS framework based on deep learning and machine learning techniques. Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) are used to generate synthetic data in order to enhance the training process and mitigate data imbalance. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) are employed for automatic feature extraction, while Support Vector Machines (SVM) are used for intrusion classification. Experimental results show that the proposed approach improves detection performance and enhances the robustness of the IDS in Smart Grid environments.

title: GraphRAG-Driven Multi-Stage Context Retrieval for Thai Text-to-SQL Using SLM Agents
authors: Chaiyanan Sompong, Kanyaphat Khanthacha, Thanapat Chaukampeng, Saranporn Panyakun, Neeracha Ngokwong, Vutthipong Puntumnunt
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Thai Text-to-SQL continues to face significant challenges due to semantic ambiguities and synonyms. These linguistic complexities often lead to inaccurate context retrieval in traditional vector searches, thereby exacerbating SQL generation errors in Small Language Models (SLMs) due to their limited reasoning capacity. To address this, this paper proposes the integration of a Knowledge Graph (KG) that creates the model of database schema relations and incorporates synonym mappings to resolve ambiguities in table and column names. A multi-stage context retrieval process is employed to extract precise relational context, ensuring high accuracy in query generation. Finally, Typhoon SLMs within the 4 B to 12 B parameter range are utilized to generate the final SQL queries based on the enriched context. The proposed framework was validated using a real-world database from the registration and admission systems of Sakon Nakhon Rajabhat University. Based on a test suite of 178 Thai natural language queries, the system achieved a peak Execution Accuracy (EX) of 70.22% using the Typhoon2.5-Qwen-4B model. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating Knowledge Graphs with SLMs for high-precision Thai Text-to-SQL tasks.

title: Real-Time Identification and Localization of Street-Roaring Vehicles Based on Lightweight CNN and FPGA Acceleration
authors: Can Lei, Liyan Luo, Jingyu Zhang, Tao Bin
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address challenges in automatically documenting high-intensity traffic noise caused by illegally modified vehicles on urban roads, this paper proposes an intelligent monitoring approach tailored for resource-constrained embedded platforms. The system adopts a joint framework of “lightweight INT8quantized CNN noise recognition on STM32 + FPGA-accelerated sound source localization.” At the front end, a multi-microphone array performs synchronous sampling and frequency-domain noise reduction. On the STM32 MCU, a lightweight CNN optimized via quantization and pruning achieves high-precision recognition. Backend processing utilizes FPGA parallel acceleration of the GCC-PHAT algorithm for localization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model maintains 87.2 % accuracy even at a challenging 0 dB SNR. The overall localization error is controlled within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2}$</tex> meters, enabling precise lane-level identification. Compared with traditional methods, the real-time spectrum subtraction carried out by the system on the FPGA before TDOA estimation improves the quality of the input signal. This enables the system to keep the error below 2 meters even at a 5 dB signal-to-noise ratio, meeting the requirements for lane-level vehicle positioning.

title: Dual Digital Twin–Enabled Hybrid ML–DL Model for Intelligent Battery Maintenance in Electric Vehicles
authors: P. Harini, Kishore Kuma Bayyarapu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In order to improve the prediction of key battery parameters, such as State of Health, Remaining Useful Life, and battery efficiency, this paper presents a Dual Digital Twin-Enhanced Hybrid ML-DL Model of battery management in electric vehicles. This model is a comprehensive machine learning and deep learning paradigm that combines Digital Twin technology with both algorithms. The hybrid model delivers real-time information to manage an EV fleet while accounting for both short-term temporal variation and long-term wear patterns. It has been demonstrated that the hybrid model produces significantly better results than the standard methods; the prediction of SoH data was enhanced by 27.77, RUL predictions were significantly more accurate than the real-life data, and the rate of error reduction each epoch was significantly quicker. Additionally, the hybrid model exhibits superior thermal management and maintains its efficiency across a range of temperatures. The proposed solution offers precise, scalable predictive maintenance solutions that aim to maximise battery health, reduce downtime, and enhance EV performance, all of which contribute to safer and more effective EVs and a more sustainable transportation environment.

title: Explaining Household Access to Heavy Rail Services Using Interpretable Machine Learning: Evidence from the 2022 U.S. National Household Travel Survey
authors: Sayed Shahriar Islam Lamun, Syeda Tamzida Akter, Jeba Tahsin, Sanupa Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Accurate modeling of household access to heavy rail services is vital for understanding transportation behavior, urban mobility, and infrastructure planning. Traditional methods often fail to capture the complex relationships influencing whether households have access to heavy rail, primarily due to assumptions inherent in classic modeling techniques. This study introduces an interpretable machine learning framework to predict and explain household access to heavy rail services using data from the 2022 U.S. National Household Travel Survey (NHTS). The analysis utilizes three supervised learning algorithms Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) to classify households based on 17 socio-demographic, vehicular, and location-specific variables. Data preprocessing involved handling missing values, checking for multicollinearity through Variance Inflation Factor (VIF), and normalizing the dataset. Model optimization was performed using grid search and 5-fold stratified cross-validation. To ensure transparency, SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) were applied to explain the impact of each feature on predictions. Empirical results indicate high model accuracy ($\geq 96 \%$) on the held-out test set, with Random Forest and SVM demonstrating superior generalization. SHAP analysis revealed that proximity to rail stations, household vehicle availability, and urban density were the most influential factors determining heavy rail access, highlighting spatial and infrastructural constraints over demographic factors like income or household size. By combining predictive power with interpretability, this study offers an insightful, scalable approach to understanding MSA heavy rail status for households, providing evidence to support data-driven urban transportation policies.

title: Cloud-Based Intelligent Inventory Tracking System
authors: Kaviya K, Jeshlin P V, Ezhil Dyana M V
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: In the fast-paced economic environment, precise prompt decision-making tools have become necessary for traders, investors, and analysts. This paper presents StockSense, an intelligent, cloud-based web application outlined to provide AI-powered analytics and real-time stock market insights. The system combines predictive models, statistical analysis, and a dynamic dashboard interface to provide users an all-inclusive view of stock performance and investment recommendations. Built using Flask, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript technologies, StockSense holds up multiple user roles—customers, managers, and administrators—enabling personalized functionalities for each. It exploites cloud services for safe data storage, live updates, and flawless access to external APIs. The key feature of the application is an AI-powered analytics engine potentially achieving up to 92% prediction accuracy, offering recent price performance based on rule-based scoring techniques and volatility metrics. Relative evaluation through graphical representation manifests notable performance improvements over conventional and basic cloud-based systems. The proposed solution not only increases inventory and stock management accuracy but also guarantees robust, scalable, and intelligent investment support. StockSense thus epitomizes the integration of cloud computing and artificial intelligence in evolving future generation financial services.

title: TPFTrackt: Temporal Prompt Learning for Multimodal Visual Object Tracking
authors: Liutao Hu, Suting Chen, Haifei Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Existing prompt-based trackers learn prompts independently for each frame, ignoring the inherent temporal continuity of visual object tracking. Furthermore, existing prompt learning is only supervised by the final tracking loss, which provides weak and indirect guidance. To alleviate these problems, this paper proposes a temporally prompt-enhanced multimodal tracking framework, TPFTrack, based on a frozen Vision Transformer backbone network. First, we introduce a temporally guided prompt fusion mechanism that uses the prompt from the previous frame as a temporal prior to guide the fusion of multimodal prompts in the current frame. Second, we propose a temporal prompt memory module that explicitly stabilizes inter-frame prompt evolution through a lightweight memory update scheme. Furthermore, we designed a prompt consistency loss to regularize the prompt representation during training. Extensive comparative experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that TPFTrack achieves good results in various multimodal tracking scenarios.

title: Diff-Conformer: A Dual-Input Difference-Aware Convolutional Transformer Network for Building Change Detection
authors: Shengchang Li, Shuo Chen, Zipeng Fan, Zeyu Li, Zishuo Zhang, Xinyu Yang, Sihao Zhang
abstract: Change detection in remote sensing images analyzes and characterizes surface changes using multi-temporal data. Yet, in complex scenes with diverse targets, deep-learning methods often yield features that lack sufficient discrimination, leading to false alarms and lost details. To solve these challenges, we introduce Diff-Conformer, a building-change detector that unites Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and transformer strengths in a single Conv-Transformer-Branch (CTB), capturing fine-to-coarse details in one shot. Moreover, decoder parts use R-Decoder, enhancing the extraction of high-frequency change cues such as edges. Our model achieved F1-scores of 90.15% on the LEVIR-CD dataset, 86.75% on the WHU-CD dataset, and 79.48% on the SYSU-CD dataset, demonstrating superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: MADGCN: a Meteorology-Aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolution Network for Long-term Air Pollution Forecasting
authors: Binwu Wang, Zhiqing Cui, Guanjun Wang, Zhengyang Zhou, Fan Meng, Jingjia Luo, Yang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Air quality forecasting has attracted increasing attention as global air pollution worsens. Spatiotemporal graph neural networks have become a leading paradigm, thanks to their ability to capture complex spatial and temporal dynamics in Air Quality Index (AQI) data. However, existing methods remain limited by weak modeling of long-range temporal dependencies and insufficient integration of meteorological factors. Building on a publicly available nationwide air quality dataset spanning eight years, we propose MADGCN, a Meteorology-Aware Decoupled Spatio-Temporal Convolutional Network that jointly addresses long-horizon temporal modeling and meteorological context fusion. MADGCN includes a dynamic causality discovery module grounded in Granger causality, which captures time-varying causal relationships between meteorological conditions and AQI dynamics. The inferred causal structures further guide a causal graph convolution module and a PatchMixer module, enabling effective spatial interaction modeling and multiscale temporal dependency learning. Extensive experiments against 16 strong baselines show that MADGCN achieves competitive performance for long-horizon air pollution forecasting and generalizes well under high-pollution regimes.

title: Interleaved Three-Phase Series-Capacitor Boost Converter with Integrated Charge-Pump and Full-Range Current Sharing
authors: Yang Qin, Dylan Dah-Chuan Lu, Yam P. Siwakoti
abstract: The conventional three-phase series-capacitor (TPSC) boost converter offers high voltage gain, low current stress, and a common-ground configuration, but requires complex current-sharing control and exhibits severe performance degradation at duty cycles below 2/3, including increased voltage stress, distorted inductor currents, elevated input current ripple, and degraded current-sharing accuracy. To overcome these limitations, this paper establishes the essential conditions for accurate automatic current sharing over the full duty-cycle range—namely, a standard two-segment inductor current waveform and a unique current path for each inductor–diode pair. Guided by these conditions, an improved TPSC boost converter is realized by hybridizing a conventional boost with two charge-pump cells using voltage-source charging paths and clamping diodes. This distinctive architecture ensures that, under continuous conduction mode (CCM) with symmetrical control, the proposed converter maintains a voltage gain of 3/(1-d), high-precision automatic current sharing, and a clamped device voltage stress of Uo/3 across the full duty-cycle range. Despite the introduction of two additional diodes and capacitors, the higher efficiency and significantly reduced input current ripple are achieved. Experimental results from a 1000-W, 50-kHz prototype (40-100V input, 400V output) demonstrate a peak efficiency of 98.5%.

title: Redefining Vitiligo Severity Assessment: Development and Validation of VEIDI, a Multifaceted Objective Scoring Framework
authors: Anjali Thachangattil, Remya Sethulekshmi, Soumya Jagadeesan
abstract: Vitiligo is a chronic depigmenting disorder characterized by progressive melanocyte loss, resulting in irregular hypopigmented or depigmented lesions. Despite its high prevalence and psychosocial burden, clinical assessment remains largely subjective and insufficiently standardized. Conventional evaluation techniques and existing scoring systems, such as VASI and VETF, are labor-intensive, observer-dependent, and limited in reproducibility. This study introduces VEIDI (Vitiligo Extent, Intensity, and Distribution Index), a comprehensive and objective scoring framework for vitiligo severity assessment. VEIDI integrates illumination-normalized image enhancement using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), intensity-driven lesion segmentation, lesion-wise geometric characterization, and spatial distribution analysis to quantify disease burden in a standardized and interpretable manner. Clinical evaluation across multiple longitudinal sessions demonstrated high agreement with expert dermatologists, achieving an intraclass correlation coefficient (ICC) of 0.957, a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.285, and 99.6% agreement within±1 severity grade. On CPU-only hardware (Intel Core i7, 16GB RAM), VEIDI required 1.18 ± 0.16 s per image for end-to-end automated inference, supporting its practical suitability for near real-time clinical use. VEIDI demonstrates reliability comparable to reported inter-dermatologist agreement levels while significantly reducing evaluation time and observer variability. By providing a robust, reproducible, and clinically interpretable severity score, VEIDI offers a practical framework for objective vitiligo assessment with strong potential for routine clinical use, longitudinal monitoring, and standardized outcome reporting in clinical trials.

title: Quantitative design of perovskite X-ray flat-panel detectors enabled by numerical parameter optimization
authors: Yuhao Zhang, Zimeng Li, Xin Zhang, Xulong Pang, Yihong Qi, Xiaokun Liang, Zonghai Sheng, Dong Liang, Hairong Zheng, Mengling Xia, Yongshuai Ge, Haodi Wu
abstract: Metal halide perovskites have emerged as promising candidates for direct X-ray detection owing to their strong attenuation, excellent charge transport, and low-temperature processability . High X-ray absorption efficiency requires thick perovskite layers, while increased thickness simultaneously raises the probability of charge carriers being trapped by defects, leading to an inherent trade-off. Achieving an optimal balance therefore requires careful optimization of key parameters such as absorber thickness and operating electric field. However, recent studies have not systematically optimized these detection parameters. Here, we establish a simulation framework based on cascaded linear system theory to systematically optimize the performance of MAPbI<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>-based X-ray detectors. The model quantifies the trade-off between X-ray absorption and charge collection, identifying an optimal thickness of 460 μm and an electric-field range that maximizes sensitivity while preserving spatial resolution. Guided by the simulation, we fabricate pixelated detectors integrated with thin-film transistor backplanes. The optimized detector exhibits a high sensitivity of 6588 μC·Gy<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">air</sub><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> and a spatial resolution of 3.23 lp/mm at a 200 μm pixel size, in good agreement with theoretical predictions. Furthermore, the detector demonstrates high-quality two-dimensional and three-dimensional imaging capabilities, highlighting its potential for advanced medical and industrial X-ray imaging applications.

title: Secure Network Function Computation for Linear Functions, Part II: Target-Function Security
authors: Yang Bai, Xuan Guang, Raymond W. Yeung
abstract: In Part I of this two-part paper, we put forward the problem of secure network function computation.We investigated securely computing linear target functions with a wiretapper who can eavesdrop any edge subset up to a certain size r, referred to as the security level, where the security function is the identity function, i.e., we need to prevent the source messages from being leaked to the wiretapper. The notion of this security is called source security. In the current Part II of the two-part paper, we consider another interesting model which is the same as the above one except that the security function is identical to the target function, i.e., we need to prevent the target function from being leaked to the wiretapper. The notion of this security is called target-function security. We first prove a non-trivial upper bound on the secure computing capacity defined as the maximum average number of times that the target function can be securely computed with zero error at the sink node for one use of the network. The upper bound obtained is applicable to arbitrary network topologies and arbitrary security levels. In particular, when the security level r is equal to 0, the upper bound reduces to the computing capacity without security consideration. This upper bound is always not less than the upper bound obtained in Part I for source security, which accords with the relation between the secure computing capacities for target-function security and source security. We further obtain a bound on the gap between the two upper bounds, and show that the gap between the two upper bounds can be unbounded. Furthermore, we present an algebraic framework for linear (function-computing) secure codes for the target-function-security model by proving two equivalent conditions for computability and target-function security, respectively. With this framework, we develop a construction of linear secure codes for the target-function-security model and thus obtain a lower bound on the secure computing capacity. In the same spirit, we also prove an equivalent algebraic condition for source security, and so obtain an algebraic framework for linear secure codes for the source-security model. With this, we not only generalize the code construction developed in Part I for the source-security model but also can present it in a more succinct and understandable way. In addition, motivated by the fact that a source-secure code is target-function-secure but not vice versa, we compare our code construction for the target-function-security model and the generalized code construction for the source-security model, and show that the codes constructed by the generalized code construction for source security are only a very special subclass of the codes constructed by the code construction for target-function security. Further, a considerably smaller field size can be used for our code construction for target-function security compared with the generalized code construction for source security.

title: Demand- and Topology-Aware Resource Allocation in Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTNs) with Multi-Satellite Beam Hopping
authors: Samuel Martínez Zamacola, Israel Leyva-Mayorga, Ramón Martínez Rodríguez-Osorio, Petar Popovski
abstract: NGSO constellations, represented by LEO and MEO satellites, require resource-allocation frameworks that jointly address payload flexibility and communication performance. This work presents a hierarchical three-stage framework for resource allocation: demand-driven satellite cell coloring (SCC), topology-aware satellite–cell association (S2C), and multi-satellite beam hopping (BH). Our framework halves the downlink power required to reach 10% unserved capacity (UC) and reduces the peak load on inter-satellite links (ISLs) by approximately 40% compared to a baseline strategy, while controlling cell handovers. The results further show that architectural parameters dominate performance: increasing the number of beams, refining cell granularity, and scaling constellation density enable approximately 2×, 10×, and 50× reductions in power consumption, respectively. These reductions in the required downlink (DL) power and inter-satellite link (ISL) load for a given performance target can be directly translated into system-level gains at payload or constellation size level. The proposed framework provides a scalable foundation for end-to-end optimization of next-generation NGSO satellite networks.

title: Regional Differences in Sentinel-3 Radar Backscatter over Greenland and its Impact on Ice Sheet Altimetry Applications
authors: Stine Kildegaard Rose, Louise Sandberg Sørensen, Kirk M. Scanlan, Jérémie Aublanc, Filomena Catapano, Alessandro Di Bella, Pierre Féménias, Sebastian B. Simonsen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite radar altimetry operating at Ku-band has provided crucial observations of ice sheet elevation change for more than three decades, offering a direct means to assess climate impacts. Surface height is derived from radar waveforms using retracking algorithms, while waveform parameters such as the backscatter coefficient and leading-edge characteristics are commonly used to identify surface melt and complex topography. Because elevation estimates are sensitive to these parameters, corrections are required to reduce their influence. We investigate temporal variations in the Ku-band radar backscatter coefficient from Sentinel-3 over two regions of the Greenland Ice Sheet. A northern region, which generally exhibits stable backscatter conditions; however, during the summer of 2023, an anomalous decrease of 16 dB was observed, deviating from its typical behaviour. This unexpected signal motivates a comparative analysis with a southwestern region, where a pronounced and recurring seasonal cycle is observed, characterised by a 16 dB increase in backscatter and a concurrent 26 m decrease in elevation in 2023, robustly linked to summer surface melt. The contrasting responses between the two regions are interpreted in terms of differences in meltwater retention and percolation, which control the balance between surface and volume scattering, as quantified using statistical and geophysical waveform parameters. Incorporating a backscatter-height correlation coefficient into the elevation change algorithm improves agreement with ICESat-2 in the southwestern region. These results highlight the need for region- and process-specific adaptations in retracking strategies to ensure robust elevation retrievals under increasingly variable surface and climate conditions.

title: Projected Gradient System-Based Trust-Tech Method for Computing Multiple ACOPF Solutions: Theory and Deterministic Solution Methodology
authors: Hsiao-Dong Chiang, Tengmu Li, Zhi-Yuan Wang
abstract: A theory-based Projected Gradient System (PGS)-based Transformation Under Stability-reTaining Equilibria Characterization (Trust-Tech, TT) method, termed PGS-TT, is proposed to systematically and deterministically compute multiple AC Optimal Power Flow (ACOPF) solutions within a feasible component. The theoretical basis for the PGS-TT method is rigorously established, including: (i) a one-to-one correspondence between stable equilibrium points (SEPs) of the PGS and local optimal solutions of ACOPF models; (ii) each feasible component is an invariant set of the PGS and consists of the closures of the stability regions of the SEPs located in that feasible component; (iii) each stability region contains exactly one OPF solution, which eliminates redundant searches for additional OPF solutions within the same stability region; and (iv) the existence conditions of multiple OPF solutions that are linked to the existence of PGS stability boundaries. Theorems and the proposed PGS-TT method are illustrated on 3-bus and 9-bus systems exhibiting multiple local OPF solutions. Additionally, the scalability of the implemented PGS-TT method is discussed based on large-scale systems, with network sizes ranging from 1,888 to 30,000 buses, underscoring its potential for complex power-grid optimization.

title: Beat Suppression Strategy Based on EEMF Harmonic Reconstruction for Electrolytic Capacitorless PMSM Drives
authors: Dawei Ding, Yuanyang Chen, Binxing Li, Haijun Xie, Chao Luan, Gaolin Wang, Qiwei Wang, Guangdong Bi, Dianguo Xu
abstract: In electrolytic capacitorless permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives, when the harmonic component of the position fluctuation is in a canceling relationship with the corresponding harmonic introduced by the DC-link voltage fluctuation, simply suppressing the position fluctuation aggravates the beat phenomenon. This article proposes a beat suppression strategy based on extended back electromotive force (EEMF) harmonic reconstruction. Small signal analysis reveals the impedance relationship between position compensation and beat current, quantitatively clarifying the nonlinear increment of beat currents. Specifically, multiple complex filtering is utilized to extract EEMF harmonics induced by DC-link voltage fluctuations. By feeding back the electrical angular frequency as the center frequency, the right sideband harmonic component is reconstructed for position estimation based on established current and EEMF frequency characteristics. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated on an experimental platform, verifying beat suppression under various speed and torque conditions.

title: A Mechanically Robust Hybrid-Structured 3-D Printed Fresnel Zone Lens Antenna
authors: Xincheng Gao, Chen Zhao, Enqi He, Xiaoyi Liao, Yan Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a hybrid-structured Fresnel lens (HSFL) tailored for 3-D printing. It addresses the low dielectric infill density and mechanical fragility of the outer subzones within the same full-wave zone in the conventional 3-D printed multi-dielectric Fresnel lens (MDFL). Static mechanical simulations are first conducted to identify a unit cell for 3-D printing with superior mechanical properties. Subsequently, based on the derived height-infill density relationship, the outer subzone height is moderately reduced to significantly enhance the mechanical strength. To validate this design methodology, a prototype operating at 31 GHz was designed and fabricated. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that compared to the conventional MDFL, the proposed HSFL reduces the axial and lateral von Mises stresses by 60% and 43%, respectively, while maintaining equivalent electromagnetic performance.

title: Low-Complexity Parallel Syndrome Computation and Chien Search Architecture Based on Reed–Muller Transform
authors: Muxun Zhang, Suwen Song, Liyang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Bose–Chaudhuri–Hocquenghem (BCH) and Reed–Solomon (RS) codes have been widely adopted in communication systems (such as Ethernet) and storage applications. The ever-increasing data transmission rates impose higher throughput demands on RS/BCH decoders, necessitating a higher degree of parallelism in their key computing modules–syndrome computation (SC) and Chien search (CS), which significantly increases the hardware complexity. The Reed–Muller (RM) transform has been introduced to reduce complexity, which decomposes a special class of Vandermonde matrices into a sparse matrix and an RM matrix. However, its fully parallel requirement renders it infeasible in real-world systems. In this article, we propose a novel partially parallel algorithm based on RM transform for SC and CS modules. First, the computation matrix required by the RM transform is partitioned according to power-of-two parallelism, and a set of transform matrices is constructed to enable mutual conversion, thereby allowing partial parallelization of both the SC and CS modules. Then, the original RM transform is applied to the partitioned matrices to preserve the inherent low-complexity advantage. Finally, we elaborately design the corresponding hardware architecture, in which a flexible matrix-reuse strategy is proposed to further reduce hardware cost. Synthesized under 28-nm CMOS technology, the proposed SC architecture achieves up to 45% area reduction compared with state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: FANet: Fovea Attention Network for Robust Aerial Geo-localization Across Diverse Weather Conditions
authors: Jiahao Wen, Hang Yu, Zhedong Zheng
abstract: Cross-view geo-localization between drone and satellite images is severely challenged by rapid weather variations, which induce appearance shifts, occlusions, and texture degradation. Inspired by human foveal attention, we propose the Fovea Attention Network (FANet), a robust dual-branch framework comprising: (1) The Weather-Adaptive Global Branch (WAGB) that explicitly injects weather cues (e.g., ‘rain/snow’) into the feature space via a style-modulation encoder, then captures large-scale structural consistency through a Learnable Region Reassembly (LRR) mechanism; (2) The Local Semantic Attention Branch (LSAB) that leverages a pretrained segmentation model to generate high-confidence masks, distilling discriminative features from salient regions. An adaptive fusion strategy module fuses global context with fine-grained semantic cues. We further adopt multi-weather adaptive training, treating weather types as related tasks with shared parameters to reduce cross-weather confounding. Extensive experiments on University-1652, SUES-200, and CVUSA show FANet achieves competitive Recall@1 across all conditions, attaining the highest overall mean with the lowest variance. Notably, it improves Recall@1 by 6.79% under severe low-illumination (‘dark’) conditions, demonstrating robustness and stability.

title: Distilling the Knowledge of LLMs for Automatic Speech Recognition
authors: Hayato Futami, Tatsuya Kawahara
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Recently, applications of large language models (LLMs) to automatic speech recognition (ASR) have been investigated, including post-processing and speech LLMs. In these approaches, ASR can benefit from the pretrained knowledge of LLMs, but suffers from the increased model size and computational cost. Knowledge distillation (KD) is a promising approach that transfers LM knowledge into ASR without increasing the model size and computational cost during inference. While bidirectional predictions of masked LMs perform better than causal LMs as teacher labels, the current mainstream of LLMs is based on causal LM architecture. In this study, we propose pseudo-bidirectional KD to obtain bidirectional teacher labels from a causal LLM by calculating the predicted probabilities of the subsequent context. In conventional KD, the vocabulary of ASR needs to be matched with that of LLM, but this is often impractical because the vocabulary of LLM is often too large for ASR, leading to performance degradation and increased model size. To address this, we also propose cross-vocabulary KD from LLM to ASR by aligning the token sequences and then the token entries. Experimental evaluations on the CSJ, LibriSpeech, and CommonVoice Galician corpora show that the proposed pseudo-bidirectional KD consistently yielded better performance improvement than the conventional KD. We also show the effectiveness of cross-vocabulary KD by adopting a vocabulary of a smaller size for ASR.
